// DBML Schema for Business: Retail
// Version: 1
// Generated on: 2026-05-04 11:09:01

Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Retail - Version 1'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record representing a SKU-node combination at a point in time. Primary key for the stock_position data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Inventory turn, GMROI, and weeks-of-supply KPIs are calculated and reported by merchandise category. Category-level stock position aggregation is required for OTB reconciliation and buyer performance ']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or dark store) where this stock position is held. Supports omnichannel fulfillment visibility across all network nodes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this stock position is recorded. Links to the product master in the Product Information Management (PIM) system and Informatica MDM golden record.']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Inventory valuation and ATP calculations require current retail pricing. Stock positions must reference active pricing to calculate extended retail value, markdown triggers based on aging inventory, a']
  available_to_promise_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU available to commit to new customer orders or fulfillment requests. Calculated as on-hand minus reserved, damaged, and quarantine quantities. Authoritative ATP signal consumed by Salesforce Commerce Cloud for omnichannel order promising (BOPIS, ROPIS, Ship-from-Store).']
  avg_daily_sales_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rolling average quantity of the SKU sold per day at this node, calculated from SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) POS transaction data. Used to compute Weeks of Supply (WOS), reorder point, and safety stock. Input to Blue Yonder Demand Planning forecasts.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Supplier or manufacturer batch/lot number for the current inventory of this SKU at this node. Required for batch-managed products (food, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics) to support FDA recall traceability, CPSC compliance, and Return to Vendor (RTV) processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR/CCPA data lifecycle management requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost and inventory valuation amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across international retail markets.']
  damaged_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU identified as damaged, defective, or unsellable at the inventory node. Excluded from Available-to-Promise (ATP) calculations. Tracked for shrinkage reporting, Return to Vendor (RTV) processing, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) write-down.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the current inventory batch for perishable or date-sensitive SKUs (grocery, pharmacy, cosmetics). Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation, FDA food safety compliance, and proactive markdown or disposal before expiry.']
  in_transit_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU currently in transit from a supplier, Distribution Center (DC), or another node and expected to arrive at this inventory node. Sourced from Blue Yonder Demand Planning and SAP S/4HANA WM transfer orders. Used for future ATP projection.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to value this SKU\'s inventory for financial reporting. FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), WAC (Weighted Average Cost), standard_cost, or retail_method. Governed by FASB ASC 330 and reported in financial P&L statements.']
  is_dead_stock boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this SKU at this node is classified as dead stock — unsold, obsolete inventory with no recent sales velocity. True when sell-through rate is zero and weeks of supply exceeds the dead stock threshold. Triggers markdown or clearance actions.']
  is_rfid_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU at this node is tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility. True enables automated cycle counts and reduces manual scanning overhead. Relevant for apparel and high-value electronics categories.']
  is_vmi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU-node stock position is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement, where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels. When true, replenishment is supplier-initiated via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) rather than retailer-triggered.']
  last_adjustment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent manual or system-generated inventory adjustment (positive or negative) for this SKU at this node. Adjustments include shrinkage write-offs, cycle count corrections, and damage write-downs posted in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical cycle count or inventory audit performed for this SKU at this node. Used to assess on-hand quantity accuracy, identify shrinkage, and schedule next count. Supports OSHA and internal audit compliance for inventory accuracy.']
  last_goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods receipt (inbound delivery) that updated the on-hand quantity for this SKU at this node. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM goods receipt postings and Manhattan Associates WMS inbound transactions. Used for freshness and lead time analysis.']
  last_sale_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent Point of Sale (POS) or e-commerce sale transaction that depleted stock for this SKU at this node. Sourced from SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR). Used for dead stock identification, velocity scoring, and markdown trigger analysis.']
  max_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable on-hand quantity for this SKU at this node, used to prevent overstock and optimize shelf/storage capacity. Defined in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) and used by replenishment planning to cap order quantities within Open-to-Buy (OTB) constraints.']
  on_hand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the SKU currently present at the inventory node, including all stock categories (sellable, reserved, damaged). Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS goods receipt and adjustment transactions. Core metric for inventory visibility.']
  on_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU on open purchase orders or replenishment orders placed with suppliers but not yet shipped. Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) purchase order lines. Represents future supply commitment within the Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock position record. Active: normal sellable stock; frozen: temporarily locked pending investigation; quarantine: damaged or suspect stock held for quality review; blocked: SAP-blocked stock not available for sale; closed: position deactivated (zero stock, node closed).']
  position_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which this stock position snapshot was captured or last updated. Represents the real-world event time of the inventory state. Critical for real-time ATP (Available-to-Promise) accuracy and omnichannel fulfillment SLA compliance.']
  quarantine_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU placed in quarantine pending quality inspection, regulatory hold, or recall investigation. Separate from damaged stock. Relevant for FDA food safety holds, CPSC recall compliance, and supplier quality disputes.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum on-hand quantity threshold at which a replenishment order is automatically triggered for this SKU at this node. Calculated by Blue Yonder Demand Planning based on lead time, demand velocity, and safety stock parameters. Core input to replenishment trigger logic.']
  replenishment_status varchar [note: 'Current replenishment workflow status for this SKU-node combination. normal: no action needed; triggered: reorder point breached, order pending; in_progress: replenishment order placed and open; suspended: replenishment paused (e.g., seasonal end, discontinuation); vmi: under Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) control.']
  reserved_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU reserved against confirmed customer orders, BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), or internal transfer orders. Not available for new ATP commitments until reservation is released or fulfilled.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Buffer stock quantity maintained above the reorder point to protect against demand variability and supply lead time uncertainty. Set by Blue Yonder Demand Planning. Prevents stockouts during peak demand or supplier delays.']
  sell_through_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of received inventory that has been sold within a defined period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75%). Key performance indicator for assortment efficiency, markdown timing, and dead stock identification. Calculated from SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) POS data.']
  shrinkage_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of inventory loss attributed to shrinkage (theft, administrative error, vendor fraud, or damage) since the last cycle count or physical inventory. Used for shrinkage monitoring, loss prevention analytics, and COGS adjustment reporting.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., SAP material document number, Manhattan Associates WMS inventory record ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation, audit, and EDI dispute resolution.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this stock position record. SAP_S4: SAP S/4HANA; ORMS: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; MAWMS: Manhattan Associates WMS; BYOND: Blue Yonder Demand Planning; SFCC: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; CAR: SAP Customer Activity Repository. Supports data lineage and Master Data Management (MDM) reconciliation.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific storage location, bin, aisle, or zone within the inventory node where this SKU is physically stored. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS. Used for pick/pack/ship operations, cycle counts, and planogram (POG) compliance in store operations.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of the SKU used for inventory valuation and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module. Confidential as it reflects supplier pricing and margin strategy. Used for GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which all quantity fields for this SKU are expressed. EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), LT (litre), M (metre), M2 (square metre), M3 (cubic metre). Aligned with GS1 unit of measure codes and SAP S/4HANA base UOM configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|KG|LB|LT|M|M2|M3 — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code (UPC) or European Article Number (EAN) barcode identifier for the SKU. Used for Point of Sale (POS) scanning, RFID tracking, and GS1-compliant product identification across the supply chain.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this stock position record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail compliance.']
  velocity_band varchar [note: 'ABC/D velocity classification of the SKU at this node based on sales frequency and volume. A: fast-moving (top sellers); B: medium-moving; C: slow-moving; D: very slow or non-moving (dead stock candidate). Used for replenishment prioritization, planogram slotting, and markdown strategy.']
  weeks_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of weeks the current on-hand quantity is projected to last based on the current average weekly sales velocity. Key inventory health metric (WOS) used by merchants and planners to identify overstock, understock, and dead stock situations.']

  Note: 'Real-time on-hand stock position for every SKU at each inventory node (store, DC, MFC, dark store). Captures on-hand quantity, available-to-promise (ATP), reserved quantity, in-transit quantity, on-order quantity, damaged/quarantine quantity, and velocity indicators. Authoritative SSOT for current inventory levels consumed by omnichannel fulfillment (ATP/promising), replenishment triggers, and store operations. Updated by goods receipts, sales depletions, adjustments, transfers, and reservations.'
}

Table inventory.stock_ledger {
  stock_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each stock ledger transaction record. Primary key for the perpetual inventory ledger at the SKU-location-date level.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Stock ledger transactions (receipts, adjustments, transfers, sales) are executed by associates. Retail operations require tracking transaction ownership for audit trails, loss prevention investigation']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_event. Business justification: Inventory transactions are examined during compliance audits (cycle count audits, shrinkage investigations, financial audits). Retail operations require linking ledger entries to audit events for evid']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Financial close process requires inventory transaction detail by category for COGS calculation, shrink attribution, and margin reconciliation. Category-level P&L impact analysis is a core retail accou']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory transactions are allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L and budget variance analysis. Stock_ledger has cost_center_code that needs FK to enable cost center performance reporting and ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Every inventory transaction posts to a GL account for financial statement preparation. Stock_ledger has gl_account_code (plain attribute) that should be a proper FK to gl_account for inventory valuati']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or dark store) where the stock movement occurred.']
  reversed_transaction_stock_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original stock_ledger_id that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag = True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal entries.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) involved in this stock movement transaction. Links to the product master for item-level inventory tracking.']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Financial reconciliation requires linking inventory movements to pricing effective at transaction time. COGS calculations, margin analysis, and GL posting validation depend on capturing the price-at-t']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier associated with this stock movement, applicable for receipt and Direct Store Delivery (DSD) transactions. Null for internal transfers and sales depletions.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The omnichannel fulfillment channel through which this stock movement was triggered. STORE = in-store POS sale; ECOMMERCE = online order depletion; BOPIS = Buy Online Pick Up In Store; SHIP_FROM_STORE = store-as-fulfillment-center; DC = Distribution Center; MFC = Micro-Fulfillment Center; DARK_STORE = fulfillment-only location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STORE|ECOMMERCE|BOPIS|SHIP_FROM_STORE|DC|MFC|DARK_STORE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this stock ledger record was first created in the data platform. Represents the system audit creation time, distinct from the business transaction_timestamp. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer processing tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which unit_cost, extended_cost, unit_retail_price, and extended_retail_value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency retail operations.']
  dead_stock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock movement is associated with dead stock (unsold, obsolete inventory being written off or liquidated). True = dead stock write-off or clearance movement. Supports inventory health reporting and markdown decision-making.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this receipt transaction was fulfilled via Direct Store Delivery (DSD), where the supplier delivers directly to the store bypassing the Distribution Center (DC). True = DSD transaction. Impacts supply chain routing and vendor compliance tracking.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the inventory units involved in this transaction. Applicable for perishable grocery, pharmaceutical, and health/beauty products. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation, dead stock identification, and FDA compliance.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost value of this stock movement, calculated as movement_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Represents the financial impact on inventory valuation and feeds the general ledger Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) account. Used for financial reconciliation.']
  extended_retail_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total retail value of this stock movement, calculated as movement_quantity multiplied by unit_retail_price. Used in the retail inventory method for inventory valuation and supports gross margin return on investment (GMROI) reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal period (year and period number, e.g., 2024-P01) to which this stock movement is attributed for financial reporting and inventory reconciliation. Supports 4-4-5 and 4-5-4 retail calendar structures.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s lot or batch number associated with the inventory units in this transaction. Critical for food safety traceability, product recall management, and FDA/CPSC compliance. Supports FIFO and specific identification costing methods.']
  movement_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of units moved in this transaction. Positive values indicate stock increases (receipts, returns, transfer-in); negative values indicate stock decreases (sales, write-offs, transfer-out). Expressed in the SKU\'s base unit of measure.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code providing additional context for the stock movement (e.g., VENDOR_RETURN, DAMAGED_GOODS, CYCLE_COUNT_VARIANCE, THEFT_SHRINKAGE, EXPIRED_PRODUCT, PROMOTIONAL_WRITE_DOWN). Supports shrinkage analysis and loss prevention reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VENDOR_RETURN|DAMAGED_GOODS|CYCLE_COUNT_VARIANCE|THEFT_SHRINKAGE|EXPIRED_PRODUCT|PROMOTIONAL_WRITE_DOWN|CUSTOMER_RETURN|INTER_DC_TRANSFER — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context or commentary on this stock movement, entered by warehouse staff, store associates, or system processes (e.g., cycle count discrepancy explanation, damage description, special handling notes). Supports operational investigation and audit.']
  on_hand_quantity_after decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total on-hand inventory quantity for this SKU at this location immediately after this transaction was applied. Represents the running balance of the perpetual inventory ledger and is the authoritative stock position for omnichannel fulfillment visibility.']
  on_hand_quantity_before decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total on-hand inventory quantity for this SKU at this location immediately before this transaction was applied. Enables point-in-time inventory reconstruction and audit trail validation of the perpetual ledger.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which this stock movement is posted to the general ledger and inventory valuation records. May differ from transaction_timestamp due to cut-off processing, period-end adjustments, or delayed DSD confirmations.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The source document number from the originating business transaction that triggered this stock movement (e.g., Purchase Order number, Transfer Order number, POS receipt number, Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) number). Enables end-to-end traceability.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of source document associated with this stock movement (e.g., PURCHASE_ORDER, TRANSFER_ORDER, POS_RECEIPT, RMA = Return Merchandise Authorization, CYCLE_COUNT, WRITE_OFF_ORDER). Enables document-level drill-through for audit and reconciliation.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock ledger entry is a reversal (counter-entry) of a previously posted transaction. True = this record reverses a prior movement. Used to identify corrective entries and maintain a clean audit trail for financial reconciliation.']
  rfid_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock movement was captured or validated using Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology. True = RFID-confirmed movement. Supports inventory accuracy benchmarking and RFID program performance reporting.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the specific inventory unit involved in this transaction. Applicable for high-value electronics, appliances, and serialized items. Supports specific identification costing, warranty tracking, and CPSC recall management.']
  shrinkage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock movement represents inventory shrinkage (loss due to theft, damage, administrative error, or vendor fraud). True = shrinkage event. Used for shrinkage monitoring, loss prevention analytics, and CPSC/NRF compliance reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this stock ledger transaction (e.g., SAP_S4 = SAP S/4HANA Retail, ORMS = Oracle Retail Merchandising System, MANHATTAN_WMS = Manhattan Associates WMS, SFCC = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP_CAR = SAP Customer Activity Repository). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this stock movement transaction, sourced from the originating system (e.g., SAP material document number, ORMS transaction reference). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit tracing.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stock ledger transaction. POSTED = confirmed and applied to on-hand balance; PENDING = awaiting confirmation; REVERSED = corrected by a counter-entry; VOIDED = cancelled before posting; ERROR = failed validation.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world date and time when the stock movement event occurred (e.g., POS scan time, receiving dock timestamp, cycle count completion time). This is the principal business event time, distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock movement event driving this ledger entry. RECEIPT = inbound from supplier or DC; SALE = Point of Sale (POS) depletion; TRANSFER_IN/OUT = inter-location movement; ADJUSTMENT = cycle count correction; RETURN = customer or Return to Vendor (RTV); WRITE_OFF = shrinkage or dead stock removal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIPT|SALE|TRANSFER_IN|TRANSFER_OUT|ADJUSTMENT|RETURN|WRITE_OFF|DSD_RECEIPT|MARKDOWN_WRITE_DOWN|CYCLE_COUNT — promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The per-unit cost (Cost of Goods Sold - COGS) applied to this stock movement at the time of the transaction. Used for inventory valuation under FIFO, Weighted Average Cost (WAC), or retail inventory method. Sourced from the active cost layer in SAP S/4HANA.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the movement_quantity is expressed (e.g., EA = each, CS = case, PK = pack, LB = pound, KG = kilogram). Aligns with GS1 unit of measure codes and the SKU\'s base UOM in the product master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|LB|KG|OZ|L|ML|FT|M — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The retail selling price per unit at the time of the transaction. Used for inventory valuation under the retail inventory method and for calculating gross margin. Supports Average Unit Retail (AUR) analytics and sell-through rate calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this stock ledger record was last modified in the data platform (e.g., due to a reversal posting, status correction, or revaluation). Used for change data capture, audit trail, and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The inventory costing method applied to this stock movement for financial valuation purposes. FIFO = First In First Out; WAC = Weighted Average Cost; RETAIL_METHOD = retail inventory method; LIFO = Last In First Out (where permitted); SPECIFIC_ID = specific identification for high-value items.']
  vmf_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock movement is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) arrangement, where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels. True = VMI-managed transaction. Impacts replenishment ownership and supplier performance tracking.']

  Note: 'Perpetual inventory ledger capturing every stock movement transaction at the SKU-location-date level. Records receipts, sales depletions, transfers, adjustments, returns, and write-offs with quantity, unit cost (COGS), and extended value. Supports inventory valuation methods (FIFO, WAC, retail method), shrinkage tracking, and financial reconciliation with the general ledger. Authoritative SSOT for inventory value and movement history within the inventory domain.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_node {
  inventory_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for every physical or logical inventory-holding location in the retail network (stores, DCs, MFCs, dark stores, vendor-managed locations, in-transit nodes). Primary key of this master record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the inventory node facility. Used for carrier routing, last-mile delivery coordination, and regulatory filings.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail (suite, dock number, building identifier) for the inventory node facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the inventory node is located. Used for geographic reporting, tax jurisdiction assignment, and carrier zone mapping.']
  close_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the inventory node was permanently closed or decommissioned. Null for active nodes. Used for historical inventory reporting and asset disposal tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the node location. Governs applicable regulatory frameworks (FDA, CPSC, GDPR/CCPA), currency, and customs rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory node master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports MDM audit trails and data lineage in Informatica MDM.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which physical inventory cycle counts are conducted at this node. Drives WMS task scheduling and shrinkage monitoring cadence.']
  district_code varchar [note: 'Internal district code within a region, used for field management span-of-control, district-level KPI reporting, and workforce scheduling via Workday HCM.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of inbound/outbound dock doors at the node. Determines receiving throughput capacity, inbound appointment scheduling windows, and cross-docking feasibility in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  format_code varchar [note: 'Retail format identifier assigned to the node (e.g., \'HYPERMARKET\', \'DISCOUNT\', \'DEPT_STORE\', \'ECOMM_FC\'). Drives assortment breadth/depth rules in ORMS and planogram (POG) assignments.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1-issued 13-digit Global Location Number uniquely identifying this node in the global supply chain. Used in EDI transactions (ASC X12 / EDIFACT), RFID-based receiving, and supplier communications.']
  is_bopis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node supports Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) fulfillment. Drives order routing logic in the OMS and Salesforce Commerce Cloud checkout flow.']
  is_drop_ship_origin boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node acts as a drop-ship origin point where a vendor ships directly to the end customer on behalf of the retailer, bypassing DC inventory.']
  is_rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is equipped with RFID infrastructure for real-time inventory tracking, cycle count automation, and shrinkage reduction.']
  is_ropis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node supports Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS), allowing customers to reserve items online and collect without pre-payment.']
  is_ship_from_store_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is configured as a Ship-from-Store (SFS) fulfillment center, allowing it to pick, pack, and ship e-commerce orders directly to customers.']
  is_vmi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node participates in a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory cycle count performed at this node. Used to assess inventory accuracy, schedule next count, and monitor shrinkage compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inventory node master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), MDM synchronization, and Silver Layer incremental processing in Databricks.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS-84 decimal latitude coordinate of the node. Enables geospatial proximity analysis for BOPIS radius calculations, last-mile routing optimization, and store clustering.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS-84 decimal longitude coordinate of the node. Used alongside latitude for geospatial routing, catchment area analysis, and omnichannel fulfillment optimization.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the store manager, DC operations manager, or facility lead responsible for this inventory node. Used for escalation routing, audit accountability, and Workday HCM workforce reporting.']
  max_sku_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) the node can actively stock based on shelf slots, bin locations, and planogram (POG) configuration. Used in assortment planning and OTB (Open to Buy) calculations.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the node as maintained in SAP S/4HANA WM and Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Used in EDI transactions, purchase orders, and replenishment plans to reference the location. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the inventory node (e.g., \'Chicago Midway DC\', \'Store #0421 – Oak Park\'). Used in operational reports, planograms, and WMS screens. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the inventory-holding location. Drives fulfillment capability rules, replenishment logic, and reporting hierarchies. Valid values: store (retail selling floor), distribution_center (DC), micro_fulfillment_center (MFC), dark_store (fulfillment-only), vendor_managed (VMI location), in_transit (goods in movement). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).']
  open_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the inventory node became operationally active and began receiving or fulfilling inventory. Used for comp sales (SSS) eligibility determination and new-store ramp analysis.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inventory node. Controls whether the node is eligible to receive stock, fulfill orders, or appear in replenishment plans. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership or operating arrangement for the node facility. Drives lease accounting under FASB ASC 842, asset capitalization rules, and P&L cost allocation.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary operational phone number for the inventory node facility. Used for carrier coordination, inbound appointment confirmation, and emergency contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the inventory node. Used for carrier zone determination, last-mile delivery routing, and geographic market analysis.']
  primary_dc_node_code varchar [note: 'Node code of the primary Distribution Center (DC) that replenishes this node. Used in replenishment routing, inbound appointment scheduling, and supply chain network optimization.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal geographic region code grouping nodes for regional P&L reporting, district management, and comp sales (SSS) analysis (e.g., \'MIDWEST\', \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST_COAST\').']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from replenishment order placement to stock receipt at this node. Used by Blue Yonder Demand Planning to calculate reorder points, weeks of supply (WOS), and safety stock levels.']
  safety_stock_days varchar [note: 'Minimum days of supply maintained as safety stock buffer at this node to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Feeds Blue Yonder replenishment planning and WOS calculations.']
  selling_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net selling floor area in square feet, excluding back-of-house, receiving docks, and storage. Used for sales-per-square-foot KPI, planogram space allocation, and GMROI analysis.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 state or province code for the node location. Drives sales tax rules, labor law compliance, and regional reporting.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer granularity within node_type (e.g., hypermarket, department_store, discount_outlet, cross_dock_dc, ambient_dc, cold_chain_dc, ship_from_store_hub). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hypermarket|department_store|discount_outlet|cross_dock_dc|ambient_dc|cold_chain_dc|ship_from_store_hub|ecommerce_fc — promote to reference product]']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Storage temperature classification for the node. Governs which SKU categories (grocery, frozen, pharma) can be stocked and drives cold-chain compliance requirements under FDA food safety regulations.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the node (e.g., \'America/Chicago\'). Critical for accurate timestamp normalization of POS transactions, cycle count scheduling, and SLA window calculations across multi-timezone networks.']
  total_storage_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total physical storage area of the node measured in square feet. Used for capacity planning, space utilization analysis, and GMROI calculations. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE (MASTER_RESOURCE canonical category).']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical or logical inventory-holding location in the network: stores, distribution centers (DCs), micro-fulfillment centers (MFCs), dark stores, vendor-managed locations, and in-transit nodes. Captures node type, storage capacity, fulfillment capabilities (BOPIS, ship-from-store, ship-from-DC), operational status, geographic coordinates, and time zone. Serves as the SSOT for inventory location identity within the inventory domain; referenced by stock_position, stock_ledger, and all movement/receipt records as the location dimension.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the replenishment order record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: Buyers approve replenishment orders for their assigned categories and monitor order status, receipt performance, and inventory investment against OTB budgets. Buyer accountability for in-stock positio']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Replenishment planning and execution is a cost center function with associated labor and system costs. Replenishment_order needs cost_center_id to allocate planning/buying costs and measure replenishm']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Purchase order costing and landed cost validation require linking replenishment orders to contracted vendor cost structures. Enables variance analysis between ordered cost and actual invoice cost, and']
  created_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Manual and emergency replenishment orders should track the creating associate for performance analysis, process improvement, and training needs assessment. Critical for understanding replenishment dec']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand forecast record that informed the replenishment quantity calculation. Links to the Blue Yonder Demand Planning forecast used to size this order.']
  destination_node_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving fulfillment node (store, DC, or MFC) where replenished inventory will be delivered. Drives last-mile delivery routing and receiving dock scheduling.']
  environmental_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_event. Business justification: Hazmat replenishment orders (chemicals, refrigerants, batteries) are linked to environmental compliance events (spills during receiving, improper disposal, manifest tracking). Retail environmental com']
  inbound_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound receiving appointment scheduled at the destination DC or store for this replenishment order. Links to supplychain.inbound_appointment for dock scheduling and receiving resource planning. Nullable until appointment is booked.']
  primary_replenishment_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating fulfillment node (Distribution Center, Micro-Fulfillment Center, or supplier location) from which inventory will be shipped. Supports omnichannel inventory routing logic.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotion event that drove this replenishment order, when trigger_type is promotional. Links to the promotion master to support promotional inventory planning and post-event analysis. Nullable for non-promotional orders.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated purchase order in SAP S/4HANA MM or Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) when the replenishment order results in a supplier PO. Nullable for internal DC-to-store transfers.']
  reorder_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the reorder policy that triggered this replenishment order. Links to the replenishment plan thresholds (reorder point, safety stock, max stock) configured in Blue Yonder Demand Planning.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being replenished. Links to the product master in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) and Informatica MDM product golden record.']
  supplychain_edi_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the EDI transaction record used to transmit this replenishment order to the supplier via Electronic Data Interchange (EDI). Links to the supplychain.supplychain_edi_transaction product for end-to-end EDI traceability. Nullable for non-EDI orders.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) fulfilling this replenishment order. Represents the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction. Links to the supplier master managed in Informatica MDM and SAP S/4HANA MM vendor master.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the replenishment order was physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Compared against expected_delivery_date to measure supplier on-time delivery performance and SLA compliance.']
  approved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity confirmed and approved for fulfillment after supplier acceptance, MOQ compliance checks, and buyer review. May differ from ordered_quantity due to supplier constraints or buyer adjustments.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time the replenishment order was approved by the buyer or automatically confirmed by the system. Marks the transition from draft/submitted to approved status in the order lifecycle.']
  buyer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the merchandise buyer or replenishment analyst providing context for manual overrides, special handling instructions, or supplier communication details for this replenishment order.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for cancelling the replenishment order. Overstock = destination node already has sufficient inventory; supplier_oos = supplier cannot fulfill; price_discrepancy = cost mismatch; duplicate_order = order already exists; demand_change = forecast revision eliminated need. Nullable for non-cancelled orders.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time the replenishment order was cancelled, if applicable. Nullable for active or completed orders. Used for supplier chargeback processing and demand planning feedback loops.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this replenishment order record was first captured in the Silver Layer data platform. Used for data lineage and pipeline monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this replenishment order (unit_cost, total_order_cost). Supports multi-currency operations for international supplier transactions.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'The anticipated date on which the replenishment order is expected to arrive at the destination node, calculated from order_date plus lead_time_days. Used for inventory availability planning and receiving dock scheduling.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The physical supply chain channel through which this replenishment order will be fulfilled. dc_to_store = Distribution Center to store; supplier_to_store = Direct Store Delivery (DSD); supplier_to_dc = inbound to DC; cross_dock = Cross-Docking without DC storage; dsd = Direct Store Delivery; drop_ship = vendor ships directly to end customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dc_to_store|supplier_to_store|supplier_to_dc|cross_dock|dsd|drop_ship — promote to reference product]']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order was flagged as an emergency order due to an imminent or active out-of-stock situation. Emergency orders bypass standard lead time and approval workflows. True = emergency; False = standard.']
  is_vmi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement where the supplier is responsible for maintaining stock levels at the destination node. True = VMI order; False = retailer-managed replenishment.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The supplier Lead Time in calendar days from order submission to expected delivery at the destination node. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing info record or Blue Yonder Demand Planning supplier master. Used to calculate expected_delivery_date and Weeks of Supply (WOS).']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) stipulated by the supplier for this SKU at the time the replenishment order was created. Used to validate that ordered_quantity meets supplier contractual requirements. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing info record.']
  moq_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ordered_quantity meets or exceeds the supplier\'s Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold, requiring buyer review or override.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'The principal business event timestamp — the date and time the replenishment order was created and submitted by the demand planning or replenishment system (Blue Yonder or SAP MM). Distinct from audit timestamps.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the replenishment order, sourced from Blue Yonder Demand Planning or SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used for cross-system traceability and supplier communication via EDI.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval, shipment, receipt at destination node, and closure. Cancelled indicates the order was voided before fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|in_transit|received|partially_received|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the replenishment order was triggered. Auto-replenishment is system-generated based on reorder policy thresholds; manual is buyer-initiated; VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) is supplier-driven; emergency covers urgent out-of-stock situations; cross_dock bypasses DC storage; drop_ship routes directly from vendor to destination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: auto_replenishment|manual|vmi|emergency|cross_dock|drop_ship — promote to reference product]']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU originally requested in the replenishment order, expressed in the SKU\'s base unit of measure (e.g., each, case, pallet). This is the raw demand signal before supplier approval or MOQ adjustment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this replenishment order, used to sequence fulfillment and allocate DC resources. Critical = imminent out-of-stock risk; high = elevated demand or promotional support; standard = routine replenishment; low = opportunistic or pre-build.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of the SKU physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Used to calculate fill rate and identify short shipments. Populated upon goods receipt in Manhattan Associates WMS or SAP S/4HANA WM.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this replenishment order. blue_yonder = Blue Yonder Demand Planning auto-replenishment; sap_mm = SAP S/4HANA MM manual purchase order; orms = Oracle Retail Merchandising System; manual = buyer-entered; vmi_portal = supplier VMI portal submission.']
  source_system_order_ref varchar [note: 'The native order identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP MM document number, Blue Yonder order ID, ORMS order number). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation with the system of record.']
  total_order_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total cost of the replenishment order calculated as approved_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Represents the financial commitment for this replenishment event. Used for Open to Buy (OTB) budget tracking and P&L reporting.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'The mechanism that triggered this replenishment order. reorder_point = on-hand fell below reorder point threshold; min_max = inventory fell below minimum level; days_of_supply = Weeks of Supply (WOS) fell below threshold; manual_override = buyer-initiated; vmi_signal = Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) supplier signal; promotional = triggered by upcoming promotion event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reorder_point|min_max|days_of_supply|manual_override|vmi_signal|promotional — promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit for this SKU at the time of the replenishment order, sourced from the SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing info record or Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Used for inventory valuation and GMROI analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which ordered, approved, and received quantities are expressed. EA = Each, CS = Case, PL = Pallet, KG = Kilogram, LB = Pound, LT = Liter. Aligns with GS1 unit of measure codes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this replenishment order record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports change detection and incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Replenishment order records generated by demand planning and replenishment systems to restock SKUs at stores or DCs. Captures order type (auto-replenishment, manual, VMI), source node, destination node, ordered quantity, approved quantity, expected delivery date, lead time, MOQ compliance, and fulfillment status. Tracks the full replenishment lifecycle from trigger (based on reorder_policy thresholds) through receipt at the destination node.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each cycle count event record at the SKU-location level.']
  adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory adjustment record created as a result of this cycle count variance exceeding the tolerance threshold. Links the count event to its financial reconciliation record.']
  approver_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved the cycle count result and any resulting variance reconciliation. Required for SOX inventory control compliance.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Cycle count programs are stratified by merchandise category (high-value categories counted more frequently). Inventory accuracy KPIs are reported by category to buyers and category managers. ABC class']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cycle counting is a cost center activity with labor costs tracked for budgeting and operational efficiency. Cycle_count needs cost_center_id to allocate counting labor costs and measure inventory accu']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), or Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC)) where the cycle count is being performed.']
  primary_cycle_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assigned to perform the physical count for this cycle count event. Supports accountability and audit trail for Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) inventory controls.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being counted in this cycle count event.']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Variance analysis requires pricing to calculate financial impact of count discrepancies. Retail value of shrinkage, variance reporting to finance, and threshold-based approval workflows depend on exte']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC classification of the SKU at the time of the cycle count, indicating inventory value and velocity tier. A = high-value/high-velocity; B = medium; C = low-value/low-velocity; D = dead stock. Drives count frequency prioritization.']
  adjustment_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an inventory_adjustment record has been generated from this cycle count variance. When True, the variance has been posted to the inventory ledger for reconciliation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the cycle count result was reviewed and approved by the designated supervisor or manager, completing the approval workflow.']
  count_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handheld scanner, mobile device, or RFID reader used to perform the physical count. Supports device-level audit trail and equipment calibration tracking.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the physical counting activity was completed and the count was submitted by the assigned counter.']
  count_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled counting frequency for this SKU-location combination, typically driven by ABC classification or velocity. High-velocity A-items are counted more frequently than slow-moving C-items.']
  count_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the counter or supervisor to record observations, exceptions, or explanations related to the cycle count result (e.g., damaged packaging, items in transit, display stock not included).']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number assigned to the cycle count event, used for cross-system tracking and audit trail. Typically generated by the WMS (e.g., Manhattan Associates) or SAP WM module.']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the physical counting activity was initiated by the assigned counter. Used for cycle time analysis and labor tracking.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the cycle count event. \'scheduled\' = count is planned but not started; \'in_progress\' = counting is underway; \'completed\' = physical count recorded; \'reconciled\' = variance reviewed and inventory adjustment posted; \'cancelled\' = count was voided.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cycle count method. \'scheduled\' = routine planned count; \'ad_hoc\' = unplanned triggered count; \'blind\' = counter does not see system quantity; \'directed\' = WMS-directed count task; \'rfid_assisted\' = count performed using Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology.']
  counted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual physical quantity of the SKU observed and recorded by the counter during the cycle count. This is the primary measured outcome of the count event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cycle count record was first created in the system, establishing the audit trail for SOX inventory controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary values (variance_cost, unit_cost) recorded in this cycle count record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal period (year and period number, e.g., 2024-P06) during which the cycle count was performed. Used for period-end inventory accuracy reporting and financial close processes.']
  planogram_location varchar [note: 'The planogram (POG) shelf position or fixture reference for the SKU at the store location, used to direct the counter to the correct physical location during in-store cycle counts.']
  recount_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of how many times this SKU-location has been recounted within the same cycle count event. First count = 1; first recount = 2; etc. Supports escalation workflows when repeated recounts still show variance.']
  recount_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount of this SKU-location is required because the variance exceeded the tolerance threshold or the count was flagged for discrepancy. When True, a follow-up count must be performed before reconciliation.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the cycle count was planned to be performed, as set in the WMS count schedule.']
  shrinkage_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the root cause of inventory shrinkage identified through this cycle count. Populated during the reconciliation phase when a negative variance is confirmed. Supports shrinkage investigation and loss prevention analytics.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this cycle count record originated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation across systems (e.g., Manhattan Associates WMS, SAP WM module).']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'The specific warehouse storage bin, shelf, or slot location within the inventory node where the SKU is physically stored and counted. Granular location identifier used by the WMS for directed counting.']
  storage_zone varchar [note: 'The logical zone or area within the inventory node (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, high-value, bulk) where the SKU is stored. Used for zone-based cycle count scheduling and analysis.']
  system_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The inventory quantity recorded in the Warehouse Management System (WMS) or ERP at the exact time the cycle count was initiated. This is the \'book\' quantity against which the physical count is compared.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason that initiated this cycle count. Supports shrinkage investigation and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: routine|negative_inventory|shrinkage_alert|receiving_discrepancy|customer_complaint|audit_request|rfid_discrepancy — promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) unit cost of the SKU at the time the cycle count was performed, used to calculate the financial impact of any inventory variance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the SKU quantity is expressed for this cycle count (e.g., EA = Each, CS = Case, PK = Pack, LB = Pound, KG = Kilogram). Aligns with the GS1 unit of measure standard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|LB|KG|OZ|FT|M|L|GAL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cycle count record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  variance_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the inventory variance, calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the unit cost at time of count. Expressed in the local operating currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact assessment and financial reporting.']
  variance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'The variance quantity expressed as a percentage of the system on-hand quantity, calculated as (variance_quantity / system_quantity) * 100. Used to assess inventory accuracy and trigger shrinkage investigations when thresholds are exceeded.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between the physically counted quantity and the system on-hand quantity (counted_quantity minus system_quantity). A negative value indicates shrinkage or loss; a positive value indicates an overcount or unrecorded receipt.']
  variance_tolerance_pct decimal(10,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable variance percentage for this SKU-location before a recount or inventory adjustment is triggered. Variances exceeding this threshold generate inventory_adjustment records for reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory cycle count events at the SKU-location level. Captures count schedule, assigned counter, counted quantity, system quantity at time of count, variance quantity, variance percentage, count status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, reconciled), and approval workflow. Supports shrinkage investigation, inventory accuracy KPIs, and Sarbanes-Oxley inventory controls. Variances exceeding tolerance thresholds generate inventory_adjustment records for reconciliation.'
}

Table inventory.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inventory adjustment record. Serves as the primary key for the adjustment data product and is referenced by the stock ledger and loss prevention systems.']
  adjustment_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or system user who originally created or submitted the inventory adjustment request. May differ from the authorizing user in approval workflows (e.g., store associate submits, store manager approves).']
  adjustment_authorizing_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or system user who authorized or approved the inventory adjustment. Required for audit trail and loss prevention accountability. Maps to the workforce/user master in Workday HCM or SAP.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Shrink and inventory adjustment reporting is aggregated by merchandise category for P&L impact analysis. Buyers are held accountable for category-level shrink rates and adjustment trends. Loss prevent']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Shrinkage and loss prevention adjustments are tracked under compliance programs for regulatory reporting, audit trails, and internal control monitoring. Retail LP teams require this link for program e']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Adjustments are charged to responsible cost centers for accountability and loss prevention KPIs. Adjustment has cost_center_code that needs FK to cost_center for shrink reporting, budget impact analys']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Shrinkage and adjustment financial impact calculations require cost basis. GL posting, P&L impact analysis, and loss prevention reporting depend on linking adjustments to the cost structure for accura']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Retail adjustments for customer returns, damages at pickup, or theft incidents require customer attribution for refund processing, fraud detection, and loss prevention analytics. Supports chargeback a']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory adjustments (shrinkage, damage, obsolescence) post to specific GL accounts for financial statement accuracy and audit compliance. Adjustment has gl_account_code that should be FK to gl_accou']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), or dark store) where the adjustment is being recorded. Supports omnichannel inventory visibility.']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction. Business justification: Inventory adjustments for shrinkage, damage, or discrepancies are often discovered during POS operations (cashier finds damaged item, customer returns defective product). Linking adjustment to origina']
  reversal_of_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original adjustment record that this record is reversing. Populated only for reversal entries to maintain a complete audit trail of corrections. Nullable for original (non-reversal) adjustments.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) whose inventory quantity is being adjusted. Links to the product master to identify the item at the variant level (size, color, style).']
  stock_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding stock ledger entry created when this adjustment was posted. Establishes the audit trail between the adjustment record and the resulting stock position change. Nullable until the adjustment reaches \'posted\' status.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier associated with this adjustment, applicable for receiving variances, Return to Vendor (RTV) adjustments, vendor fraud shrinkage, or Direct Store Delivery (DSD) discrepancies. Nullable for non-supplier-related adjustments.']
  adjusted_quantity decimal(15,4) [note: 'The net quantity change applied to on-hand inventory for the SKU at the specified location. Negative values represent inventory reductions (shrinkage, damage, write-offs); positive values represent inventory additions (found stock, receiving corrections). Expressed in the SKU\'s unit of measure.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the adjustment transaction. Used in audit trails, loss prevention case references, and cross-system reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA and ORMS. Format: ADJ- followed by 10 digits.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the adjustment record. Controls whether the adjustment has been applied to the stock ledger. \'posted\' indicates the adjustment has updated on-hand quantities; \'reversed\' indicates a correcting entry was made.']
  adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time when the inventory adjustment event occurred (e.g., when shrinkage was detected, when a cycle count discrepancy was identified, when damage was observed). Distinct from system audit timestamps. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment has been reviewed and approved through the authorization workflow. \'auto_approved\' applies to system-generated adjustments below a defined threshold that bypass manual review. Distinct from adjustment_status which tracks the full posting lifecycle.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the adjustment was approved or rejected by the authorizing user. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance tracking on adjustment processing turnaround. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the inventory being adjusted, applicable for batch-tracked SKUs such as food, pharmaceuticals, and regulated products. Required for FDA recall traceability and CPSC product safety compliance. Nullable for non-batch-tracked items.']
  cost_impact decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total financial impact of the inventory adjustment on Cost of Goods Sold (COGS), calculated as adjusted_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Negative values represent write-downs (losses); positive values represent write-ups (gains). Posted to the general ledger via SAP FI/CO integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when the adjustment record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and SLA tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the cost impact and unit cost are expressed (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency retail operations and financial consolidation.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise department code associated with the SKU being adjusted, sourced from the product hierarchy in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Enables shrinkage and adjustment reporting by department for category management and P&L analysis.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the inventory discrepancy or adjustment need was identified. Supports shrinkage root-cause analysis and RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) program effectiveness measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cycle_count|physical_inventory|rfid_scan|pos_exception|receiving_audit|system_alert|manual_observation|loss_prevention_investigation — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the inventory being written off, applicable for perishable grocery, food, and pharmaceutical SKUs. Required for FDA food safety compliance and expiry write-off reporting. Nullable for non-perishable items. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal accounting period (year and period number) to which this adjustment is attributed for financial reporting and shrinkage rate analysis. Format: YYYY-P## (e.g., 2024-P01 through 2024-P13 for 4-4-5 retail calendar).']
  is_shrinkage boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this adjustment represents a shrinkage event (inventory loss due to theft, damage, administrative error, or vendor fraud). Used to filter and aggregate shrinkage metrics for loss prevention reporting and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.']
  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the adjustment was automatically generated by a system process (e.g., Blue Yonder replenishment system, RFID reconciliation engine, WMS cycle count automation) rather than manually entered by a user.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory node type where the adjustment occurred. Distinguishes between store-level adjustments, Distribution Center (DC) adjustments, Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC) adjustments, and dark store adjustments for segmented shrinkage and variance reporting.']
  lp_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this inventory adjustment to an active or closed Loss Prevention (LP) investigation case (e.g., shoplifting incident, employee theft case). Nullable — only populated when the adjustment is associated with a formal LP case in Salesforce Service Cloud or a dedicated LP system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or explanations provided by the initiating or authorizing user regarding the inventory adjustment. Supports audit trail completeness and loss prevention investigation documentation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the inventory adjustment is posted to the stock ledger and general ledger. May differ from adjustment_timestamp when adjustments are batched or backdated for period-end closing. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  quantity_after_adjustment decimal(15,4) [note: 'The on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location immediately after this adjustment is applied. Derived at posting time and stored for audit trail and stock ledger reconciliation. Expressed in the SKU\'s unit of measure.']
  quantity_before_adjustment decimal(15,4) [note: 'The on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location immediately prior to this adjustment being applied. Captured at the time of adjustment for audit trail completeness and variance analysis. Expressed in the SKU\'s unit of measure.']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of why the inventory adjustment is being made. Drives reporting buckets for shrinkage monitoring, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) impact analysis, and loss prevention dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shrinkage|damage|cycle_count_correction|receiving_variance|expiry_write_off|rtv|admin_correction|found_stock — promote to reference product]']
  reason_sub_category varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the reason category providing specific cause detail. Examples for shrinkage: shoplifting, employee theft, administrative error, vendor fraud. Examples for damage: in-transit damage, handling damage, shelf damage. Feeds loss prevention case analysis and shrinkage root-cause reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shoplifting|employee_theft|admin_error|vendor_fraud|in_transit_damage|handling_damage|shelf_damage|expired|recalled|mis-ship — promote to reference product]']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this inventory adjustment is associated with a product recall event initiated by the CPSC, FDA, or the retailer. Enables rapid identification and financial quantification of recall-related inventory write-offs.']
  rfid_tag_count varchar [note: 'Number of RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tags scanned during the detection event that triggered this adjustment. Populated only for RFID-based detection methods. Supports RFID program accuracy measurement and tag read-rate analysis.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the originating source document (e.g., cycle count ID, RTV authorization number, receiving document number, damage report ID). Enables cross-system traceability and audit trail linkage.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of source business document that triggered or supports this inventory adjustment. Provides traceability back to the originating business process (e.g., cycle count sheet, Return to Vendor (RTV) authorization, receiving document). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cycle_count|physical_inventory|rtv_authorization|receiving_document|damage_report|expiry_report|pos_exception_report — promote to reference product]']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit at the time of adjustment, used to calculate the total cost impact of the inventory change. Sourced from the inventory valuation layer in SAP S/4HANA. Business-confidential as it reflects supplier pricing.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the adjusted quantity is expressed (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram). Aligns with the SKU\'s base unit of measure in the product master to ensure accurate stock ledger posting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|LB|KG|OZ|L|M|FT|PK|BX — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when the adjustment record was most recently modified. Tracks approval workflow updates, status changes, and corrections. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Records all inventory quantity adjustments at the SKU-location level including shrinkage events, damage write-downs, cycle count corrections, receiving variances, expiry write-offs, and RTVs. Captures adjustment reason category, sub-reason (e.g., shoplifting, employee theft, admin error for shrinkage), adjusted quantity, cost impact, detection method, authorizing user, approval status, and loss prevention case reference where applicable. Feeds the stock_ledger and serves as the SSOT for all non-transactional inventory changes and shrinkage monitoring.'
}

Table inventory.stock_transfer {
  stock_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each inter-node stock transfer record in the inventory management system.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider responsible for transporting the stock transfer between nodes. Used for carrier performance analytics and SLA compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Transfer operations incur labor and freight costs allocated to cost centers for P&L tracking. Stock_transfer needs cost_center_id to charge transfer costs to responsible departments and measure transf']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Transfer accounting and intercompany pricing require cost data for inventory valuation during movement. Store-to-store transfers, DC-to-store shipments, and cross-dock operations need cost basis for f']
  destination_node_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or vendor location) to which the stock transfer is directed. Used for inventory receipt confirmation and network replenishment tracking.']
  initiating_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Store-to-store and ship-from-store transfers are initiated by specific associates. Tracking this supports fulfillment performance analytics, operational accountability, and process improvement initiat']
  primary_stock_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or vendor location) from which the stock transfer is dispatched. Supports network balancing and omnichannel fulfillment analytics.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order that triggered this stock transfer, applicable for DC-to-store replenishment flows sourced from supplier inbound receipts. Links transfer to procurement context.']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the replenishment plan that generated this stock transfer, linking the physical movement to the demand-driven planning signal from Blue Yonder or Oracle Retail. Supports replenishment accuracy analytics.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being transferred. The SKU is the atomic unit of inventory tracking, uniquely identifying a product variant (size, color, style) within the retail assortment.']
  supplychain_edi_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transaction record associated with this stock transfer, applicable for supplier-initiated VMI transfers or 3PL-managed shipments communicated via EDI 943/944 warehouse transfer messages.']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically received and confirmed at the destination node. Compared against expected_receipt_date to measure carrier and DC performance against SLA targets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this stock transfer record was first persisted in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transfer cost is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.']
  expected_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date by which the stock transfer is expected to arrive at the destination node. Used for replenishment planning, weeks of supply (WOS) calculations, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock transfer record was first created in the system, representing the business event of transfer initiation. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the transfer lifecycle.']
  inventory_cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) value per unit for the transferred SKU at time of transfer, used for inventory valuation, GMROI calculations, and financial reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO standard cost or moving average cost.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is executed as a cross-docking (cross-dock) movement, where inventory is transferred directly from inbound to outbound at the DC without putaway storage. Supports DC throughput and flow-through analytics.']
  is_ship_from_store boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a ship-from-store (SFS) omnichannel fulfillment flow, where a retail store acts as a fulfillment node dispatching inventory to satisfy an e-commerce or BOPIS order.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program, where the supplier initiates and manages the replenishment transfer rather than the retailer\'s internal planning team.']
