  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments associated with the stock transfer, capturing exception details, special handling instructions, or carrier communication relevant to the transfer execution.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the stock transfer, influencing pick sequencing, carrier selection, and dispatch scheduling at the DC or store. URGENT transfers may be triggered by stockout alerts or product recalls.']
  received_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of units confirmed received at the destination node upon delivery. May differ from transfer_quantity due to transit damage, shrinkage, or carrier discrepancies. Drives inventory reconciliation and shrinkage monitoring.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this stock transfer was tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility during transit. Supports RFID-based inventory accuracy programs.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the stock transfer was physically dispatched from the source node. Used for lead time calculation, carrier SLA tracking, and inventory in-transit reporting.']
  source_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the originating inventory node. STORE: retail store; DC: distribution center; MFC: micro-fulfillment center; VENDOR: supplier location for RTV or DSD; DARK_STORE: fulfillment-only location.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this stock transfer record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across the retail technology stack.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned shipment tracking number for the stock transfer, enabling real-time in-transit visibility and last-mile delivery monitoring. Used for exception management and carrier SLA validation.']
  transfer_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total logistics cost incurred for executing this stock transfer, including carrier freight charges, handling fees, and any 3PL surcharges. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), P&L allocation, and supply chain cost optimization.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number for the stock transfer, used for tracking and communication across supply chain partners, carriers, and store operations. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM transfer order numbering.']
  transfer_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU authorized and dispatched in this stock transfer. Represents the planned transfer quantity as recorded at time of transfer creation.']
  transfer_reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. REPLENISHMENT: routine inventory restocking; NETWORK_BALANCE: lateral balancing to address overstock/understock; RTV: return to vendor; RECALL: product safety recall per CPSC; DAMAGE_RETURN: return of damaged goods; OMNICHANNEL: ship-from-store or BOPIS fulfillment support.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stock transfer. DRAFT: initiated but not yet dispatched; IN_TRANSIT: shipment dispatched from source node; RECEIVED: confirmed receipt at destination node; CANCELLED: transfer voided before completion; EXCEPTION: discrepancy or damage reported during transit.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inter-node inventory movement. DC_TO_STORE: distribution center replenishment to store; STORE_TO_STORE: lateral balancing between stores; STORE_TO_DC: reverse logistics or consolidation; CROSS_DOCK: direct transfer without storage per cross-docking (DSD) model; RTV: Return to Vendor; MFC_TO_STORE: micro-fulfillment center to store for omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which the transfer quantity is expressed. EACH: individual sellable unit; CASE: standard case pack; PALLET: full pallet load; CARTON: shipping carton; PACK: multi-unit consumer pack. Aligns with WMS and ERP unit-of-measure configuration.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the transferred SKU, used for scanning and verification during pick, pack, and receipt operations at DC and store nodes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this stock transfer record, such as status updates, quantity adjustments, or receipt confirmations. Supports change data capture and audit compliance.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'Difference between transfer_quantity and received_quantity, representing units lost, damaged, or unaccounted for during transit. Positive value indicates shortage; negative indicates overage. Drives shrinkage monitoring and carrier chargeback processes.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining the cause of any quantity variance between dispatched and received units. Used for shrinkage root cause analysis, carrier chargeback (RMA) processing, and inventory reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Inter-node stock transfer records capturing movement of inventory between stores, DCs, MFCs, and vendor locations. Captures transfer type (store-to-store, DC-to-store, cross-dock, RTV), source node, destination node, SKU, transfer quantity, shipment date, expected receipt date, actual receipt date, carrier, and transfer status. Supports omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store) and network balancing.'
}

Table inventory.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each goods receipt record in the inventory domain. Primary key for the goods_receipt data product.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: Buyers monitor receipt performance (on-time, quantity accuracy, quality issues) for their purchase orders. Receipt variance resolution and vendor performance scorecarding are buyer responsibilities. B']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving operations are cost centers with labor, equipment, and overhead costs tracked for budgeting and efficiency analysis. Goods_receipt needs cost_center_id to allocate receiving costs and measur']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Receiving process validates invoice costs against contracted pricing and updates landed cost. Three-way match (PO-receipt-invoice) depends on linking goods receipt to cost structure for variance detec']
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_plan. Business justification: Receiving inspections for food products must reference the active food safety plan to ensure HACCP compliance. FDA/FSMA requires documented receiving procedures tied to facility food safety plans for ']
  haccp_control_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.haccp_control_point. Business justification: Temperature verification and quality checks at receiving are critical control points in HACCP plans. Retail food operations require linking receipts to specific CCPs for regulatory compliance and corr']
  inbound_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled inbound dock appointment at the receiving node. Links to the inbound_appointment product for dock scheduling, carrier arrival tracking, and receiving labor planning.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location (DC, store, or MFC) where the merchandise was received. Supports inventory accuracy at point of induction and omnichannel fulfillment visibility.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific purchase order line item being received. Enables line-level PO matching, partial receipt tracking, and SKU-level discrepancy analysis.']
  receiving_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Receiving operations require tracking which associate physically received goods for accountability, discrepancy resolution, training assessment, and performance metrics. The existing received_by text ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being received. Links to the product master in Informatica MDM or Oracle Retail Merchandising System for item-level inventory induction.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor from whom the merchandise was received. Links to the supplier master record in Informatica MDM. Used for vendor compliance chargeback processing and fill rate analysis.']
  accepted_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units accepted into inventory after quality inspection. Excludes rejected, damaged, or wrong-SKU units. This is the quantity inducted into on-hand inventory and used for COGS valuation.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number from the carrier\'s shipping documentation. Used for shipment tracking, freight audit, carrier dispute resolution, and customs compliance for international shipments.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier or logistics provider that delivered the merchandise to the receiving node. Used for carrier performance tracking, SLA compliance, and freight cost allocation.']
  chargeback_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt record has identified discrepancies that qualify for a vendor compliance chargeback. True = chargeback may be issued to supplier; False = no chargeback applicable. Drives the vendor compliance chargeback workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this goods receipt record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit cost and total receipt cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency retail operations and financial reporting.']
  discrepancy_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the discrepancy resolution workflow. \'pending\' = discrepancy logged, not yet actioned; \'under_review\' = being investigated with supplier; \'resolved\' = discrepancy closed; \'chargeback_issued\' = vendor compliance chargeback raised; \'waived\' = discrepancy accepted without penalty.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary discrepancy identified during receiving. \'shortage\' = fewer units than expected; \'overage\' = more units than expected; \'wrong_sku\' = incorrect item received; \'damage\' = merchandise physically damaged; \'expired\' = past sell-by or use-by date; \'none\' = no discrepancy. Drives chargeback and resolution workflows.']
  expected_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units expected at the receiving node based on the ASN or replenishment plan. May differ from ordered quantity due to partial shipments or split deliveries. Used for receiving dock planning and discrepancy detection.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date on which the quality inspection of received merchandise was completed. Used for compliance reporting, CPSC/FDA audit trails, and receiving cycle time analysis.']
  ordered_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units originally ordered on the purchase order line. Used as the baseline for receipt variance calculation, fill rate measurement, and vendor compliance scoring.']
  overage_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units received in excess of the ASN or PO expectation (received_qty minus expected_qty when positive). Overages require disposition decisions and may trigger vendor compliance chargebacks.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known purchase order number as referenced on supplier documentation and EDI transactions. Retained for operational readability, vendor communication, and three-way match without requiring a join.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. Enables PO-to-receipt matching, open order tracking, and three-way match (PO, receipt, invoice) for accounts payable.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality inspection performed on received merchandise. \'not_required\' = inspection waived per supplier agreement; \'pending\' = awaiting inspection; \'passed\' = all units meet quality standards; \'failed\' = units do not meet standards; \'partial_pass\' = some units accepted, some rejected. Drives accepted/rejected quantity split.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the physical goods receipt was posted. Used for inventory valuation, vendor compliance reporting, and lead time analysis. Aligns with SAP Posting Date (BUDAT) in the MM module.']
  receipt_method varchar [note: 'Method by which merchandise was physically received. \'dsd\' = Direct Store Delivery from supplier; \'dc_to_store\' = replenishment from Distribution Center; \'cross_dock\' = direct transfer without storage; \'drop_ship\' = vendor direct-to-customer; \'transfer_order\' = inter-node stock transfer. Drives receiving workflow and inventory valuation logic.']
  receipt_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the receiving associate documenting special conditions, discrepancy details, handling instructions, or other observations at the time of receipt. Supports discrepancy investigation and vendor communication.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the goods receipt document, typically generated by the WMS or ERP upon physical receipt confirmation. Maps to SAP Material Document Number (MBLNR) or Manhattan WMS Receipt Number.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the goods receipt workflow. \'draft\' indicates initiated but not confirmed; \'open\' indicates awaiting physical receipt; \'in_progress\' indicates receiving underway; \'completed\' indicates fully processed and inventory inducted; \'cancelled\' indicates voided; \'on_hold\' indicates paused pending resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the physical merchandise was received and confirmed at the receiving node (DC, store, or MFC). Distinct from system audit timestamps. Maps to SAP Posting Date/Time in MIGO.']
  received_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of units physically counted and received at the dock, prior to quality inspection. Includes all units regardless of condition. Used as the gross receipt quantity for inventory induction and discrepancy calculation.']
  receiving_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical location type where goods were received. \'dc\' = Distribution Center; \'store\' = retail store; \'mfc\' = Micro-Fulfillment Center; \'dark_store\' = fulfillment-only location. Determines applicable receiving workflows and inventory induction rules.']
  rejected_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units rejected during quality inspection and not inducted into inventory. Drives Return to Vendor (RTV) processing, vendor compliance chargebacks, and RMA initiation.']
  rfid_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received merchandise was verified using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) scanning during the receiving process. True = RFID scan confirmed; False = manual or barcode-only verification. Supports inventory accuracy at point of induction.']
  rtv_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Return to Vendor (RTV) process has been initiated for rejected units from this goods receipt. True = RTV in progress; False = no RTV initiated. Supports reverse logistics and vendor debit memo processing.']
  shortage_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units short-shipped relative to the ASN or PO expectation (expected_qty minus received_qty when negative). Used for vendor compliance chargeback calculation and fill rate reporting.']
  total_receipt_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of accepted merchandise received (accepted_qty × unit_cost). Used for inventory valuation, COGS posting, and financial reconciliation. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the received merchandise as per the purchase order or supplier invoice. Used for inventory valuation (COGS), gross margin calculation (GMROI), and financial posting to the general ledger. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which receipt quantities are expressed (e.g., EA = Each, CS = Case, PK = Pack, LB = Pound, KG = Kilogram). Aligns with GS1 unit of measure codes and SAP base unit of measure (MEINS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|LB|KG|OZ|L|M|FT — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the received item. Used for RFID/barcode scanning during receiving, inventory accuracy validation, and GS1 compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this goods receipt record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, discrepancy resolution audits, and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Records physical receipt of merchandise at DCs, stores, and MFCs against purchase orders, replenishment orders, or transfer orders. Captures receipt date, receiving node, supplier/source, PO/ASN reference, line-level received/accepted/rejected quantities with discrepancy details (shortage, overage, wrong SKU, damage), unit cost, receipt method (DSD, DC-to-store, cross-dock), quality inspection status, and discrepancy resolution status. Supports receiving workflows, vendor compliance chargebacks, and inventory accuracy at point of induction.'
}

Table inventory.rfid_tag {
  rfid_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each RFID tag record in the inventory tracking system. Primary key for the rfid_tag master record.']
  associated_order_order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order or fulfillment order with which this RFID tag is associated, enabling traceability from supplier shipment through DC receiving to store floor. Supports BOPIS, ship-from-store, and DSD tracking.']
  cycle_count_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recent cycle count event during which this RFID tag was scanned and reconciled. Supports inventory accuracy reporting, shrinkage identification, and audit trail for RFID-based cycle counting programs.']
  primary_rfid_encoding_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or node (store, DC, MFC, or supplier site) where the RFID tag was encoded. Supports encoding quality audits, location-based defect analysis, and supply chain traceability.']
  safety_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.safety_inspection. Business justification: RFID infrastructure (readers, antennas, power systems) is inspected during workplace safety audits for electrical safety, equipment maintenance, and OSHA compliance. Retail stores and DCs require link']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal SKU master record associated with this RFID tag. Enables inventory position updates, replenishment triggers, and sell-through rate calculations at the SKU level.']
  tertiary_rfid_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where the RFID tag is currently assigned or was last associated. Supports store-level inventory visibility, cycle count reconciliation, and omnichannel fulfillment (BOPIS, ROPIS, ship-from-store).']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who shipped the inventory unit associated with this RFID tag. Enables supplier-level RFID compliance tracking, chargeback management, and VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) reconciliation.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag meets the retailer\'s supplier compliance requirements (e.g., correct EPC encoding, approved tag model, proper placement). Non-compliant tags may trigger chargebacks to the supplier per vendor compliance agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the RFID tag master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and record lifecycle management.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for decommissioning the RFID tag. Sold indicates the item was purchased and the tag was killed at POS; returned indicates a customer return; damaged indicates physical tag failure; lost indicates the tag cannot be located; recalled indicates a product recall; expired indicates end of product shelf life; retagged indicates the item was assigned a new tag. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sold|returned|damaged|lost|recalled|expired|retagged — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  decommission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the RFID tag was decommissioned or killed. Supports tag lifecycle reporting, shrinkage analysis, and audit trail requirements.']
  encoding_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the RFID tag was encoded with its EPC and associated product data. Supports tag age analysis, encoding throughput reporting, and traceability back to the encoding event.']
  encoding_standard varchar [note: 'The GS1 EPC encoding scheme used to write the Electronic Product Code onto the tag (e.g., SGTIN-96 for serialized trade items, SSCC-96 for shipping containers, GIAI-96 for individual assets). Determines how the EPC is decoded and interpreted by readers.']
  epc_code varchar [note: 'The globally unique Electronic Product Code encoded on the RFID tag, conforming to GS1 EPC standards (e.g., SGTIN-96, SSCC-96). Serves as the primary business identifier for the tag and enables interoperability across supply chain partners and retail nodes.']
  epc_memory_bank_size varchar [note: 'The size in bits of the EPC memory bank on the RFID tag chip. Determines the maximum length of the EPC that can be stored and influences encoding standard selection.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the product unit associated with this RFID tag. Applicable for perishable grocery, pharmaceutical, and health/beauty items. Supports FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management and FDA labeling compliance.']
  frequency_band varchar [note: 'The radio frequency band used by the RFID tag: UHF (Ultra High Frequency, 860-960 MHz, most common in retail supply chain), HF (High Frequency, 13.56 MHz, used in apparel and library), LF (Low Frequency, 125-134 kHz), or NFC (Near Field Communication). Determines reader compatibility and read range.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The 14-digit GS1 Global Trade Item Number of the product associated with this RFID tag. Links the tag to the product master and enables cross-system product identification across POS, WMS, and OMS platforms.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag memory has been locked to prevent further writes after encoding. Locked tags protect EPC integrity and prevent unauthorized modification in the supply chain.']
  is_password_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag has an access or kill password set to restrict read/write operations. Supports tag security policy compliance and anti-counterfeiting measures.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product unit associated with this RFID tag is a private label (store brand) product. Supports differentiated inventory tracking, margin analysis, and assortment reporting for own-brand merchandise.']
  is_recalled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product unit associated with this RFID tag is subject to an active product recall. Enables rapid identification and quarantine of recalled items across all inventory nodes. Supports CPSC and FDA recall compliance.']
  kill_password_set boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a kill password has been programmed on the tag, enabling permanent deactivation at point of sale for consumer privacy compliance. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance in item-level RFID deployments.']
  last_scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent successful RFID read event for this tag. Used to determine tag freshness, identify lost or missing tags, and support cycle count reconciliation.']
  last_scan_zone varchar [note: 'The specific zone or area within the last scan location where the tag was read (e.g., receiving dock, sales floor, backroom, fitting room, shipping dock). Provides sub-location granularity for inventory positioning and planogram compliance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing lot or batch number of the product unit associated with this RFID tag. Critical for food safety traceability (FDA FSMA compliance), product recall management, and CPSC compliance for consumer goods.']
  read_count varchar [note: 'The total number of times this RFID tag has been successfully read since encoding. Supports tag durability analysis, read rate benchmarking, and identification of high-traffic inventory items.']
  read_error_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of failed or erroneous read attempts recorded for this RFID tag. High error counts indicate tag damage, interference issues, or reader calibration problems requiring investigation.']
  reader_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RFID reader device that performed the last successful scan of this tag. Supports reader performance monitoring, read rate analysis, and hardware maintenance scheduling.']
  rfid_program_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the tagging program under which this RFID tag was applied: source_tagging (applied at manufacturer/supplier), store_tagging (applied at retail store), dc_tagging (applied at distribution center), or supplier_tagging (applied at supplier DC). Influences cost allocation, compliance tracking, and tag quality benchmarking.']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'The received signal strength indicator (RSSI) in decibel-milliwatts (dBm) recorded during the last successful RFID read. Used for reader placement optimization, read zone calibration, and tag quality assessment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this RFID tag record originated (e.g., Manhattan Associates WMS, SAP S/4HANA WM module, RFID middleware platform). Supports data lineage, master data management reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  tag_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or vendor who produced the physical RFID tag inlay or label (e.g., Impinj, Alien Technology, Avery Dennison). Used for vendor performance tracking, defect rate analysis, and procurement decisions.']
  tag_model varchar [note: 'The specific model or part number of the RFID tag inlay or label (e.g., Impinj Monza R6, Alien Higgs-9). Supports hardware compatibility analysis, read performance benchmarking, and tag lifecycle management.']
  tag_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number printed or encoded on the physical RFID tag inlay or label. Used for hardware traceability, warranty claims, and tag defect analysis.']
  tag_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RFID tag. Active tags are in use and trackable; decommissioned tags have been retired from service; lost tags have not been scanned within the expected window; damaged tags have failed read/write operations; unassigned tags have been encoded but not yet associated with inventory.']
  tag_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the granularity level at which the RFID tag is applied: item-level (individual SKU unit), case-level (shipping case), pallet-level (full pallet), or tote-level (reusable tote). Drives cycle count logic, inventory aggregation rules, and omnichannel fulfillment visibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the RFID tag master record was most recently modified. Supports change data capture (CDC) for downstream Silver layer processing, audit trails, and data freshness monitoring.']
  user_memory_data varchar [note: 'Optional data written to the user memory bank of the RFID tag, beyond the EPC. May contain additional product attributes, batch/lot numbers, or supply chain event data encoded by the retailer or supplier.']

  Note: 'Master record for RFID tags assigned to individual inventory items, cases, or pallets. Captures EPC (Electronic Product Code), tag type (item-level, case-level, pallet-level), associated SKU/GTIN, tag status (active, decommissioned, lost), encoding date, encoding location, last known scan location, and last scan timestamp. Supports item-level inventory tracking, RFID-based cycle counting, and real-time inventory visibility for omnichannel fulfillment.'
}

Table inventory.vmi_agreement {
  vmi_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the VMI agreement record. Primary key for the vmi_agreement data product.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise category covered by this VMI agreement. Supports category management and assortment planning alignment with the VMI program scope.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: VMI agreements in retail are negotiated at merchandise hierarchy/category level (e.g., vendor manages all \'Dairy\' or \'Snacks\'), not individual SKUs. Agreement scope definition, vendor performance KPIs']
  license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.license_permit. Business justification: VMI agreements for food, pharmaceuticals, or hazmat require specific licenses (food handling permits, hazmat storage permits). Retail compliance teams must link VMI agreements to facility permits to e']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail node (store, DC, or MFC) where the supplier manages inventory under this agreement. A single agreement may cover one primary location; multi-location coverage is handled via child records.']
  primary_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal retail buyer or category manager responsible for managing this VMI agreement on behalf of the retailer. Links to the workforce/employee master in Workday HCM.']
  primary_vmi_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) responsible for managing inventory under this VMI agreement. Links to the supplier master record in Informatica MDM and SAP S/4HANA vendor master.']
  vmi_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier.vendor']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number for the VMI agreement, used in supplier communications, EDI transactions, and contract documentation. Corresponds to the contract reference in SAP S/4HANA MM module.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VMI arrangement defining the inventory ownership and replenishment model. Standard VMI transfers replenishment responsibility to the supplier; consignment retains supplier ownership until sold; scan-based trading (SBT) triggers payment at POS scan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|consignment|scan_based_trading|cross_dock|drop_ship — promote to reference product]']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this VMI agreement automatically renews upon reaching the effective_end_date without requiring explicit renegotiation. True enables automatic renewal; False requires manual renewal action before expiry.']
  chargeback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer is contractually permitted to issue Chargebacks (Vendor Compliance Penalties) to the supplier for SLA violations (e.g., missed fill rate, late delivery) under this VMI agreement. True enables chargeback processing; False means no financial penalties apply.']
  chargeback_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the invoice value or a fixed rate applied as a Chargeback (Vendor Compliance Penalty) when the supplier fails to meet SLA thresholds defined in this VMI agreement. Applicable only when chargeback_enabled is True. Commercially sensitive contract term.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this VMI agreement record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this VMI agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting and multi-currency support.']
  edi_transaction_set varchar [note: 'The GS1/ASC X12 EDI transaction set number used for inventory data exchange under this VMI agreement (e.g., 852 for Product Activity Data, 855 for Purchase Order Acknowledgment, 856 for Advance Ship Notice). Applicable when inventory_visibility_method is EDI.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the VMI agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the VMI agreement becomes binding and the supplier assumes responsibility for inventory management at the covered location(s). Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  inventory_ownership varchar [note: 'Defines who holds legal title to the inventory at the covered location under this VMI agreement. Retailer ownership means the retailer takes title upon receipt; supplier ownership (consignment) means the supplier retains title until the item is sold; consignment is a hybrid model with specific transfer-of-title triggers.']
  inventory_visibility_method varchar [note: 'The agreed mechanism by which the retailer shares real-time inventory data with the supplier to enable VMI replenishment decisions. EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) uses standardized transaction sets (e.g., 852 Product Activity Data); RFID uses Radio Frequency Identification scanning; API uses direct system integration; portal uses a supplier web portal; manual uses periodic reports.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the retail node covered by this VMI agreement. Store indicates a physical retail location; DC is a Distribution Center (DC); MFC is a Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC); dark_store is a fulfillment-only location.']
  max_inventory_units varchar [note: 'The maximum on-hand inventory quantity (in units) allowed at the covered location under the VMI agreement. Exceeding this threshold signals overstock and may require supplier action to reduce inventory. Prevents excess stock and supports GMROI optimization.']
  min_inventory_units varchar [note: 'The minimum on-hand inventory quantity (in units) that the supplier must maintain at the covered location. Falling below this threshold triggers a replenishment obligation under the VMI agreement. Supports inventory replenishment planning in Blue Yonder.']
  moq varchar [note: 'The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) per replenishment order as agreed in the VMI contract. Defines the smallest quantity the supplier will ship in a single replenishment event, impacting inventory planning and working capital.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional agreement terms, special conditions, operational instructions, or exception handling notes not covered by structured fields. Used by buyers and supply chain planners for contextual documentation.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days\' advance notice required by either party to terminate or not renew this VMI agreement. Governs the contractual exit process and operational wind-down planning.']
  order_transmission_method varchar [note: 'The agreed channel through which the supplier transmits replenishment purchase orders to the retailer\'s OMS (Order Management System) or ERP under this VMI agreement.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date by which the VMI agreement must be reviewed and renewed to avoid expiry. Used to trigger renegotiation workflows and supplier performance reviews.']
  replenishment_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed cadence at which the supplier reviews inventory levels and initiates replenishment orders under the VMI agreement. Daily and on_demand frequencies are typical for fast-moving consumer goods; weekly or biweekly for slower-moving categories.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed Lead Time in calendar days from the supplier\'s replenishment order placement to delivery at the covered location. Used as the SLA baseline for supplier performance tracking and replenishment planning.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The agreed frequency at which the retailer and supplier formally review VMI agreement performance, SLA compliance, and terms. Drives the cadence of supplier performance scorecards and potential agreement amendments.']
  sku_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of SKU coverage under this VMI agreement. All_category_skus means the supplier manages all SKUs in the covered category; selected_skus means only specific SKUs are included; new_items_only restricts to newly introduced items; seasonal_only covers seasonal assortment only.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this VMI agreement record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and MDM reconciliation.']
  supplier_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact for VMI replenishment communications and SLA escalations under this agreement.']
  supplier_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary supplier contact responsible for managing inventory replenishment under this VMI agreement. Used for operational communication and escalation.']
  target_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually agreed Fill Rate (Order Completion Percentage) that the supplier must achieve under this VMI agreement, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.50 = 98.5%). Fill Rate is the percentage of replenishment orders fulfilled completely and on time. Used as a key SLA (Service Level Agreement) KPI for supplier performance measurement.']
  target_inventory_units varchar [note: 'The optimal on-hand inventory quantity (in units) that the supplier aims to maintain at the covered location. Represents the ideal stock position between the minimum and maximum thresholds for service level and working capital balance.']
  target_otd_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually agreed on-time delivery rate the supplier must achieve, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Measures the proportion of replenishment deliveries arriving within the agreed lead time window. Distinct from fill rate which measures order completeness.']
  target_weeks_of_supply decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target number of weeks of inventory coverage (Weeks of Supply / WOS) the supplier is contractually required to maintain at the covered location. Calculated as on-hand units divided by average weekly sales rate; the target value is defined in the agreement.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why this VMI agreement was terminated or not renewed. Populated only when status is terminated or expired. Supports supplier relationship analysis and VMI program retrospectives.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used for inventory thresholds and replenishment quantities in this VMI agreement. EA = Each; CS = Case; PK = Pack; PAL = Pallet; KG = Kilogram; LB = Pound. Aligns with GS1 unit of measure codes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this VMI agreement record was last modified, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Tracks the most recent change for audit and change management purposes.']
  vmi_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the VMI agreement. Draft indicates the agreement is under negotiation; active means the supplier is actively managing inventory; suspended indicates a temporary halt; terminated means the agreement has been ended before expiry; expired means the end date has passed without renewal; pending_renewal indicates the agreement is under renegotiation.']

  Note: 'Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement records defining the terms under which a supplier manages inventory levels at specific retail locations. Captures supplier reference, covered SKUs, managed location(s), min/max inventory thresholds, replenishment frequency, VMI start/end dates, performance SLAs (fill rate, lead time), and agreement status. Supports VMI program management and supplier performance tracking.'
}

Table inventory.expiry_tracking {
  expiry_tracking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each expiry tracking record at the SKU-location-batch level. Primary key for this data product.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Expiry/perishable management programs are organized by category (food, pharmacy, cosmetics). Category managers own markdown and disposition strategies for near-expiry inventory. Regulatory reporting f']
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_plan. Business justification: Expiry monitoring and FEFO (First Expired, First Out) procedures are integral to food safety plans. Retail grocers must link expiry tracking to facility food safety plans for regulatory compliance, au']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory node (store, DC, MFC, dark store) where the batch is physically held. Supports omnichannel inventory visibility.']
  markdown_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown. Business justification: Near-expiry inventory triggers automated markdown workflows to accelerate sell-through before disposal. Perishables management, FEFO optimization, and waste reduction programs depend on linking expiry']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order under which this batch was procured. Enables end-to-end traceability from supplier PO to expiry event for recall and chargeback management.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which expiry is being tracked. Links to the product master.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who produced or delivered this batch. Used for food safety traceability, recall management, and chargeback (vendor compliance penalty) processing for expired goods received below MRSL.']
  action_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the recommended_action was fully executed (e.g., markdown applied, product pulled from shelf, donation completed). Null if action_status is pending or in_progress.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Execution status of the recommended_action: pending (action not yet started), in_progress (action initiated), completed (action fully executed), waived (action overridden by manager with justification). Tracks operational follow-through on expiry management.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-assigned or internally generated batch or lot number uniquely identifying a production run of the SKU. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking logic and food safety recall traceability.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'Date until which the product is guaranteed to be at peak quality. Distinct from expiry_date — product may still be safe after this date but quality may degrade. Used for markdown and donation decision triggers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this expiry tracking record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  critical_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiry at which the batch escalates to critical risk status, triggering urgent pull-from-shelf or destroy actions. Configurable at the SKU or category level.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory_cost_per_unit (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency retail operations.']
  days_to_expiry varchar [note: 'Calculated number of calendar days remaining from the current date to the expiry_date. Negative values indicate the batch has already expired. Used to drive expiry risk classification and recommended action assignment. Refreshed daily by the Silver layer pipeline.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of expired or near-expiry inventory: destroyed (waste disposal per regulatory guidelines), donated (food bank or charity), returned_to_vendor (RTV), composted (organic waste program), none (no disposal yet). Required for waste reduction program reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The hard expiry date after which the product must not be sold or consumed. Drives pull-from-shelf actions, waste management, and regulatory compliance. Primary date field for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking sequencing.']
  expiry_risk_status varchar [note: 'Current risk classification of the batch based on days_to_expiry thresholds: fresh (well within shelf life), near_expiry (approaching threshold), critical (imminent expiry requiring urgent action), expired (past expiry_date), pulled (removed from shelf). Drives recommended action and waste reduction workflows.']
  fefo_sequence varchar [note: 'Picking sequence rank assigned to this batch within the same SKU-location combination, ordered by ascending expiry_date. Rank 1 = earliest expiring batch to be picked first. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) fulfillment logic in WMS and store replenishment.']
  inventory_cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) value per unit for this batch at the time of receipt. Used to calculate the financial write-off value of expired or disposed inventory. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module.']
  is_recall_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is subject to an active product recall issued by the supplier, FDA, or CPSC. True = recall in effect, requiring immediate pull-from-shelf and quarantine. Integrates with Salesforce Service Cloud for customer notification.']
  is_rfid_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is tracked using RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology for real-time inventory visibility. True = RFID tag attached and active; False = barcode-only tracking.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement where the supplier retains ownership and responsibility for replenishment until sold. Affects COGS recognition timing.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical cycle count or inventory audit that verified the quantity_on_hand for this batch. Used to assess data freshness and trigger recount workflows for high-risk expiry batches.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory node holding this batch: store (retail floor), DC (Distribution Center), MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center), dark_store (fulfillment-only), or vendor (VMI-managed stock).']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was manufactured or produced by the supplier. Used to calculate shelf life and validate compliance with minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) requirements.']
  near_expiry_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiry at which the batch transitions to near_expiry risk status. Configurable at the SKU or category level to reflect product-specific shelf life policies (e.g., dairy = 3 days, bakery = 1 day, pharmaceuticals = 30 days).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Merchandise category of the SKU (e.g., Dairy, Bakery, Produce, Meat, Pharmaceuticals, Frozen Foods). Used for category-level expiry policy configuration, waste reporting, and food safety compliance segmentation.']
  quantity_at_risk decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of quantity_on_hand that is classified as near_expiry or critical based on current expiry_risk_status. Used for waste forecasting, markdown planning, and shrinkage reporting.']
  quantity_disposed decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of this batch that has been disposed of (destroyed, donated, or returned to vendor) due to expiry. Contributes to shrinkage (inventory loss) reporting and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) write-off calculations.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current physical quantity of the batch available at the inventory node, expressed in the SKU\'s base unit of measure. Updated by POS sales, cycle counts, adjustments, and RFID scans.']
  recall_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official recall reference number issued by the FDA, CPSC, or supplier for the active recall affecting this batch. Populated only when is_recall_active = True. Used for regulatory reporting and audit trail.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the batch was received at the inventory node from the supplier or DC. Used to calculate time-in-location and validate minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) at receipt.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'System-recommended disposition action for the batch based on expiry risk status and business rules: none (no action needed), markdown (apply price reduction to accelerate sell-through), pull_from_shelf (remove from sale floor), donate (transfer to food bank or charity), destroy (dispose per regulatory guidelines), return_to_vendor (RTV). Supports waste reduction programs and food safety compliance.']
  remaining_shelf_life_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total shelf life remaining at the current date, calculated as (days_to_expiry / shelf_life_days) * 100. Used to enforce minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) thresholds for receiving and selling.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unit-level serial number for serialized perishable or date-sensitive items (e.g., pharmaceutical products, high-value electronics with warranty expiry). Nullable for non-serialized bulk items.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the SKU in calendar days from manufacture date to expiry date. Used to calculate remaining shelf life percentage and minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) compliance at receipt.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this expiry tracking record was sourced: SAP_S4 (SAP S/4HANA), ORMS (Oracle Retail Merchandising System), MANHATTAN_WMS (Manhattan Associates WMS), INFORMATICA_MDM (Informatica MDM), MANUAL (manual entry). Supports data lineage and audit.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature zone for this batch: ambient (room temperature), chilled (2–8°C), frozen (below -18°C), controlled (specific temperature-controlled environment). Used to validate storage compliance and flag temperature excursions that may accelerate expiry.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the quantity on hand (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Litre, CS=Case). Aligns with GS1 unit of measure codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|LB|L|ML|OZ|CS|PK|DZ — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code (UPC) or GTIN-14 barcode associated with the SKU. Used for POS scanning, RFID matching, and food safety traceability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this expiry tracking record (e.g., quantity adjustment, status change, action completion). Follows ISO 8601 format. Used for change data capture (CDC) and incremental processing.']

  Note: 'Tracks perishable and date-sensitive inventory at the SKU-location-batch level. Captures batch/lot number, manufacture date, expiry date, best-before date, quantity on hand, days-to-expiry, expiry risk status (fresh, near-expiry, expired), and recommended action (markdown, pull-from-shelf, donate, destroy). Supports FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking logic, grocery and perishables compliance with food safety regulations, and waste reduction programs. Serves as the SSOT for batch/lot identity and date-sensitive inventory within the inventory domain.'
}

Table inventory.reservation {
  reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inventory reservation or hold record. Primary key for the reservation data product.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the employee or system user who authorized the inventory hold or reservation. Required for hard holds (quality, legal, regulatory) for audit and compliance purposes. For automated soft allocations, this may reference a system service account.']
  cart_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.cart. Business justification: Inventory reservations are created from cart items during checkout to hold stock. Tracks cart-to-reservation lifecycle for abandoned cart inventory release and reservation expiry management.']
  checkout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.checkout. Business justification: Reservations are created during checkout to hold inventory during payment authorization. Links reservation lifecycle to checkout session for payment-hold-release coordination and checkout abandonment ']
  fulfillment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_line. Business justification: Reservations are SKU-level allocations matching fulfillment line granularity. Retail WMS systems reserve specific SKU quantities for specific order lines during wave planning and pick task generation.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Inventory reservations are created during fulfillment order allocation in retail operations. When an online order or BOPIS order enters fulfillment, the WMS reserves specific inventory for that fulfil']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order (OMS order) that triggered this inventory reservation. Populated for BOPIS, ROPIS, ship-from-store, and e-commerce hold encumbrances. Null for non-order-driven holds (quality, legal, regulatory). Links reservation to the order fulfillment lifecycle.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the inventory node (store, DC, MFC, dark store) where the reserved stock physically resides. Central to ATP calculation and omnichannel fulfillment routing (BOPIS, SFS, ship-from-DC).']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Retail operations reserve inventory for loyalty members (VIP early access, tier-based allocation, BOPIS for members, exclusive product launches). Enables member-specific holds, tier-based inventory pr']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.profile. Business justification: Retail omnichannel fulfillment (BOPIS, ship-from-store, curbside pickup) requires tracking which customer profile created each inventory reservation. Essential for customer service, order fulfillment,']
  rfid_tag_id varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier associated with the specific unit(s) reserved, enabling item-level tracking and automated hold verification. Populated when RFID-based inventory tracking is active at the node. Supports real-time inventory visibility for omnichannel fulfillment.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) whose inventory is being reserved or held. Links to the product master for item-level ATP (Available to Promise) calculation and omnichannel fulfillment routing.']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Order promising and ATP calculations require current pricing to validate order profitability and margin. Reservations for BOPIS, ship-from-store, and e-commerce fulfillment need pricing context for ma']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the stock_position record at the SKU-node level that this reservation encumbers. Enables direct linkage between the reservation and the ATP calculation on the stock_position product, ensuring accurate real-time inventory visibility.']
  supplychain_edi_transaction_id varchar [note: 'EDI transaction identifier when the reservation was triggered by an EDI message (e.g., EDI 940 Warehouse Shipping Order, EDI 947 Warehouse Inventory Adjustment). Enables end-to-end traceability for supplier-driven holds and VMI transactions.']
  authorizing_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the business department that authorized the hold (e.g., Quality Assurance, Legal, Compliance, Store Operations, E-Commerce Fulfillment). Supports departmental accountability reporting and hold resolution workflows.']
  case_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the service case, quality investigation, legal matter, or regulatory recall case that authorized a hard hold. Populated for quality_hold, legal_hold, regulatory_recall, and damage_hold encumbrance types. Links to Salesforce Service Cloud case management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp when the reservation record was first written to the data platform (Silver layer ingestion time). Distinct from reservation_timestamp which captures the business event time. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory_cost_per_unit value (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for international retail operations.']
  encumbrance_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the inventory encumbrance. Soft allocations (e.g., BOPIS reservation, ship-from-store allocation, e-commerce hold) are temporary and customer-driven; hard holds (quality hold, regulatory recall, legal hold, damage hold, price investigation) restrict stock from sale pending resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: soft_allocation|hard_hold|quality_hold|regulatory_recall|legal_hold|damage_hold|price_investigation — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the reservation automatically expires if not fulfilled or manually extended. Critical for BOPIS and e-commerce holds where stock must be released back to general availability after a defined window (e.g., 24–48 hours). Null for indefinite holds such as legal or regulatory holds.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Retail fiscal period (year and period number, e.g., 2024-P07) during which the reservation was created. Supports financial reporting of encumbered inventory valuation, OTB (Open to Buy) impact analysis, and period-end inventory reconciliation per FASB ASC 330.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'The omnichannel fulfillment method associated with this reservation. BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), ship_from_store (SFS), ecommerce (ship from DC), drop_ship (vendor direct), in_store (POS hold). Null for non-order-driven hard holds.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific business reason for the reservation or hold (e.g., BOPIS_ALLOC, SFS_ALLOC, ECOM_HOLD, QA_FAIL, RECALL_CPSC, LEGAL_HOLD, DMG_INBOUND, PRICE_INVEST). Enables operational reporting and root-cause analysis of inventory encumbrances.']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for the inventory hold or reservation, providing operational context beyond the structured hold_reason_code. Required for legal holds and regulatory recalls per compliance documentation standards.']
  inventory_cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit for the reserved SKU at the time of reservation creation. Used for financial valuation of encumbered inventory, shrinkage reporting, and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Retail FI/CO module.']
  is_recalled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reservation was created in response to a regulatory product recall (CPSC, FDA, or other governing body). When true, the hold cannot be released without explicit compliance authorization. Supports mandatory recall reporting and quarantine workflows.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reserved inventory is managed under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement, where the supplier retains ownership and replenishment responsibility. Affects financial liability and hold authorization workflows.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the reservation, governing queue position in fulfillment workflows and hold resolution SLAs. \'critical\' applies to regulatory recalls and legal holds; \'high\' to same-day BOPIS; \'standard\' to regular e-commerce holds; \'low\' to pre-emptive soft allocations.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing resolution details, disposition instructions, or compliance notes recorded at the time of hold release. Required for regulatory recalls and legal holds to document the resolution rationale for audit purposes.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for releasing a hard hold. \'pending\' — release request submitted awaiting authorization; \'approved\' — release authorized by responsible department; \'rejected\' — release denied, hold remains active; \'auto_released\' — system-triggered release upon expiry or order fulfillment; \'escalated\' — requires senior authorization.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the reservation was released, cancelled, or converted. Null while the reservation is active. Used for hold duration analysis, SLA compliance reporting, and ATP recalculation upon release.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the reservation, used in operational communications, order management, and customer-facing references (e.g., BOPIS confirmation codes). Sourced from OMS or WMS upon creation.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inventory reservation. \'active\' — stock is currently encumbered; \'expired\' — reservation lapsed past expiry without fulfillment; \'released\' — hold manually or systematically lifted; \'cancelled\' — reservation voided before fulfillment; \'pending_release\' — awaiting authorization to release; \'converted\' — soft allocation fulfilled and converted to a shipment.']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the inventory encumbrance was created — the principal business event timestamp. For BOPIS/e-commerce holds, this is when the customer completed checkout. For quality/legal holds, this is when the hold was authorized.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units of the SKU encumbered by this reservation. Subtracted from on-hand quantity to derive ATP (Available to Promise) on the stock_position product. Must be positive and not exceed available on-hand at time of reservation creation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this reservation record (e.g., WMS = Manhattan Associates WMS, OMS = Order Management System, ECOM = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, ERP = SAP S/4HANA, MANUAL = manually entered hold). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WMS|OMS|ECOM|ERP|ORMS|CAR|SFCC|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, or slot location within the node where the reserved inventory is physically stored. Enables warehouse associates to locate and pick reserved stock efficiently. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS bin management.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the reserved_quantity (e.g., EA = Each, CS = Case, LB = Pound, KG = Kilogram). Aligns with the SKU\'s base unit of measure in the product master to ensure accurate inventory accounting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|LB|KG|OZ|FT|M|L|GAL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the reserved item. Used for RFID scanning, POS reconciliation, and regulatory recall identification per GS1 standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp of the most recent modification to the reservation record (e.g., quantity adjustment, status change, expiry extension). Used for change data capture (CDC) and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Records all inventory encumbrances that restrict stock from general availability — including soft-allocations (BOPIS reservation, ship-from-store allocation, e-commerce hold) and hard holds (quality hold, regulatory recall, legal hold, damage hold, price investigation). Captures encumbrance type, SKU, quantity, node, start/expiry timestamps, associated order or case reference, authorizing party, hold reason, authorizing department, release status, and resolution details. Single source of truth for all reserved and held stock, enabling accurate ATP calculation on stock_position.'
}

Table inventory.reorder_policy {
  reorder_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the reorder policy record. Primary key for the reorder_policy data product in the inventory domain.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand forecast record from Blue Yonder Demand Planning that drives replenishment quantity calculations for forecast_driven reorder method. Null for non-forecast-driven methods.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory node (store, Distribution Center (DC), or Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC)) where this reorder policy governs stock replenishment. Combined with sku_id, forms the natural business key.']
  margin_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.margin_target. Business justification: Replenishment planning must respect category margin targets to maintain financial objectives. Automated reorder policies need margin guardrails to prevent over-buying low-margin SKUs and ensure replen']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent replenishment plan that governs or generated this reorder policy. Links the policy to the broader supply chain planning context managed in Blue Yonder Demand Planning.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this reorder policy applies. Combined with location_id, forms the natural business key for a reorder policy.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred supplier for replenishment orders generated by this policy. Used by the replenishment engine to route purchase orders. May be overridden at order generation time if the supplier is unavailable.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC classification of the SKU at this location based on sales velocity and revenue contribution: A (high-velocity/high-value), B (medium), C (low-velocity/low-value), D (dead stock or near-dead). Influences replenishment priority, safety stock levels, and review cycle frequency.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the replenishment planner or manager who approved this reorder policy for activation. Supports governance, audit trail, and accountability for policy parameters.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reorder policy was formally approved for activation by the authorized planner or manager. Supports audit trail and policy lifecycle management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reorder policy record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and policy lifecycle tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this reorder policy becomes active and the replenishment engine begins applying its parameters. Supports time-bound policy management and seasonal policy transitions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The calendar date after which this reorder policy is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no planned expiration. Used to manage seasonal policies, promotional replenishment windows, and policy version transitions.']
  is_auto_replenishment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether replenishment orders are automatically generated and submitted when the reorder trigger is breached (True), or whether a replenishment planner must manually review and approve the suggested order (False).']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reorder policy applies seasonal adjustments to replenishment parameters (e.g., elevated safety stock or WOS targets during peak seasons). When True, seasonal override parameters may be applied by the replenishment engine.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU-location replenishment is managed by the supplier under a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreement. When True, the supplier is responsible for monitoring stock levels and initiating replenishment orders rather than the retailer\'s automated engine.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which a replenishment planner reviewed and validated the parameters of this reorder policy. Supports governance and audit of policy maintenance cadence.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The expected number of calendar days from placement of a replenishment purchase order to receipt of goods at the destination location. Used to calculate the reorder point and ensure stock does not fall below safety stock during the replenishment cycle.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory node type to which this policy applies: store (retail store), DC (Distribution Center), MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center), dark_store (fulfillment-only location), or warehouse. Drives replenishment logic differences by node type.']
  max_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable on-hand inventory level (in units) for this SKU at this location. Used in min/max reorder method as the upper bound. Replenishment orders are sized to bring stock up to this level without exceeding it.']
  min_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum allowable on-hand inventory level (in units) for this SKU at this location. Used in min/max reorder method as the lower bound. Stock falling below this level triggers an urgent replenishment order.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum number of units that must be ordered per replenishment order as stipulated by the supplier contract. Replenishment engine enforces this floor when generating inventory_replenishment_order records.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next planner review of this reorder policy\'s parameters. Calculated from last_reviewed_date plus the review_cycle_days. Drives planner workqueue prioritization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for replenishment planners to document rationale for policy parameter choices, exceptions, seasonal considerations, or supplier-specific constraints associated with this reorder policy.']
  order_multiple decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity increment in which orders must be placed, reflecting the supplier\'s case pack size or Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ). All replenishment order quantities are rounded up to the nearest multiple of this value to comply with supplier constraints.']
  order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The fixed quantity (in units) to order each time a replenishment trigger is activated. Used for fixed_quantity reorder method. For other methods, this field may be null and the order quantity is dynamically calculated.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique alphanumeric code identifying the reorder policy, used by replenishment planners and referenced in automated replenishment engines (e.g., Blue Yonder). Externally visible identifier across systems.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the reorder policy, used by replenishment planners to identify and manage policies (e.g., \'DC Grocery Min/Max Weekly\', \'Store Electronics WOS-Based\').']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reorder policy. Controls whether the policy is actively used by the replenishment engine to generate inventory_replenishment_order records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|draft|pending_approval|expired|suspended — promote to reference product]']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Incrementing version number for this reorder policy record, incremented each time parameters are materially changed. Supports policy change history tracking and rollback capability for replenishment planners.']
  reorder_method varchar [note: 'The replenishment calculation method applied by this policy: min_max (reorder when stock falls below minimum, order up to maximum), days_of_supply (maintain a target number of days of inventory), wos_based (Weeks of Supply (WOS) target), forecast_driven (demand forecast from Blue Yonder), fixed_quantity (constant order quantity), vendor_managed (VMI — Vendor Managed Inventory where supplier controls replenishment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: min_max|days_of_supply|wos_based|forecast_driven|fixed_quantity|vendor_managed — promote to reference product]']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The on-hand stock level (in units) at which a replenishment order is automatically triggered. When the stock_position in inventory falls at or below this threshold, an inventory_replenishment_order record is generated. Core parameter for min/max and fixed-quantity reorder methods.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'The frequency in calendar days at which the replenishment engine evaluates this policy and checks whether a reorder trigger has been breached. Defines the periodic review interval (e.g., daily=1, weekly=7, bi-weekly=14).']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Buffer stock quantity (in units) maintained above the reorder point to protect against demand variability and supply lead time uncertainty. Prevents stockouts during replenishment cycles. A key input to the reorder point calculation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this reorder policy was originated or last updated: ORMS (Oracle Retail Merchandising System), SAP_MM (SAP S/4HANA MM Module), BLUE_YONDER (Blue Yonder Demand Planning), MANUAL (planner-entered), MDM (Informatica MDM). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  target_days_of_supply decimal(10,2) [note: 'The desired number of days of inventory coverage to maintain at this location for this SKU. Used in days_of_supply reorder method to calculate the replenishment order quantity based on forecasted daily demand.']
  target_wos decimal(10,2) [note: 'The desired number of weeks of inventory coverage (Weeks of Supply — WOS) to maintain at this location for this SKU. Used in wos_based reorder method. Replenishment orders are sized to achieve this WOS target based on forecasted weekly demand.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which all quantity fields (reorder_point_quantity, safety_stock_quantity, min/max, order_quantity, etc.) are expressed for this policy. Aligns with the SKU\'s base unit of measure in the product master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|LB|KG|OZ|L|ML|FT|M — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reorder policy record was most recently modified. Tracks parameter changes made by replenishment planners and supports change audit trails.']

  Note: 'SKU-location level replenishment parameters defining when and how much to reorder. Captures reorder method (min/max, days-of-supply, WOS-based, forecast-driven), reorder point, safety stock, min/max levels, order multiple (MOQ/case pack), lead time, review cycle, supplier preference, and policy effective/expiry dates. Trigger thresholds generate inventory_replenishment_order records when stock_position breaches defined levels. Owned and maintained by replenishment planners; consumed by automated replenishment engines.'
}

Table inventory.asn {
  asn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Advance Shipment Notice record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  asn_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating inventory node (DC, MFC, store, or supplier facility) from which the shipment was dispatched. Used for inter-node transfer ASNs.']
  asn_supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier.vendor']
  asn_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who originated the shipment. Populated when asn_type is \'supplier\' or \'drop_ship\'. Links to the supplier master record in Informatica MDM.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier responsible for transporting the shipment. Used for carrier performance tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  destination_node_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving inventory node (store, DC, or MFC) where the shipment is expected to arrive. Drives dock scheduling and pre-receiving workflows in the WMS.']
  dock_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dock scheduling appointment associated with this ASN arrival. Links to supplychain.inbound_appointment for dock door and labor resource planning.']
  food_safety_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_log. Business justification: Inbound shipments trigger temperature and quality monitoring logs at receiving. Retail food operations require linking ASNs to food safety logs for HACCP compliance, critical limit verification, and c']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory goods receipt record created upon physical receipt of this shipment. Links ASN to the goods receipt for discrepancy detection and inventory valuation.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that this ASN fulfills. Links to the supplychain.purchase_order product for PO-to-ASN reconciliation and receiving discrepancy detection.']
  supplier_edi_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the EDI 856 transaction set that originated this ASN record. Used for EDI audit trails, reprocessing, and trading partner dispute resolution.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date the shipment physically arrived at the destination node dock. Compared against expected_arrival_date to measure carrier on-time delivery performance and lead time actuals.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the ASN as transmitted by the supplier or DC via EDI 856 or API. Used for cross-system reconciliation and dock scheduling.']
  asn_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ASN. \'sent\' = transmitted by sender; \'in_transit\' = shipment en route; \'arrived\' = at destination dock; \'receiving\' = active receiving in WMS; \'received\' = fully received; \'discrepant\' = quantity or SKU variance detected; \'cancelled\' = voided prior to receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sent|in_transit|arrived|receiving|received|discrepant|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  asn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ASN by origin and fulfillment pattern. \'supplier\' = inbound from vendor; \'dc_transfer\' = inter-DC or DC-to-store transfer; \'cross_dock\' = direct cross-dock flow; \'drop_ship\' = vendor direct-to-customer; \'return_to_dc\' = store or customer return to DC.']
