  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number issued by the carrier for the shipment. Legal document of title used for freight claims, customs clearance, and receiving verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN record was first created in the system, either upon EDI 856 inbound processing or manual entry. Audit trail for record lineage.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving node. Determines which receiving workflow and dock door assignment logic applies in the WMS.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a receiving discrepancy (quantity overage, shortage, or SKU mismatch) was detected when comparing the ASN to the actual goods receipt. Triggers discrepancy resolution workflow.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receiving discrepancy detected against the ASN. \'overage\' = more units received than declared; \'shortage\' = fewer units; \'sku_mismatch\' = wrong SKU received; \'damage\' = goods received in damaged condition; \'none\' = no discrepancy.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Assigned dock door at the destination node for unloading this shipment. Pre-assigned during dock scheduling to optimize receiving throughput and yard management.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination node. Drives dock door scheduling, labor planning, and replenishment availability projections.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Freight payment responsibility terms for the shipment. \'prepaid\' = supplier pays freight; \'collect\' = receiver pays; \'third_party\' = billed to a third party. Drives accounts payable freight accruals.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment is designated for cross-docking (direct transfer without storage) at the destination DC. Triggers cross-dock workflow in the WMS and bypasses putaway.']
  is_rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID tags are present on cartons or pallets in this shipment, enabling automated receiving and inventory tracking via RFID readers at the dock.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'External purchase order number as referenced on the ASN document. Denormalized from the PO for cross-system matching with EDI 856 transmissions and supplier invoices.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number for LTL (Less-Than-Truckload) shipments. Used for freight bill matching and carrier invoice reconciliation.']
  receiving_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when all line items on the ASN were fully received and confirmed in the WMS. Marks the transition from \'receiving\' to \'received\' or \'discrepant\' status.']
  ship_date date [note: 'Date the shipment was physically dispatched from the source node or supplier facility. Used to calculate lead time and measure supplier on-time shipping performance.']
  shipment_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used for the shipment. Drives freight cost benchmarking, carrier selection analytics, and carbon footprint reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truckload|ltl|parcel|rail|air|ocean|intermodal — promote to reference product]']
  source_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the originating node. Distinguishes supplier facilities from internal DC, MFC, store, or dark store nodes to support routing and receiving workflow logic.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this ASN record. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  total_cartons varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons (cases/boxes) included in the shipment as declared on the ASN. Used for dock receiving verification, pallet count planning, and discrepancy detection.']
  total_pallets varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets in the shipment as declared on the ASN. Used for dock door capacity planning, unloading labor scheduling, and yard management.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of sellable units across all SKU lines declared on the ASN. Used for pre-receiving inventory projection and receiving discrepancy detection against goods receipt.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the shipment in cubic meters as declared on the ASN. Used for trailer utilization analysis, DC storage capacity planning, and freight density calculations.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the entire shipment in kilograms as declared on the ASN. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier compliance, and dock equipment planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Enables real-time in-transit visibility and last-mile delivery monitoring via carrier APIs.']
  transmission_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the ASN was received. \'edi_856\' = GS1 EDI 856 standard; \'api\' = REST/SOAP API integration; \'manual\' = entered by receiving staff; \'portal\' = supplier self-service portal.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN was transmitted by the supplier or DC via EDI 856 or API. Distinct from created_timestamp; used to measure EDI lead time and supplier advance notice compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ASN record, including status transitions, quantity updates, or carrier information changes. Supports change data capture in the lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Advance Shipment Notice (ASN) records received from suppliers or DCs prior to physical goods arrival. Captures ASN number, sender (supplier or DC), destination node, expected arrival date, carrier, tracking number, total cartons, total units, SKU-level line details, and ASN status (sent, in-transit, received, discrepant). Enables pre-receiving workflows, dock scheduling, and receiving discrepancy detection against corresponding inventory_goods_receipt records. Received via EDI 856 or API from trading partners.'
}

Table inventory.lot {
  lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory lot or batch record. Primary key for the lot entity.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Product recall and quality issue management requires category-level aggregation for buyer notification and regulatory reporting. Lot traceability reporting is organized by merchandise category. Catego']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Lot-level costing for FEFO/FIFO valuation and batch-specific cost tracking. Perishables, pharmaceuticals, and regulated products require lot-specific cost basis for accurate inventory valuation, recal']
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_plan. Business justification: Lot tracking is foundational to food safety plans for traceability, recall readiness, and FSMA compliance. Retail grocers must link lot records to facility food safety plans to demonstrate supply chai']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt transaction that brought this lot into inventory. Links to receiving documentation.']
  parent_lot_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lot (parent_lot_id)']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that sourced this lot. Links lot traceability to procurement records.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU that this lot represents. Links lot-level tracking to product master data.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided this lot. Critical for recall management and vendor performance tracking.']
  available_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Current quantity available for sale or allocation from this lot. Decreases as units are sold or reserved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier assigned batch identifier. May differ from internal lot number for vendor-managed inventory scenarios.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'Date indicating optimal quality period. Product may still be safe after this date but quality may degrade. Distinct from expiry date.']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Product certification or compliance code for the lot. Examples: organic, kosher, halal, fair trade, non-GMO. Supports labeling and marketing claims.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or grown. Required for customs and labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lot record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for unit cost. Standard codes include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, etc.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the product lot expires and should no longer be sold or consumed. Mandatory for perishable goods and pharmaceuticals.']
  fefo_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number for FEFO picking logic. Lower numbers indicate earlier expiry and should be picked first to minimize waste.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for the product. May be UPC, EAN, or other GTIN format. Used for cross-enterprise traceability.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when quality inspection was performed on the lot. Required for compliance with food safety and product safety regulations.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Detailed outcome or notes from the quality inspection process. Captures defects, non-conformances, or approval notes.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is a private label or store brand product. True if private label; false if national brand.']
  is_recalled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is subject to a product recall. True if recalled; false otherwise. Triggers immediate hold and customer notification.']
  is_vendor_managed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is part of a vendor-managed inventory agreement. True if VMI; false if retailer-owned.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the lot or batch. Human-readable code used for tracking and traceability across the supply chain.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lot. Determines whether the lot is available for sale, held for quality inspection, or restricted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|quarantine|hold|released|expired|recalled|disposed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date when the product lot was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf life calculation and food safety compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the lot. Captures special handling instructions, quality observations, or operational remarks.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Quality inspection outcome for the lot. Approved lots are available for sale; rejected lots must be returned or disposed.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units in the lot at receipt. Used for inventory valuation and traceability reporting.']
  recall_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official recall case or reference number issued by regulatory authority or manufacturer. Links to recall management system.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was received into inventory at the destination node. Used for aging analysis and FEFO sequencing.']
  remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining until expiry as of the current date. Calculated field used for near-expiry alerts and markdown triggers.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life of the product in days from manufacture date to expiry date. Used for aging analysis and markdown planning.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that created or owns this lot record. Examples: WMS, ERP, MDM. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse bin, zone, or storage location where the lot is physically stored. Supports pick/pack operations and cycle counting.']
  supplier_lot_reference varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s original lot or batch identifier. Used for traceability back to the supplier\'s manufacturing records.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature zone for the lot. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for this lot. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculation. May vary by lot due to pricing changes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for lot quantities. Standard codes: EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, LT=Liter, GL=Gallon. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|KG|LB|LT|GL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lot record was last modified. Tracks changes to lot status, quantity, or other attributes for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Master record for inventory lot/batch tracking at the SKU level. Captures lot number, manufacture date, expiry date, supplier lot reference, and quality status. Supports traceability for food safety (FDA FSMA) and recall management.'
}

Table inventory.node_assortment {
  node_assortment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this node-category assortment plan record']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the inventory node (store, DC, MFC) where this category is carried']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchandise hierarchy node (category, subcategory, segment) carried at this location']
  actual_sku_count varchar [note: 'Current number of distinct SKUs from this category actively stocked at this location. Compared against target_sku_count for assortment compliance monitoring']
  assortment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this category at this location. ACTIVE means category is currently stocked and sold; PLANNED means approved for future rollout; DISCONTINUED means category removed from location; SEASONAL_INACTIVE means temporarily not carried']
  category_manager_override_name varchar [note: 'Name of category manager responsible for this specific node-category combination when it differs from the hierarchy-level default. Used for regional or format-specific category ownership']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this node assortment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and assortment planning history']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this category assortment was discontinued at this location. Null for currently active assortments. Supports assortment history and space reallocation analysis']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this category assortment became active at this location. Supports assortment history tracking and seasonal rollout planning']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this node assortment record was last updated. Tracks assortment plan changes and space reallocation events']
  local_assortment_override boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this location has local merchandising authority to deviate from corporate assortment plan for this category. True for high-autonomy formats or test stores']
  max_presentation_qty varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units that can be displayed on shelf for this category at this location based on allocated space and planogram design']
  min_presentation_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be displayed on shelf for this category at this location to maintain acceptable customer presentation standards']
  planogram_count varchar [note: 'Number of active planograms assigned to this category at this location. Planograms define shelf layout and product placement; count indicates space allocation complexity']
  replenishment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification for replenishing this category at this location. CRITICAL for high-velocity destination categories; HIGH for key traffic drivers; MEDIUM for routine; LOW for convenience/impulse']
  shelf_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Total shelf capacity allocated to this category at this location, measured in unit facings or shelf positions. Drives maximum inventory display quantity']
  space_allocation_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical floor space allocated to this category at this location, measured in square feet. Used for space productivity analysis and category performance reporting']
  target_sku_count varchar [note: 'Target number of distinct SKUs from this category that should be carried at this location. Drives assortment depth decisions based on node format and customer demographics']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assortment planning decision between inventory_node and item_hierarchy. It captures the strategic and operational decisions about which merchandise categories are carried at which locations, including space allocation, planogram assignments, and replenishment rules. Each record links one inventory_node to one item_hierarchy node with attributes that govern how that category is merchandised at that specific location.. Existence Justification: In retail operations, assortment planning is a core business process where each inventory node (store, DC, MFC) carries multiple merchandise categories, and each category is carried across multiple locations with location-specific merchandising rules. Store planners and category managers actively manage these node-category assortment decisions, allocating shelf space, assigning planograms, setting presentation quantities, and defining replenishment priorities that vary by location format, customer demographics, and regional preferences. This is not a derived analytical correlation but an operational planning entity that humans create, update, and monitor.'
}

Table inventory.promo_stock_allocation {
  promo_stock_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each promotional stock allocation record']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the promotional offer to which this stock position is allocated']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific SKU-location stock position allocated to this promotional offer']
  actual_units_sold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of units sold from this stock position under this promotional offer, tracked for promotional performance analysis']
  allocation_created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this promotional stock allocation']
  allocation_created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promotional stock allocation record was created by the promotion planning system']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this promotional stock allocation. planned: allocation created but offer not yet active; active: offer live and inventory allocated; depleted: reserved inventory exhausted; cancelled: allocation cancelled before completion; completed: offer period ended normally']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for promotional price and revenue amounts']
  display_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific in-store display location code for this promotional stock allocation (e.g., ENDCAP_A1, FEATURE_TABLE_3, GONDOLA_END_B5), indicating where this SKU should be merchandised for this promotion']
  offer_end_date date [note: 'The date when this specific stock position is no longer eligible for this promotional offer at this location']
  offer_start_date date [note: 'The date when this specific stock position becomes eligible for this promotional offer at this location (may differ from campaign-level dates due to inventory availability or regional rollout)']
  promotional_inventory_reserve_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of inventory at this stock position reserved specifically for this promotional offer, ensuring adequate stock for the promotion duration and preventing depletion by non-promotional sales']
  promotional_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The specific promotional price for this SKU at this location under this offer, calculated based on the offer discount mechanics applied to the regular price']
  promotional_quantity_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum or target quantity threshold for this SKU-location to participate in the promotional offer (e.g., minimum display quantity, BOGO threshold)']
  promotional_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated from sales of this SKU at this location under this promotional offer']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment between stock_position and promo_offer. It captures the allocation of specific SKU-location inventory to promotional campaigns, including promotional pricing, reserved quantities, display placement, and offer-specific timing. Each record links one stock position to one promotional offer with attributes that exist only in the context of this promotional inventory allocation.. Existence Justification: In retail operations, promotional inventory allocation is an active operational process where promotion managers assign specific SKU-location stock positions to promotional campaigns. A single stock position can participate in multiple concurrent promotional offers (e.g., a loyalty-exclusive discount stacked with a category-wide sale, or sequential promotions as inventory rolls from one campaign to another). Conversely, a single promotional offer applies to multiple stock positions across stores, DCs, and e-commerce fulfillment nodes. The business actively manages these allocations with relationship-specific data including promotional pricing, reserved quantities, display locations, and offer timing that varies by location.'
}

Table inventory.location_assignment {
  location_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each location assignment record. Primary key for the association.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the retail workforce member (associate) who is assigned to the inventory node location.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to inventory.inventory_node. Identifies the location where the associate is assigned.']
  node_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the inventory node (store, DC, MFC, or other location) where the associate is assigned to work.']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Level of system and physical access the associate has at this specific inventory node. Values: FULL (full operational access), LIMITED (restricted areas only), TRAINING (supervised access during cross-training), EMERGENCY (backup coverage only). Drives security permissions, badge access, and system authorization at the location level.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the associate\'s assignment to this inventory node location ended. Null for active assignments. Used for tracking transfers, temporary assignments, and historical workforce deployment.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the associate\'s assignment to this inventory node location began. Used for tracking assignment history, tenure at location, and temporal workforce deployment analysis.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment nature. PERMANENT indicates standard long-term assignment, TEMPORARY for defined-duration transfers, SEASONAL for holiday/peak period deployment, CROSS_TRAINING for skill development assignments, EMERGENCY_COVERAGE for short-term staffing needs. Drives scheduling rules and labor planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this location assignment record was created. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  primary_location_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory node is the associate\'s primary work location (true) or a secondary/cross-training location (false). Used for labor cost allocation, scheduling priority, and determining home base for workforce planning.']
  role_at_location varchar [note: 'Specific role or function the associate performs at this inventory node, which may differ from their primary job title. Examples: Floor Associate, Receiving Clerk, Shift Supervisor, Cross-Training, Seasonal Support. Used for location-specific scheduling, task assignment, and workforce capability tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this location assignment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment between inventory_node and associate. It captures the operational assignment of retail workforce members to specific inventory-holding locations (stores, DCs, MFCs) including cross-training assignments, seasonal redeployments, temporary transfers, and multi-location coverage. Each record links one inventory_node to one associate with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment relationship, enabling tracking of primary vs secondary locations, assignment duration, role-specific access levels, and workforce deployment history across the retail network.. Existence Justification: In retail operations, associates routinely work at multiple locations throughout their employment: store associates provide cross-store coverage during peak periods, DC workers are temporarily deployed to stores for inventory events, seasonal staff rotate between locations, and employees receive cross-training assignments at secondary facilities. Simultaneously, each inventory node (store, DC, MFC) has many associates assigned to it with varying roles, access levels, and assignment types. The business actively manages these assignments through workforce management systems, tracking assignment dates, primary vs secondary location status, role-specific access, and deployment history.'
}

Table inventory.assortment_deployment {
  assortment_deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this deployment record. Primary key.']
  assortment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the strategic assortment plan being deployed to this location']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the inventory node (store, DC, MFC) where this assortment plan is deployed']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific planogram (shelf layout) used to implement this assortment plan at this location. May vary by store format or size.']
  responsible_buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise buyer responsible for ensuring this deployment is executed successfully at this location.']
  actual_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of SKUs actually stocked and available at this inventory node as of the last measurement. Used to calculate compliance.']
  compliance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of planned SKUs that are actually in stock at this location, calculated as (actual_sku_count / planned_sku_count) * 100. Key metric for assortment execution quality.']
  deployment_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the assortment was fully deployed and reached initial compliance at this location.']
  deployment_start_date date [note: 'Date when deployment activities began at this location (receiving, stocking, planogram setup).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this assortment plan became effective at this specific inventory node. Marks the start of deployment execution.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assortment deployment at this location: planned (not yet started), in_progress (actively rolling out), deployed (fully implemented), non_compliant (deployed but below compliance threshold), discontinued (plan no longer active at this location).']
  last_compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance measurement for this deployment. Compliance is typically measured weekly or monthly.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about deployment challenges, adjustments, or special circumstances at this location (e.g., \'Reduced SKU count due to limited shelf space\', \'Delayed deployment due to remodel\').']
  planned_sku_count varchar [note: 'Number of SKUs planned to be carried at this specific inventory node under this assortment plan. May differ from the plan-level target due to store cluster or format-specific adjustments.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the deployment of a strategic assortment plan to a specific inventory node (store, DC, or fulfillment center). It captures the implementation status, compliance metrics, and timing of assortment execution at each location. Each record links one inventory_node to one assortment_plan with attributes that track how well the planned assortment is being executed at that specific location.. Existence Justification: In retail operations, assortment plans are deployed to multiple inventory nodes (stores, DCs, fulfillment centers), and each node carries multiple assortment plans across different categories and seasons. Merchandising teams actively manage these deployments by tracking compliance (planned vs actual SKU count), implementation status, and timing at each location-plan combination. This is a recognized operational relationship where the deployment itself has meaningful attributes that belong to neither the plan nor the node alone.'
}

Table customer.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer profile. Primary key for the golden customer record in Informatica MDM.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer\'s preferred or most frequently visited store location, used for localized assortment and clienteling.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer was originally acquired, used for CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) analysis and channel effectiveness measurement.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the customer first engaged with the business, marking the start of the customer lifecycle.']
  cac_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to acquire this customer, including marketing spend, promotional discounts, and channel costs. Used for CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) to CAC ratio analysis.']
  ccpa_opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when CCPA opt-out request was processed, required for compliance audit trails.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has opted out of personal data sale under CCPA regulations.']
  cltv_score decimal(15,2) [note: 'Predicted total revenue value this customer will generate over their entire relationship with the business, calculated using RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) models and predictive analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer profile record was first created in the master data management system.']
  customer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of customer as individual consumer (B2C) or corporate buyer (B2B), including special categories such as employee or VIP.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the customer, used for age verification, lifecycle marketing, and personalized birthday promotions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for customer communication across all channels including e-commerce, loyalty programs, and promotional campaigns.']
  email_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating customer consent to receive promotional emails and newsletters.']
  employee_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer is an employee eligible for employee discount programs.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name or given name of the customer as recorded in the master data management system.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the customer, typically concatenated from first, middle, and last name components.']
  gdpr_consent_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when GDPR consent was granted by the customer, required for compliance audit trails.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR requirements.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-identified gender of the customer, used for personalized merchandising and assortment planning.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this customer to a household group for family-level analytics and shared loyalty benefits.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the customer\'s most recent interaction with the business across any touchpoint including POS, e-commerce, mobile app, or customer service.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any attribute in the customer profile record.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name or family name of the customer as recorded in the master data management system.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the customer\'s most recent purchase transaction across all channels, used for recency analysis in RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the customer lifecycle journey, used for targeted marketing campaigns and retention strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospect|new|active|at_risk|dormant|churned|reactivated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level in the loyalty program, determining benefits, discounts, and personalized services.']
  marketing_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has consented to receive marketing communications including email, SMS, and direct mail.']
  mdm_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0-100) from Informatica MDM indicating the quality and reliability of the golden record match and data completeness.']
  mdm_last_match_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last MDM matching and survivorship process execution that updated this golden record.']
  mdm_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the primary source system that contributed the authoritative data for this golden record (e.g., SAP CAR, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, POS).']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the customer. Nullable field for customers without a middle name.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number used for SMS notifications, mobile app authentication, and last-mile delivery coordination.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer\'s nationality or citizenship.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score indicating customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend, scored from -100 to 100.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for customer service, order notifications, and BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) communications.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred method for receiving communications and notifications from the business.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the customer\'s preferred language for communication and marketing materials.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the customer profile, controlling access to services and transactions across all channels.']
  sms_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating customer consent to receive promotional SMS messages and mobile notifications.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer is designated as a VIP for special clienteling services and exclusive offers.']

  Note: 'Golden record for every customer (B2C and B2B) served by Retail. Stores core identity attributes sourced from Informatica MDM including full legal name, date of birth, gender, preferred language, nationality, customer type (individual/corporate), acquisition channel, CAC, CLTV score, NPS score, lifecycle stage, privacy consent flags (GDPR/CCPA), and golden-record confidence score. This is the single authoritative master entity for all customer identity data across POS, e-commerce, and mobile channels.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee assigned as the dedicated account manager for this account (typically for high-value B2B or VIP accounts). Null for standard consumer accounts without dedicated management.']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the default billing address for this account. Used for invoicing, credit checks, and payment processing. Null if no billing address on file.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s preferred or home store location for in-store pickup, returns, and personalized store-based services. Null if no preference set.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: B2B and corporate accounts require contract-specific pricing (volume discounts, negotiated rates, special terms). Account managers assign custom price lists during contract setup. Essential for wholes']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer profile who owns this account. One customer may have multiple accounts (e.g., personal and business).']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the default shipping address for this account. Used for order fulfillment and delivery. Null if no default shipping address set.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique account number used for customer communication, statements, and customer service lookup. Distinct from internal account_id.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account. Active accounts can transact; suspended accounts are temporarily blocked; closed accounts are permanently terminated; pending_activation awaits customer verification; frozen accounts are under investigation; dormant accounts have no recent activity.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier or membership level of the account, determining benefits, discounts, and service priority. Standard is base tier; premium, VIP, and platinum offer escalating benefits.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account indicating the nature of the commercial relationship: personal (B2C consumer), business (B2B corporate), employee (staff discount account), or wholesale (bulk buyer).']
  b2b_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account receives B2B contract pricing instead of standard retail pricing. True for wholesale and corporate accounts; false for consumer accounts.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the account was permanently closed. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis and account lifecycle reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount (in base currency) extended to this account for deferred payment or credit purchases. Null for cash-only accounts. Used for credit management and risk assessment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account\'s base currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All monetary transactions and balances for this account are denominated in this currency.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for analytics, advertising, or personalization. True if consented; false otherwise. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  default_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s default payment method for this account (e.g., credit card on file, bank account). Null if no default set. Used for one-click checkout and recurring billing.']
  employee_discount_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is eligible for employee discount pricing. True for employee accounts and authorized family members; false otherwise.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase or transaction on this account. Used for recency analysis, dormancy detection, and customer lifecycle segmentation (RFM analysis).']
  loyalty_program_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in the retailer\'s loyalty or rewards program. True if enrolled; false otherwise. Used for loyalty analytics and targeted promotions.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has consented to receive marketing communications (email, SMS, direct mail). True if opted in; false if opted out. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  marketing_opt_in_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder provided marketing consent. Null if never opted in. Used for consent audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  marketing_opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder withdrew marketing consent. Null if currently opted in or never opted in. Used for consent audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes about the account (e.g., special handling instructions, VIP preferences, service history, escalation notes). Used by customer service and account management teams.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the account was first opened and activated. Used for account age calculations, tenure-based benefits, and lifecycle analytics.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance owed by the account holder (in base currency). Positive values indicate amounts owed to Retail; negative values indicate credit balances. Used for accounts receivable and collections.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred interaction channel for shopping and service. Used for omnichannel routing, personalized marketing, and channel-specific offers.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that is the authoritative source for this account record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail, Informatica MDM). Used for data lineage and master data management.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date when the account was most recently suspended. Null if never suspended or currently active. Used for compliance and risk management reporting.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the account was suspended (e.g., payment default, fraud investigation, customer request, policy violation). Null if never suspended.']
  tax_exempt_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax exemption certificate number for tax-exempt accounts. Null for non-exempt accounts. Required for audit and compliance verification.']
  tax_exempt_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the tax exemption certificate. Null for non-exempt accounts or perpetual exemptions. Used for compliance monitoring and renewal reminders.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is exempt from sales tax (e.g., non-profit organizations, government entities, resellers with valid tax exemption certificates). True if exempt; false otherwise.']
  total_lifetime_orders varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all orders placed by this account since inception. Used for frequency analysis in RFM segmentation and customer value scoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']

  Note: 'Commercial relationship record between a customer and Retail, distinct from profile (identity). Tracks account number, status (active/suspended/closed), open date, tier (standard/premium/VIP), credit limit, preferred store, preferred channel (in-store/online/mobile), default payment method reference, and account-level flags (employee discount eligibility, tax-exempt status, B2B pricing flag). One profile may have multiple accounts (e.g., personal + business). Supports account-level reporting, credit management, and omnichannel preference routing.'
}

Table customer.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the household unit. Primary key for the household entity.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store location most frequently visited by household members. Used for localized promotions and inventory planning.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary customer profile within the household. This member typically represents the household in loyalty programs and receives primary communications.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the household. Used for delivery, geo-targeting, and household matching.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field for household address.']
  average_basket_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transaction value (ATV) across all household purchases. Used for household-level pricing and promotion strategies.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the household address. Used for regional marketing and store assignment.']
  combined_cltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregated Customer Lifetime Value across all household members. Represents the total predicted revenue from the household over its lifetime. Used for household-level loyalty tier assignment and VIP treatment.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred method for household-level marketing communications. Honors consent and privacy preferences.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the household address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for international operations and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household record was first created in the system. Used for audit and data lineage.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to sharing data with third-party partners. True if consented, False otherwise.']
  estimated_income_band varchar [note: 'Estimated annual household income range. Derived from third-party data enrichment or modeled from purchase behavior. Used for targeted marketing and assortment planning.']
  external_household_code varchar [note: 'Household identifier from the source system or external partner. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data integration.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the household (e.g., \'Smith Family\', \'Johnson Household\'). Used for clienteling and personalized marketing.']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household record. Active households are eligible for household-level promotions and loyalty benefits.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household structure. Used for targeted marketing and assortment planning.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase made by any household member. Used for recency analysis in RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Household-level loyalty program tier based on combined spending and engagement. Determines household-level benefits and promotions.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to receive marketing communications. True if opted in, False otherwise.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the household address. Critical for delivery routing, geo-analytics, and demographic segmentation.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'Primary shopping channel used by the household. Supports omnichannel personalization and channel-specific promotions.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing the household\'s preferred language for communications (e.g., en, es, fr). Used for localized marketing and customer service.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Marketing or behavioral segment assigned to the household based on RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) analysis, purchase patterns, and demographics. Used for targeted campaigns.']
  size varchar [note: 'Number of individual members in the household. Used for basket size prediction and household-level promotions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this household record (e.g., Informatica MDM, Salesforce, SAP CAR). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the household address. Used for tax calculation and regional compliance.']
  total_loyalty_points varchar [note: 'Aggregated loyalty points balance across all household members. Used for household-level redemption and rewards.']
  total_purchase_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transactions made by all household members. Used for frequency analysis in RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.']
  total_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all purchases made by household members. Used for monetary analysis in RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household record was last modified. Used for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Groups individual customer profiles into a household unit for B2C clienteling, basket analysis, and loyalty aggregation. Stores household identifier, household name, primary member profile reference, household address, estimated household income band, household size, and combined CLTV. Enables Retail to recognize family purchasing patterns, apply household-level promotions, and support omnichannel personalization across all household members.'
}

Table customer.corporate_account {
  corporate_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the B2B corporate customer account. Primary key for the corporate account entity.']
  account_manager_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: B2B corporate accounts require dedicated account managers (sales reps or account executives) who handle contract negotiations, pricing agreements, credit terms, and ongoing relationship management. Es']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.address']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent corporate account if this account is part of a multi-location or subsidiary hierarchy. Null for standalone accounts or top-level parent accounts. Enables consolidated billing and enterprise-wide reporting.']
  primary_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Corporate accounts have a designated primary contact person. Currently, corporate_account stores primary_contact_name, primary_contact_email, primary_contact_phone as denormalized columns. Adding prim']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.address']
  account_established_date date [note: 'Date when the corporate account was first created in the system. Used to calculate customer tenure and lifetime value metrics.']
  annual_spend_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the corporate account based on annual purchase volume. Used to determine discount levels, service priority, and account management resources. Tier_1 = highest spend (>$1M), Tier_5 = lowest spend (<$10K). Tiers recalculated annually.']
  business_entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the corporate entity. Values: sole_proprietorship, partnership, llc (Limited Liability Company), corporation (C-Corp), s_corp (S-Corporation), non_profit (501c3 or similar), government (public sector entity). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sole_proprietorship|partnership|llc|corporation|s_corp|non_profit|government — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corporate account has negotiated contract pricing that overrides standard catalog prices. True = custom pricing agreement in place; False = standard pricing applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this corporate account record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for the corporate account before requiring payment or credit hold. Expressed in USD. Null if account is cash-only.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current credit approval status for the corporate account. Determines whether the customer can purchase on credit terms. Values: approved (credit line active), pending (application submitted), declined (credit denied), under_review (periodic credit review in progress), suspended (credit privileges temporarily revoked).']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or fictitious business name under which the corporate customer operates, if different from legal name. Used for marketing and customer-facing communications.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to establish business credit profile and track commercial credit history. Used for supplier onboarding and credit evaluation.']
  industry_classification_naics varchar [note: 'Six-digit code classifying the corporate customer\'s primary industry using the NAICS standard. Provides more granular industry segmentation than SIC for modern business categories.']
  industry_classification_sic varchar [note: 'Four-digit code classifying the corporate customer\'s primary industry sector using the U.S. Standard Industrial Classification system. Used for market segmentation and industry analysis.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order placed by this corporate account. Used for recency analysis and dormant account identification.']
  legal_business_name varchar [note: 'The official registered legal name of the corporate entity as it appears on government filings and tax documents. Used for contracts, invoicing, and legal compliance.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the corporate customer. Values: net_30 (payment due 30 days after invoice), net_45, net_60, net_90, due_on_receipt (immediate payment), prepay (payment before shipment), custom (non-standard terms documented separately). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|prepay|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preferred_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Default shipping method preference for corporate orders. Values: standard_ground, expedited, next_day, freight (for large/bulk orders), customer_pickup (will-call).']
  tax_exempt_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax exemption certificate number. Required for tax-exempt accounts to validate exemption status during order processing and audits.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corporate account is exempt from sales tax (e.g., government entities, non-profits with valid exemption certificates). True = tax exempt; False = taxable.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number (EIN) assigned by the IRS for business tax reporting and compliance. Format: XX-XXXXXXX.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this corporate account record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']

  Note: 'B2B corporate customer entity representing business clients such as small businesses, restaurants, and institutional buyers purchasing from Retail. Stores legal business name, tax ID / EIN, DUNS number, industry classification (SIC/NAICS), credit terms, assigned account manager, annual spend tier, contract pricing flag, and parent-subsidiary hierarchy reference. Supports B2B procurement workflows distinct from B2C consumer accounts.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contact method record. Primary key.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer profile or corporate account that owns this contact method. Links to the customer master record in Informatica MDM.']
  bounce_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of times communication attempts via this contact method have bounced or failed. Used to trigger contact validation workflows and suppress invalid addresses.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint where the customer provided consent for this contact method (e.g., web registration, mobile app, POS, call center, in-store signup). Supports consent audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|pos|call_center|email|in_store|third_party — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact method. Active indicates usable for communication; bounced/invalid indicates delivery failure; suppressed indicates customer request or compliance block; pending_verification indicates awaiting confirmation.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of contact method (e.g., primary email, mobile phone, work phone, WhatsApp, SMS, fax). Determines the channel and priority for omnichannel outreach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary_email|secondary_email|mobile|home_phone|work_phone|whatsapp|sms|fax — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual contact point value: email address, phone number, or social media handle. This is the primary data element used for customer communication and CRM campaigns via Salesforce Service Cloud. Classified as restricted PII as it may contain email, phone, or other personal identifiers.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country associated with this contact method (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Used for regional compliance and localization of communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contact method record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this contact method ceased to be active or valid. Nullable for currently active contact methods. Supports temporal tracking and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this contact method became active and valid for customer communication. Supports temporal tracking and historical analysis.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary contact method for the customer. Used to prioritize communication channels in omnichannel campaigns and clienteling.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the contact method has been verified (e.g., email verification link clicked, phone number confirmed via OTP). Ensures data quality for marketing and transactional communications.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the customers preferred language for communications via this contact method (e.g., en, es, fr). Supports personalized omnichannel outreach.']
  last_bounce_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent bounce or delivery failure for this contact method. Nullable if no bounces have occurred.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this contact method record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit trails.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'The date when this contact method was last successfully used for customer communication (email sent, SMS delivered, call completed). Helps identify stale or inactive contact points.']
  opt_in_date date [note: 'The date when the customer provided consent for communication via this contact method. Required for audit trails under GDPR and CCPA.']
  opt_in_marketing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has opted in to receive marketing communications via this contact method. Critical for GDPR and CCPA consent enforcement.']
  opt_in_transactional boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has opted in to receive transactional communications (order confirmations, shipping updates, receipts) via this contact method.']
  opt_out_date date [note: 'The date when the customer withdrew consent or opted out of communications via this contact method. Nullable if customer has not opted out.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating the priority order of this contact method relative to other contact methods for the same customer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for fallback logic in omnichannel campaigns.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or platform that originally captured this contact method (e.g., Salesforce Service Cloud, SAP CAR, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, POS, Informatica MDM). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this contact method in the source system. Enables traceability and cross-system reconciliation.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when the contact method was last verified by the customer. Used to track data freshness and trigger re-verification workflows.']

  Note: 'Stores all contact points for a customer profile or corporate account including email addresses, phone numbers, and social handles. Each row represents a single contact method with type (primary email, mobile, work phone, WhatsApp), verification status, opt-in/opt-out flags per channel, verification date, and source system. Supports omnichannel outreach, CRM campaigns via Salesforce Service Cloud, and GDPR/CCPA consent enforcement at the contact-method level.'
}

Table customer.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the address record. Primary key.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or corporate account associated with this address.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active addresses are available for use in orders and shipments.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose: billing, shipping, store pickup (BOPIS/ROPIS), home, work, or mailing.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name for the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR) identifying the country of the address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special delivery instructions provided by the customer (e.g., gate code, leave at door, ring bell).']
  delivery_point_barcode varchar [note: 'USPS Delivery Point Barcode (DPBC) or equivalent postal barcode for automated mail sorting and delivery.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this address becomes active and valid for use in transactions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this address remains active. Null indicates the address is currently active with no end date.']
  is_default_billing boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this address is the default billing address for the customer.']
  is_default_shipping boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this address is the default shipping destination for the customer.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Date when this address was last used in a transaction (order, shipment, or billing event).']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate in decimal degrees for geolocation and last-mile delivery optimization.']
  line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including street number, street name, and unit/apartment number.']
  line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as building name, floor, suite, or department.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate in decimal degrees for geolocation and last-mile delivery optimization.']
  military_address_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address is a military address (APO/FPO/DPO). Requires special handling for international military mail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was last modified or updated.']
  nickname varchar [note: 'Customer-provided friendly name for the address (e.g., Home, Office, Mom\'s House) for easy identification.']
  po_box_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address is a PO Box. Some carriers and products cannot deliver to PO Boxes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code, ZIP code, or postcode for the address. Used for delivery routing and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  residential_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address is a residential location (true) or commercial/business location (false). Impacts shipping rates and delivery windows.']
  standardization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been standardized to postal service formatting rules (USPS Publication 28, etc.).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region code or name. For US addresses, use two-letter state abbreviation (e.g., CA, NY).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction identifier derived from the address, used to determine applicable sales tax rates and rules.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of address validation against postal service standards (USPS, Canada Post, etc.). Validated addresses reduce delivery failures.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address was last verified against postal service databases or address validation services.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with a customer profile or corporate account. Stores address type (billing, shipping, store pickup, home, work), full address lines, city, state/province, postal code, country, geocoordinates (lat/lng), address validation status, USPS/postal standardization flag, and default shipping flag. Critical for last-mile delivery, BOPIS/ROPIS fulfillment, and tax jurisdiction determination.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for segment']
  owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Customer segments are owned by marketing analysts, merchandising managers, or CRM specialists who define qualification criteria, monitor segment performance, design targeted campaigns, and refresh mem']
  preferred_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Customer segments defined by category affinity ("Premium Electronics Buyers", "Organic Enthusiasts") for category-targeted campaigns and segment-aware assortment planning. Replaces denormalized prefer']
  preferred_item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Customer segments are often defined by product category affinity. Existing \'preferred_product_category\' text field should be proper FK to item_hierarchy for segment-based assortment planning, targeted']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Customer segments drive targeted pricing strategies (loyalty tier pricing, VIP exclusive pricing, student/senior discounts). Merchandising teams create segment-specific price lists to optimize convers']
  price_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_strategy. Business justification: Price strategies are designed for specific customer segments (high-value customers get EDLP, price-sensitive get Hi-Lo promotional). Category managers define strategy-segment mappings during annual pl']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Customer segments are explicitly targeted by promotional campaigns (promo_campaign.customer_segment_target exists as text field). Creating FK enables direct segment-to-campaign assignment for targeted']
  auto_refresh_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer membership in this segment is automatically recalculated on a scheduled basis (True) or manually managed (False). True for dynamic segments (e.g., RFM tiers recalculated nightly); False for static segments (e.g., one-time campaign lists).']
  average_aov decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean Average Order Value for customers in this segment over the measurement period. Key metric for pricing strategy, promotion design, and basket optimization. Expressed in base currency (USD).']
  average_cltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mean Customer Lifetime Value across all customers currently assigned to this segment. Used for segment valuation, investment prioritization, and ROI forecasting. Expressed in base currency (USD).']
  average_purchase_frequency decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean number of transactions per customer in this segment over the measurement period (typically 12 months). Used for replenishment forecasting, loyalty program design, and churn prediction.']
  b2b_b2c_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this segment applies to B2B customers (business accounts, wholesale, corporate), B2C customers (individual consumers), or both. Drives differentiated engagement strategies, pricing models, and service levels.']
  ccpa_opt_out_honored_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment respects CCPA \'Do Not Sell My Personal Information\' opt-out requests (True) or is exempt (False, e.g., service-related communications). Ensures compliance for California residents.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregate churn risk score for this segment, ranging from 0.00 (no risk) to 100.00 (high risk). Calculated from recency, frequency, and engagement metrics. Drives retention campaign prioritization and at-risk customer interventions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment definition was first created in the system. Audit trail for segment lifecycle tracking and governance.']
  discount_sensitivity varchar [note: 'Behavioral classification of segment\'s responsiveness to promotional pricing. High: primarily purchases on promotion (Hi-Lo shoppers). Medium: balanced full-price and promotional purchases. Low: primarily full-price purchases (EDLP preference or premium segment).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments. Enables segment lifecycle management and historical reporting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became active and available for customer assignment. Supports temporal segmentation strategies and historical analysis.']
  gdpr_consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit customer consent is required under GDPR for marketing communications to this segment (True) or whether legitimate interest or other legal basis applies (False). Ensures regulatory compliance for EU customers.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic boundary or region to which this segment applies (e.g., \'USA\', \'Northeast Region\', \'Urban Markets\', \'Global\'). Supports localized segmentation strategies and regional campaign targeting. Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where applicable.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment definition was last updated (criteria, name, status, or any other attribute). Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when segment membership was last recalculated and customer assignments were updated. Critical for data freshness validation and SLA monitoring.']
  max_rfm_score varchar [note: 'Maximum RFM composite score for customer assignment to this segment (if segment_type is rfm_tier). Defines the upper bound of the RFM tier. Null for non-RFM segments.']
  membership_count varchar [note: 'Current count of customers assigned to this segment. Updated periodically based on auto-refresh schedule. Used for segment sizing, campaign planning, and capacity forecasting.']
  min_rfm_score varchar [note: 'Minimum RFM composite score required for customer assignment to this segment (if segment_type is rfm_tier). RFM scores typically range from 3 (lowest: 1-1-1) to 15 (highest: 5-5-5). Null for non-RFM segments.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average Net Promoter Score for customers in this segment, ranging from -100.00 (all detractors) to +100.00 (all promoters). Measures customer satisfaction, loyalty, and likelihood to recommend. Used for segment health monitoring and experience improvement prioritization.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the business unit or department responsible for defining, maintaining, and governing this segment (e.g., \'Marketing Analytics\', \'Customer Insights\', \'Loyalty Program Management\'). Establishes accountability for segment quality and usage.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Service and engagement priority level assigned to this segment. Platinum: highest CLTV, white-glove service. Gold: high-value, priority support. Silver: mid-value, standard service. Bronze: emerging value. Standard: baseline service. Drives clienteling strategies and resource allocation.']
  qualification_criteria varchar [note: 'Summary of the business rules and thresholds used to assign customers to this segment (e.g., \'Purchased in last 30 days AND total spend > $5000 in last 12 months\', \'RFM Score >= 9\', \'Age 25-34 AND lives in urban area\'). Human-readable representation of the segmentation logic.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which segment membership is recalculated for auto-refresh segments. Daily: high-velocity behavioral segments. Weekly: standard RFM tiers. Monthly: demographic or lifecycle segments. Quarterly: strategic value tiers. On-Demand: manual trigger only.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment for operational use (e.g., VIP_GOLD, LAPSED_90D, HIGH_VALUE_B2B). Used in marketing campaigns, pricing rules, and POS systems.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the segment\'s defining characteristics, business rationale, and strategic purpose. Includes target customer profile, expected behaviors, and recommended engagement strategies.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the segment (e.g., \'VIP Gold Tier\', \'Lapsed Customers - 90 Days\', \'High-Value B2B Accounts\'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and customer-facing communications.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active: in production use for customer assignment and campaigns. Inactive: temporarily suspended, no new assignments. Archived: retired, historical reference only. Draft: under development, not yet released.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology applied. RFM Tier: Recency, Frequency, Monetary analysis. Demographic: age, gender, income. Behavioral: purchase patterns, product affinity. Psychographic: lifestyle, values. Lifecycle Stage: new, active, at-risk, lapsed. Geographic: region, climate zone. Channel Preference: omnichannel, e-commerce-only, store-only. Value Tier: CLTV-based stratification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rfm_tier|demographic|behavioral|psychographic|lifecycle_stage|geographic|channel_preference|value_tier — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_marketing_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel recommended for engaging customers in this segment. Aligns segment strategy with channel preference and regulatory consent. All Channels: omnichannel engagement strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|push_notification|direct_mail|in_store|call_center|social_media|all_channels — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Defines market segmentation classifications applied to customer profiles for targeted marketing, personalized pricing, and assortment decisions. Stores segment code, segment name, segment type (RFM tier, demographic, behavioral, psychographic, lifecycle stage, geographic, channel preference, value tier), segment description, qualification criteria summary, membership count, effective date range, auto-refresh flag, owning business unit, and profile-to-segment assignments with effective dates and expiry dates. Enables Retail to execute clienteling strategies, personalized promotions, and differentiated service levels across POS and e-commerce channels.'
}

Table customer.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer preference record. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.category. Business justification: Customers express merchandise category preferences ("interested in electronics", "loves organic food") for personalized marketing, homepage customization, and category affinity scoring. Retail standar']
  preferred_item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Category and department preferences enable targeted marketing campaigns and personalized navigation. Retail marketing segments customers by preferred categories (e.g., \'Beauty shoppers\', \'Electronics ']
  preferred_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Customer product preferences at SKU level (favorite items, repeat purchases) drive personalization engines, targeted promotions, and replenishment recommendations. Retail personalization requires link']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who holds this preference. Links to the customer master record.']
  superseded_by_preference_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preference record that supersedes or replaces this preference. Nullable if this is the current active preference. Supports preference lineage and history tracking.']
  application_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of times this preference has been applied or used in customer interactions, recommendations, or personalization events. Supports preference effectiveness measurement and RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) analytics.']
  channel_captured varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint through which the preference was originally captured (e.g., web, mobile app, POS (Point of Sale), call center, in-store kiosk, email, survey, third-party). Supports omnichannel attribution and data quality assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|pos|call_center|in_store_kiosk|email|survey|third_party — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the reliability or certainty of the preference, especially for inferred or behavioral preferences. Higher values indicate stronger confidence. Self-declared preferences typically have 100.00 confidence.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has provided explicit consent for this preference to be used for personalization, marketing, or data processing. True indicates consent granted; False indicates consent not granted or withdrawn.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the customer provided or withdrew consent for this preference. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this preference record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
