Existence Justification: In retail operations, high-value customers (VIP, corporate executives) are assigned multiple specialized associates simultaneously - a customer may work with a personal stylist, a registry consultant, and a business account representative across different departments. Conversely, each associate (stylist, relationship manager) manages a portfolio of multiple VIP clients. The business actively manages these assignments with specific relationship types, dates, primary contact designations, and communication preferences.'
}

Table customer.b2b_contract {
  b2b_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for b2b_contract']
  approved_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who provided final approval for this contract.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for managing this B2B customer relationship and contract.']
  contract_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard contract template used as the basis for this agreement.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the B2B customer organization that is party to this contract.']
  master_b2b_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on b2b_contract (master_b2b_contract_id)']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic or organizational sales territory to which this contract is assigned.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the contract received final internal approval.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is configured to automatically renew upon expiration.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated and payments are due under this contract.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Reference link or storage location for the signed contract document.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the contract, used in communications and documentation.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract indicating its operational validity and enforceability.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract based on its business purpose and legal structure.']
  contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract over its full term, representing the expected revenue or commitment amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding credit amount allowed for the customer under this contract.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount rate applied to list prices for this B2B customer under the contract terms.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms expire or are scheduled to terminate. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms become legally binding and operational.']
  exclusive_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an exclusive supply or distribution agreement preventing the customer from engaging competitors.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law under which the contract is interpreted and enforced.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase amount required per order or billing period as stipulated in the contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the contract for internal reference.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Description of payment conditions including timing, method, and any special arrangements (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, advance payment).']
  penalty_terms varchar [note: 'Description of financial penalties or remedies for breach of contract terms or service level failures.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing level or tier assigned to this contract, determining applicable rates and discounts.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that renewal notice must be provided, as specified in contract terms.']
  renewal_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews, requires manual renewal, or is non-renewable.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Description of service level commitments including delivery times, quality standards, and performance metrics.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was executed and signed by all parties.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date when the contract was terminated, if applicable. Null for active contracts.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for contract termination by either party.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or business reason for contract termination, if terminated.']
  volume_commitment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total volume or quantity the customer commits to purchase over the contract term.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for b2b_contract. Referenced by b2b_contract_id.'
}

Table customer.contract_template {
  contract_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract_template']
  parent_contract_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract_template (parent_contract_template_id)']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level or role required to approve contracts generated from this template.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contracts generated from this template require formal approval before execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or authority who approved this template for active use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract template was formally approved for use.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contracts generated from this template include automatic renewal provisions by default.']
  compliance_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory or compliance frameworks this template adheres to, such as GDPR, CCPA, SOX, or industry-specific regulations.']
  confidentiality_clause_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the template includes confidentiality and non-disclosure provisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract template record was first created in the system.']
  default_term_length_days varchar [note: 'Standard duration in days for contracts generated from this template, representing the default commitment period.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the contract template purpose, scope, and intended use cases.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Primary mechanism specified in the template for resolving disputes between contracting parties.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this contract template version is no longer valid for generating new contracts. Nullable for templates with indefinite validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this contract template version becomes valid and available for use in generating new contracts.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Specific legal framework, statute, or body of law that governs contracts created from this template.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code indicating the primary language in which the contract template is written.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which this template was used to generate a contract.']
  legal_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the primary legal jurisdiction under which contracts generated from this template are governed.']
  liability_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum liability amount specified in the template, representing the upper limit of financial exposure.']
  liability_cap_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the liability cap amount.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or system that most recently modified this contract template.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this contract template record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes, comments, or instructions related to the use or maintenance of this contract template.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required before contract renewal or termination, as specified in the template.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this contract template to ensure continued legal and business relevance.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract template indicating its availability for use in contract generation.']
  template_category varchar [note: 'High-level category indicating the primary counterparty type this template is designed for.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier code for the contract template, used for reference across systems and documentation.']
  template_content varchar [note: 'Full text content of the contract template including placeholders, clauses, terms, and conditions. May contain markup or template syntax.']
  template_format varchar [note: 'Technical format or markup language used to structure the template content.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the contract template that clearly identifies its purpose and use case.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract template by its primary business purpose and legal structure.']
  termination_clause_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the template includes standard termination provisions and exit clauses.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of contracts that have been generated using this template, tracking template adoption and popularity.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the contract template following major.minor.patch convention to track template evolution.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or system that originally created this contract template.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for contract_template. Referenced by contract_template_id.'
}

Table product.sku {
  sku_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Stock Keeping Unit. Primary key for the SKU master record.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: sku has department_code, category_code, subcategory_code (all STRING) which should be normalized to a single FK to item_hierarchy. The hierarchy table is the authoritative source for merchandise taxon']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Store-level assortment planning and authorization. Retail assortment systems maintain store-SKU authorization matrices determining which items each store is authorized to carry. Critical for planogram']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_brand. Business justification: Retail marketing operations require linking SKUs to marketing brand entities for campaign planning, brand performance analysis, and marketing attribution reporting. Distinct from product_brand (supply']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: sku.unit_of_measure (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK to uom reference table. UOM is the authoritative master for all units of measure with conversion factors and standards compliance (GS1,']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier or vendor who provides this SKU to the retailer. Links to the supplier master data.']
  age_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU has age restrictions for purchase (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, mature-rated products). True if age-restricted, False if not.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product was manufactured or produced. Required for customs and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SKU record was first created in the master data system. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cube decimal(10,3) [note: 'Cubic volume of the SKU package calculated as length × width × height. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization. Measured in cubic feet or cubic meters.']
  dimension_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for length, width, and height fields. IN (Inch), CM (Centimeter), FT (Foot), M (Meter).']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU will be or was discontinued and removed from the active assortment. Null if the SKU is not planned for discontinuation. Follows format yyyy-MM-dd.']
  ean varchar [note: '13-digit European Article Number used for product identification globally. International barcode standard.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU becomes active and available for sale. Used for new product launches and seasonal assortment planning. Follows format yyyy-MM-dd.']
  gross_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the SKU including packaging, measured in the unit specified by weight_unit_of_measure. Used for shipping and logistics calculations.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number that uniquely identifies trade items worldwide. Can be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits. Umbrella term encompassing UPC, EAN, and other GS1 identifiers.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and transportation procedures. True if hazmat, False if not.']
  height decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the SKU package, measured in the unit specified by dimension_unit_of_measure. Used for space planning and logistics.']
  hi varchar [note: 'Number of layers (tiers) that can be stacked on a pallet. Part of the Ti-Hi pallet configuration used in warehouse and distribution center operations.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the primary product image. Used for e-commerce, mobile apps, and digital signage.']
  internal_item_number varchar [note: 'Retailer-specific internal product identifier used in legacy systems and internal operations. May differ from external GTIN/UPC.']
  length decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the SKU package, measured in the unit specified by dimension_unit_of_measure. Used for space planning and logistics.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SKU in the product assortment. ACTIVE (currently sold), DISCONTINUED (no longer ordered), SEASONAL (sold during specific periods), CLEARANCE (being phased out), PENDING_SETUP (not yet available for sale), INACTIVE (temporarily unavailable).']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase this SKU. Null if no age restriction applies. Typically 18 or 21 for restricted products.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SKU record was last modified in the master data system. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  net_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the product contents excluding packaging, measured in the unit specified by weight_unit_of_measure. Used for regulatory compliance and consumer information.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of consumer units contained in a single sellable pack. For example, a 6-pack of soda has pack_size = 6.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a private label (store brand) product or a national brand. True for private label, False for national brand.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the product remains sellable and safe for consumption from the date of manufacture or receipt. Critical for perishable goods and inventory rotation.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Abbreviated product description for use in constrained display contexts such as receipts, labels, and mobile interfaces. Typically 40-60 characters.']
  sku_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the SKU including key product attributes, features, and specifications. Used for product information management and customer-facing displays.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU can be safely stacked during storage and transportation. True if stackable, False if not.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Storage and transportation temperature requirement for the SKU. AMBIENT (room temperature), REFRIGERATED (32-40°F), FROZEN (below 0°F), CONTROLLED (specific temperature range).']
  ti varchar [note: 'Number of units per layer on a pallet. Part of the Ti-Hi pallet configuration used in warehouse and distribution center operations.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code used for point-of-sale scanning in North America. Standard barcode identifier for retail products.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s own product identifier for this SKU. Used for purchase orders and vendor communication.']
  volume decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume or capacity of the product, measured in the unit specified by volume_unit_of_measure. Relevant for liquid and bulk products.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume field. GAL (Gallon), LTR (Liter), ML (Milliliter), OZ (Fluid Ounce), QT (Quart).']
  weight_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for gross_weight and net_weight fields. LB (Pound), KG (Kilogram), OZ (Ounce), G (Gram).']
  width decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the SKU package, measured in the unit specified by dimension_unit_of_measure. Used for space planning and logistics.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for every Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) in the retail assortment. This is the SSOT for all SKU-level product identity and physical characteristics across the enterprise. Captures the unique sellable unit identity including UPC, EAN, GTIN, internal item number, SKU description, brand reference, unit of measure, pack size, weight, dimensions (gross/net weight, length, width, height, volume, cube), ti-hi pallet configuration, temperature requirement, stackability, country of origin, hazmat flag, age-restriction flag, shelf life, product lifecycle status (active, discontinued, seasonal, clearance, pending setup), and creation/modification timestamps. Aligned with GS1 GDSN Trade Item standard. Sourced from Informatica MDM and Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS). Central hub entity — all other product domain entities reference this record.'
}

Table product.item_hierarchy {
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item hierarchy node. Primary key for the item hierarchy entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key for each node in the merchandise hierarchy tree.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  parent_hierarchy_node_item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent node in the hierarchy tree. Self-referencing relationship enabling tree traversal and roll-up aggregations. Null for top-level division nodes.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  allows_direct_sku_assignment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether SKUs can be directly assigned to this hierarchy node. Typically True for leaf nodes (subcategory, segment, class) and False for parent nodes (division, department, category). Controls data quality and hierarchy integrity in PIM (Product Information Management) systems.']
  assortment_breadth_target varchar [note: 'Target number of distinct subcategories or product families within this category. Assortment breadth measures the range of categories offered. Used in category management and OTB (Open to Buy) planning.']
  assortment_depth_target varchar [note: 'Target number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) within a subcategory or product family. Assortment depth measures the variety within a category. Deeper assortments offer more choice but increase inventory complexity and carrying costs.']
  category_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the category manager responsible for strategic category management, assortment optimization, and category P&L performance. Category managers define assortment breadth and depth targets and drive GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and master data governance. Automatically populated by Informatica MDM (Master Data Management) or source system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated data quality score (0-100) calculated by Informatica MDM based on completeness, accuracy, consistency, and timeliness rules. Scores below threshold trigger data stewardship workflows. Supports master data governance and continuous improvement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node is retired or deprecated. Null for currently active nodes. Supports historical reporting and category lifecycle management. Enables analysis of discontinued categories and assortment changes over time.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node becomes active and available for use in assortment planning, purchase orders, and reporting. Supports time-variant hierarchy structures and seasonal category introductions.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from source system or third-party data provider. Used for cross-system reconciliation, EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration, and external reporting. Examples: GS1 category codes, NRF ARTS hierarchy IDs, supplier category codes.']
  hierarchy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the hierarchy node purpose, scope, and business rules. Includes category definition, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and merchandising guidelines. Used for training, onboarding, and cross-functional alignment.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level of this node within the merchandise hierarchy tree. Defines the depth and classification granularity. Division is the highest level, followed by department, category, subcategory, segment, and class as the most granular.']
  hierarchy_node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the hierarchy node. This is the externally-known unique code used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) and SAP S/4HANA MM module for category management and reporting. Examples: DEPT01, CAT-ELEC, SUBCAT-TV.']
  hierarchy_node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the hierarchy node. Examples: Electronics, Home Appliances, Televisions, 4K Smart TVs. Used in reporting, planograms, and category management dashboards.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full path from root to current node using node codes separated by forward slashes. Example: DIVISION01/DEPT05/CAT-ELEC/SUBCAT-TV. Enables efficient hierarchy queries and reporting roll-ups without recursive joins.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchy purpose. Operational hierarchies support daily store operations and planograms. Strategic hierarchies support category management and assortment planning. Financial hierarchies align with P&L reporting. Planning hierarchies support OTB (Open to Buy) and MRP (Material Requirements Planning).']
  is_leaf_node boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this node is a leaf node (has no children) in the hierarchy tree. Leaf nodes are the most granular classification level and are directly associated with SKUs. True for leaf nodes, False for parent nodes.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this hierarchy node record. Supports audit trail, accountability, and master data governance. Captured from Informatica MDM workflow or source system authentication.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was last updated. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing. Updated automatically on every modification in Informatica MDM or source system.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average supplier delivery lead time in days for this category. Used in demand planning, safety stock calculations, and OTB (Open to Buy) planning. Managed in Blue Yonder Demand Planning and Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hierarchy node. Active nodes are in use for assortment planning and reporting. Inactive nodes are temporarily disabled. Pending nodes are awaiting approval. Deprecated nodes are being phased out. Archived nodes are retained for historical reporting only.']
  markdown_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency and timing of planned price markdowns for this category. Weekly and biweekly cadences support fast fashion and perishables. Monthly cadences support durable goods. Seasonal cadences align with end-of-season clearance. Event-driven markdowns respond to competitive pressure or excess inventory. Managed in Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM).']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by suppliers for this category. Impacts purchase order planning, inventory carrying costs, and cash flow management. Negotiated by buyers and enforced in procurement systems.']
  omnichannel_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this category is available across all channels (stores, e-commerce, mobile app, BOPIS, ship-from-store). True for omnichannel categories, False for channel-specific categories. Supports unified commerce strategy and cross-channel inventory visibility.']
  planner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the merchandise planner responsible for demand forecasting, inventory planning, and replenishment for this category. Planners use Blue Yonder Demand Planning to optimize inventory levels and minimize stockouts and overstock.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing strategy classification for this category. EDLP (Everyday Low Price) maintains consistent low prices. Hi-Lo (High-Low Pricing Strategy) uses frequent promotions and markdowns. Premium targets higher-margin positioning. Competitive matches market pricing. Value emphasizes affordability. Managed in Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM).']
  private_label_penetration_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of category sales from private label (store brand) products. Private label products typically offer higher margins than national brands. Used in assortment planning and supplier negotiations to optimize category profitability.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Inventory replenishment method for this category. Auto uses system-driven replenishment based on demand forecasting. Manual requires planner intervention. Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) delegates replenishment to suppliers. Cross-docking transfers inventory directly from receiving to shipping. Drop ship sends directly from vendor to customer.']
  safety_stock_weeks decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target safety stock level expressed in weeks of supply (WOS). Buffer inventory to protect against demand variability and supply chain disruptions. Calculated based on service level targets and lead time variability in Blue Yonder Demand Planning.']
  seasonality_indicator varchar [note: 'Seasonal demand pattern classification for this category. Non-seasonal categories have consistent year-round demand. Seasonal categories have predictable demand peaks tied to calendar periods. Used in demand forecasting, markdown planning, and inventory optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_seasonal|spring|summer|fall|winter|holiday|back_to_school — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence for ordering sibling nodes within the same parent. Used for consistent presentation in reports, planograms, and digital storefronts. Lower numbers appear first.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that is the authoritative source for this hierarchy node. ORMS (Oracle Retail Merchandising System) for merchandising hierarchies, SAP MM (Materials Management) for procurement hierarchies, Informatica MDM for golden record hierarchies, Manual for user-created nodes.']
  strategic_classification varchar [note: 'Strategic role of the category in the overall merchandising strategy. Destination categories drive store traffic and differentiation. Routine categories are frequently purchased staples. Convenience categories are impulse or fill-in purchases. Seasonal categories have time-bound demand. Private label vs. national brand classification supports margin optimization.']
  target_gross_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage for this category, expressed as a percentage of net sales. Used in pricing strategy, markdown planning, and category performance evaluation. Supports GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) optimization.']

  Note: 'Defines the full merchandise hierarchy and category taxonomy used to organize SKUs into departments, categories, subcategories, and segments for both operational and strategic purposes. This is the SSOT for all product classification and category management taxonomy. Captures hierarchy node identity, node code, node name, level (department, category, subcategory, segment), parent node reference (self-referencing for tree traversal), hierarchy path, hierarchy type (operational, strategic/category management), buyer assignment, planner assignment, strategic classification (destination, routine, convenience, seasonal), assortment breadth/depth targets, category manager, and effective dates. Supports category management, assortment planning, planogram design, financial reporting roll-ups, and NRF ARTS merchandise hierarchy alignment. Managed in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) and SAP S/4HANA MM module.'
}

Table product.attribute {
  attribute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product attribute record. Primary key for the product_attribute entity.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent product (SKU) to which this attribute belongs. Links to the product master catalog.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the person who approved this product attribute value for publication. Supports accountability and audit trails in PIM workflows.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this product attribute value was approved for publication or use in operational systems. Supports PIM workflow and governance processes.']
  attribute_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping or category of the attribute (e.g., \'physical\', \'technical\', \'descriptive\', \'nutritional\', \'environmental\', \'regulatory\', \'marketing\', \'quality\', \'packaging\', \'pricing\'). Enables faceted search and attribute organization in PIM systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physical|technical|descriptive|nutritional|environmental|regulatory|marketing|quality|packaging|pricing — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  attribute_name varchar [note: 'The name or label of the product attribute (e.g., \'color\', \'size\', \'fabric\', \'flavor\', \'wattage\', \'nutritional_info\'). Represents the semantic key in the entity-attribute-value (EAV) pattern.']
  attribute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this attribute record (e.g., \'active\', \'inactive\', \'pending_approval\', \'deprecated\', \'archived\'). Supports attribute governance and PIM workflow management.']
  attribute_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value of the product attribute (e.g., \'Red\', \'Large\', \'100% Cotton\', \'Vanilla\', \'60W\', \'15g protein per serving\'). Stores the descriptive, technical, or measurement data for the attribute.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'The name of the organization or authority that certified this attribute value (e.g., \'USDA Organic\', \'Energy Star\', \'Fair Trade\', \'UL Listed\'). Applicable when is_certified is True.']
  certification_date date [note: 'The date on which this attribute value was certified. Applicable when is_certified is True. Supports audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric conversion factor to translate this attribute\'s unit of measure to a base or standard unit (e.g., 1 lb = 0.453592 kg). Enables cross-UOM calculations and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this product attribute record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the completeness and accuracy of this attribute value. Used for GS1 GDSN readiness assessment and PIM data quality dashboards.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'The data type of the attribute value (e.g., \'string\', \'numeric\', \'boolean\', \'date\', \'timestamp\', \'decimal\'). Used for validation and type-safe processing in downstream systems.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence controlling the display order of attributes on product detail pages, labels, and reports. Lower numbers appear first.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date until which this attribute value is effective. Null indicates the attribute is currently active. Supports historical attribute tracking and product lifecycle management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this attribute value is effective and should be used in product displays, transactions, and reporting. Supports time-variant attribute management.']
  is_certified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this attribute value has been certified or verified by an authoritative source (e.g., environmental certification, organic certification, safety compliance). True if certified, False otherwise.']
  is_comparable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this attribute should be displayed in product comparison views (True) or not (False). Supports side-by-side product comparison features in e-commerce.']
  is_regulatory_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this attribute is required by regulatory or legal mandate (e.g., FDA nutritional labeling, CPSC safety warnings, FTC advertising disclosures). True if mandated, False otherwise.']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this attribute is mandatory for the product (True) or optional (False). Used for data completeness scoring and GS1 GDSN readiness checks.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this attribute should be indexed for faceted search on e-commerce platforms (True) or not (False). Drives search and filter capabilities in digital commerce.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this product attribute record was last modified. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  locale varchar [note: 'The locale or language-region code for this attribute value (e.g., \'en_US\', \'es_MX\', \'fr_CA\'). Supports multilingual and multi-market product catalogs for global retail operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about this product attribute. Used for internal documentation, special handling instructions, or data steward annotations.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation or reference to the specific regulation, statute, or standard that mandates this attribute (e.g., \'FDA 21 CFR 101.9\', \'CPSC 16 CFR 1500\', \'FTC 16 CFR Part 255\'). Applicable when is_regulatory_required is True.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or module from which this attribute was sourced (e.g., \'Informatica MDM\', \'Salesforce Commerce Cloud\', \'Oracle Retail Merchandising System\', \'Supplier EDI\'). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or key for this attribute record in the source system. Enables traceability and cross-system reconciliation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for numeric or measurement attributes (e.g., \'kg\', \'lb\', \'cm\', \'inch\', \'ml\', \'oz\', \'watt\', \'volt\', \'calorie\'). Supports multi-UOM transaction processing across procurement, inventory, and POS.']
  validation_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule or regex pattern used to validate the attribute value (e.g., \'must be positive integer\', \'must match color palette\', \'must be ISO 8601 date\'). Enforces data quality at attribute capture time.']

  Note: 'Stores extended descriptive, technical, and measurement attributes for each SKU beyond core identity fields using a flexible entity-attribute-value (EAV) pattern. Captures attribute name, attribute value, attribute group (e.g., fabric, color, size, flavor, nutritional supplement, technical spec, UOM conversion, environmental certification, data quality score), data type, unit of measure, conversion factor, validation rule, source system, and locale/language for multilingual and multi-market support. Enables faceted search on e-commerce platforms, product comparison, PIM-driven enrichment, multi-UOM transaction support across procurement/inventory/POS, data completeness scoring for GS1 GDSN readiness, and regulatory attribute capture. Sourced from Informatica MDM PIM module and Salesforce Commerce Cloud product catalog. Aligned with GS1 GDSN attribute standards for trade item data synchronization.'
}

Table product.product_brand {
  product_brand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product brand record. Primary key.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary supplier or vendor who provides products under this brand. Links to the supplier master data for vendor negotiations, chargeback processing, and Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) programs.']
  average_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average gross margin percentage achieved across all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) under this brand. Calculated as (Average Unit Retail - Cost of Goods Sold) / Average Unit Retail * 100. Critical for brand profitability analysis and private label vs. national brand comparison.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the brand for internal identification and system integration. Used in merchandising, procurement, and reporting systems.']
  brand_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the brand positioning, value proposition, target customer segment, and key differentiators. Used for internal merchandising guidance and external marketing content.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the brand as it appears on product packaging and marketing materials. Consumer-facing brand identifier.']
  brand_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the brand in the retail assortment. Active brands are available for new item setup and replenishment; inactive brands are temporarily suspended; discontinued brands are permanently removed; pending approval brands are under review for introduction.']
  brand_tier varchar [note: 'Market positioning tier indicating price point and quality perception. Premium brands command higher margins and target affluent consumers; value and economy brands focus on price-sensitive segments. Drives assortment strategy and shelf placement.']
  brand_type varchar [note: 'Classification of brand ownership and distribution model. National brands are manufacturer-owned and widely distributed; private label are retailer-owned store brands; exclusive brands are sold only through specific retail partnerships; licensed brands use third-party intellectual property.']
  country_of_origin_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country where products under this brand are manufactured or sourced. Required for customs compliance and consumer transparency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand record was first created in the Master Data Management (MDM) system. Used for data lineage tracking and audit trail compliance.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when the brand was permanently removed from the retail assortment. Null for active brands. Used for historical analysis and Return to Vendor (RTV) processing.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this brand is sold exclusively through this retailer or a limited retail partnership. True for exclusive brands, false for widely distributed brands. Supports competitive differentiation strategy.']
  is_licensed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this brand operates under a licensing agreement using third-party intellectual property (e.g., character brands, celebrity brands, sports team brands). True for licensed brands, false otherwise. Impacts royalty payments and contract management.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this brand is a retailer-owned private label (store brand) product. True for private label brands, false for national or licensed brands. Critical for margin analysis and private label penetration reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand record was most recently updated in the Master Data Management (MDM) system. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the brand was first introduced into the retail assortment. Used for new brand performance tracking, vendor scorecard evaluation, and assortment lifecycle analytics.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from purchase order placement to product delivery at the Distribution Center (DC) for this brand. Used for demand planning, safety stock calculation, and Weeks of Supply (WOS) analysis.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the licensing agreement for this brand expires. Null for non-licensed brands. Used for contract renewal planning and inventory liquidation strategy for expiring licenses.']
  logo_asset_url varchar [note: 'Reference URL or path to the digital brand logo asset stored in the Product Information Management (PIM) or Digital Asset Management (DAM) system. Used for e-commerce product pages, marketing materials, and mobile app display.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the supplier for purchase orders of products under this brand. Used for Open to Buy (OTB) planning and inventory replenishment optimization.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this brand record. Used for audit trail and data governance accountability.']
  owner_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the brand owner is headquartered. Used for country-of-origin reporting and supplier diversity analytics.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company or entity that owns the intellectual property rights to the brand. May be the retailer (for private label) or a manufacturer/supplier (for national brands).']
  portfolio_group varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping of related brands under a common portfolio or brand family. Enables portfolio-level margin analysis, vendor negotiations, and strategic assortment planning across brand families.']
  quality_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Internal quality assessment score for the brand based on product defect rates, customer returns, and quality audits. Scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Used for vendor scorecard evaluation and assortment quality management.']
  return_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of units sold under this brand that are returned by customers. Calculated as (units returned / units sold) * 100. High return rates may indicate quality issues or misaligned customer expectations.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or social responsibility certifications held by the brand (e.g., Fair Trade, Organic, B Corp, Carbon Neutral). Supports ESG reporting and consumer filtering on e-commerce platforms for sustainable products.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary demographic or psychographic customer segment that this brand is designed to appeal to (e.g., millennials, families, health-conscious, budget shoppers). Supports personalized marketing and assortment localization.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the brand, providing additional product information, brand story, and consumer engagement content. Used for e-commerce product page enrichment and customer education.']

  Note: 'Master record for all brands carried in the retail assortment, covering national brands, private label (store brand), exclusive, and licensed products. Captures brand name, brand owner, brand type (national, private label, exclusive, licensed), brand tier (premium, value, standard), country of origin, brand logo asset reference, brand status, launch date, and brand portfolio grouping. Supports private label vs. national brand assortment strategy, vendor brand negotiations, brand-level margin analysis, and consumer-facing brand filtering on e-commerce platforms.'
}

Table product.item_variant {
  item_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item variant relationship record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that approved this variant or substitution relationship for use. Used for accountability and compliance in merchandising and product data governance.']
  primary_item_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent or source SKU in the variant or substitution relationship. For variant relationships, this is the parent style or base item. For substitution relationships, this is the original SKU being substituted.']
  target_item_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the child or target SKU in the variant or substitution relationship. For variant relationships, this is the child SKU (specific size, color, flavor). For substitution relationships, this is the replacement SKU.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this variant or substitution relationship was approved for activation. Used to track the approval workflow and ensure proper governance of product relationships.']
  auto_substitute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this substitution can be applied automatically by fulfillment systems without manual intervention. True enables automatic substitution during picking when the source item is out of stock. False requires manual approval or customer consent.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Sales and fulfillment channels where this variant or substitution relationship is valid. All indicates the relationship applies across all channels. Specific values restrict the relationship to designated channels. Critical for omnichannel fulfillment and BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) and ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store) scenarios. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|in_store|online|bopis|ropis|mobile_app|call_center — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this variant relationship record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit customer consent is required before applying this substitution. True means the customer must approve the substitution (e.g., for price upgrades or significant product differences). False means the substitution can be applied automatically (e.g., for equivalent items). Applicable primarily for substitution relationships.']
  display_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order for displaying variants in e-commerce product pages, mobile apps, and digital catalogs. Lower numbers appear first. Used to control the presentation order of size-color grids and variant selectors.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this variant or substitution relationship expires and is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended relationship. Used to manage product lifecycle transitions and discontinuations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this variant or substitution relationship becomes active and can be used by merchandising, e-commerce, and fulfillment systems. Supports time-bound relationships for seasonal assortments or promotional periods.']
  inventory_interchangeable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the source and target items can be treated as interchangeable for inventory allocation and fulfillment purposes. True means inventory of either item can fulfill demand for the other. False means they must be tracked separately. Relevant for substitution relationships.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this variant relationship record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, business rules, or special handling instructions related to this variant or substitution relationship. Used by merchandising and fulfillment teams for operational guidance.']
  price_differential_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The price difference between the source and target items. Positive values indicate the target is more expensive (upgrade). Negative values indicate the target is less expensive (downgrade). Zero or null indicates equivalent pricing. Used for substitution cost impact analysis.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the variant or substitution relationship. Active means the relationship is in use. Inactive means temporarily disabled. Pending means awaiting approval or activation. Discontinued means permanently retired.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Type of relationship between source and target items. Variant indicates parent-child SKU relationship (e.g., style to size-color). Substitution indicates approved replacement for out-of-stock scenarios. Cross-sell and upsell support merchandising. Bundle and accessory support product associations.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that created or manages this variant relationship record. Examples: PIM for Product Information Management, MDM for Master Data Management, OMS for Order Management System. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  substitution_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating the priority order for substitution when multiple substitutes are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by fulfillment systems to select the best substitute when the source item is out of stock.']
  substitution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the substitution relationship. Equivalent indicates same quality and price point. Upgrade indicates higher quality or price. Downgrade indicates lower quality or price. Alternative indicates different but acceptable replacement. Applicable only when relationship_type is substitution.']
  variant_dimension_type varchar [note: 'The dimension along which the variant differs from the parent item. Applicable only when relationship_type is variant. Examples: size for apparel, flavor for beverages, scent for personal care, pack configuration for multi-packs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: size|color|flavor|scent|pack_configuration|material|style|fit|length|width — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variant_dimension_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific value of the variant dimension for the target item. Examples: Small, Medium, Large for size; Red, Blue, Green for color; Vanilla, Chocolate for flavor; 6-pack, 12-pack for pack configuration.']
  variant_ean varchar [note: 'The 13-digit EAN barcode assigned to the target variant item. Used internationally for product identification and scanning. Common in European and global retail operations.']
  variant_group_code varchar [note: 'A code that groups related variants together under a common parent style or product family. Used to aggregate sales, inventory, and performance metrics across all variants of a base item. Supports category management and assortment planning.']
  variant_gtin varchar [note: 'The Global Trade Item Number assigned to the target variant item. GTIN is the umbrella term for UPC, EAN, and other GS1 product identifiers. Used for global supply chain and EDI transactions.']
  variant_upc varchar [note: 'The 12-digit UPC barcode assigned to the target variant item. Used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking. Each variant SKU typically has its own unique UPC.']

  Note: 'Captures all SKU-to-SKU relationships including variant relationships (parent style/base item to child SKUs across size, color, flavor, scent, pack configuration) and approved substitution relationships (equivalent, upgrade, downgrade, cross-sell for fulfillment and out-of-stock scenarios). This is the SSOT for all inter-SKU relationships within the product domain. Stores source SKU reference, target SKU reference, relationship type (variant, substitution), dimension type (size, color, flavor, etc.), dimension value, substitution priority rank, channel applicability (in-store, online, BOPIS), customer consent required flag, variant-level UPC, and effective dates. Critical for apparel size-color grids, grocery flavor/size variants, omnichannel fulfillment substitution, BOPIS/ROPIS picking, and reducing lost sales from stockouts. Enables parent-child item navigation in PIM and e-commerce.'
}

Table product.image {
  image_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product image asset record.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: product_image.sku (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK. Images are assets associated with specific SKUs. This is a standard 1:N relationship (one SKU has many images).']
  uploaded_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Retail digital asset management requires tracking which associate uploaded/approved each product image for content workflow, quality control, audit trails, and accountability. Critical for e-commerce ']
  alt_text varchar [note: 'Descriptive text for the image used for accessibility (screen readers) and SEO. Describes the image content for visually impaired users.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval state of the image asset in the content workflow: draft (initial upload), pending_review (awaiting approval), approved (ready for publication), rejected (failed quality check), archived (no longer active).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the merchandising or marketing user who approved this image for publication.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this image was approved for publication.']
  aspect_ratio varchar [note: 'The proportional relationship between width and height (e.g., 1:1, 4:3, 16:9). Used for responsive design and layout planning.']
  background_color varchar [note: 'The hexadecimal color code of the image background (e.g., #FFFFFF for white). Used for consistent product presentation and dynamic background replacement.']
  caption varchar [note: 'Optional marketing or descriptive caption displayed alongside the image in certain contexts (e.g., lifestyle images with usage tips).']
  cdn_asset_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or key used by the Content Delivery Network to cache and serve this image asset globally.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of channels where this image is approved for use (web, mobile, print, in-store, social, marketplace). Supports omnichannel merchandising.']
  color_profile varchar [note: 'The color space or profile used in the image (sRGB, Adobe RGB, ProPhoto RGB, CMYK). Critical for color accuracy across web, print, and in-store displays.']
  copyright_holder varchar [note: 'The legal entity or individual who holds the copyright to this image asset. Critical for rights management and legal compliance.']
  dpi varchar [note: 'The resolution of the image in dots per inch. Typically 72 DPI for web and 300 DPI for print. Used to determine print quality suitability.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the license to use this image expires. Null for perpetual licenses. Used to manage rights and prevent unauthorized use.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'The digital file format of the image asset (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, TIFF).']
  file_size_kb decimal(10,2) [note: 'The file size of the image asset in kilobytes. Used for performance optimization and bandwidth management.']
  has_transparency boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the image contains transparent pixels (alpha channel). Relevant for PNG and GIF formats used in layered designs.']
  image_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the image purpose: hero (primary product shot), alternate (additional angles), swatch (color/material sample), lifestyle (in-use context), packaging (box/container), detail (close-up feature).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this image is currently active and available for display. Inactive images are retained for historical reference but not shown to customers.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary/hero image for the SKU. Only one image per SKU should be marked as primary.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this image record or the image file itself was last updated or modified.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'The licensing model under which the retailer has rights to use this image: owned (created in-house), licensed (paid third-party), royalty_free (unlimited use), creative_commons (open license), vendor_supplied (provided by supplier).']
  locale varchar [note: 'The language and region code (e.g., en_US, fr_FR, es_MX) indicating which market or locale this image is intended for. Supports localized product imagery.']
  mobile_optimized boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this image has been optimized for mobile device display (compressed, resized, or formatted for mobile bandwidth and screen size).']
  photographer_credit varchar [note: 'Attribution to the photographer or content creator who produced the image. Used for rights management and licensing compliance.']
  planogram_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this image is suitable for use in planogram design and shelf layout planning. Typically requires consistent background and perspective.']
  print_ready boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this image meets the resolution and color profile requirements for print media (catalogs, circulars, in-store signage).']
  quality_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'A computed or manually assigned quality score (0.00 to 5.00) representing the technical and aesthetic quality of the image. Used for quality control and merchandising decisions.']
  resolution_height varchar [note: 'The height of the image in pixels. Used to determine image quality and suitability for different channels (web, mobile, print).']
  resolution_width varchar [note: 'The width of the image in pixels. Used to determine image quality and suitability for different channels (web, mobile, print).']
  seo_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords associated with this image for search engine optimization and internal search relevance.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The display order of this image within the product\'s image gallery. Lower numbers appear first in the carousel or gallery view.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or platform from which this image was sourced (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, PIM, vendor portal, photography studio).']
  upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this image was first uploaded to the digital asset management system.']
  url varchar [note: 'The full URL path to the digital image asset, typically hosted on a Content Delivery Network (CDN) or digital asset management system.']
  usage_rights_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special terms, restrictions, or notes regarding the usage rights of this image (e.g., geographic restrictions, channel limitations, attribution requirements).']
  vendor_image_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned to this image by the vendor or supplier who provided it. Used for cross-reference and vendor collaboration.']
  view_angle varchar [note: 'The perspective or angle from which the product is photographed (front, back, left, right, top, bottom, angle). Helps customers understand product dimensions and features. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front|back|left|right|top|bottom|angle — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  zoom_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this image supports zoom functionality on the e-commerce platform. High-resolution images typically enable zoom for detailed product inspection.']

  Note: 'Manages digital image assets associated with each SKU including primary product images, alternate angles, lifestyle images, and swatch images. Captures image URL, image type (hero, alternate, swatch, lifestyle, packaging), resolution, file format, locale, channel applicability (web, mobile, print, in-store), CDN asset reference, and approval status. Sourced from Salesforce Commerce Cloud and PIM digital asset management. Supports e-commerce product display and planogram design.'
}

Table product.item_lifecycle_event {
  item_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each item lifecycle event record. Primary key for the item lifecycle event log.']
  approver_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'The system user identifier of the person who approved or rejected this lifecycle event. Null if approval is not required or still pending.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the merchandise buyer responsible for this SKU during the lifecycle event. Used for accountability and category management tracking.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: New item launches and discontinuations are phased by store or store cluster. Lifecycle events track which stores receive new items in each wave. Essential for launch wave planning and discontinuation ']
  primary_item_associate_id varchar [note: 'The system user identifier of the person or automated process that initiated this lifecycle event. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: item_lifecycle_event.sku (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK. Lifecycle events track state changes for specific SKUs. The department/category/subcategory codes are redundant - they can be ret']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the supplier or vendor associated with this SKU at the time of the lifecycle event. Relevant for new item introductions, vendor changes, and discontinuations.']
  actual_launch_date date [note: 'The actual date when the SKU became active and available for sale. Used to measure performance against target launch date and track new item setup SLA compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval state of this lifecycle event. Tracks whether the event required approval and if it was granted, rejected, or automatically approved per business rules.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the lifecycle event was approved or rejected. Null if approval is not required or still pending.']
  compliance_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for regulatory compliance documentation (e.g., FDA submission number, CPSC recall number). Supports audit trails for regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lifecycle event record was first created in the data lakehouse. Audit field for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date when the SKU was or will be discontinued and removed from active assortment. Applicable to discontinuation and end-of-life events.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the new lifecycle status becomes effective. May differ from event timestamp for planned future transitions.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the lifecycle event occurred in the source system. Represents the real-world business event time, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of lifecycle state change event. Categorizes the nature of the transition (e.g., new item introduction, range extension, reformulation, repackaging, seasonal activation/deactivation, markdown initiation, clearance transition, discontinuation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_item_introduction|range_extension|reformulation|repackaging|seasonal_activation|seasonal_deactivation|markdown_initiation|clearance_transition|discontinuation|product_recall|vendor_change|assortment_exit — promote to reference product]']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is a private label (store brand) product. Used to track private label development milestones and lifecycle management separately from national brands.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lifecycle event record was last updated in the data lakehouse. Audit field for tracking data changes and lineage.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The lifecycle status of the SKU after this event was applied. Represents the current state following the transition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|seasonal_inactive|markdown|clearance|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The lifecycle status of the SKU immediately before this event occurred. Enables tracking of state transitions and audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|seasonal_inactive|markdown|clearance|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'A standardized code indicating the business reason for the lifecycle event (e.g., seasonal refresh, vendor discontinuation, poor sales performance, regulatory compliance, quality issue, competitive response).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context and details about why this lifecycle event was triggered. Complements the reason code with specific business justification.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event was triggered by or requires regulatory compliance action (e.g., Food and Drug Administration (FDA) labeling change, Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) recall, safety standard update).']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the person or team who originally requested this lifecycle event. Used for new item setup workflow tracking and accountability.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system that originated this lifecycle event record (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System, Informatica MDM, manual entry).']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this lifecycle event in the source operational system. Used for traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  supporting_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes and comments providing context, instructions, or documentation for this lifecycle event. Used for knowledge transfer and audit trail completeness.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'The planned date for the SKU to become active and available for sale. Applicable primarily to new item introduction and seasonal activation events. Used for new item setup Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  workflow_stage varchar [note: 'The current stage in the new item setup or lifecycle change workflow. Tracks progression through the approval chain from initial submission to final approval or rejection.']

  Note: 'Transactional log of significant lifecycle state changes for each SKU from initial vendor submission through end-of-life. Captures new item introduction (setup workflow with requestor, vendor, buyer assignment, data steward, target launch date, approval chain), range extension, reformulation, repackaging, seasonal activation/deactivation, markdown initiation, clearance transition, and discontinuation. Stores event type, event date, triggered-by user, approval status, prior status, new status, reason code, and supporting notes. Supports product lifecycle management, new item setup SLA tracking, private label development milestones, and audit trails for regulatory compliance (FDA, CPSC). Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System.'
}

Table product.product_compliance {
  product_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product compliance record. Primary key.']
  approved_carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Specific carrier service levels have distinct compliance capabilities (e.g., ground hazmat vs air hazmat, refrigerated vs ambient). Retail compliance teams pre-approve specific carrier services for re']
  fulfillment_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier. Business justification: Retail operations require matching product compliance requirements (hazmat certification, temperature control, age-restricted handling) to carrier capabilities. Hazmat-certified products can only ship']
  officer_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Regulatory compliance management requires designated compliance officers (associates) responsible for maintaining certifications, audit records, and regulatory submissions. Essential for FDA, CPSC, an']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product (SKU) that this compliance record applies to.']
  age_restriction_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product requires age verification at point of sale due to regulatory restrictions (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, certain chemicals).']
  allergen_declaration_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product labeling complies with allergen disclosure requirements (e.g., contains milk, eggs, peanuts, tree nuts, fish, shellfish, soy, wheat).']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique certification or approval number issued by the certifying body for this compliance record.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or third-party organization that issued the compliance certification (e.g., FDA, CPSC, UL, NSF, USDA).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the product for this regulatory requirement.']
  compliance_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory compliance requirement (e.g., food safety, product safety, hazardous material classification, age restriction, import/export, labeling, environmental). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_safety|product_safety|hazmat|age_restriction|import_export|labeling|environmental — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the jurisdiction or market where this compliance requirement applies (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, DEU).']
  country_of_origin_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product labeling meets country-of-origin marking requirements for customs and consumer disclosure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance certification becomes effective and the product is authorized for sale or distribution.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance certification expires and requires renewal or re-certification. Null if certification does not expire.']
  fda_food_facility_registration varchar [note: 'FDA registration number for the manufacturing facility where food products are produced. Required for food safety compliance. Null for non-food items.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Classification code for hazardous materials according to transportation and storage regulations (e.g., UN number, DOT hazard class). Null if product is not hazardous.']
  import_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued import license or permit number required for cross-border trade of regulated products. Null if not applicable.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit or inspection conducted by the certifying body or internal quality assurance team.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance record was last updated or modified.']
  minimum_age_years varchar [note: 'Minimum age in years required to purchase this product. Null if no age restriction applies.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the compliance record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit or inspection. Used for proactive compliance management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional compliance information, special handling instructions, or notes from compliance audits.']
  nutrition_labeling_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product meets FDA nutrition labeling requirements (Nutrition Facts panel). Applicable to food and beverage products.']
  organic_certification varchar [note: 'Organic certification number or designation (e.g., USDA Organic, EU Organic). Null if product is not certified organic.']
  prop_65_chemical_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Prop 65 chemicals present in the product that trigger warning requirements. Null if no Prop 65 warning required.']
  prop_65_warning_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product requires a California Prop 65 warning label due to presence of chemicals known to cause cancer, birth defects, or reproductive harm.']
  recall_date date [note: 'Date when the product recall was initiated. Null if no recall has been issued.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reason for the product recall (e.g., contamination, safety hazard, labeling error). Null if no recall.']
  recall_severity_level varchar [note: 'FDA classification of recall severity: Class 1 (serious health hazard or death), Class 2 (temporary health problem), Class 3 (unlikely to cause adverse health effects). Null if no recall.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current recall status of the product indicating whether it is subject to a safety or quality recall.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Sub-national region or state code where specific compliance requirements apply (e.g., CA for California Prop 65, NY for New York regulations).']
  responsible_party_contact varchar [note: 'Contact information (phone or email) for the responsible party. Used for regulatory inquiries and recall coordination.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability or environmental certification (e.g., Fair Trade, Rainforest Alliance, Marine Stewardship Council, Forest Stewardship Council). Null if not certified.']
  tariff_classification_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized Tariff Schedule (HTS) code used for customs classification and duty calculation for imported products.']
  test_report_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance test report issued by the testing laboratory. Null if no test report available.']
  testing_laboratory varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited third-party laboratory that conducted compliance testing (e.g., UL, Intertek, SGS, Bureau Veritas). Null if no third-party testing performed.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory and safety compliance attributes for each SKU including FDA food labeling compliance, CPSC safety certifications, FTC advertising standards, hazardous material classification, age restriction requirements, country-specific import compliance, and recall status. Captures compliance type, certifying body, certification number, effective date, expiry date, compliance status, and last audit date. Supports regulatory reporting and product recall management.'
}

Table product.recall {
  recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product recall event. Primary key for the product recall record.']
  coordinator_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Product recall management requires designated recall coordinators to manage regulatory notifications, customer communications, and recovery operations. Critical for FDA/CPSC compliance and liability m']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Product recalls incur significant costs (recovery, disposal, refunds, legal) that must be tracked against specific cost centers for budget management, variance analysis, and financial reporting. Retai']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Recall expenses must be posted to specific GL accounts (product liability reserves, disposal costs, customer refunds) for GAAP-compliant financial statements and accurate P&L reporting. Required for a']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Product recalls must identify which stores received affected lots for customer notification, product removal from shelves, and recovery tracking. Recall execution systems require store-level tracking ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: product_recall.sku (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK. Recalls are initiated for specific SKUs. The upc and gtin columns are redundant - they can be retrieved via sku → gtin_registry join.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier or vendor who manufactured or provided the recalled product. Used for vendor accountability and chargeback processing.']
  affected_date_range_end date [note: 'End date of the production or distribution period for affected products. Used to identify inventory within the recall scope.']
  affected_date_range_start date [note: 'Start date of the production or distribution period for affected products. Used to identify inventory within the recall scope.']
  affected_lot_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of production lot numbers or batch codes affected by the recall. Used to identify specific inventory units.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total chargeback amount assessed to the supplier or vendor for recall-related costs, including recovery, disposal, and customer remedies.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was officially closed or completed, indicating all recovery and remediation actions have been finalized.']
  coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the recall coordinator for internal and external communication regarding the recall event.']
  coordinator_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the recall coordinator for urgent communication and escalation regarding the recall event.']
