  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions implemented to prevent recurrence of the issue, including process changes, supplier audits, or quality control enhancements.']
  country_of_origin_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the recalled product was manufactured or produced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product recall record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_notification_method varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of methods used to notify customers of the recall: email, direct mail, phone, in-store signage, website, social media, press release.']
  estimated_financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the recall in USD, including product costs, logistics, customer remedies, and regulatory penalties.']
  hazard_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specific hazard or risk posed by the recalled product to consumers, including potential injuries or health impacts.']
  initiation_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was officially initiated and communicated to internal teams and external stakeholders.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the recalled product is a private label (store brand) product. True if private label, False if national brand.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product recall record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or producer of the recalled product. May differ from the supplier if using third-party manufacturing.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the user who last modified the product recall record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  press_release_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official press release or public announcement document for the recall event, hosted on company or regulatory body website.']
  public_announcement_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was publicly announced through press releases, website postings, or regulatory body publications.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the reason for the recall, including safety hazard, contamination, labeling error, or quality defect.']
  recall_class varchar [note: 'FDA recall classification indicating severity: Class I (serious health hazard or death), Class II (temporary health problem), Class III (unlikely to cause adverse health reaction).']
  recall_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the recall: national (all locations), regional (specific regions or states), or store-specific (individual store locations).']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recall event: open (initiated), in-progress (recovery underway), closed (completed), or terminated (discontinued).']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recall action: mandatory (regulatory-mandated), voluntary (company-initiated), or market withdrawal (removal without regulatory involvement).']
  recovery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of affected units successfully recovered, calculated as (units_recovered / units_affected) * 100. Key performance indicator for recall effectiveness.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External recall reference number assigned by regulatory body or internal quality team. Used for tracking and reporting to CPSC, FDA, or other governing bodies.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing body that mandated or was notified of the recall: CPSC (Consumer Product Safety Commission), FDA (Food and Drug Administration), FTC (Federal Trade Commission), internal (company-initiated), or other.']
  regulatory_case_number varchar [note: 'Case or docket number assigned by the regulatory body (CPSC, FDA) for tracking and official correspondence related to the recall.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory body (CPSC, FDA, etc.) was officially notified of the recall event. Required for compliance reporting.']
  remedy_type varchar [note: 'Type of remedy offered to customers for the recalled product: refund (money back), replacement (new product), repair (fix defect), or disposal (safe destruction).']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Summary of the root cause analysis findings identifying the underlying cause of the product defect or safety issue that triggered the recall.']
  units_affected varchar [note: 'Total number of product units subject to the recall across all channels (stores, distribution centers, customer hands).']
  units_in_customer_hands varchar [note: 'Estimated number of recalled units that were sold to customers and remain outstanding (not yet returned or recovered).']
  units_recovered varchar [note: 'Number of recalled product units successfully recovered from stores, distribution centers, and customers through return or disposal.']

  Note: 'Operational record of product safety recalls and withdrawal events initiated by CPSC, FDA, or internal quality teams. Captures recall reference number, SKU reference, recall type (mandatory, voluntary, market withdrawal), recall reason, affected lot numbers, affected date range, recall scope (national, regional, store-specific), recall status (open, in-progress, closed), units affected, units recovered, and regulatory body notification date. Supports compliance reporting and reverse logistics coordination.'
}

Table product.item_bundle {
  item_bundle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item bundle configuration. Primary key.']
  bundle_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: item_bundle.bundle_sku (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK. The bundle itself is a SKU in the catalog. This links the bundle definition to its SKU master record. bundle_gtin can be retrieved ']
  created_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Merchandising associates create promotional bundles and multi-packs. Tracking creator enables workflow management, approval routing, performance measurement, and audit trails for promotional planning ']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Promotional bundles are often authorized only for specific stores or store clusters (e.g., seasonal bundles in tourist locations, regional promotions). Bundle authorization by store is required for pr']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary vendor or supplier responsible for providing the bundle or its components. Used for vendor managed inventory and procurement.']
  assortment_category varchar [note: 'The merchandising category or department this bundle belongs to for assortment planning and category management (e.g., Grocery, Apparel, Electronics).']
  bundle_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the bundle contents and value proposition for merchandising and marketing purposes.']
  bundle_name varchar [note: 'The customer-facing name of the bundle (e.g., \'Summer BBQ Pack\', \'Family Meal Deal\', \'Holiday Gift Set\').']
  bundle_price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The selling price of the bundle when pricing_method is bundle_price. Represents the total customer-facing price.']
  bundle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bundle: active (available for sale), inactive (temporarily unavailable), pending (awaiting approval), discontinued (permanently removed), seasonal (available during specific periods).']
  bundle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bundle configuration: fixed_bundle (pre-defined components), mix_and_match (customer selects from options), gift_set (curated gift collection), kit (assembly required), variety_pack (multiple flavors/variants), multi_pack (quantity of same item).']
  channel_availability varchar [note: 'Defines which sales channels can sell this bundle: all_channels, store_only, online_only, bopis_eligible (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ship_from_store (fulfillment from store inventory).']
  component_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of the component SKU included in one unit of the bundle. Supports fractional quantities for weight-based items.']
  component_sequence varchar [note: 'The display order or assembly sequence of this component within the bundle. Used for kit assembly instructions and merchandising display.']
  component_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of an individual component item included in this bundle. References the product master catalog.']
  component_substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component can be substituted with an equivalent item during fulfillment (e.g., out-of-stock scenarios, mix-and-match bundles).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this bundle configuration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for bundle pricing (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fixed dollar discount applied to the component sum when pricing_method is discount_amount.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the component sum when pricing_method is discount_percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this bundle configuration expires or is discontinued. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this bundle configuration becomes active and available for sale across all channels.']
  inventory_deduction_method varchar [note: 'Defines how inventory is tracked and decremented: component_level (deduct each component SKU), bundle_level (track bundle as single inventory unit), hybrid (track both bundle and components).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this bundle configuration record was last updated.']
  loyalty_points_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers can earn loyalty program points on bundle purchases.']
  maximum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of bundle units a customer can purchase in a single transaction. Used to prevent stockouts and ensure fair distribution of limited-time offers.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum number of bundle units a customer must purchase in a single transaction. Typically 1, but may be higher for wholesale or bulk bundles.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who last modified this bundle configuration. Used for audit trail and change management.']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Defines how the bundle price is calculated: bundle_price (fixed price for entire bundle), component_sum (sum of individual component prices), discount_percentage (percentage off component sum), discount_amount (fixed dollar discount off component sum).']
  promotion_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bundle can be included in additional promotional offers or discounts beyond its configured bundle pricing.']
  return_policy_code varchar [note: 'Reference code to the specific return policy applicable to this bundle (e.g., standard 30-day, final sale, exchange only).']
  returnable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bundle can be returned. Some bundles (e.g., opened gift sets, perishable meal kits) may not be returnable.']

  Note: 'Defines product bundle and kit configurations where multiple component SKUs are sold together as a single sellable unit. Captures bundle SKU reference (the parent sellable unit), component SKU references, component quantity, component sequence, bundle type (fixed bundle, mix-and-match, gift set, kit, variety pack, multi-pack), pricing method (bundle price, component sum, discount percentage), component substitution allowed flag, and effective dates. Supports promotional bundling, gift set creation, variety packs, multi-pack configurations, and meal deal combos across all channels. Critical for POS scanning (bundle has its own GTIN), inventory component deduction, and promotional planning.'
}

Table product.gtin_registry {
  gtin_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the GTIN registry record. Primary key for the GTIN registry product.']
  data_steward_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: GTIN registry maintenance requires data stewards to ensure GS1 compliance, barcode accuracy, and GDSN synchronization. Essential for master data governance, supply chain integration, and point-of-sale']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: gtin_registry.sku_code (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK. GTINs are registered for specific SKUs - this is the authoritative link between global trade item numbers and internal SKU master. ']
  barcode_image_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the digital barcode image asset (PNG, SVG, or EPS format). Used for printing labels, packaging artwork, and digital commerce applications. Points to asset management system or CDN location.']
  barcode_symbology varchar [note: 'The barcode encoding standard used to represent this GTIN. EAN (European Article Number bars), UPC (Universal Product Code bars), ITF-14 (Interleaved 2 of 5 for cases), GS1-128 (application identifier barcodes), QR (2D matrix code). Determines scanner compatibility.']
  check_digit varchar [note: 'The final digit of the GTIN calculated using GS1 check digit algorithm (modulo 10). Used to validate GTIN integrity during scanning and data entry. Automatically calculated but stored for validation purposes.']
  child_gtin_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of child GTINs (next lower packaging level) contained within this GTIN. For example, a case GTIN with child_gtin_quantity=24 contains 24 unit GTINs. Null for unit-level GTINs. Used to calculate inventory at different packaging levels.']
  country_of_sale varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this GTIN is authorized for sale. A single SKU may have different GTINs for different countries due to regulatory labeling requirements. For example, USA, CAN, MEX, GBR, DEU, FRA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GTIN registry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Immutable after initial creation.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date when this GTIN is retired and should no longer be used in new transactions. Null for active GTINs. Used for product lifecycle management and to prevent ordering of discontinued items. Historical transactions retain the GTIN for audit purposes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this GTIN becomes active and can be used in transactions. For new product launches, this is the go-live date. For packaging changes, this is when the new GTIN replaces the old one. Critical for supply chain cutover planning.']
  gdsn_last_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization of this GTIN data to the GDSN data pool. Used to track data freshness and identify GTINs requiring re-publication after attribute changes.']
  gdsn_publication_status varchar [note: 'Status of this GTIN in the GS1 Global Data Synchronization Network. Published (live in GDSN data pool), unpublished (not yet shared), pending (awaiting data pool validation), synchronized (confirmed by trading partners), rejected (validation errors). Required for supplier collaboration and EDI.']
  gross_weight_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for gross_weight_value. Typically g (gram), kg (kilogram), oz (ounce), or lb (pound). Must be consistent across supply chain for accurate logistics planning.']
  gross_weight_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total weight of the packaged item including product and packaging materials. Used for shipping calculations, freight costing, and warehouse capacity planning. Measured in gross_weight_unit.']
  gs1_company_prefix varchar [note: 'The GS1-issued company prefix portion of the GTIN. Identifies the brand owner or manufacturer who registered this GTIN with GS1. Typically 6-12 digits depending on the company size and GS1 member organization.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The actual GTIN barcode value. Can be 8-digit (EAN-8, UPC-E), 12-digit (UPC-A), 13-digit (EAN-13), or 14-digit (GTIN-14/ITF-14) format. This is the scannable barcode number used at POS, warehouse receiving, and throughout the supply chain.']
  gtin_type varchar [note: 'The specific GTIN format type. UPC-A (12-digit North America standard), UPC-E (8-digit compressed UPC), EAN-13 (13-digit international standard), EAN-8 (8-digit short EAN), GTIN-14 (14-digit case/pallet level), ITF-14 (Interleaved 2 of 5 barcode for GTIN-14).']
  is_base_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GTIN is the base unit of measure for inventory tracking. Typically true for the smallest sellable unit (each). All other packaging levels are multiples of the base unit. Used for inventory conversion calculations.']
  is_consumer_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GTIN represents a consumer-facing unit sold at POS. True for retail each units. False for case/pallet GTINs used only in supply chain. Determines whether GTIN should appear in e-commerce catalog and POS systems.']
  is_orderable_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GTIN can be ordered directly via EDI purchase orders. True for case and pallet GTINs typically ordered from suppliers. False for unit GTINs that are only sold at retail. Controls EDI transaction validation.']
  is_variable_measure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GTIN represents a variable measure item (e.g., meat, produce, cheese sold by weight). True for items where price varies by weight/quantity. Affects POS scanning behavior and pricing logic.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this GTIN registry record. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used to track data freshness and trigger downstream synchronization processes.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this GTIN registry record. Used for audit trail and accountability. Links to identity management system for user details.']
  net_content_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for net_content_value. Standard units: g (gram), kg (kilogram), mg (milligram), oz (ounce), lb (pound), mL (milliliter), L (liter), gal (gallon), qt (quart), pt (pint), ea (each), ct (count). Must align with regulatory labeling requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: g|kg|mg|oz|lb|mL|L|gal|qt|pt|ea|ct — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  net_content_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric quantity of product contained in this GTIN package. For example, 500 (when unit is mL), 12 (when unit is oz), 1.5 (when unit is kg). Used with net_content_unit to describe package size. Required for GDSN synchronization.']
  package_depth_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Depth dimension of the package. Used with height and width for cubic volume calculations. Essential for warehouse bin sizing and transportation load planning. Measured in package_dimension_unit.']
  package_dimension_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for package dimensions (height, width, depth). Standard units: mm (millimeter), cm (centimeter), m (meter), in (inch), ft (foot). Must be consistent for all three dimensions.']
  package_height_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Height dimension of the package. Used with package_width_value and package_depth_value to calculate cubic volume for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and shelf space allocation. Measured in package_dimension_unit.']
  package_width_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Width dimension of the package. Critical for planogram design, shelf allocation, and transportation cube optimization. Measured in package_dimension_unit.']
  packaging_level varchar [note: 'The packaging hierarchy level this GTIN represents. Unit (each/consumer unit), inner pack (multi-pack sold as one), case (shipping carton), pallet (full pallet load), display (retail display shipper). Critical for warehouse receiving and inventory management.']
  parent_gtin varchar [note: 'Self-referencing GTIN that represents the next higher packaging level in the hierarchy. For example, a unit GTIN\'s parent would be the case GTIN. Null for the highest packaging level (typically pallet). Enables resolution of case receipts to constituent unit GTINs.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this GTIN registration. Active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), pending (awaiting approval), suspended (compliance issue), retired (permanently discontinued). Controls whether the GTIN can be used in transactions.']
  regulatory_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this GTIN meets all applicable regulatory requirements for its country of sale. Compliant (approved for sale), non_compliant (blocked), pending_review (under evaluation), exempt (not subject to specific regulations). Enforced by quality assurance and legal teams.']
  replacement_gtin varchar [note: 'The new GTIN that supersedes this one when discontinued. Used for packaging changes, reformulations, or product transitions. Enables automatic substitution in ordering systems and maintains continuity in assortment planning.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record that originated or manages this GTIN data. PIM (Product Information Management), MDM (Master Data Management), ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning), GDSN (Global Data Synchronization Network), MANUAL (manual data entry). Used for data lineage and conflict resolution.']

  Note: 'Authoritative registry of all Global Trade Item Numbers (GTINs) including UPC-A, UPC-E, EAN-13, EAN-8, and ITF-14 barcodes associated with each SKU. Captures GTIN value, GTIN type (UPC, EAN, GTIN-14), SKU reference, issuing GS1 company prefix, barcode image reference, packaging level (unit, inner pack, case, pallet), parent GTIN reference (self-referencing for packaging hierarchy — e.g., case GTIN contains N unit GTINs), child GTIN count per parent, and registration status. Models the full GS1 packaging hierarchy enabling warehouse receiving at case level to resolve to constituent unit GTINs. Aligned with GS1 global standards. Supports EDI transactions (ASN, PO, invoice), warehouse receiving, POS scanning, and GDSN data synchronization.'
}

Table product.item_nutritional {
  item_nutritional_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the nutritional information record. Primary key for the item nutritional data product.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the SKU product. Links nutritional information to the specific product item.']
  validated_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Nutritional labeling requires validation by qualified associates (dietitians, compliance specialists) for FDA compliance, allergen accuracy, and regulatory audit trails. Critical for food safety, liab']
  added_sugars_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Added sugar content per serving measured in grams. Required under updated FDA nutrition facts label rules.']
  allergen_declaration varchar [note: 'Declaration of major food allergens present in the product. Must identify milk, eggs, fish, crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, and soybeans per FALCPA requirements.']
  calcium_mg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Calcium content per serving measured in milligrams. Required under updated FDA nutrition facts label.']
  calories_from_fat varchar [note: 'Number of calories derived from fat per serving. Optional field under updated FDA labeling rules.']
  calories_per_serving varchar [note: 'Total caloric content per serving, measured in kilocalories (kcal). Required on FDA nutrition facts label.']
  cholesterol_mg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Cholesterol content per serving measured in milligrams. Required FDA nutrition facts component.']
  contains_eggs boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains eggs or egg-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_fish boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains fish or fish-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_milk boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains milk or milk-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_peanuts boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains peanuts or peanut-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_shellfish boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains crustacean shellfish or shellfish-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_soybeans boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains soybeans or soy-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_tree_nuts boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains tree nuts or tree nut-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  contains_wheat boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains wheat or wheat-derived ingredients. Supports allergen filtering and dietary restriction compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this nutritional information record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dietary_fiber_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Dietary fiber content per serving measured in grams. Required FDA nutrition facts component.']
  ingredient_statement varchar [note: 'Complete list of ingredients in descending order of predominance by weight as required by FDA labeling regulations. Includes sub-ingredients for compound ingredients.']
  iron_mg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Iron content per serving measured in milligrams. Required under updated FDA nutrition facts label.']
  is_gluten_free boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product meets FDA gluten-free labeling standards (less than 20 ppm gluten). Supports dietary restriction filtering.']
  is_halal_certified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is certified halal by a recognized halal certification body. Supports dietary and religious preference filtering.']
  is_kosher_certified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency. Supports dietary and religious preference filtering.']
  is_non_gmo_verified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is verified as non-GMO by the Non-GMO Project or equivalent verification program. Supports dietary preference filtering.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product is certified organic by USDA or equivalent certifying body. Supports organic product filtering and merchandising.']
  is_vegan boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains no animal-derived ingredients or by-products. Supports dietary preference filtering.']
  is_vegetarian boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the product contains no meat, poultry, or fish ingredients. May contain dairy or eggs. Supports dietary preference filtering.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this nutritional information record was last updated. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this nutritional information record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  monounsaturated_fat_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Monounsaturated fat content per serving measured in grams. Optional voluntary declaration.']
  nutritional_data_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this nutritional information became effective for the product. Supports version control and regulatory compliance tracking.']
  nutritional_data_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this nutritional information expires or is superseded by updated data. Null if currently active. Supports version control and compliance tracking.']
  nutritional_data_source varchar [note: 'Source of the nutritional information (laboratory analysis, USDA database, supplier-provided, calculated from ingredients, third-party database). Supports data quality and compliance auditing.']
  organic_certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that certified the product as organic (e.g., USDA, CCOF, Oregon Tilth). Null if product is not organic certified.']
  polyunsaturated_fat_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Polyunsaturated fat content per serving measured in grams. Optional voluntary declaration.']
  potassium_mg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Potassium content per serving measured in milligrams. Required under updated FDA nutrition facts label.']
  protein_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Protein content per serving measured in grams. Required FDA nutrition facts component.']
  saturated_fat_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Saturated fat content per serving measured in grams. Required FDA nutrition facts component.']
  serving_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the serving size (grams, milliliters, ounces, cups, pieces, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: g|mg|kg|ml|l|oz|fl oz|cup|tbsp|tsp|piece|slice|container — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  serving_size_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value representing the standard serving size for this product as defined by FDA regulations.']
  servings_per_container decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of servings contained in the package. May be approximate for variable-weight items.']
  sodium_mg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Sodium content per serving measured in milligrams. Required FDA nutrition facts component.']
  total_carbohydrate_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total carbohydrate content per serving measured in grams. Includes dietary fiber, sugars, and other carbohydrates.']
  total_fat_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total fat content per serving measured in grams. Includes saturated, trans, monounsaturated, and polyunsaturated fats.']
  total_sugars_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total sugar content per serving measured in grams. Includes naturally occurring and added sugars.']
  trans_fat_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Trans fat content per serving measured in grams. Must be declared per FDA regulations.']
  vitamin_d_mcg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vitamin D content per serving measured in micrograms. Required under updated FDA nutrition facts label.']

  Note: 'Stores FDA-mandated nutritional facts and ingredient information for food and beverage SKUs. Captures serving size, servings per container, calories, total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, sodium, total carbohydrates, dietary fiber, total sugars, added sugars, protein, vitamins and minerals, ingredient list, allergen declarations, and organic/non-GMO certifications. Supports FDA food labeling compliance, e-commerce product display, and dietary filtering.'
}

Table product.item_cross_reference {
  item_cross_reference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the item cross reference record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who validated this cross reference mapping. Provides accountability for data quality assurance.']
  internal_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: item_cross_reference.internal_sku (STRING) should be normalized to a proper FK. This table maps external system identifiers to internal SKUs - the internal_sku is the core link to the authoritative SK']
  superseded_by_reference_item_cross_reference_id varchar [note: 'The item_cross_reference_id of the newer mapping that replaces this one when status is superseded. Creates a chain of reference history for audit and troubleshooting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trading partner (supplier, vendor, distributor, marketplace) associated with this cross reference. Used for EDI transactions and supplier collaboration.']
  conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric conversion factor used when the external system uses different units of measure than the internal system. For example, if internal system tracks in eaches but external system tracks in cases, this would be the case pack quantity.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person or system process that created this cross reference record. Provides accountability and audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cross reference record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cross_reference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cross reference mapping. Active indicates the mapping is currently in use, inactive means it is no longer valid, pending indicates awaiting approval, deprecated means phasing out, and superseded means replaced by a newer mapping.']
  cross_reference_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cross reference relationship. Indicates the nature of the external identifier such as supplier part number, legacy item number from a retired system, marketplace ASIN for Amazon or other platforms, vendor-specific SKU, distributor code, or manufacturer code.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this cross reference mapping is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended mapping that remains active until explicitly terminated.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this cross reference mapping becomes valid and should be used for system integration and EDI transactions.']
  external_item_description varchar [note: 'The item description as it appears in the external system. Useful for reconciliation and troubleshooting when descriptions differ across systems.']
  external_item_number varchar [note: 'The item identifier used by the external system. This could be a supplier part number, legacy item code, marketplace ASIN, or system-specific SKU.']
  external_system_instance varchar [note: 'Specific instance or environment of the external system when multiple instances exist (e.g., SAP Production, SAP QA, ORMS US, ORMS EU). Helps disambiguate cross references when the same system is deployed in multiple regions or environments.']
  external_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external or legacy system that uses the external item identifier. Examples include SAP, Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS), Manhattan WMS, supplier EDI systems, e-commerce platforms, or legacy systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP|ORMS|WMS|EDI|E-COMMERCE|POS|SUPPLIER|MARKETPLACE|LEGACY|OTHER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  external_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used by the external system for this item (e.g., EACH, CASE, PALLET, KG, LB). Used in conjunction with conversion factor for accurate quantity translation.']
  internal_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used internally by the retailer for this item. Provides context for the conversion factor calculation.']
  is_primary_reference boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary or preferred cross reference for the given external system. True means this is the authoritative mapping when multiple mappings exist for the same system.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person or system process that most recently modified this cross reference record. Provides accountability for changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cross reference record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'The most recent date this cross reference was used in a transaction or system integration. Helps identify obsolete mappings that can be archived.']
  mapping_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A score from 0 to 100 indicating the confidence level in the accuracy of this cross reference mapping. Higher scores indicate greater certainty. Used for automated mappings that may require validation.']
  mapping_source varchar [note: 'Indicates how this cross reference mapping was created. Values include manual entry, EDI transaction, API integration, file import, system migration, supplier catalog load, or MDM system synchronization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MANUAL|EDI|API|FILE_IMPORT|SYSTEM_MIGRATION|SUPPLIER_CATALOG|MDM — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about this cross reference mapping. May include special handling instructions, known issues, or historical context.']
  trading_partner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the trading partner organization associated with this cross reference. Provides human-readable context for the trading partner.']
  upc_ean_gtin varchar [note: 'The standard barcode identifier (UPC, EAN, or GTIN) associated with this cross reference. Used for point-of-sale scanning and global product identification.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'The total number of times this cross reference has been used in transactions or system integrations. Indicates the importance and activity level of the mapping.']
  validation_date date [note: 'The date when this cross reference mapping was last validated for accuracy. Used to track data quality assurance activities.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this cross reference mapping has been validated for accuracy. Validated means confirmed correct, unvalidated means not yet checked, failed means validation found errors, pending review means awaiting manual verification.']

  Note: 'Maps internal SKU identifiers to external and legacy system identifiers across trading partners and operational systems. Captures internal SKU reference, external system name (SAP, ORMS, WMS, supplier EDI, e-commerce platform), external item number, cross-reference type (supplier part number, legacy item number, marketplace ASIN, retailer-specific UPC), and effective dates. Supports system integration, EDI transactions, and multi-system reconciliation.'
}

Table product.uom {
  uom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the unit of measure. Primary key for the UOM reference master.']
  base_uom_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on uom (base_uom_id)']
  conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric multiplier to convert this unit of measure to the base unit. For example, if 1 case = 12 each, the conversion factor is 12. A value of 1 indicates this is the base unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit of measure record was first created in the master data system. Part of audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0-100) representing the completeness, accuracy, and consistency of this unit of measure record based on Master Data Management (MDM) data quality rules and validation checks.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this unit of measure is no longer valid for new transactions. Null indicates no planned end date. Part of temporal validity tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this unit of measure becomes valid and available for use across retail systems. Part of temporal validity tracking.']
  gs1_uom_code varchar [note: 'Standardized GS1 unit of measure code used for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and global supply chain interoperability. Maps internal UOM codes to GS1 standards.']
  inverse_conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Reciprocal of the conversion factor, used for reverse conversions from base unit to this unit. Calculated as 1 divided by conversion_factor.']
  is_base_unit boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the base unit of measure in its class (True) or a derived/alternate unit (False). Base units have a conversion factor of 1.']
  is_consumer_unit boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this unit of measure represents a consumer-facing selling unit (True for each, single item, False for case, pallet, or bulk units).']
  is_fractional_allowed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether fractional quantities are permitted for this unit of measure (True for weight/volume units like pounds or liters, False for discrete count units like cases or pallets).']
  is_inventory_tracked boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether inventory quantities are tracked and managed in this unit of measure (True if used in Warehouse Management System (WMS) and inventory systems, False if conversion-only).']
  is_orderable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this unit of measure can be used for purchase order creation and supplier ordering (True if valid for procurement, False if display-only or internal-use).']
  is_variable_measure boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this unit of measure is used for variable-weight or variable-measure items (True for items sold by weight or volume with varying quantities, False for fixed-count items).']
  iso_uom_code varchar [note: 'International Organization for Standardization (ISO) unit code per ISO 80000 and ISO 31 standards, ensuring global consistency in measurement representation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this unit of measure record was most recently updated. Part of audit trail for data governance and change tracking.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit of measure: active (in use), inactive (not currently used but retained for historical data), deprecated (being phased out), or pending (awaiting approval for use).']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the data steward or system user who last modified this unit of measure record. Part of audit trail for accountability and data governance.']
  precision_decimal_places varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to which quantities in this unit of measure should be rounded for display and calculation purposes (e.g., 0 for whole units like cases, 2 for currency-like precision, 3 for weight measurements).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of units of measure in user interfaces and reports. Lower values appear first.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or code of the source system from which this unit of measure record originated (e.g., Informatica MDM, SAP S/4HANA MM, Oracle Retail Merchandising System). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  superseded_by_uom_code varchar [note: 'Code of the replacement unit of measure that supersedes this one when deprecated. Used to maintain continuity during UOM transitions and system migrations.']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Standard abbreviated symbol for the unit of measure (e.g., kg for kilogram, lb for pound, L for liter, oz for ounce). Used in labels, reports, and user interfaces.']
  unece_code varchar [note: 'United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UN/ECE) Recommendation 20 unit code, widely used in international trade and customs documentation.']
  uom_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the unit of measure by measurement type: weight (mass), volume (capacity), count (discrete units), length (distance), area (surface), or time (duration).']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the unit of measure (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, LB for pound, KG for kilogram). Used as the business identifier across ordering, inventory, pricing, and distribution systems.']
  uom_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the unit of measure, including usage context and business rules for application across retail operations.']
  uom_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the unit of measure (e.g., Each, Case, Pound, Kilogram, Liter, Ounce, Pallet).']
  uom_type varchar [note: 'Functional type indicating the primary business context where this UOM is used: base (fundamental unit), alternate (conversion unit), display (customer-facing), ordering (procurement), inventory (stock tracking), pricing (selling unit), or shipping (logistics). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base|alternate|display|ordering|inventory|pricing|shipping — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  usage_context varchar [note: 'Textual description of the business contexts and operational scenarios where this unit of measure is typically applied (e.g., used for bulk ordering of dry goods, used for pricing fresh produce, used for shipping pallet quantities).']

  Note: 'Unit of measure reference master defining all valid units (each, case, pallet, pound, kilogram, liter, ounce) and conversion factors used across ordering, inventory, pricing, and distribution. Captures UOM code, description, UOM class (weight, volume, count, length), base UOM conversion factor, and GS1 standard mapping.'
}

Table product.category_campaign_plan {
  category_campaign_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this category-campaign plan record. Primary key.']
  approved_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee (typically category manager or marketing director) who approved this category inclusion and budget allocation.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that includes this category in its execution plan.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchandise hierarchy node (category, subcategory, or department) included in this campaign plan.']
  actual_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual revenue generated by this category during the campaign period. Used to calculate campaign effectiveness and ROI.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual expenditure incurred for this category within this campaign. Populated during and after campaign execution for ROI analysis.']
  actual_units_sold varchar [note: 'Actual number of units sold for this category during the campaign period. Compared against target_units_goal for performance measurement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this category-campaign plan was approved for execution.']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total campaign budget allocated specifically to this category. Used to track spending and ROI at the category level within the campaign.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this category-campaign plan record was first created in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this category-campaign plan record.']
  media_weight_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of campaign media impressions, creative assets, or channel mix allocated to featuring this category. Indicates relative prominence in campaign messaging.']
  performance_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Relative priority ranking of this category within the campaign (1 = highest priority). Used to guide resource allocation and performance monitoring focus.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this category-campaign plan. Tracks approval workflow and execution state.']
  promotional_depth_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average discount percentage or promotional intensity planned for this category within this campaign. Expressed as percentage off regular price.']
  target_revenue_goal decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue target set for this specific category within this campaign. Aggregates up to the campaign-level revenue goal.']
  target_units_goal varchar [note: 'Target number of units (SKUs) expected to sell for this category during the campaign period. Used for inventory planning and performance measurement.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the tactical marketing plan linking specific merchandise categories to marketing campaigns. It captures budget allocation, revenue targets, promotional depth, and media investment for each category-campaign pairing. Each record represents a deliberate business decision to include a specific category in a campaign with its own performance goals and resource allocation. Managed by category managers and campaign managers collaboratively during campaign planning cycles.. Existence Justification: In retail operations, marketing campaigns routinely target multiple merchandise categories simultaneously (e.g., back-to-school campaigns include apparel, electronics, and school supplies), and individual categories participate in multiple concurrent campaigns (seasonal promotions, brand campaigns, clearance events). The business actively manages category-campaign plans as operational entities with specific budget allocations, revenue targets, promotional depths, and media weights for each category-campaign pairing. These plans are created during campaign planning cycles, approved by category and marketing managers, and tracked for performance measurement.'
}

Table product.assortment {
  assortment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this SKU-to-node assortment assignment record. Primary key.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fulfillment node master record. Identifies which location is authorized to stock this SKU.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU master record. Identifies which product is assigned to this fulfillment node.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for inventory allocation when supply is constrained. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (1 = highest priority). Used by allocation engines to determine which nodes receive inventory first during shortages. Typically based on sales velocity, strategic importance, or customer service level agreements.']
  assignment_effective_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU was first authorized to be stocked at this node. Marks the beginning of the assortment assignment. Used for assortment history tracking and performance analysis.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU was removed from the authorized assortment at this node. Null for currently active assignments. Used for assortment history and discontinued product tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assortment assignment record was created in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this assortment assignment record was last updated. Tracks changes to stocking status, min/max quantities, or other assignment parameters.']
  last_received_date date [note: 'Date when this SKU was last received into inventory at this node. Used to identify slow-moving or stale inventory, support FIFO/FEFO rotation, and flag potential obsolescence. Updated by warehouse management system upon receipt confirmation.']
  max_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum inventory quantity that should be stocked at this node for this SKU. Prevents overstocking and ensures efficient use of storage capacity. Set based on storage constraints, demand forecasts, and inventory turn targets. Node-specific based on storage capacity and sales velocity.']
  min_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum inventory quantity that should be maintained at this node for this SKU. Triggers replenishment when on-hand inventory falls below this threshold. Set based on lead time demand, safety stock calculations, and service level targets. Node-specific based on local demand patterns.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to replenish this SKU at this specific node from the time a replenishment order is placed. Includes order processing, picking, packing, transit, and receiving time. Node-specific based on distance from supply source, transportation mode, and receiving capacity.']
  stocking_status varchar [note: 'Current stocking status of this SKU at this specific node. ACTIVE (currently stocked and replenished), DISCONTINUED (no longer carried at this location), SEASONAL (stocked only during specific seasons), CLEARANCE (final inventory selldown), PENDING_SETUP (authorized but not yet received), TEMPORARY_OUT (temporarily not stocked). This is node-specific and can differ from the SKU\'s global lifecycle_status.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of SKUs to fulfillment nodes in the retail network. It captures which products are authorized to be stocked at which locations, along with node-specific inventory policies, allocation rules, and stocking parameters. Each record links one SKU to one fulfillment node with attributes that govern how that SKU is managed at that specific location. This is the authoritative source for product assortment decisions and location-specific inventory control parameters.. Existence Justification: In retail omnichannel operations, a single SKU is stocked across multiple fulfillment nodes (distribution centers, stores acting as ship-from-store locations, micro-fulfillment centers, dark stores), and each node carries thousands of different SKUs. The business actively manages these SKU-to-node assignments through assortment planning processes, setting node-specific inventory policies, allocation priorities, and stocking parameters for each SKU at each location. This is a core operational relationship managed by merchandising and supply chain teams.'
}

Table product.category_kpi_target {
  category_kpi_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this category-KPI target configuration record. Primary key.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchandise category or hierarchy node for which this KPI target is configured']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the KPI definition being tracked for this category']
  alert_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The threshold value at which alerts should be triggered for this category-KPI combination. When actual performance crosses this threshold, notifications are sent to responsible_role. Example: Alert when shrink exceeds 2.5% for Electronics.']
  configuration_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining the rationale for this specific target configuration, special business context, or seasonal adjustments. Example: \'Holiday season target increased by 15% based on 3-year average lift\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this category-KPI target configuration was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and governance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this category-KPI target configuration expires or is replaced. Null for currently active configurations. Supports temporal tracking of performance targets.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this category-KPI target configuration becomes active. Supports historical tracking of target changes over time (e.g., Q1 targets vs Q4 holiday targets).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this category-KPI configuration is currently active and in use for performance reporting. Inactive configurations are retained for historical analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this configuration was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this KPI is calculated and reported for this specific category. May differ from the enterprise-level reporting_frequency in kpi_definition based on category volatility and business needs. High-velocity categories may require daily tracking while stable categories use weekly.']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'The business role or job function responsible for monitoring and acting on this KPI for this category. Examples: Category Manager, Merchandise Planner, Store Operations Director. May differ from the category_manager_name or planner_name in item_hierarchy as KPI ownership can be specialized.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The specific target value for this KPI at this category level. Overrides the enterprise-level target_threshold_value from kpi_definition for this specific category. Example: Electronics department may have a 35% gross margin target while Grocery has 22%.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the performance measurement configuration between item_hierarchy categories and kpi_definition metrics. It captures category-specific KPI targets, thresholds, measurement frequencies, and ownership assignments that exist only in the context of a specific category-KPI pairing. Each record links one merchandise category to one KPI definition with the specific performance targets and alert rules configured for that category.. Existence Justification: In retail performance management, merchandise categories track multiple KPIs (sales, margin, inventory turns, shrink, comp store sales, etc.) and each KPI is measured across multiple categories with category-specific targets and thresholds. Category managers actively configure and manage these category-KPI pairings with specific target values, alert thresholds, measurement frequencies, and role assignments that vary by category characteristics (velocity, margin profile, seasonality). This is an operational configuration that the business actively maintains, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table order.header {
  header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order header record. Primary key for the order header entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B and account-based retail operations require tracking orders against customer accounts for credit limit enforcement, account statement generation, billing cycle management, and corporate purchasing']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Orders subject to regulatory frameworks (GDPR for EU customers, CCPA for CA residents, export controls) must link to compliance programs. Enables compliance reporting, audit trails, and regulatory fil']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.corporate_account. Business justification: Corporate B2B retail requires linking orders to corporate accounts (distinct from individual accounts) for contract pricing application, consolidated invoicing, credit terms enforcement, annual spend ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Retail orders require cost center allocation for internal accounting, departmental P&L tracking, B2B order costing, and management reporting. Standard practice in multi-department retail operations fo']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store, distribution center, or warehouse responsible for fulfilling this order. Cross-domain reference to inventory or store domain.']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Online and phone orders must link to loyalty membership for points accrual, tier-based pricing, personalized promotions, and member purchase history tracking—core retail loyalty operations.']
  privacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_assessment. Business justification: Orders containing personal data trigger GDPR Article 35 DPIAs and CCPA risk assessments. DPOs assess order processing activities for privacy risks, data minimization, and subject rights. Links orders ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the order. Links to customer master data.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Retail operations tag customer orders with selling season for seasonal performance analysis, trend reporting, merchandising plan effectiveness measurement, and comparing actual sales against seasonal ']
  source_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.purchase_order. Business justification: Retail drop-ship orders where vendor ships directly to customer require tracking the source supplier PO for cost reconciliation, vendor chargeback processing, and fulfillment exception handling. Essen']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date the order was delivered to the customer. Null until delivery is confirmed.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address associated with the payment method.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address for apartment, suite, or unit number.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality for the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region for the billing address.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the shipping carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider handling delivery (e.g., UPS, FedEx, USPS).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the order was originated. Distinguishes omnichannel order sources for analytics and fulfillment routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order header record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this order (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided by the customer for order confirmation and communication.']
  customer_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for delivery coordination and customer service.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the order including promotional discounts, coupons, loyalty rewards, and markdowns.']
  grand_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final order total including subtotal, tax, shipping, minus discounts. Amount charged to customer payment method.']
  locale varchar [note: 'Language and regional locale for the order (e.g., en_US, fr_CA). Used for localized content and formatting.']
  oms_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the Order Management System for end-to-end order orchestration and tracking.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was placed by the customer. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the order. Used for customer communication, tracking, and cross-system reference.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the order in the fulfillment workflow. Tracks progression from creation through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|confirmed|processing|picking|packing|shipped|delivered|completed|cancelled|returned — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of order fulfillment method. BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), SFS (Ship From Store), or standard home delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|bopis|ropis|ship_from_store|drop_ship|exchange|replacement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used for the order. Identifies the payment type without exposing sensitive card details. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay|gift_card|store_credit|cash|check|bank_transfer — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment processing for the order. Tracks authorization, capture, and settlement lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|captured|paid|failed|refunded|partially_refunded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date committed to the customer for order delivery. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer-requested target delivery date. Used for scheduling and SLA tracking.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the shipping address including street number and name. Primary delivery location identifier.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the shipping address for apartment, suite, or unit number. Optional address detail.']
  shipping_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Shipping and handling charges for order delivery. Zero for in-store pickup orders.']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality for the shipping address.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the shipping address (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the shipping address. Critical for carrier routing and delivery zone determination.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region for the shipping address. Used for tax jurisdiction and carrier routing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system that created this order record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, Store POS).']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Customer-provided delivery instructions or notes (e.g., gate code, leave at door, signature required).']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all line item amounts before taxes, discounts, and shipping charges. Base merchandise value.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales tax applied to the order based on jurisdiction and product taxability rules.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and customer self-service tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order header record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every omnichannel order. Captures order number, channel origin (web, app, call center, marketplace), order date/time, customer reference, fulfillment node reference (FK to store/distribution domain), order status, currency, locale, totals (subtotal, tax, discount, shipping, grand total), OMS reference ID, source system, and audit timestamps. SSOT for order identity and lifecycle. Carrier and fulfillment node masters are owned by peer domains and referenced via cross-domain FK.'
}

Table order.order_line {
  order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order line item. Primary key for the order line product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line-level cost center assignment enables accurate departmental P&L in mixed-cart orders, supports product category profitability analysis, and is required for internal management accounting and segme']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center, store, or warehouse assigned to fulfill this line. Supports split-fulfillment where different lines ship from different nodes.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent order header. Links this line item to its containing order transaction.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Lot traceability is mandatory for recalls, quality issues, and regulatory compliance in retail (especially food, pharma, cosmetics). Fulfillment systems must record which specific lot/batch was picked']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center allocation at line level is essential for channel profitability analysis, store/DC performance measurement, segment reporting, and financial consolidation. Retail standard for tracking m']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion applied to this line item. Links to the promotion master for discount calculation and campaign tracking.']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.replenishment_plan. Business justification: Promotional merchandise pre-positioning and seasonal demand planning require linking actual order lines to the replenishment plans that forecasted them. Enables forecast accuracy measurement, safety s']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Order lines must reference the product SKU being ordered. The product_description column becomes redundant as it can be joined from product.sku. This is a fundamental cross-domain relationship between']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Order lines capture actual transaction prices. Linking to sku_price enables margin variance analysis, promotional effectiveness measurement, price compliance auditing, and reconciliation between plann']
  uom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.uom. Business justification: Order line fulfillment requires UOM conversion factor lookups for inventory deduction accuracy, carrier weight calculations, and cross-channel unit reconciliation. Current unit_of_measure string preve']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Actual date when this line item was shipped from the fulfillment node. Used for SLA performance measurement and delivery estimation.']
  backorder_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is on backorder due to insufficient inventory at order time. True if the item will be shipped when inventory becomes available.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why this line was cancelled. Populated only when line_status is CANCELLED. Enables root cause analysis of order cancellations.']
  cancelled_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity cancelled and not fulfilled. Tracks partial cancellations where only a portion of the ordered quantity is cancelled.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the shipping carrier assigned to deliver this line item. Supports multi-carrier fulfillment strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FEDEX|UPS|USPS|DHL|ONTRAC|LASERSHIP|LOCAL_COURIER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Unit cost of the product at the time of order fulfillment. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting. Business-confidential financial data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order line record was first created in the system. Represents the moment the line item was added to the order.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Supports multi-currency transactions in global retail operations.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to this line item. Includes promotional discounts, markdowns, coupons, and loyalty rewards applied at the line level.']
  expected_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date when this line item is expected to ship from the fulfillment node. Used for customer communication and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  extended_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total price for this line item before discounts and taxes. Calculated as ordered_quantity multiplied by unit_retail_price.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which this line will be fulfilled. Supports omnichannel fulfillment including ship-to-home, Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS), Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS), Ship-from-Store (SFS), and drop-ship.']
  gift_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is marked as a gift. True if the customer designated this item for gift wrapping, gift messaging, or special handling.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Customer-provided gift message to be included with this line item. Populated only when gift_flag is true.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit Global Trade Item Number. International standard for product identification across the supply chain.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the order. Determines the display and processing order of line items within a single order header.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this order line. Tracks the line through the fulfillment lifecycle from order confirmation to final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING|CONFIRMED|ALLOCATED|PICKED|PACKED|SHIPPED|DELIVERED|CANCELLED|RETURNED — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  margin_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross margin for this line item calculated as line_total minus cost_of_goods_sold. Key metric for profitability analysis and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) calculations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order line record was last modified. Tracks any updates to quantity, status, pricing, or fulfillment details.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of the product ordered by the customer. Supports fractional quantities for weight-based or volume-based items.']
  original_sku varchar [note: 'Original SKU that was ordered by the customer, populated only when substitution_flag is true. Enables tracking of substitution patterns and customer acceptance rates.']
  personalization_notes varchar [note: 'Special instructions for product personalization such as monogramming, engraving, or custom text. Captured at order time and passed to fulfillment operations.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why this line was returned by the customer. Populated when returned_quantity is greater than zero. Supports Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing.']
  returned_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity returned by the customer after delivery. Supports partial returns and links to Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual quantity shipped to the customer. May differ from ordered_quantity due to partial fulfillment, substitutions, or inventory constraints.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit code identifying the specific product variant at the most granular level. Used for inventory tracking and fulfillment.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a substitution for an out-of-stock product. True if a substitute product was provided instead of the originally ordered item.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on this line item. Includes sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction and product category.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax jurisdiction for this line item. Determines applicable tax rates based on ship-to location and product tax category.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Effective tax rate applied to this line item expressed as a decimal. For example, 0.0825 represents 8.25 percent sales tax.']
  total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final total amount for this line item after discounts and taxes. Calculated as extended_price minus discount_amount plus tax_amount.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for this line item shipment. Enables customer self-service tracking and last-mile delivery visibility.']
  unit_retail_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per unit at the time of order. Represents the Average Unit Retail (AUR) before any line-level discounts or promotions.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier. Standard North American product identifier used at point of sale.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within an order header. Captures SKU/UPC/GTIN, product description snapshot, ordered quantity, unit retail price (AUR), extended price, discount amount, tax amount, line status, fulfillment method per line (ship, BOPIS, ROPIS, SFS, drop-ship), substitution flag, gift flag, personalization notes, and line-level cancellation reason. Supports split-fulfillment where different lines ship from different nodes.'
}

Table order.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each status transition record in the order lifecycle. Primary key for the immutable audit trail.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Order status transitions track service level changes (upgrade to overnight, downgrade due to inventory). Business requires carrier_service_id to analyze SLA breach rates by service tier and generate c']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical or logical node (store, DC, MFC, dark store, vendor) responsible for this stage of fulfillment. Enables location-based performance tracking.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order header that this status transition belongs to. Links this status event to the parent order entity.']
  primary_status_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service account that triggered the status change. Null for automated system transitions. Supports audit and accountability requirements.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the order was successfully delivered to the customer. Populated only for DELIVERED status. Critical for SLA compliance measurement.']
  assigned_to_team_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the team or department responsible for resolving this exception. Supports workload distribution and capacity planning.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping carrier responsible for delivery at this stage. Populated for SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, and DELIVERED statuses.']
  created_by_process varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ETL job, API call, or data ingestion process that created this record in the lakehouse. Supports data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was created in the data platform. Distinct from transition_timestamp which represents the business event time.']
  customer_notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of this status change. Null if no notification was sent.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether a customer notification (email, SMS, push) was sent for this status transition. Supports customer communication audit.']
  duration_in_previous_status_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes the order spent in the previous status before this transition. Enables stage-level performance analysis.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected delivery date communicated to the customer at this status transition. Updated as the order progresses through fulfillment stages.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of status transitions for this order. Enables chronological sorting and gap detection.']
  exception_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the exception type. Enables exception trend analysis and root cause identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: INVENTORY|PAYMENT|ADDRESS|CARRIER|FRAUD|QUALITY|SYSTEM|CUSTOMER|NONE — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this status transition represents an exception or abnormal condition requiring intervention. True for statuses like FAILED, ON_HOLD, CANCELLED.']
