  fulfillment_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment node handling this order stage. Supports omnichannel fulfillment strategy analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STORE|DC|MFC|DARK_STORE|VENDOR|3PL|CROSS_DOCK — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to this status transition. Captures operational context not covered by structured fields.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order fulfillment model. Denormalized for performance in fulfillment-type-specific analysis. BOPIS = Buy Online Pick Up In Store, ROPIS = Reserve Online Pick Up In Store, SFS = Ship From Store. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: STANDARD|BOPIS|ROPIS|SFS|DROP_SHIP|SUBSCRIPTION|RUSH — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  previous_status_code varchar [note: 'The status that the order was in immediately before this transition. Enables tracking of status flow and identifying invalid state transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREATED|CONFIRMED|ALLOCATED|PICKED|PACKED|SHIPPED|IN_TRANSIT|OUT_FOR_DELIVERY|DELIVERED|CANCELLED|RETURNED|REFUNDED|FAILED|ON_HOLD|BACKORDERED — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this status transition or exception. Drives resolution sequencing and resource allocation.']
  resolution_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this status transition requires manual resolution or intervention. True for exceptions that cannot be auto-resolved.']
  sla_breach_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes by which the SLA was breached. Null if no breach occurred. Enables quantitative analysis of service failures.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this status transition violated the defined SLA target. True if transition occurred after the SLA target timestamp.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The target date and time by which this status transition should have occurred per the service level agreement. Enables SLA breach detection.']
  source_order_channel varchar [note: 'The originating channel through which the order was placed. Denormalized for performance in channel-specific SLA analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WEB|MOBILE_APP|STORE|CALL_CENTER|MARKETPLACE|SOCIAL|KIOSK — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Unique transaction or event identifier from the originating system. Enables end-to-end traceability and reconciliation across systems.']
  status_code varchar [note: 'The new status that the order transitioned to at this point in time. Represents the current state in the order fulfillment lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREATED|CONFIRMED|ALLOCATED|PICKED|PACKED|SHIPPED|IN_TRANSIT|OUT_FOR_DELIVERY|DELIVERED|CANCELLED|RETURNED|REFUNDED|FAILED|ON_HOLD|BACKORDERED — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the shipment. Enables customer self-service tracking and carrier performance monitoring.']
  transition_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the status transition occurred. Enables root cause analysis for exceptions and delays. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUSTOMER_REQUEST|INVENTORY_UNAVAILABLE|PAYMENT_FAILED|ADDRESS_INVALID|CARRIER_DELAY|SYSTEM_ERROR|FRAUD_DETECTED|QUALITY_ISSUE|DAMAGED_IN_TRANSIT|CUSTOMER_REFUSED|BUSINESS_RULE|MANUAL_OVERRIDE|AUTOMATED_PROCESS — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transition_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context for the status transition. Captures details not covered by the standardized reason code.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the order status changed to the new status. Critical for SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking and customer-facing order tracking.']
  triggering_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or application that initiated this status transition. Enables system-level performance analysis and troubleshooting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OMS|WMS|TMS|POS|ECOM|CARRIER_API|PAYMENT_GATEWAY|FRAUD_ENGINE|CSR_PORTAL|BATCH_JOB|EDI|MOBILE_APP — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the user who triggered the status change. Provides human-readable context for manual interventions.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of every status transition for an order header throughout its lifecycle (e.g., CREATED → CONFIRMED → ALLOCATED → PICKED → SHIPPED → DELIVERED → RETURNED). Captures status code, previous status, transition timestamp, triggering system/user, reason code, and node identifier. Enables SLA tracking, exception management, and customer-facing order tracking.'
}

Table order.line_status_history {
  line_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each status transition event in the order line lifecycle. Primary key for the status history log.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping carrier assigned to this line for delivery (e.g., FedEx, UPS, USPS, regional courier). Populated when status transitions to shipped or in_transit.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Line-level status events capture service tier at granular pick/pack/ship stages. Business uses carrier_service_id for service-level SLA compliance reporting, carrier performance analysis by service ti']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment location (DC, store, dark store, MFC) responsible for this line at the time of the status transition. Tracks which node is handling each stage of fulfillment.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order header for context and cross-referencing. Enables quick lookup of all line status events for a given order.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item (SKU line) whose status transition is being recorded. Links this status event to the parent order line entity.']
  primary_line_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who picked this line. Populated when status transitions to picked. Enables picker productivity and accuracy analysis.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment container that this line is part of. Enables grouping of lines that shipped together and tracking of split shipments.']
  tertiary_line_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user (customer service rep, warehouse manager, system admin) who manually triggered this status change, if applicable. Null for automated system transitions.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'The unique transaction or message identifier from the source system that triggered this status change. Enables end-to-end traceability and troubleshooting.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse picking wave that processed this line. Links status transitions to warehouse execution batches for operational analysis.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the line was delivered to the customer. Populated when status transitions to delivered. Used for on-time delivery SLA measurement.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'The shipping service level selected for this line (e.g., standard ground, 2-day air, overnight). Impacts delivery SLA and cost.']
  current_status varchar [note: 'The new status that the order line transitioned TO at this event. Represents the current state of the line after this transition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|in_transit|out_for_delivery|delivered|cancelled|returned|refunded — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The carrier-provided or system-calculated estimated delivery date for this line. Updated as the line progresses through fulfillment stages.']
  exception_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the exception type when exception_flag is True. Enables aggregated exception reporting and trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inventory_shortage|customer_cancellation|quality_issue|carrier_delay|address_issue|payment_failure|system_error — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition represents an exception or deviation from normal fulfillment flow (e.g., cancellation, return, inventory shortage). True if exception, False if normal progression.']
  fulfillment_node_type varchar [note: 'The type of fulfillment location handling this line. Enables analysis of fulfillment performance by node type (e.g., ship-from-store vs DC).']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The line item number within the parent order (e.g., line 1, line 2). Denormalized for quick identification without joining to order_line.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments added by users or systems to provide additional context about this status transition. Used for exception documentation and customer service reference.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status that the order line transitioned FROM. Enables reconstruction of the full status flow and identification of status reversals or exceptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|in_transit|out_for_delivery|delivered|cancelled|returned|refunded — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quantity_affected decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU affected by this status transition. Supports partial fulfillment scenarios where a line is split across multiple shipments with different status progressions.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was inserted into the data platform. Distinct from status_transition_timestamp; used for data pipeline monitoring and late-arriving data detection.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture and data quality auditing.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The product SKU identifier for the line item. Denormalized to enable direct analysis of status transitions by product without joining to product master.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status transition occurred within the SLA target window. True if on-time or early, False if late. Critical for fulfillment performance KPIs.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The target date and time by which this status transition should have occurred per the order SLA. Used to calculate on-time performance at each fulfillment stage.']
  status_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining WHY the status transition occurred (e.g., CUST_CANCEL, INV_SHORTAGE, CARRIER_DELAY, QUALITY_ISSUE). Critical for root-cause analysis of fulfillment exceptions.']
  status_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable explanation of the status transition reason. Provides additional context beyond the reason code for customer service and operations teams.']
  status_transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the order line transitioned to the current status. Business event timestamp for SLA measurement and fulfillment analytics.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The source system that generated this status transition event (e.g., OMS, WMS, TMS, carrier API). Enables data lineage tracking and system integration monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oms|wms|tms|pos|ecommerce|customer_service|carrier_api|manual — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-provided tracking number for this line shipment. Enables customer self-service tracking and last-mile visibility. Populated when status transitions to shipped.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity affected (e.g., each, case, pound). Denormalized from product master for direct interpretation of quantity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Granular status transition log at the order line level, tracking each SKU line through its own fulfillment lifecycle independently of the header. Captures line status, previous status, transition timestamp, fulfillment node, carrier, and reason code. Critical for split-shipment scenarios where individual lines progress at different rates.'
}

Table order.pos_transaction {
  pos_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the POS transaction record. Primary key for in-store register transactions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: POS transactions must be allocated to cost centers for store-level P&L, department performance tracking, and internal management reporting. Required for daily sales accounting, variance analysis, and ']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order header for omnichannel transactions such as BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) pickups or ship-from-store fulfillments that originated as digital orders.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store location where the transaction occurred.']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: POS transactions require direct membership link for real-time points posting, tier validation, member-only pricing, receipt balance printing, and daily loyalty reconciliation—critical in-store loyalty']
  original_transaction_pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original POS transaction being returned or exchanged when transaction_type is return or exchange.']
  pci_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pci_assessment. Business justification: PCI DSS assessments scope cardholder data environments including POS terminals. Auditors trace transactions to validate encryption, tokenization, and secure transmission controls. Links transactions t']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the POS terminal device used to process the transaction.']
  primary_pos_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store associate or cashier who processed the transaction.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who made the purchase. Populated when loyalty card is scanned or customer account is identified.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Every POS transaction requires profit center assignment for segment reporting, store profitability analysis, financial consolidation, and management reporting. Fundamental requirement for retail finan']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: POS transactions analyzed by season for in-store seasonal sell-through reporting, validating merchandising plans against actual store performance, seasonal GMROI calculation, and measuring effectivene']
  stock_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_ledger. Business justification: POS transactions create inventory movement records in stock ledger for perpetual inventory accounting, shrinkage analysis, and financial reconciliation. Retail operations require linking each sale to ']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'FK to store.store_location.store_location_id — POS transaction to store location is required for all store-level sales reporting, comparable sales calculations, and store P&L. Every retail operational report needs this join.']
  business_date date [note: 'Retail business day to which this transaction is attributed for financial reporting. May differ from transaction timestamp for transactions after store closing but before end-of-day processing.']
  change_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash change returned to the customer when tender_amount exceeds total_amount.']
  coupon_count varchar [note: 'Number of coupons redeemed in the transaction including manufacturer coupons and store coupons.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the POS transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction monetary amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the transaction including promotional discounts, coupons, markdowns, and employee discounts.']
  fiscal_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Government-mandated fiscal receipt identifier for tax compliance and audit purposes. Required in jurisdictions with fiscal receipt regulations.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Fulfillment method for the transaction distinguishing between traditional in-store purchases and omnichannel fulfillment scenarios.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct line items in the transaction basket. Used for basket analysis and UPT (Units Per Transaction) calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the POS transaction record was last updated. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  loyalty_card_number varchar [note: 'Loyalty program card number scanned during the transaction for points accrual and customer identification.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned by the customer for this transaction based on purchase amount and promotional multipliers.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed by the customer during this transaction for discounts or rewards.']
  manager_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manager override was required during the transaction for actions such as large discounts, voids, or policy exceptions.']
  payment_method_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct payment methods used in the transaction. Supports split-tender analysis.']
  primary_payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment instrument used for the transaction. For split-tender transactions, this is the method with the largest payment amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|mobile_payment|check|store_credit — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  promotion_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct promotions applied to the transaction. Used for promotional effectiveness analysis.']
  receipt_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided by the customer for digital receipt delivery.']
  receipt_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number provided by the customer for SMS receipt delivery or order pickup notifications.']
  register_number varchar [note: 'Physical register or checkout lane number within the store where the transaction was processed.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for return when transaction_type is return. Used for return analytics and product quality monitoring.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all line item amounts before taxes, fees, and discounts are applied.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total sales tax charged on the transaction based on applicable tax jurisdiction and product tax categories.']
  tender_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount tendered by the customer across all payment methods. May exceed total_amount when cash change is given.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final total amount tendered by the customer including all items, taxes, fees, and after all discounts. Represents the net transaction value and ATV (Average Transaction Value).']
  total_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of all items purchased in the transaction, summed across all line items. May differ from item_count when multiple units of the same SKU are purchased.']
  training_mode_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was processed in training mode for new cashier onboarding. Training transactions are excluded from financial reporting.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable transaction number printed on the customer receipt. Externally visible identifier for customer service and returns processing.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the POS transaction indicating whether it was completed, voided, suspended for later completion, or refunded.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transaction was completed at the register. Primary business event timestamp for sales analytics and footfall analysis.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of POS transaction distinguishing between sales, returns, exchanges, voids, and no-sale register opens.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for voiding the transaction when transaction_status is voided. Used for fraud detection and operational quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Point-of-Sale transaction record for in-store purchase events at registers. Captures POS terminal ID, register number, cashier/associate ID, store number, transaction date/time, transaction type (sale, return, exchange, void), total tender, tax, discount, loyalty card number, basket metrics (item count, UPT, ATV), and fiscal receipt number. SSOT for brick-and-mortar register transactions. Complements order_header for transactions originating at physical registers rather than digital order flow. Payment tenders linked via the payment product.'
}

Table order.pos_transaction_line {
  pos_transaction_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the individual line item within a POS transaction. Primary key for the transaction line detail record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the employee who processed this transaction line. Used for cashier performance tracking and audit trails.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line-level cost center tracking enables department-specific performance measurement within store transactions, supports mixed-department basket analysis, and provides granular data for departmental P&']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent POS transaction header. Links this line item to the overall transaction.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Line-level profit center allocation supports accurate segment reporting for mixed-department transactions, enables product category profitability analysis, and is required for financial consolidation ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the master product record. Links to product information management system for detailed product attributes.']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: POS lines record actual selling prices at point of sale. Linking to sku_price enables real-time price verification, override detection, shrinkage analysis from pricing discrepancies, and margin tracki']
  stock_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_ledger. Business justification: Each POS line item generates a stock ledger transaction recording the inventory movement at SKU level. Required for item-level inventory accounting, variance analysis, and reconciling physical sales t']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier or vendor who provided this product. Used for vendor performance analysis and chargebacks.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Product category or subcategory code within the department. Supports assortment planning and basket analysis.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit cost of the product at the time of sale. Used for gross margin calculation and profitability analysis. Business confidential.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or store coupon code applied to this line item. Used for coupon redemption tracking and vendor chargebacks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transaction line record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trails.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Retail department or category code for this product. Used for departmental sales reporting and category management.']
  ean varchar [note: '13-digit European Article Number for international product identification. Used for global product tracking and cross-border commerce.']
  extended_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total price for this line item calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price before discounts. Gross line amount before markdown.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which this line item was fulfilled. Supports omnichannel order orchestration and fulfillment analytics.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number that uniquely identifies trade items worldwide. Can be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits depending on product type.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the product as it appears on the receipt. Includes brand, product name, size, and variant details.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the transaction. Determines the order in which items were scanned or entered at the point of sale.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points earned for this line item purchase. Used for customer loyalty program management and engagement.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed to discount this line item. Reduces the net amount paid by the customer.']
  markdown_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to this line item. Includes promotional discounts, coupons, and price reductions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transaction line record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  net_line_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount for this line item after markdown but before tax. Calculated as extended price minus markdown amount.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is a store brand or private label item. True for private label, false for national brands.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional campaign code applied to this line item. Links to promotion management system for campaign tracking and analysis.']
  quantity_sold decimal(10,3) [note: 'Number of units sold for this line item. Supports fractional quantities for variable-weight items such as produce and deli products.']
  return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item represents a product return. True for returned items, false for normal sales.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for product return. Used for return analytics and quality management.']
  scanned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this item was scanned or entered at the point of sale. Used for transaction timing analysis and basket sequence.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized products such as electronics and appliances. Used for warranty tracking and theft prevention.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit identifier for the product sold. Used for inventory tracking and assortment management.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total sales tax charged for this line item. Calculated by applying the tax rate to the net line amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction and category code for this line item. Determines applicable sales tax rate based on product category and location.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Sales tax rate applied to this line item expressed as a decimal. Example: 0.0825 represents 8.25 percent sales tax.']
  total_line_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final total amount for this line item including tax. Sum of net line amount and tax amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity sold. Indicates whether the item is sold by each, weight, or bulk packaging. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|lb|kg|oz|g|case|pack|box — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_retail_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Regular retail price per unit before any discounts or promotions. Represents the shelf price or list price of the item.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code scanned at point of sale. Standard barcode identifier for North American retail products.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for voiding this line item. Used for shrinkage analysis and operational improvement.']
  voided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was voided or cancelled during the transaction. True for voided items, false for completed sales.']
  weight_actual decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual weight of variable-weight items measured at point of sale. Captured for produce, deli, meat, and bakery items sold by weight.']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the actual weight. Indicates whether weight is measured in pounds, kilograms, ounces, or grams.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within a POS transaction. Captures SKU, UPC, EAN, GTIN, item description, quantity sold, unit retail price, extended price, markdown amount, promotion code applied, tax code, tax amount, return reason (if applicable), serial number (for electronics), and weight (for variable-weight items). Supports basket analysis and sell-through rate calculations.'
}

Table order.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment tender record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Retail associates process payments at POS terminals and customer service desks. Tracking which associate processed each payment enables cash drawer reconciliation, fraud investigation, refund authoriz']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Payment settlement requires linking to specific bank accounts for cash management, daily reconciliation, treasury operations, and bank statement matching. Essential for payment processing, fraud detec']
  bnpl_installment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the BNPL installment plan associated with this payment. Used for tracking payment schedules and reconciliation with BNPL provider.']
  gift_card_id varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized gift card number used for this payment tender. Applicable only when payment method type is gift card.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order or POS (Point of Sale) transaction to which this payment tender is applied. Links payment to the originating order header.']
  pci_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pci_assessment. Business justification: Payment processing is core PCI scope. QSAs sample payment records during assessments to validate requirement compliance (encryption at rest/transit, tokenization, key management). Links payments to as']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount applied by this payment tender toward the order total. In split-tender scenarios, multiple payment records sum to the order total.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the payment processor or issuing bank confirming approval of the payment transaction. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was authorized by the payment processor or issuing bank. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this payment tender.']
  avs_result_code varchar [note: 'Result code returned by Address Verification Service (AVS) indicating match status of billing address and postal code. Used for fraud prevention and risk assessment.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address associated with the payment method. Used for AVS (Address Verification Service) and fraud prevention.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field for AVS (Address Verification Service).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address associated with the payment method. Used for AVS (Address Verification Service) and fraud prevention.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the billing address (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Used for AVS (Address Verification Service) and international payment processing.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Critical for AVS (Address Verification Service) validation and fraud prevention.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address. Used for AVS (Address Verification Service) and tax calculation.']
  bnpl_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) service provider (e.g., Affirm, Klarna, Afterpay). Applicable only when payment method type is BNPL.']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the authorized payment was captured (funds marked for settlement). Typically occurs at order fulfillment or shipment.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Brand or network of the payment card used (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover). Applicable only when payment method type is credit or debit card. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|diners_club|unionpay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  card_expiry_month varchar [note: 'Expiration month of the payment card (1-12). Used for validation and recurring payment processing. Stored securely per PCI DSS requirements.']
  card_expiry_year varchar [note: 'Expiration year of the payment card (four-digit format, e.g., 2025). Used for validation and recurring payment processing. Stored securely per PCI DSS requirements.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment card. Used for verification and fraud prevention. Stored securely per PCI DSS and GDPR requirements.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the payment was initiated. Distinguishes web, mobile app, in-store POS (Point of Sale), call center, kiosk, or third-party platform.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment tender record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Supports multi-currency transactions.']
  cvv_result_code varchar [note: 'Result code indicating whether the Card Verification Value (CVV/CVV2/CVC) provided by the cardholder matched the issuer\'s records. Used for fraud prevention.']
  digital_wallet_type varchar [note: 'Type of digital wallet used for this payment tender (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, PayPal, Venmo). Applicable only when payment method type is digital wallet.']
  fraud_decision varchar [note: 'Decision outcome from fraud detection system. Indicates whether the payment was approved, declined, flagged for manual review, or challenged with additional authentication.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score assigned by fraud detection system, typically ranging from 0 (low risk) to 100 (high risk). Used for payment approval decisions and risk management.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed as payment for this tender. Applicable only when payment method type is loyalty points.']
  masked_pan varchar [note: 'Masked or truncated version of the payment card Primary Account Number (PAN), typically showing only the last four digits (e.g., ****1234). Used for display and customer service without exposing full card number.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument used for this tender. Includes credit card, debit card, gift card, loyalty points, BNPL (Buy Now Pay Later), cash, digital wallet (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay), or check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|loyalty_points|bnpl|cash|digital_wallet|check — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment tender. Tracks progression from authorization through settlement, including declined, voided, or refunded states.']
  processor_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway or processor. Used for tracing and reconciliation with external payment systems.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded to the customer for this payment tender. May be partial or full refund of the original payment amount.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a refund was processed for this payment tender. Applicable for returns and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) scenarios.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was settled and funds were transferred from the customer\'s account to the merchant\'s account. Final step in payment lifecycle.']
  tender_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number indicating the order in which this payment tender was applied in a split-tender scenario. First tender is 1, second is 2, etc.']
  three_ds_authentication_result varchar [note: 'Result of 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication protocol for card-not-present transactions. Indicates whether cardholder was successfully authenticated, reducing fraud liability.']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3-D Secure protocol used for authentication (e.g., 1.0.2, 2.1.0, 2.2.0). Newer versions provide enhanced security and user experience.']
  token varchar [note: 'Tokenized reference to the payment instrument, replacing sensitive card data. Token is generated by PCI DSS compliant tokenization service and used for recurring payments or stored payment methods.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment tender record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  void_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment authorization was voided (cancelled before capture). Applicable when order is cancelled before fulfillment.']

  Note: 'Payment tender record capturing each payment instrument applied to an order or POS transaction. Stores payment method type (credit card, debit, gift card, loyalty points, BNPL, cash, digital wallet), masked PAN/token, payment processor reference, authorization code, settlement status (authorized, captured, settled, declined, voided), payment amount, currency, tender sequence for split-tender scenarios, PCI DSS tokenization reference, fraud score, and 3DS authentication result. Supports split-tender where multiple payment methods cover a single order total. Integrates with PCI SSC compliance requirements and payment gateway APIs.'
}

Table order.discount {
  discount_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for discount']
  discount_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (manager or supervisor) who approved the discount application. Null if auto-applied or pending approval.']
  discount_cashier_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cashier or sales associate who applied the discount at the Point of Sale (POS). Null for online or automated discount applications.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Discount postings must reference specific GL accounts for financial reporting, promotional expense tracking, markdown accounting, and audit trails. Required for accurate revenue recognition, promotion']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order to which this discount is applied. Links to the order header.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical store location where the discount was applied. Null for online or non-store channels.']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who redeemed points or received a loyalty-based discount. Null if discount is not loyalty-based.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item to which this discount is applied. Null if discount is applied at order header level.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal or register where the discount was applied. Null for online channels.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing or promotional campaign that generated this discount. Links to the promotion campaign master data for attribution and performance analysis.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied, in the order currency. Represents the total reduction in price due to this discount.']
  applied_at_level varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the discount was applied at the order header level (affecting the entire order) or at the line item level (affecting a specific product).']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the discount was applied to the order or line item. Represents the business event time of discount application.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status of the discount application. Indicates whether the discount was automatically applied, requires manager approval, has been approved, or was rejected.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the discount was approved. Null if auto-applied or pending approval.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the discount was applied. Indicates whether the discount was applied via online e-commerce, in-store Point of Sale (POS), mobile app, call center, or kiosk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this discount record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount. Typically matches the order currency.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'The promotional code, coupon code, or discount identifier entered or applied by the customer or system. Examples include promo codes, loyalty reward codes, employee discount codes.']
  discount_method varchar [note: 'The calculation method used to determine the discount value. Indicates whether the discount is a percentage off, fixed dollar amount, tiered based on quantity or spend, Buy One Get One (BOGO) offer, or free shipping.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanism. Indicates the nature of the discount applied (e.g., coupon, promotional code, loyalty reward, markdown, Buy One Get One (BOGO), clearance, employee discount, volume discount, seasonal promotion, bundle discount, price override, manager override). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coupon|promo_code|loyalty_reward|markdown|bogo|clearance|employee_discount|volume_discount|seasonal_promotion|bundle_discount|price_override|manager_override — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  final_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The final price after the discount has been applied. Represents the net amount the customer pays for the discounted item or order.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'The number of loyalty program points redeemed to obtain this discount. Null if discount is not loyalty-based.']
  original_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original price of the item or order before the discount was applied. Used to calculate discount impact and markdown analysis.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the order or line item. Populated when discount_method is percentage. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority order in which this discount is applied when multiple discounts are present. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to determine discount application sequence.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'A code indicating the business reason for the discount. Examples include customer complaint resolution, price match, damaged goods, loyalty reward, seasonal clearance, or promotional campaign.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context or justification for the discount application. Used for audit trail and customer service reference.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system that originated this discount record. Examples include Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM), Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR), or Point of Sale (POS) system.']
  source_system_discount_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this discount record in the source system. Used for traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be combined (stacked) with other discounts on the same order or line item. True if stackable, False if exclusive.']
  tax_treatment_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is applied before tax calculation (pre-tax) or after tax calculation (post-tax). Affects the taxable amount and final tax computation.']
  taxable_amount_adjustment decimal(15,2) [note: 'The adjustment to the taxable base amount resulting from this discount. Used to recalculate sales tax when discount affects the tax basis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this discount record was last modified in the data platform. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The start date from which the discount code or promotion is valid. Used to enforce discount eligibility windows.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The end date until which the discount code or promotion is valid. Used to enforce discount eligibility windows. Null for open-ended discounts.']

  Note: 'Discount and promotion application record at the order header or line level. Captures discount type (coupon, promo code, loyalty reward, markdown, BOGO, clearance, employee discount), discount code, discount amount or percentage, promotion campaign reference, stackability flag, applied-at level (header vs line), and approval status. Enables promotion attribution, markdown tracking, sell-through analysis, and P&L impact per order. Tax calculation attributes (taxable amount adjustment) included where discount affects tax basis.'
}

Table order.cancellation {
  cancellation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cancellation event. Primary key for the cancellation record.']
  approver_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who approved the cancellation. Null if no approval required or system-auto-approved.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Cancellations may result from compliance audit findings (fraud detection failures, sanctions screening gaps, address verification issues). Links cancellations to findings for root cause analysis, corr']
  cancellation_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent or employee who processed or approved the cancellation. Null for system-initiated cancellations.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cancellation accounting (restocking fees, cancellation charges, refund processing) requires GL account assignment for proper revenue/expense recognition, financial reporting, and audit compliance. Sta']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order that was cancelled. Links this cancellation to the originating order header.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item that was cancelled. Null if the entire order was cancelled rather than individual lines.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer associated with the cancelled order. Links cancellation to the customer master record for analytics and customer service.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation requires managerial or supervisory approval before execution. True for high-value orders or policy exceptions.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation was approved by an authorized agent or manager. Null if no approval required or not yet approved.']
  cancellation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique cancellation reference number used for customer communication and tracking. Format: CXL-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  cancellation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the cancellation request. Tracks the cancellation through its approval and execution workflow.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation was initiated or requested. Represents the business event time of the cancellation action.']
  cancelled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total order or line value that was cancelled, including product cost, taxes, and fees. Represents the gross cancelled transaction value.']
  cancelled_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'Quantity of the product or item that was cancelled. Relevant for line-level cancellations. Measured in the unit of measure of the ordered item.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the cancellation was submitted. Tracks omnichannel cancellation touchpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|call_center|store|email|chat|system_automated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cancellation confirmation notification was sent to the customer via email, SMS, or app notification. True if notification sent.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation confirmation notification was sent to the customer. Null if notification not sent or failed.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Administrative or processing fee charged for the cancellation. Zero if no cancellation fee applies per business policy or customer segment.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation was triggered by fraud detection rules or suspected fraudulent activity. True if fraud-related cancellation.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned by the fraud detection system at the time of cancellation. Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk. Null if not fraud-related.']
  initiator_type varchar [note: 'Category of entity that initiated the cancellation. Distinguishes between customer-initiated, system-initiated, agent-initiated, and automated rule-based cancellations.']
  inventory_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when allocated inventory was released back to available stock following the cancellation. Null if inventory was never allocated or not yet released.']
  inventory_released_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory allocated to the cancelled order or line has been released back to available stock. True if inventory release confirmed.']
  processing_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or automated process that executed the cancellation. Used for system-initiated cancellations to track which rule engine or service triggered the action.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|out_of_stock|pricing_error|duplicate_order|payment_declined|fraud_suspected|delivery_delay|product_unavailable|customer_changed_mind|incorrect_item_ordered|found_better_price|shipping_cost_too_high|delivery_time_too_long|order_placed_by_mistake|item_no_longer_needed|quality_concerns|other — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or additional details provided by the initiator explaining the reason for cancellation. Captures customer comments or agent notes.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount to be refunded to the customer for this cancellation. Includes product cost, taxes, and fees as applicable.']
  refund_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount. Indicates the currency in which the refund will be processed.']
  refund_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancelled order or line is eligible for a refund based on payment status and business rules. True if refund should be processed.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund will be issued to the customer. Typically matches the original payment method but may differ based on business rules.']
  refund_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was successfully processed and submitted to the payment gateway or finance system. Null if refund not yet processed.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for cancelling the order after certain fulfillment stages. Zero if no restocking fee applies per business policy.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cancellation was subsequently reversed and the order reinstated. True if the cancellation was undone.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the cancellation was reversed. Captures business justification for reinstating the order.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cancellation was reversed and the order reinstated. Null if no reversal occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that originated the cancellation record. Used for data lineage and integration tracking across OMS, e-commerce, and POS systems.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Order fulfillment stage at which the cancellation occurred. Indicates how far the order progressed before cancellation and impacts inventory release and refund processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cancellation record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes to cancellation status, refund processing, or other attributes.']

  Note: 'Order or line-level cancellation event record capturing the full context of why and when an order or individual line was cancelled before shipment. Captures cancellation number, order/line reference, cancellation initiator (customer-initiated via web/app/call-center, system-initiated via OMS rules, fraud-hold auto-cancel, inventory allocation failure), cancellation reason code, cancellation timestamp, cancellation stage (pre-allocation, post-allocation-pre-pick, post-pick-pre-ship), refund eligibility flag, inventory release confirmation, and processing agent/system ID. Distinct from RMA which covers post-shipment returns.'
}

Table order.hold {
  hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order hold record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent or fraud analyst assigned to review and resolve this hold. Nullable if not yet assigned.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Holds placed due to compliance control failures (fraud screening, sanctions hits, address verification) link to audit findings. Enables auditors to validate hold procedures, measure control effectiven']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order that is subject to this hold. Links to the order header.']
  inventory_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory reservation record associated with this hold, particularly for Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) or Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) orders.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item subject to this hold. Nullable if the hold applies to the entire order rather than a specific line.']
  pickup_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store or fulfillment node where the customer will pick up the order for Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) or Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) holds.']
  superseded_hold_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on hold (superseded_hold_id)']
  address_verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the address verification process indicating whether the shipping address passed validation checks. Relevant for address verification failure holds.']
  assigned_to_team_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the team or department responsible for resolving this hold (e.g., fraud team, payment operations, customer service).']
  auto_release_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold is eligible for automatic release by system rules without manual intervention.']
  auto_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the system will automatically release the hold if no manual action is taken. Nullable if auto-release is not configured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customer_notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer about the hold status (email, SMS, push notification, phone call, or in-app message).']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been notified about the hold via email, SMS, or other communication channel.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer notification about the hold was sent. Nullable if no notification has been sent.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Calculated duration of the hold in minutes from start to end timestamp. Used for performance analysis and SLA compliance reporting.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was released or resolved, allowing order processing to resume. Nullable if hold is still active.']
  expected_resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Anticipated date and time when the hold is expected to be resolved based on business rules and Service Level Agreement (SLA) targets.']
  fraud_decision varchar [note: 'Decision rendered by the fraud detection system or fraud analyst regarding the order: approve, decline, manual review, or customer challenge required.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned by the fraud detection system indicating the likelihood of fraudulent activity. Higher scores indicate higher risk.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the hold, used for tracking and customer service reference.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold indicating whether it is active, resolved, expired, cancelled, or escalated for further review.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold indicating the reason category for the suspension. Includes fraud review, payment issues, pickup reservations (BOPIS/ROPIS), and compliance holds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fraud_review|payment_authorization_failure|address_verification_failure|inventory_reservation|bopis_pickup_reservation|ropis_pickup_reservation|credit_limit_exceeded|compliance_review — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manual_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold requires manual review and approval by a customer service agent or fraud analyst before release.']
  payment_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code from the payment processor associated with this hold. Relevant for payment authorization failure holds.']
  payment_processor_response varchar [note: 'Response message or code returned by the payment processor explaining the reason for payment authorization failure.']
  pickup_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time after which the pickup reservation expires and the order may be cancelled or returned to inventory if not collected.']
  pickup_ready_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order is ready for customer pickup at the designated location. Relevant for Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) and Reserve Online Pick Up In Store (ROPIS) holds.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the hold resolution based on order value, customer tier, or urgency. Determines queue position for manual review.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold. Used for categorization and reporting.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed human-readable explanation of why the hold was placed, providing context for customer service and operations teams.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'The action taken to resolve the hold, such as releasing the order, cancelling it, escalating for review, or applying a manual override.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the resolution process, actions taken, and any special circumstances or customer interactions.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the hold resolution exceeded the SLA target timestamp, triggering potential customer compensation or escalation.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The deadline by which the hold must be resolved to meet Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments to the customer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system that created or initiated the hold (e.g., Order Management System, fraud detection system, payment gateway).']
  source_system_hold_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this hold in the originating source system, used for cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was initiated and order processing was suspended. Critical for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hold record was last modified in the data warehouse. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Temporary suspension or reservation of order processing including BOPIS/ROPIS pickup reservations, fraud review holds, payment authorization failures, and address verification holds. Captures hold type, hold reason, hold start/end timestamps, resolution action, and associated order reference.'
}

Table order.promise {
  promise_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order promise record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier. Business justification: Promise calculation engine uses carrier-specific transit times as primary input. Business needs carrier_id to trace which carrier\'s SLA drove the customer promise for promise accuracy analysis, carrie']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Promise dates calculated from carrier service level transit times (2-day vs. overnight). Business requires carrier_service_id to validate promise accuracy against service-level SLAs, analyze promise v']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment node (DC, store, dark store) from which the order will be shipped.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order header for which this delivery promise was made.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line if the promise is line-level; null if promise is order-level.']
  revised_promise_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on promise (revised_promise_id)']
  accuracy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery was completed within the promised window (true = on-time, false = missed).']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the order was delivered to the customer, used for promise accuracy measurement.']
  calculation_engine_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the promise calculation engine or algorithm used.']
  calculation_source varchar [note: 'System or method used to calculate the delivery promise (carrier API, static SLA table, DOM engine, manual override).']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Specific service level offered by the carrier (e.g., Ground, Express, Priority).']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0-100) indicating the likelihood of meeting the delivery promise based on historical performance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery promise was initially created and communicated to the customer.']
  customer_notification_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer was notified of the delivery promise.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer was notified of the delivery promise or any revisions.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer was notified of the delivery promise or revision.']
  cutoff_time_applied varchar [note: 'Order cutoff time applied for same-day or next-day promise calculation (e.g., 2:00 PM local time).']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the order will be fulfilled (ship-to-home, BOPIS, ROPIS, ship-from-store, curbside pickup).']
  inventory_availability_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when inventory availability was confirmed for promise calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this promise record was last updated in the data platform.']
  last_revision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent revision to the delivery promise.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order was placed, influencing promise calculation logic.']
  peak_season_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promise was made during a peak season (e.g., holiday shopping period) affecting delivery times.']
  promise_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for the delivery promise, used for customer communication and tracking.']
  promise_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery promise.']
  promise_type varchar [note: 'Type of delivery promise made to the customer at checkout or order confirmation.']
  promised_delivery_date_end date [note: 'End date of the promised delivery window communicated to the customer.']
  promised_delivery_date_start date [note: 'Start date of the promised delivery window communicated to the customer.']
  promised_delivery_time_end timestamp [note: 'Precise end timestamp of the promised delivery window for time-specific promises.']
  promised_delivery_time_start timestamp [note: 'Precise start timestamp of the promised delivery window for time-specific promises.']
  revision_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the delivery promise has been revised after initial creation.']
  revision_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for promise revision (e.g., inventory delay, carrier delay, weather).']
  revision_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of why the delivery promise was revised.']
  shipping_address_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the delivery destination.']
  shipping_address_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the delivery destination, used for promise calculation and carrier routing.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery promise was breached (actual delivery exceeded promised window).']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target number of hours from order placement to delivery as defined by the SLA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that created the delivery promise record (OMS, e-commerce platform, DOM engine).']
  variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in hours between actual delivery and promised delivery (positive = late, negative = early).']
  weather_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether weather conditions were factored into the promise calculation or caused a delay.']

  Note: 'Delivery commitment made to the customer at checkout or order confirmation. Captures promise type (standard, expedited, same-day, next-day), promised delivery window (start/end), calculation source (carrier API, static SLA table, DOM engine), revision history when promises are updated, actual vs promised variance, and SLA breach flag. Enables promise accuracy KPI tracking, customer experience measurement, and carrier performance evaluation.'
}

Table order.gift_card {
  gift_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gift card record. Primary key.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order transaction in which this gift card was purchased. Links gift card to originating sales transaction. Nullable for cards issued outside normal order flow (e.g., corporate bulk issuance).']
  liability_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Gift card liability tracking requires specific GL account for deferred revenue, breakage recognition, balance sheet reporting, and ASC 606 compliance. Role-prefixed because gift cards have multiple GL']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical store location where the gift card was issued or sold. Nullable for non-store channels.']
  pci_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pci_assessment. Business justification: Gift cards with PANs fall under PCI DSS scope. Assessors validate gift card issuance, activation, and redemption controls. Links gift cards to assessment scope for cardholder data environment validati']
  primary_gift_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who purchased the gift card. May differ from the recipient. Nullable if purchased anonymously or by guest.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign associated with this gift card issuance (e.g., holiday bonus card, purchase-with-purchase offer). Nullable for non-promotional cards.']
  replacement_for_card_gift_card_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original gift card that this card replaces (e.g., lost or damaged card replacement). Nullable for original issuances. Balance is typically transferred from original card.']
  replacement_for_gift_card_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on gift_card (replacement_for_gift_card_id)']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.storefront. Business justification: Gift cards sold or redeemed through digital channels require storefront attribution for revenue recognition, liability tracking by channel, and fraud prevention. Retailers must track which digital sto']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the gift card was activated and became available for redemption. May differ from issue date if activation is delayed (e.g., physical cards activated at first use).']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode value encoded on physical gift cards for scanning at Point of Sale (POS). May be UPC (Universal Product Code), EAN (European Article Number), or proprietary format.']
  card_number varchar [note: 'Unique card number printed or encoded on the gift card. Typically 16-19 digits. Used for identification and redemption at Point of Sale (POS) or e-commerce checkout.']
  card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gift card. Inactive: issued but not yet activated; Active: available for use; Suspended: temporarily blocked; Expired: past validity period; Depleted: balance fully redeemed; Cancelled: voided or returned.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of gift card instrument. Physical cards are tangible plastic cards; digital/virtual cards are delivered electronically via email or app; mobile wallet cards are provisioned into digital wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift card record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the denomination of the gift card balance (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD).']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining monetary value available on the gift card after all redemptions, adjustments, and fees. Updated in real-time with each transaction.']
  escheatment_date date [note: 'Date when the gift card balance was escheated (transferred) to state unclaimed property authority. Nullable if not yet escheated.']
  escheatment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card balance is eligible for escheatment (transfer to state unclaimed property) based on dormancy period and state regulations. True: eligible; False: not eligible.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the gift card expires and can no longer be redeemed. Nullable for cards with no expiration. Subject to regulatory requirements (e.g., CARD Act in USA mandates minimum 5-year validity).']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gift card has been flagged for suspected fraudulent activity (e.g., unauthorized use, balance tampering, stolen card). True: fraud suspected; False: no fraud detected.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for fraud flag (e.g., STOLEN, COUNTERFEIT, BALANCE_TAMPERING, SUSPICIOUS_ACTIVITY). Nullable if fraud_flag is false.']
  initial_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original monetary value loaded onto the gift card at the time of issuance or purchase. Represents the starting balance before any redemptions or adjustments.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the gift card was originally issued or sold to the customer. Marks the beginning of the card lifecycle.']
  issuing_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the gift card was originally issued or purchased. Store: in-store POS; Ecommerce: online storefront; Mobile App: mobile commerce; Call Center: phone order; Corporate Sales: bulk B2B sales; Third Party: external marketplace or partner.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift card record was last updated. Tracks data currency and supports change data capture (CDC) processes.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transaction (redemption, reload, or adjustment) on this gift card. Used for dormancy tracking and escheatment compliance.']
  pin_code varchar [note: 'Personal Identification Number (PIN) required for online redemption or balance inquiry. Typically 4-8 digits. Stored encrypted in production systems.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the gift card program or product line (e.g., standard gift card, promotional card, employee reward card, corporate incentive card). Used for segmentation and reporting.']
  qr_code varchar [note: 'Quick Response (QR) code data for mobile wallet provisioning or contactless redemption. Encoded with card number and validation data.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the gift card recipient. Used for digital card delivery and balance notifications. Required for digital/virtual cards.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the gift card recipient. Used for SMS delivery of digital cards and balance inquiries.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Total number of redemption transactions (purchases) made using this gift card since activation.']
  reload_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the gift card has been reloaded with additional funds since issuance. Zero for non-reloadable or never-reloaded cards.']
  reloadable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gift card can be reloaded with additional funds after initial issuance. True: reloadable; False: single-load only.']
  terms_and_conditions_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terms and conditions that govern this gift card (e.g., expiration policy, fee schedule, usage restrictions). Used for compliance and customer service.']
  total_fees_charged decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative fees deducted from the gift card balance (e.g., dormancy fees, maintenance fees, replacement fees). Subject to regulatory restrictions (e.g., CARD Act limits dormancy fees).']
  total_redeemed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value redeemed from the gift card across all transactions since activation. Equals initial_balance + total_reloaded_amount - current_balance - total_fees.']
  total_reloaded_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value added to the gift card through all reload transactions since issuance. Excludes initial balance.']

  Note: 'Master record for gift cards and stored-value instruments sold and redeemed across all channels. Captures card number, card type (physical, digital), initial balance, current balance, activation date, expiry date, and issuing channel.'
}

Table order.gift_card_transaction {
  gift_card_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gift card transaction record.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cashier or associate who processed the transaction at POS.']
  gift_card_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the gift card involved in this transaction. May be masked for security.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each gift card transaction (issuance, redemption, reload, escheatment) posts to specific GL accounts for proper accounting treatment, revenue recognition, liability management, and financial reporting']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the online order if this gift card activity was part of an e-commerce transaction.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical store where the transaction occurred. Null for online channels.']
  original_transaction_gift_card_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original gift card transaction that is being reversed or refunded.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal or kiosk where the transaction was processed.']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the POS transaction if this gift card activity was part of a store purchase or return.']
  primary_gift_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who performed the transaction. May be null for anonymous gift card purchases.']
  reversal_gift_card_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on gift_card_transaction (reversal_gift_card_transaction_id)']
  activation_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for activating or loading the gift card, if applicable.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization or approval code returned by the gift card processor for this transaction.']
  balance_after decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gift card balance immediately after this transaction was applied.']
  balance_before decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gift card balance immediately before this transaction was applied.']
  business_date date [note: 'Fiscal business date on which the transaction is recorded for accounting and reporting purposes.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the gift card transaction was initiated (POS, e-commerce, mobile app, call center, kiosk, third-party partner).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gift card transaction record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).']
  escheatment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction represents an escheatment event where unclaimed gift card funds are transferred to state authorities.']
