  chargeback_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary penalty amount (in base currency) assessed per EDI compliance violation. Chargebacks are vendor compliance penalties for failing to meet EDI standards or SLAs.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'The technical protocol used to transmit EDI documents between trading partners. AS2 is the most common secure protocol; VAN refers to Value-Added Network services.']
  compliance_threshold_error_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum acceptable error rate (as a percentage) for EDI transactions before compliance penalties or chargebacks are triggered. Example: 2.00 means 2% error rate threshold.']
  connection_endpoint varchar [note: 'The URL, IP address, or network address used to connect to the trading partner for EDI transmission. May be an AS2 URL, SFTP host, or VAN mailbox identifier.']
  connection_port varchar [note: 'The network port number used for EDI communication. Common ports: 22 for SFTP, 443 for AS2 over HTTPS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this EDI configuration record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  edi_version varchar [note: 'The EDI standard version supported by this configuration. Examples: 4010, 5010 for ANSI X12; D96A, D01B for EDIFACT.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this EDI configuration ceases to be effective. Null for currently active configurations. Supports temporal validity tracking and configuration history.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this EDI configuration becomes effective and valid for use. Supports temporal validity tracking.']
  environment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this EDI configuration is for production use or a non-production environment (test, development, staging). Test environments are used during EDI onboarding and integration testing.']
  fa_sla_hours varchar [note: 'The maximum number of hours allowed to send a functional acknowledgment (997) after receiving an EDI document. Failure to meet this SLA may trigger compliance chargebacks.']
  functional_acknowledgment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trading partner requires a 997 Functional Acknowledgment to be sent in response to received EDI documents. True if FA is mandatory per trading partner agreement.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'The date when the EDI configuration was activated for production transaction processing. Marks the transition from test to production environment.']
  gs_qualifier varchar [note: 'GS qualifier code that identifies the type of identification being used in the functional group envelope. Defines how the sender/receiver codes are interpreted.']
  inbound_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is enabled to receive inbound EDI documents from the trading partner. True if inbound processing is active.']
  integration_health_status varchar [note: 'Current operational health status of the EDI integration. Derived from recent transmission success rates, error rates, and SLA compliance. Used for proactive monitoring.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EDI configuration is currently active and operational. False if the configuration has been deactivated or suspended.']
  isa_qualifier varchar [note: 'ISA qualifier code that identifies the type of identification being used in the EDI interchange envelope. Common values: 01=DUNS, 14=DUNS+4, ZZ=Mutually Defined. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|14|20|27|28|29|30|33|ZZ — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_error_code varchar [note: 'The error code or identifier for the most recent EDI processing failure. Used in conjunction with last_error_timestamp for diagnostics.']
  last_error_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the most recent EDI error. Provides context for troubleshooting and resolution.']
  last_error_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent EDI transmission or processing error. Used for troubleshooting and error resolution tracking.']
  last_successful_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent successful EDI document transmission (inbound or outbound). Used for integration health monitoring and alerting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this EDI configuration record was most recently modified. Audit trail field for change tracking.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times a failed EDI transmission will be retried before being marked as permanently failed and requiring manual intervention.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional configuration details, special requirements, or historical context about the EDI integration.']
  onboarding_start_date date [note: 'The date when EDI onboarding activities began for this trading partner. Marks the start of the integration project.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the EDI onboarding lifecycle for this trading partner. Tracks progression from initial setup through testing, certification, and production activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|testing|certified|active|suspended|terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  outbound_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is enabled to send outbound EDI documents to the trading partner. True if outbound processing is active.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'The retry strategy applied when EDI transmission failures occur. Defines how and when failed transmissions are retried.']
  supported_transaction_sets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EDI transaction set codes supported by this trading partner. Common retail sets: 850 (Purchase Order), 855 (PO Acknowledgment), 856 (Advance Ship Notice), 810 (Invoice), 832 (Price/Sales Catalog), 846 (Inventory Inquiry/Advice), 997 (Functional Acknowledgment).']

  Note: 'EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration configuration for each trading partner defining connection parameters and supported transaction sets. Captures vendor trading partner ID, ISA/GS qualifiers, supported EDI documents (850, 855, 856, 810, 832, 846), communication protocol (AS2, SFTP, VAN), test vs production status, functional acknowledgment SLA, and compliance thresholds that trigger chargebacks. Manages EDI onboarding lifecycle, ongoing integration health monitoring, and serves as the reference record for edi_transaction processing and error resolution.'
}

Table supplier.supplier_edi_transaction {
  supplier_edi_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EDI transaction log entry. Primary key.']
  dq_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.dq_result. Business justification: EDI transaction processing generates data quality results (error rates, acknowledgment status, retry counts). Links transaction execution to quality measurement for EDI health dashboards and supplier ']
  edi_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.edi_config. Business justification: EDI transactions should directly reference the edi_config that governs their processing rules, acknowledgment requirements, and retry policies. Currently, the relationship is indirect via vendor_id. A']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor involved in this EDI transaction.']
  acknowledgment_code varchar [note: 'ANSI X12 acknowledgment code from the 997/999 FA: A=Accepted, E=Accepted with Errors, R=Rejected, P=Partially Accepted, M=Rejected due to Missing Data, W=Rejected due to Wrong Data, X=Rejected due to Unrecognized Transaction Set. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|E|R|P|M|W|X — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the functional acknowledgment (997/999) was received or sent for this transaction.']
  archive_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this EDI transaction was archived to long-term storage.']
  archived_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this EDI transaction has been archived to long-term storage and purged from active operational tables.']
  business_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying business document (e.g., purchase order number, invoice number, shipment number) that this EDI transaction represents.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount assessed to the vendor for EDI non-compliance (e.g., missing ASN, late acknowledgment, data quality failure).']
  chargeback_eligible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this EDI transaction failure or non-compliance is eligible for a vendor chargeback penalty.']
  chargeback_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific EDI compliance violation that triggered the chargeback (e.g., LATE_ASN, MISSING_997, DATA_ERROR, DUPLICATE_ICN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this EDI transaction log record was first created in the system.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Direction of the EDI message flow: inbound (received from vendor) or outbound (sent to vendor).']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned by the EDI translator or validation engine if the transaction failed processing. May reference ANSI X12 syntax error codes or internal error codes.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the error encountered during EDI processing, including segment, element, and validation failure details.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the EDI file as received or sent, used for file-based EDI transmissions.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the EDI file in bytes, used for transmission monitoring and capacity planning.']
  functional_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the 997 or 999 functional acknowledgment received or sent for this transaction, indicating whether the EDI message was accepted, rejected, or accepted with errors.']
  functional_group_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number assigned to the functional group (GS segment) within the EDI interchange.']
  interchange_control_number varchar [note: 'Unique control number assigned to the EDI interchange envelope (ISA segment) for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this EDI transaction log record was last modified, typically updated when processing status or acknowledgment status changes.']
  max_retry_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts allowed for this transaction type before escalation or manual intervention is required.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the EDI transaction within the internal EDI pipeline (received, parsed, validated, processed, failed, retrying, archived). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|parsed|validated|processed|failed|retrying|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the EDI transaction was processed by the internal EDI translation and integration engine.']
  receiver_code varchar [note: 'EDI receiver identifier from the ISA08 segment, typically the retailer\'s EDI ID or DUNS number.']
  receiver_qualifier varchar [note: 'Qualifier code identifying the type of receiver ID (e.g., 01=DUNS, 08=UCC/EAN, 12=Phone, 14=DUNS+4, ZZ=Mutually Defined). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|08|09|10|11|12|13|14|15|16|20|27|28|29|30|33|ZZ — 18 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the EDI transaction has been retried after initial processing failure. Used to track retry attempts and enforce retry limits.']
  segment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of EDI segments in the transaction set, used for validation and completeness checks.']
  sender_code varchar [note: 'EDI sender identifier from the ISA06 segment, typically the vendor\'s EDI ID or DUNS number.']
  sender_qualifier varchar [note: 'Qualifier code identifying the type of sender ID (e.g., 01=DUNS, 08=UCC/EAN, 12=Phone, 14=DUNS+4, ZZ=Mutually Defined). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|08|09|10|11|12|13|14|15|16|20|27|28|29|30|33|ZZ — 18 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a test/sandbox EDI transaction (true) or a production transaction (false).']
  transaction_set_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number assigned to the individual transaction set (ST segment) for unique identification within the functional group.']
  transaction_set_type varchar [note: 'The ANSI X12 transaction set identifier (e.g., 850=Purchase Order, 810=Invoice, 856=Advance Ship Notice, 860=Purchase Order Change, 997/999=Functional Acknowledgment, 846=Inventory Inquiry, 855=Purchase Order Acknowledgment, 875=Grocery Products Purchase Order, 204=Motor Carrier Load Tender, 214=Transportation Carrier Shipment Status). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 850|810|856|860|997|999|846|855|875|204|214 — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the EDI message was transmitted (for outbound) or received (for inbound) by the EDI gateway.']

  Note: 'Log of EDI message exchanges with vendors capturing transaction set type, direction (inbound/outbound), interchange control number, functional acknowledgment status (997/999), processing timestamp, error codes, and retry count. Provides operational visibility into EDI pipeline health and supports chargeback issuance for EDI non-compliance.'
}

Table supplier.lead_time_agreement {
  lead_time_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lead time agreement record. Primary key.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or warehouse that receives shipments under this agreement.']
  destination_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Lead time agreements specify delivery terms to specific DCs, stores, or cross-dock facilities. Essential for replenishment planning, inbound appointment scheduling, and on-time delivery SLA measuremen']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Lead time adherence is a core supply chain KPI measured against agreement terms. Links lead time agreements to KPI definitions for supplier performance measurement and replenishment planning analytics']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category covered by this agreement when scope_level is category or subcategory.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_contract. Business justification: Lead time agreements define MOQ, lead times, and delivery SLAs that are typically negotiated as part of vendor contracts. Linking to vendor_contract enables tracking agreement lineage, contract renewa']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor party covered by this lead time agreement.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the lead time agreement for easy identification.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the lead time agreement, used for external reference and vendor communication.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead time agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lead time agreement based on its purpose and operational context. VMI indicates Vendor Managed Inventory agreements.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement automatically renews upon expiration.']
  compliance_penalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied as chargeback penalty for vendor non-compliance with lead time or delivery terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead time agreement record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard frequency of deliveries from vendor. DSD indicates Direct Store Delivery model.']
  delivery_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'End time of the agreed delivery window at distribution center (HH:MM format).']
  delivery_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Start time of the agreed delivery window at distribution center (HH:MM format).']
  edi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchase orders and ASNs are exchanged via EDI for this agreement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this lead time agreement expires or is terminated. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this lead time agreement becomes active and enforceable.']
  expedited_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Reduced lead time in days available for rush or expedited orders, typically at premium cost.']
  fill_rate_sla_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of ordered quantity that vendor must deliver to meet order completion SLA.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Standard trade term defining responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and customs (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  inner_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of consumer units in an inner pack within the master case, used for store-level replenishment.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead time agreement record was last modified.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum number of units that must be ordered per purchase order to meet vendor requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional terms, special instructions, or exceptions related to this lead time agreement.']
  on_time_delivery_sla_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of deliveries that must arrive within the agreed lead time window to meet SLA.']
  order_increment_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity increment in which orders must be placed above the MOQ (e.g., must order in multiples of 12 units).']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or facility from which vendor ships products (city, state, country).']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'Number of consumer units contained in a single vendor pack or case.']
  pallet_quantity varchar [note: 'Standard number of cases or units per pallet for warehouse receiving and cross-docking operations.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required before agreement renewal or termination.']
  scope_level varchar [note: 'Granularity level at which this lead time agreement applies: vendor-wide, product category, subcategory, individual SKU, or product group.']
  seasonal_lead_time_adjustment_days varchar [note: 'Additional days added to standard lead time during peak seasonal periods (e.g., holiday season, back-to-school).']
  seasonal_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the seasonal period when lead time adjustments apply.']
  seasonal_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the seasonal period when lead time adjustments apply.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Specific SKU identifier when this agreement applies to an individual product. Null when agreement is at vendor or category level.']
  standard_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from purchase order placement to goods receipt at distribution center under normal operating conditions.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for shipments under this agreement.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for quantities in this agreement (each, case, pallet, weight, volume). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vmi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement operates under a VMI model where vendor manages inventory replenishment.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the procurement specialist who created this agreement record.']

  Note: 'Documented lead time and MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) agreements per vendor and product category or SKU. Captures standard lead time (days), expedited lead time, MOQ units, order increment, pack size, inner pack quantity, and seasonal lead time adjustments. Feeds Blue Yonder demand planning replenishment parameters and OTB calculations.'
}

Table supplier.vmi_config {
  vmi_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VMI configuration record. Primary key.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific DC or store location where this VMI configuration applies.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: VMI program performance metrics (stockout rates, inventory turns, fill rates) are tracked KPIs with defined SLA targets. Links VMI configuration to KPI definitions for VMI program effectiveness dashbo']
  lead_time_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.lead_time_agreement. Business justification: VMI configuration replenishment timing should reference the lead_time_agreement that defines vendor lead times and delivery windows. This ensures VMI replenishment calculations use contractually agree']
  product_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category covered by this VMI configuration. Null if configuration applies to a specific product.']
  reorder_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.reorder_policy. Business justification: VMI configurations operationalize reorder policies for vendor-managed SKUs. Linking config to policy enables automated replenishment execution, policy compliance monitoring, and vendor performance eva']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product or SKU covered by this VMI configuration. Null if configuration applies to a product category.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor who manages inventory under this VMI agreement.']
  vmi_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent VMI agreement that governs this configuration.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether retailer approval is required before the vendor can execute a replenishment order under this configuration.']
  auto_replenishment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is authorized to automatically generate and fulfill replenishment orders without retailer approval.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this VMI configuration for identification and reporting purposes.']
  config_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special instructions, exceptions, or business context for this VMI configuration.']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VMI configuration indicating whether it is operational.']
  consignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this VMI arrangement operates under a consignment model where the vendor retains ownership until sale or consumption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VMI configuration record was first created in the system.']
  data_sharing_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which inventory position and consumption data is shared with the vendor.']
  edi_transaction_set varchar [note: 'The EDI transaction set codes used for VMI data exchange (e.g., 852 Product Activity Data, 846 Inventory Inquiry).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this VMI configuration expires or is terminated. Null for open-ended configurations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this VMI configuration becomes active and operational.']
  excess_inventory_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial penalty amount charged to the vendor for maintaining inventory levels above the maximum threshold.']
  last_replenishment_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor last executed a replenishment order under this VMI configuration.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor last reviewed inventory levels under this VMI configuration.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VMI configuration record was last modified.']
  max_inventory_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum stock level that the vendor should maintain at the location.']
  min_inventory_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum stock level that triggers a replenishment action by the vendor.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next inventory review by the vendor under this VMI configuration.']
  ownership_transfer_point varchar [note: 'The point in the supply chain at which inventory ownership transfers from the vendor to the retailer.']
  performance_sla_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target service level percentage (e.g., 98.5%) that the vendor must maintain for in-stock availability under this VMI configuration.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'The inventory level at which the vendor initiates a replenishment order.']
  replenishment_frequency varchar [note: 'The cadence at which the vendor reviews and replenishes inventory under this VMI configuration.']
  replenishment_model varchar [note: 'The inventory replenishment strategy used by the vendor under this VMI configuration.']
  reporting_format varchar [note: 'The technical format used for sharing inventory and consumption reports with the vendor.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'The frequency in days at which the vendor reviews inventory levels to determine replenishment needs.']
  safety_stock_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'The buffer inventory quantity maintained to protect against stockouts due to demand variability or supply delays.']
  stockout_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The financial penalty amount charged to the vendor for each stockout incident under this VMI configuration.']
  target_inventory_level decimal(18,4) [note: 'The optimal stock level that the vendor aims to maintain under normal operating conditions.']
  threshold_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for all inventory threshold quantities (min, max, target, safety stock, reorder point). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|GAL|LTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_access_level varchar [note: 'The level of system access granted to the vendor for inventory visibility and replenishment management.']

  Note: 'Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) configuration defining the terms under which a vendor manages stock levels at designated DC or store locations. Captures min/max inventory thresholds, replenishment trigger rules, vendor access permissions, reporting frequency, ownership transfer point, and VMI program status. Supports automated replenishment and reduces stockout risk.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_item {
  vendor_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor-item relationship record. Primary key.']
  lead_time_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.lead_time_agreement. Business justification: Vendor_item captures item-specific lead times and MOQ values that should be governed by a lead_time_agreement. Adding this FK normalizes lead time and MOQ data to the authoritative agreement record. R']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.po_line. Business justification: Vendor_item defines vendor\'s catalog entry (pack size, cost, lead time). PO lines reference vendor_item_number (denormalized). Real-world procurement systems link PO lines to vendor item master for co']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal product master record. Links vendor catalog to internal SKU (Stock Keeping Unit).']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor providing this item. Links to the vendor master record.']
  case_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual units contained in one vendor case pack. Used for ordering and receiving calculations.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cost_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the current unit cost is effective. Used for cost change tracking and historical cost analysis.']
  cost_expiration_date date [note: 'The date through which the current unit cost remains valid. Null indicates open-ended pricing.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the item is manufactured or produced. Required for customs, tariffs, and trade compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this vendor-item record was first created in the system.']
  dsd_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item can be delivered directly to stores by the vendor, bypassing the DC (Distribution Center). Common for beverages, bread, and snacks.']
  ean varchar [note: '13-digit European Article Number barcode identifier used internationally for retail product identification.']
  edi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchase orders and ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) transactions for this vendor-item are transmitted via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date through which this vendor-item relationship remains active. Null indicates an open-ended relationship.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this vendor-item relationship became active and available for ordering.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number assigned by GS1 for worldwide product identification. Can be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits.']
  inner_pack_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual units contained in one inner pack within a case. Used for shelf-ready packaging and store replenishment.']
  last_cost_change_date date [note: 'The date when the unit cost was last modified. Used for cost trend analysis and vendor negotiation tracking.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'The date when this vendor-item combination was last ordered. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete vendor relationships.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this vendor-item record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information about this vendor-item relationship, such as special handling instructions or ordering restrictions.']
  pallet_hi varchar [note: 'Number of layers (tiers) stacked on a pallet. Part of the TI/HI pallet configuration used in warehouse and DC operations.']
  pallet_ti varchar [note: 'Number of cases per layer on a pallet. Part of the TI/HI pallet configuration used in warehouse and DC (Distribution Center) operations.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred source for this item when multiple vendors supply the same product. Used in automated sourcing decisions.']
  private_label_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this item is a private label (store brand) product manufactured exclusively for the retailer by this vendor.']
  seasonal_lead_time_adjustment_days varchar [note: 'Additional lead time days required during peak seasonal periods (e.g., holiday season). Added to standard lead time for seasonal planning.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The cost per individual unit charged by the vendor. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculations and margin analysis. Excludes freight and other charges.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier used primarily in North America for retail scanning at POS (Point of Sale).']
  vendor_item_category varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s product category classification for this item. May differ from internal merchandising hierarchy.']
  vendor_item_description varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s description of the item as it appears in their catalog. May differ from internal product description.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s unique part number or catalog identifier for this item. Used in purchase orders and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transactions.']
  vendor_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this vendor-item relationship. Active items are available for ordering; discontinued items are being phased out.']
  vendor_pack_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of how the vendor packages this item (e.g., case pack, master carton, pallet). Defines the vendor\'s standard shipping unit.']
  vmi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor-item combination is managed under a VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) agreement where the vendor controls replenishment.']

  Note: 'Vendor-specific item catalog linking vendor part numbers, UPCs, GTINs, and EANs to internal SKUs. Captures vendor item number, vendor pack configuration, case pack quantity, inner pack, pallet TI/HI, unit cost, cost effective date, country of origin, and item status. Includes lead time and MOQ agreements per vendor-item combination: standard lead time (days), expedited lead time, MOQ units, order increment, pack size, inner pack quantity, seasonal lead time adjustments, and category-level defaults. Serves as the cross-reference between vendor catalogs and the internal product master, and feeds Blue Yonder demand planning replenishment parameters and OTB calculations.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_allowance {
  vendor_allowance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor allowance record. Primary key.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: Vendor allowances are negotiated and tracked by the buyer responsible for that vendor. OTB budget reconciliation, margin planning, and vendor negotiation workflows require buyer-level allowance visibi']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Vendor allowances (co-op marketing funds, promotional allowances) directly fund specific retail marketing campaigns. Retailers must track which campaign consumed which vendor allowance for accrual rec']
  kpi_value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_value. Business justification: Allowance accrual rates, settlement rates, and disputed amounts are financial KPIs tracked by vendor and period. Links allowance transactions to KPI measurement for trade spend analytics and vendor fu']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the allowance to a specific promotional campaign or event if the allowance is promotion-specific.']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Vendor allowances frequently fund specific promotional offers (e.g., temporary price reductions, BOGOs) rather than entire campaigns. Merchandising teams reconcile allowance accruals and claims agains']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Vendor allowances (scan-based trading, promotional funding, volume rebates) are frequently SKU-specific or apply to defined SKU lists. Business process: promotional cost accounting, margin analysis, a']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.purchase_order. Business justification: Allowances (rebates, volume discounts, promotional funding) accrue based on qualifying PO spend. Retail finance teams reconcile allowance claims to PO history for accrual accuracy, settlement validati']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_contract. Business justification: Vendor_allowance currently has contract_reference as a STRING field. Trade allowances, co-op funds, and promotional funding are typically defined in vendor contracts. Normalizing this to a FK enables ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor providing the allowance. Links to the vendor master record.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the allowance is accrued in financial systems: invoice-based (at purchase), sales-based (at POS), volume-based (cumulative tier), time-based (periodic), or event-based (specific milestone).']
  accrued_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of allowances earned but not yet settled or paid by the vendor.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary value of the allowance if structured as a lump sum payment or credit.']
  allowance_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the allowance terms, purpose, and business rationale.']
  allowance_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or agreement number for the vendor allowance as referenced in contracts and supplier communications.']
  allowance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to qualifying purchase volume or sales to calculate the allowance value.']
  allowance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor allowance agreement.']
  allowance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor allowance mechanism: off-invoice (deducted at purchase), bill-back (claimed post-sale), scan-based (tied to POS data), slotting fee (shelf placement), co-op advertising (marketing fund), or volume rebate (tier-based discount).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status for the allowance setup or claim.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the procurement or finance manager who approved the allowance agreement or claim.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allowance was approved in the system.']
  claimed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of allowances formally claimed from the vendor through bill-back or settlement process.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center or profit center code associated with the allowance for internal financial allocation and P&L reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor allowance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allowance monetary values.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of allowances under dispute or chargeback contention with the vendor.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor allowance agreement expires or is no longer eligible for new accruals.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor allowance agreement becomes active and eligible for accrual.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the vendor allowance is posted for financial reporting and cost of goods sold (COGS) adjustment.']
  last_claim_date date [note: 'Most recent date when an allowance claim was submitted to the vendor.']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Most recent date when the vendor made a payment or credit against the allowance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the vendor allowance record was most recently modified.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase value required to qualify for the allowance.']
  minimum_purchase_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity (MOQ) in units required to qualify for the allowance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or clarifications regarding the allowance agreement or settlement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the vendor settles the allowance: credit memo, check, electronic funds transfer (EFT), invoice offset, or promotional credit.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated terms for allowance settlement timing and conditions, such as net 30, net 60, or upon claim submission.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Merchandise category or product line to which the allowance applies, used for category management and gross margin return on investment (GMROI) analysis.']
  qualifying_condition varchar [note: 'Business rules and criteria that must be met to earn or claim the allowance, such as minimum order quantity (MOQ), purchase volume thresholds, promotional participation, or compliance requirements.']
  redemption_end_date date [note: 'Final date by which accrued allowances must be claimed or they expire.']
  redemption_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date when accrued allowances can be claimed or redeemed from the vendor.']
  settled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of allowances paid or credited by the vendor.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current state of financial settlement for the allowance: pending claim, partially paid, fully paid, under dispute, or written off.']
  sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of specific SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) eligible for the allowance, if restricted to specific items.']

  Note: 'Vendor-funded trade allowances, co-op advertising funds, slotting fees, promotional funding, and volume rebates negotiated with suppliers. Tracks allowance type (off-invoice, bill-back, scan-based), allowance amount or percentage, qualifying conditions, accrual method, redemption period, and settlement status. Critical for net landed cost and P&L accuracy.'
}

Business justification: Vendor onboarding workflow tracking requires linking the associate who modified the request for audit trail, workload analysis, and process improvement. Replaces denormalized modified_by_user text fie']
  third_party_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.third_party_assessment. Business justification: New vendor onboarding requires third-party compliance assessments (data security, labor practices, ethical sourcing, certifications) before approval. Retail supplier onboarding process mandates linkin']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor master record being onboarded. Links to the vendor entity once the onboarding is approved and the vendor record is created.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mbe|wbe|vbe|dbe|lgbtbe|sdvosb|hubzone|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The 9-digit DUNS number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for unique identification of the vendor\'s business entity.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this onboarding request record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from purchase order placement to delivery at the distribution center or store, as committed by the vendor.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the vendor onboarding process.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application|document_review|compliance_check|edi_setup|item_setup|first_po|completed|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|in_review|pending_info|approved|rejected|on_hold|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code agreed upon with the vendor (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30) defining the payment due date and any early payment discounts.']
