[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: birthday|anniversary|win_back|rfm_segment|tier_upgrade|milestone|behavioral|seasonal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this offer record.']

  Note: 'Personalized or segment-targeted offer assigned to a loyalty member or member cohort, distinct from promotion-domain deals which are broad-market. Captures offer name, offer type (bonus points, discount, free item, early access, experiential reward), target membership or segment, offer trigger (birthday, anniversary, win-back, RFM segment, tier upgrade celebration), points multiplier or discount value, minimum spend requirement, offer start/end dates, channel applicability, presentation priority, and offer status (pending, presented, activated, redeemed, expired, declined). SSOT for member-level offer assignments — the promotion domain owns the broad promotional mechanics while this product owns the 1:1 or segment-level loyalty offer lifecycle. Redemption of offers is recorded in the redemption product with type discriminator.'
}

The engagement_campaign.budget_amount is denormalized budget data']

Business justification: Loyalty engagement campaigns are often sub-campaigns within broader marketing campaigns in retail operations. Linking enables unified campaign performance reporting, consolidated budget tracking, and ']
  member_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target member segment for this campaign. Defines the audience eligible to participate based on RFM, tier, behavior, or demographic criteria.']
  privacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_assessment. Business justification: Retail loyalty engagement campaigns processing member personal data require GDPR/CCPA privacy impact assessments. Privacy teams must evaluate campaign data flows, retention, and consent mechanisms bef']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Loyalty engagement campaigns frequently promote specific retail promotional campaigns to members (e.g., \'Members: early access to Summer Sale\') - strategic coordination requires linking engagement cam']
  target_store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Campaigns target member segments within specific store clusters (urban vs suburban, high-income vs value) to align offers with cluster demographics and shopping behaviors. Standard retail loyalty prac']
  target_store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Campaigns target members by preferred/enrollment store for in-store activation events, localized offers, store-specific promotions. Retail loyalty operations execute geo-targeted campaigns tied to spe']
  actual_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of members who enrolled or opted-in to the campaign. Updated in real-time or batch as enrollments occur.']
  actual_incremental_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual incremental spend generated by campaign participants, measured as the difference between campaign-period spend and baseline spend. Key ROI metric.']
  actual_participation_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of members who completed qualifying actions and actively participated in the campaign. Used to calculate participation rate.']
  actual_participation_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual participation rate achieved, calculated as (actual_participation_count / actual_enrollment_count) * 100. Measured against target for campaign success assessment.']
  actual_tier_upgrade_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of members who upgraded to a higher loyalty tier during or as a result of the campaign. Measured against target for tier challenge campaigns.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign was approved by the appropriate authority (e.g., marketing director, finance). Null if campaign is still in draft status.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved the campaign. Used for audit trail and governance.']
  bonus_points_amount varchar [note: 'Fixed number of bonus points awarded to members who complete campaign qualifying actions. Null if campaign does not award fixed bonus points.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the campaign, used in marketing materials, tracking URLs, and operational reporting. Externally visible code.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the campaign objectives, mechanics, and value proposition. Used for internal alignment and external member communication.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the engagement campaign, used in internal reporting and customer-facing communications.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign. Draft indicates planning phase; approved indicates ready for launch; active indicates currently running; paused indicates temporarily suspended; completed indicates ended successfully; archived indicates historical record; cancelled indicates terminated before completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|active|paused|completed|archived|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign by its primary objective and mechanic. Enrollment drive focuses on new member acquisition; double-points event accelerates points accrual; tier challenge incentivizes tier upgrades; birthday campaign celebrates member milestones; reactivation targets dormant members; streak bonus rewards consecutive engagement; spend milestone rewards cumulative spend thresholds; referral incentive drives member-get-member growth. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enrollment_drive|double_points_event|tier_challenge|birthday_campaign|reactivation|streak_bonus|spend_milestone|referral_incentive — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel_app_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-app notifications or banners are used as a communication channel for this campaign. True if app notification is part of the channel mix.']
  channel_direct_mail_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical direct mail is used as a communication channel for this campaign. True if direct mail is part of the channel mix.']
  channel_email_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether email is used as a communication channel for this campaign. True if email is part of the channel mix.']
  channel_in_store_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether in-store signage, POS displays, or associate-driven communication are used for this campaign. True if in-store is part of the channel mix.']
  channel_push_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether mobile app push notifications are used as a communication channel for this campaign. True if push is part of the channel mix.']
  channel_sms_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SMS is used as a communication channel for this campaign. True if SMS is part of the channel mix.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign concludes and members can no longer participate. Nullable for evergreen campaigns with no defined end date.']
  expected_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'Projected number of members expected to enroll or opt-in to the campaign. Used for capacity planning and success measurement.']
  expected_participation_count varchar [note: 'Projected number of members expected to actively participate in the campaign by completing qualifying actions. Used for ROI forecasting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was last modified. Updated on any change to campaign attributes. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  opt_in_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members must explicitly opt-in to participate in the campaign. True if opt-in is required; false if all eligible members are automatically enrolled.']
  personalization_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether campaign communications and offers are personalized based on member preferences, purchase history, or behavioral data. True if personalization is enabled.']
  points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points earning rate during the campaign. For example, 2.0 for double points, 3.0 for triple points. Null if campaign does not modify points earning.']
  qualifying_spend_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum spend amount required for a member to qualify for campaign rewards. Null if campaign has no spend threshold requirement.']
  qualifying_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of transactions required for a member to qualify for campaign rewards. Null if campaign has no transaction count requirement.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the campaign becomes active and members can begin participating. Campaign mechanics and offers become effective from this date.']
  target_incremental_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target incremental spend expected to be generated by campaign participants above their baseline spend. Used to calculate campaign ROI.']
  target_participation_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target participation rate expressed as a percentage of enrolled members. Key performance indicator for campaign effectiveness.']
  target_tier_upgrade_count varchar [note: 'Target number of members expected to upgrade to a higher loyalty tier as a result of campaign participation. Relevant for tier challenge campaigns.']

  Note: 'Master record for loyalty engagement campaigns designed to drive member activation, retention, or win-back — distinct from promotion-domain campaigns which focus on price/deal mechanics. Captures campaign name, campaign type (enrollment drive, double-points event, tier challenge, birthday campaign, reactivation, streak bonus, spend milestone), target segment reference, program association, campaign start/end dates, budget allocation, expected enrollment/participation, channel mix (email, SMS, push, in-store signage, app notification), success metrics (participation rate, incremental spend, tier upgrades), and campaign status (draft, approved, active, paused, completed, archived). SSOT for loyalty-specific campaign definitions — coordinates with member_segment for targeting and member_offer for individual offer distribution.'
}

Retail operations track interaction location at department level for associ']

  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who participated in this clienteling interaction.']

Adding offer_id FK allows tracking which offers were presented or discussed during high-touch interactions. No columns']

  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.web_session.   currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the interaction value amount is denominated.']

  interaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the clienteling interaction, used for tracking and reference in customer service and reporting contexts.']
  interaction_outcome varchar [note: 'The business result or action taken as a consequence of the clienteling interaction, used to measure interaction effectiveness and conversion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_made|appointment_booked|wishlist_updated|product_reserved|no_action|referral_provided|feedback_collected|complaint_resolved — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the clienteling interaction, tracking progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the clienteling interaction began, capturing the exact moment of engagement for detailed analytics and associate performance tracking.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store_consultation|outbound_call|personal_styling_session|vip_event_invitation|product_launch_preview|thank_you_note|anniversary_outreach|birthday_outreach|follow_up_call|virtual_consultation|email_outreach|text_message_outreach — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this clienteling interaction record was most recently updated, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  member_preferences_discussed varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing customer preferences, style interests, size information, color preferences, brand affinities, and other personalization details discussed during the interaction.']
  next_appointment_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next clienteling appointment or interaction with this customer, supporting relationship continuity and engagement planning.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) rating provided by the customer following the interaction, on a scale of 0-10, measuring likelihood to recommend based on the personalized service experience.']
  products_purchased varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) codes for products actually purchased as a result of this interaction, enabling recommendation-to-purchase conversion tracking.']
  products_recommended varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) codes for products recommended to the customer during the interaction, supporting product affinity and recommendation effectiveness analysis.']
  purchase_made_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the clienteling interaction resulted in an immediate purchase transaction, supporting conversion rate analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system or application from which this clienteling interaction record originated, supporting data lineage and integration traceability.']
  total_interaction_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of purchases made as a direct result of this clienteling interaction, measured in the store operating currency, used to calculate clienteling Return on Investment (ROI).']
  vip_tier_at_interaction varchar [note: 'The loyalty program tier or VIP status of the member at the time of the interaction, enabling tier-specific engagement analysis and service level tracking.']

  Note: 'Records personalized high-touch interactions between store associates and loyalty members, primarily for top-tier and high-value customers in department store and specialty retail contexts. Captures interaction type (in-store consultation, outbound call, personal styling session, VIP event invitation, product launch preview, thank-you note, anniversary outreach), associate reference, store location, date/duration, member preferences discussed, products recommended (SKU references), follow-up actions scheduled with due dates, interaction outcome (purchase made, appointment booked, wishlist updated, no action), and sentiment/satisfaction indicator. Supports relationship-based selling strategies, drives incremental CLTV through personalized service, and provides associate performance data for workforce domain integration.'
}

Table loyalty.member_segment {
  member_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty member segment. Primary key.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program this segment belongs to. Enables multi-program segmentation strategies.']
  marketing_audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Retail operations synchronize loyalty member segments with marketing audience segments for coordinated targeting across channels. Marketers need to activate loyalty segments in paid media, email, and ']
  segment_owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Member segments require business owner associates for strategy ownership, campaign planning, and segment performance accountability. Replaces denormalized segment_owner string with proper FK for organ']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this segment for production use. Null for segments not requiring approval. Supports governance and compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment was approved for production use. Null for segments not requiring approval. Supports governance audit trail.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign members to this segment: rule-based (deterministic business rules), ml_model (machine learning model prediction), manual (analyst-driven assignment), hybrid (combination of automated and manual).']
  average_annual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average annual spend (trailing 12 months) of members in this segment. Used for spend-based segmentation validation and campaign ROI forecasting. Currency implied by program configuration.']
  average_ltv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) of members in this segment. Calculated metric used for segment value ranking and ROI analysis. Currency implied by program configuration.']
  average_points_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average loyalty points balance across all members in this segment. Used for points liability forecasting and segment value analysis.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale and expected outcomes for creating this segment. Documents strategic intent, target KPIs, and success criteria. Supports segment portfolio governance and ROI tracking.']
  campaign_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether members in this segment are eligible for targeted marketing campaigns. False may indicate test segments or internal analytical segments not intended for outbound communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment definition was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  definition_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules or criteria defining segment membership. May be SQL-like expression, natural language rule description, or reference to external rule engine configuration. Examples: \'Points earned > 5000 in last 90 days AND redemption count >= 2\', \'RFM score R>=4 AND F>=3\'.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this segment definition is no longer active. Null for open-ended segments. Supports time-bound seasonal or campaign-specific segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this segment definition becomes active and eligible for member assignment and campaign targeting. Supports future-dated segment launches.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether members can belong to only this segment (true) or can be assigned to multiple segments simultaneously (false). Supports mutually exclusive vs. overlapping segmentation strategies.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent segment membership refresh execution. Used to monitor data freshness and SLA compliance.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Member lifecycle stage classification: new (recently enrolled), active (regular activity), engaged (high interaction), at-risk (declining activity), lapsed (inactive period), dormant (extended inactivity), win-back (re-engagement target), churned (permanently inactive). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|active|engaged|at_risk|lapsed|dormant|win_back|churned — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current count of loyalty members assigned to this segment. Updated with each segment refresh cycle. Used for campaign sizing and ROI forecasting.']
  minimum_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum confidence threshold (0.0000 to 1.0000) required for ML-based segment assignment. Members with confidence scores below this threshold are not assigned to the segment. Null for rule-based segments.']
  ml_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the ML model used for segment assignment (e.g., model name, version, registry URI). Null for non-ML-based segments. Enables model lineage tracking and reproducibility.']
  ml_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ML model used for segment assignment. Supports model versioning and A/B testing of segmentation strategies.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this segment definition. Supports audit trail and change management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment definition was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  next_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next segment membership refresh. Null for on-demand segments. Supports proactive monitoring and capacity planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments about this segment. Used for documenting business context, performance observations, or operational considerations.']
  offer_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether members in this segment are eligible for personalized offers and promotions. Supports offer targeting and exclusion rules.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for segment assignment when a member qualifies for multiple segments. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve segment overlap conflicts.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which segment membership is recalculated and updated: real-time (streaming), hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or on-demand (manual trigger). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: real_time|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|on_demand — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment within the program (e.g., HIGH_VALUE_FREQ, AT_RISK_30D). Used in campaign targeting and reporting.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the segment purpose, target audience characteristics, and intended use cases for marketing and engagement strategies.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the segment (e.g., High-Value Frequent Shoppers, At-Risk Members). Used in business reporting and campaign management interfaces.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the segment definition: active (in use for targeting), inactive (temporarily disabled), archived (historical reference only), draft (under development), pending approval (awaiting business sign-off).']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology: RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) based, spend-based, lifecycle stage (new/active/at-risk/lapsed/win-back), engagement-based, redemption behavior, or channel preference.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business tags or labels for segment categorization and search (e.g., high-value, seasonal, test, omnichannel). Supports flexible segment discovery and reporting.']
  target_member_count varchar [note: 'Target or expected member count for this segment based on business planning. Used to monitor segment size drift and validate segmentation model performance.']
  tier_qualification_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this segment is used for loyalty tier qualification or upgrade/downgrade decisions. True for segments that drive tier movement logic.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this segment definition. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Defines loyalty-specific behavioral segments and manages member-to-segment assignments for targeted offers, campaign eligibility, and tier management. Captures segment name, type (RFM-based, spend-based, lifecycle stage: new/active/at-risk/lapsed/win-back), definition criteria (rules or ML model reference), program association, segment size/member count, refresh frequency and last refresh timestamp, and individual member assignments with assignment method (rule-based, manual, ML-model), confidence score, effective/expiry dates, and override flag. Distinct from customer domain demographic segments — these are loyalty-program-specific behavioral cohorts derived exclusively from program activity data (points velocity, redemption patterns, engagement frequency).'
}

Table loyalty.partner_program {
  partner_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the coalition or partner loyalty arrangement. Primary key.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent loyalty program that this partner arrangement is associated with. Links to the core loyalty program master.']
  third_party_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.third_party_assessment. Business justification: Partner programs sharing customer loyalty data or processing payments require third-party compliance assessments for data protection, PCI-DSS, and contractual obligations. Retail compliance teams asse']
  api_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Base URL for the partner API endpoint used for real-time transaction posting and member validation. Confidential technical integration detail.']
  auto_enrollment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether loyalty program members are automatically enrolled in this partner program or must opt-in explicitly.']
  contract_renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the partner program contract is scheduled for renewal or renegotiation. Used for relationship management and planning.']
  cost_per_point_earned decimal(10,6) [note: 'Cost to the retailer for each loyalty point earned by members at the partner location. Used for financial liability tracking and program ROI analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner program record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  data_sharing_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the legal data sharing agreement governing member data exchange between the retailer and partner. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  earn_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which partner transactions are denominated for points accrual calculation.']
  earn_rate_multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base loyalty points when members make purchases at the partner. For example, 1.5 means members earn 1.5x points per dollar spent at partner locations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the partner program arrangement expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended partnerships.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the partner program arrangement becomes active and members can begin earning or redeeming points with the partner.']
  exclusion_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules defining product categories, transaction types, or conditions that are excluded from points earning or redemption at the partner (e.g., gift cards, alcohol, tobacco).']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or countries where the partner program is available to members. Pipe-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region descriptors.']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration approach used to exchange transaction and member data between the retailer and partner systems.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner program record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial reconciliation and settlement between the retailer and partner for points earned and redeemed.']
  maximum_earn_per_transaction decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cap on the number of loyalty points that can be earned in a single transaction at the partner. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  member_communication_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner is permitted to send marketing communications directly to loyalty program members under the data sharing agreement.']
  member_eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules or criteria defining which loyalty program members are eligible to participate in this partner program (e.g., tier requirements, geographic restrictions, age limits).']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum purchase amount required at the partner location for the transaction to qualify for loyalty points accrual. Nullable if no minimum applies.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this partner program record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code identifying the partner program for integration and operational reference. Used in API calls, EDI transactions, and partner communications.']
  partner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact at the partner organization. Used for operational communications and issue resolution.']
  partner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact at the partner organization responsible for the loyalty program relationship.']
  partner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact at the partner organization. Used for operational communications and issue resolution.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the coalition partner organization (e.g., airline, hotel chain, fuel retailer, financial institution).']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner program arrangement. Controls whether members can earn or redeem points with this partner.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner organization by industry vertical or business category. Determines applicable earn and redemption rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: airline|hotel|fuel_retailer|financial_institution|restaurant|entertainment|travel|ecommerce|telecom|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  performance_sla_target varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement (SLA) performance targets for the partner integration, such as API uptime percentage, transaction processing time, or data accuracy thresholds.']
  promotional_bonus_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner program is eligible for promotional bonus point campaigns and limited-time earn rate multipliers.']
  redeem_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which partner redemptions are valued and settled.']
  redeem_rate_multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied when members redeem loyalty points for partner rewards or services. Determines the value conversion rate from points to partner currency.']
  revenue_per_point_redeemed decimal(10,6) [note: 'Revenue received by the retailer for each loyalty point redeemed by members for partner rewards. Used for financial settlement and program ROI analysis.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the currency in which financial settlements between the retailer and partner are conducted.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which financial settlements for points earned or redeemed are reconciled and settled between the retailer and partner.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the partner program arrangement, as specified in the contract.']

  Note: 'Master record for coalition or partner loyalty arrangements where members can earn or redeem points with external partners (airlines, hotels, fuel retailers, financial partners). Captures partner name, partner type, program association, earn rate at partner, redeem rate at partner, data sharing agreement reference, integration method (API, batch EDI), partner program code, and active status. Supports omnichannel loyalty recognition beyond the retailer\'s own touchpoints.'
}

Table loyalty.partner_transaction {
  partner_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner transaction record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign or promotion that drove this partner transaction, if applicable.']
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who earned or redeemed points through this partner transaction.']
  original_transaction_partner_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original partner transaction that this record reverses or adjusts.']
  partner_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the coalition partner program through which this transaction was executed.']
  settlement_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Partner transactions require GL account mapping for settlement accounting. Partner commissions, points purchases from partners, and reconciliation entries all flow through specific GL accounts. Requir']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the transaction at the partner location before points conversion.']
  bonus_points decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional promotional or campaign-driven bonus points awarded on top of base points for this transaction.']
  commission_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the partner commission amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner transaction record was first created in the loyalty system.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction is under dispute between the retailer and coalition partner.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the transaction is flagged for dispute resolution.']
  member_tier_at_transaction varchar [note: 'Loyalty program tier level of the member at the time of this transaction, used for tier-based points calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this partner transaction for operational or audit purposes.']
  partner_category varchar [note: 'Business category or industry vertical of the coalition partner (e.g., travel, dining, fuel, entertainment).']
  partner_commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commission or interchange fee paid to or received from the coalition partner for this transaction.']
  partner_file_batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner data file or Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) batch that contained this transaction record.']
  partner_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific partner location or outlet where the transaction occurred.']
  partner_merchant_code varchar [note: 'Unique merchant identifier assigned by the partner organization for transaction tracking.']
  points_awarded decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points awarded to the member for this partner transaction. Positive for earn transactions, zero for redemptions.']
  points_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the points awarded in this transaction will expire if not redeemed.']
  points_multiplier decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the base points calculation for promotional or tier-based bonus points.']
  points_redeemed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points deducted from the member account for this partner redemption transaction. Positive for redemptions, zero for earn transactions.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the partner transaction was successfully reconciled with partner records.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the reconciliation process between internal records and partner-provided transaction data.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External transaction reference number provided by the coalition partner system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for transaction reversal or cancellation, if applicable.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction was reversed or cancelled.']
  settlement_batch_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial settlement batch in which this partner transaction was reconciled and settled.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the partner transaction was financially settled between the retailer and coalition partner.']
  transaction_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the partner transaction was initiated by the member.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the partner transaction amount.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction occurred at the partner location or platform.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the partner transaction in the reconciliation workflow.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the transaction was executed at the partner system, including time zone information.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of partner transaction indicating whether points were earned, redeemed, reversed, or adjusted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner transaction record was last modified in the loyalty system.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of points earned or redeemed by a loyalty member through a coalition partner. Captures membership reference, partner program reference, transaction date, transaction amount at partner, points awarded or deducted, partner transaction reference number, processing status (pending, confirmed, rejected), and settlement batch reference. Enables reconciliation of partner-sourced points and supports coalition billing.'
}

Table loyalty.referral {
  referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the referral transaction. Primary key for the referral data product.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store associate who facilitated the referral or enrollment. Used for associate performance tracking and commission attribution.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engagement campaign associated with this referral. Links referral activity to specific marketing initiatives and promotional periods.']
  first_purchase_order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Referral programs require linking referee\'s qualifying first order to automate reward fulfillment, validate qualification criteria, detect fraud, and measure referral campaign ROI—standard retail loya']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the referral was initiated or where the referred individual enrolled. Null for digital-only referrals.']
  originating_referral_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on referral (originating_referral_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who was referred. May be null if the referred individual has not yet enrolled or been matched to a customer record.']
  referral_loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program member who initiated the referral. Links to the member who is advocating for the program.']
  referral_referred_member_membership_loyalty_membership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty membership created for the referred individual upon successful enrollment. Null until the referred individual completes enrollment.']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.web_session. Business justification: Referral conversions often occur through web sessions (referral link clicks, enrollment flows); tracking the session enables attribution of referral-driven online enrollments and purchases, referral c']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the referral was shared by the referring member. Tracks the medium used for advocacy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|social_media|unique_link|in_store|mobile_app|direct_mail — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral. Tracks progression from initial referral through enrollment, qualification, and reward fulfillment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this referral record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the referred individual successfully enrolled in the loyalty program. Null if enrollment has not yet occurred.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the referral code or offer expires. After this date, the referral is no longer valid for qualification or reward issuance.']
  first_purchase_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the first purchase made by the referred individual. Used to evaluate referral quality and program ROI.']
  first_purchase_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the first purchase amount. Supports multi-currency loyalty program operations.']
  first_purchase_date date [note: 'Date when the referred individual completed their first purchase. Used to track conversion from enrollment to active customer status.']
  fraud_detection_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score from fraud detection algorithms indicating the likelihood of fraudulent referral activity. Higher scores indicate greater fraud risk.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral has been flagged for potential fraudulent activity. True if fraud is suspected, false otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this referral record was most recently updated. Tracks the latest change to any attribute for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context about the referral. May include special circumstances, customer feedback, or operational observations.']
  qualification_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the business rules that must be satisfied for the referral to qualify for rewards. Examples include first purchase threshold, enrollment completion, or minimum spend amount.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the referral met all qualification criteria for reward eligibility. Null if qualification has not been achieved.']
  qualification_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the referred individual has satisfied all qualification criteria. True when qualification is complete, false otherwise.']
  referee_reward_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary rewards granted to the referred individual. Null for non-monetary rewards.']
  referee_reward_date date [note: 'Date when the reward was granted to the referred individual. Null if the reward has not yet been issued.']
  referee_reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of reward granted to the referred individual upon enrollment or first purchase. Incentivizes new member acquisition.']
  referee_reward_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary or points value of the reward granted to the referred individual. Denominated in points or currency depending on reward type.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the referring member for tracking referral activity. Used by the referred individual during enrollment or first purchase.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date when the referral was initiated by the referring member. Represents the business event timestamp for the referral action.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Detailed source attribution for the referral. May include specific social media platform, email campaign identifier, or in-store associate identifier.']
  referral_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the referral was created in the system. Captures the exact moment of referral initiation for audit and sequencing purposes.']
  referrer_reward_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary rewards granted to the referring member. Null for non-monetary rewards such as points or tier credits.']
  referrer_reward_date date [note: 'Date when the reward was granted to the referring member. Null if the reward has not yet been issued.']
  referrer_reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of reward granted to the referring member upon successful referral qualification. Defines the incentive structure for advocacy.']
  referrer_reward_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary or points value of the reward granted to the referring member. Denominated in points or currency depending on reward type.']
  viral_coefficient_contribution decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated metric representing this referrals contribution to program viral growth. Measures the multiplier effect of member advocacy on program enrollment.']

  Note: 'Tracks member-to-member referral activity within loyalty programs. Captures referring member, referred individual, referral channel (in-store, email, social media, unique link), referral code, referral date, conversion status (pending, enrolled, qualified, rewarded), qualification criteria (e.g., referred member must make first purchase), reward type for referrer (bonus points, tier credit, gift card), reward type for referee (welcome bonus), referral campaign association, and expiry date. Supports refer-a-friend programs, social advocacy tracking, and program growth attribution. Drives referral-sourced enrollment KPIs and program viral coefficient measurement.'
}

Table loyalty.campaign_storefront_deployment {
  campaign_storefront_deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign-storefront deployment record. Primary key.']
  engagement_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty engagement campaign being deployed']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital storefront where the campaign is deployed']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign-storefront deployment record was created in the system.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this campaign deployment on this storefront. Scheduled indicates pre-launch; active indicates currently running; paused indicates temporarily suspended; completed indicates successfully concluded; cancelled indicates terminated before completion.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this deployment record was last modified, typically updated as performance metrics are refreshed.']
  storefront_budget_allocation decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the campaign\'s total budget allocated to this specific storefront, used for channel-specific promotional spend, incentives, or advertising.']
  storefront_conversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The conversion rate (percentage) of enrolled members who became active participants on this specific storefront, calculated as (storefront_participation_count / storefront_enrollment_count) * 100.']
  storefront_end_date date [note: 'The date when this campaign concluded on this specific storefront. May differ from the campaign\'s global end_date to accommodate storefront-specific timing strategies.']
  storefront_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'The number of members who enrolled or opted into the campaign through this specific storefront channel.']
  storefront_launch_date date [note: 'The date when this campaign was launched on this specific storefront. May differ from the campaign\'s global start_date to accommodate storefront-specific timing strategies.']
  storefront_participation_count varchar [note: 'The number of members who actively participated in the campaign (completed qualifying actions) through this specific storefront channel.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the deployment of a loyalty engagement campaign to a specific digital storefront. It captures storefront-specific campaign configuration, launch timing, budget allocation, and performance metrics that exist only in the context of this campaign-storefront pairing. Each record links one engagement_campaign to one storefront with attributes tracking how that campaign performs on that specific channel.. Existence Justification: Loyalty engagement campaigns are deployed across multiple digital storefronts simultaneously as part of omnichannel marketing strategies, and each storefront hosts multiple campaigns concurrently. The business actively manages campaign-storefront deployments as operational entities, tracking storefront-specific launch timing, budget allocation, enrollment counts, participation metrics, and conversion rates. This is not an analytical correlation but an operational business process where campaign managers deploy campaigns TO storefronts and monitor performance BY storefront.'
}

Table ecommerce.storefront {
  storefront_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each digital storefront or online channel record. Primary key for the storefront master data product within the e-commerce domain.']
  catalog_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product catalog assigned to this storefront within Salesforce Commerce Cloud or the PIM system. Determines which assortment of SKUs, categories, and product content is surfaced to customers on this channel. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS) assortment planning and Informatica MDM product golden records.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Storefront configuration defines primary fulfillment node for ship-from-store and BOPIS operations. Business process: storefront fulfillment network assignment, default inventory source for online ord']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Storefronts often represent specific physical stores\' online presence for store-specific inventory visibility, local pricing, BOPIS fulfillment, and "shop my store" features. Real business process: st']
  pci_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pci_assessment. Business justification: Each storefront accepting card payments requires PCI compliance assessment. Retail must track which assessment covers each storefront\'s payment processing to maintain valid Attestation of Compliance (']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price book or pricing list assigned to this storefront, sourced from Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) or Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Governs the base retail prices (AUR — Average Unit Retail) and EDLP (Everyday Low Price) rules applied to products displayed on this channel. Critical for channel-specific pricing strategy execution.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each storefront (site, channel, locale) maps to a profit center for P&L reporting, segment analysis, management accounting, and channel profitability tracking in retail finance systems.']
  analytics_tracking_code varchar [note: 'The web analytics platform tracking identifier assigned to this storefront (e.g., Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID \'G-XXXXXXXXXX\' or Adobe Analytics Report Suite ID). Used to attribute digital customer journey events, CTR (Click-Through Rate), CR (Conversion Rate), and session data to the correct storefront channel. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary analytics configuration.']
  ccpa_opt_out_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront has activated the CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) \'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information\' opt-out mechanism, required for storefronts serving California consumers. Governs data sharing controls and consumer rights workflows.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the digital commerce channel operated by this storefront. Segments storefronts by their primary consumer interface: web (desktop/browser), mobile_app (iOS/Android native), marketplace (third-party platform such as Amazon or eBay), social_commerce (shoppable social media), or dark_store (fulfillment-only digital channel). Drives channel-specific analytics including CTR, CR, and GMV reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the primary geographic market served by this storefront (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'GBR\'). Used for regulatory compliance scoping (GDPR, CCPA, FTC), tax jurisdiction assignment, and regional assortment planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this storefront record was first created in the system of record (Salesforce Commerce Cloud or Informatica MDM). Represents the audit trail creation event for data governance and lineage tracking purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transactional currency for this storefront (e.g., \'USD\', \'CAD\', \'EUR\'). Governs pricing display, checkout totals, AOV calculations, and GMV reporting. Aligns with Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) currency configuration.']
  customer_service_email varchar [note: 'The operational customer service email address displayed to customers on this storefront for support inquiries, returns (RMA — Return Merchandise Authorization), and complaints. Routed to Salesforce Service Cloud for case management. Classified confidential as an organizational contact identifier.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this storefront was or is scheduled to be permanently retired and removed from active digital commerce operations. Nullable for currently active storefronts. Used for channel sunset planning, data retention scheduling, and regulatory compliance archival.']
  default_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code representing the default display language for this storefront (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'es\'). Used when a customer\'s browser language preference cannot be resolved or when the storefront operates in a single-language mode. Distinct from locale_code which also encodes regional formatting.']
  domain_url varchar [note: 'The primary public-facing domain URL for this storefront (e.g., \'https://www.retail.com\' or \'https://m.retail.com\'). Used for SEO configuration, SSL certificate management, CDN routing, and digital marketing attribution. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary digital infrastructure configuration.']
  free_shipping_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum order value (in the storefront\'s primary currency) at which free standard shipping is automatically applied at checkout. A key lever for AOV (Average Order Value) optimization and customer acquisition. Configured in Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) or Salesforce Commerce Cloud promotion rules.']
  gdpr_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront is subject to GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) consent requirements, triggering cookie consent banners, data processing notices, and opt-in workflows for customers in EU/EEA jurisdictions. Drives compliance configuration in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and CRM systems.']
  is_bopis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront supports the BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) fulfillment mode, allowing customers to purchase online and collect from a physical store location. Drives fulfillment routing logic in the OMS (Order Management System) and store operations workflows.']
  is_drop_ship_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront supports drop-ship fulfillment, where vendors ship products directly to customers without passing through Retail\'s DC (Distribution Center). Relevant for extended assortment and marketplace-style operations. Aligns with supplier management and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration.']
  is_loyalty_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront is integrated with Retail\'s customer loyalty and engagement program, enabling points accrual, redemption, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) tracking at the digital channel level. Drives loyalty-specific promotional targeting and RFM (Recency Frequency Monetary) segmentation.']
  is_ropis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront supports ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), allowing customers to reserve items online without payment and collect them in-store. Distinct from BOPIS in that no payment transaction occurs at the digital checkout stage.']
  is_ship_from_store_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront supports SFS (Ship-from-Store) fulfillment, where physical store locations act as fulfillment centers for online orders. Enables distributed inventory utilization and faster last-mile delivery. Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS for store-level pick/pack/ship operations.']
  is_ship_to_home_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront supports ship-to-home fulfillment, where orders are dispatched from a DC (Distribution Center) or store directly to the customer\'s delivery address. Governs last-mile delivery eligibility and carrier integration activation for this channel.']
  is_tax_inclusive_pricing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether product prices displayed on this storefront include taxes (tax-inclusive, common in EU/UK markets) or exclude taxes (tax-exclusive, common in US markets). Governs price display logic at the product listing and checkout stages. Aligns with Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM) tax display configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this storefront record was most recently updated in the system of record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this storefront was first made publicly available to customers. Used for channel age analysis, anniversary promotions, and historical performance benchmarking. Represents the effective-from date for the storefront\'s commercial lifecycle.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 locale code defining the primary language and regional formatting for this storefront (e.g., \'en_US\', \'fr_CA\', \'es_MX\'). Controls language display, date/number formatting, and content localization within Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Critical for omnichannel customer experience consistency.']
  max_cart_line_items varchar [note: 'The maximum number of distinct line items (SKUs) permitted in a single shopping cart session on this storefront. Governs cart capacity limits configured in Salesforce Commerce Cloud to manage checkout performance and order processing capacity. Relevant for B2B or bulk-purchase storefronts.']
  min_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum cart value (in the storefront\'s primary currency) required for a customer to complete checkout on this storefront. Used to enforce MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) value thresholds, qualify for free shipping promotions, or meet marketplace seller requirements. Configured in Salesforce Commerce Cloud checkout rules.']
  pci_compliance_level varchar [note: 'The PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) merchant compliance level assigned to this storefront, determined by annual card transaction volume (Level 1: >6M transactions; Level 2: 1M–6M; Level 3: 20K–1M; Level 4: <20K). Governs the scope of annual security assessments and SAQ (Self-Assessment Questionnaire) requirements. Classified confidential as it reveals security posture.']
  platform_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC) platform release deployed for this storefront (e.g., \'SFCC 23.7\', \'B2C Commerce 22.10\'). Used for release management, compatibility tracking, and upgrade planning across the digital commerce estate.']
  robots_indexing_policy varchar [note: 'The default robots meta directive applied to this storefront, controlling whether search engine crawlers may index pages and follow links (e.g., \'index_follow\' for the main site, \'noindex_nofollow\' for staging or dark-store channels). Impacts organic search visibility and SEO strategy.']
  seo_meta_title varchar [note: 'The default HTML meta title tag content for the storefront\'s homepage, used by search engines for indexing and SERP (Search Engine Results Page) display. Impacts organic search traffic and digital marketing performance. Managed within Salesforce Commerce Cloud site preferences.']
  session_timeout_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes of customer inactivity after which a shopping session is automatically expired on this storefront. Governs cart abandonment timing, session-based analytics, and security compliance for authenticated customer sessions. Configured in Salesforce Commerce Cloud site security settings.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique site identifier assigned within Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC) for this storefront. Used as the operational reference key across digital commerce platform configurations, API calls, and integration touchpoints. Aligns to the SFCC Site object\'s siteID field.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this storefront record was sourced or last updated (e.g., \'SFCC\' for Salesforce Commerce Cloud, \'MDM\' for Informatica MDM, \'MANUAL\' for manually entered records). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data governance within the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  storefront_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the digital storefront or online channel (e.g., \'Retail Main Site\', \'Retail Mobile App\', \'Retail Marketplace — Amazon\'). Used in reporting dashboards, operational tooling, and business communications.']
  storefront_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the storefront. \'active\' indicates the storefront is live and accepting customer traffic; \'maintenance\' indicates a temporary service window; \'draft\' indicates pre-launch configuration; \'decommissioned\' indicates the channel has been permanently retired. Governs whether the storefront is eligible for traffic routing, promotional targeting, and digital commerce operations.']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of payment methods accepted at checkout on this storefront (e.g., \'credit_card,debit_card,paypal,apple_pay,google_pay,gift_card,buy_now_pay_later\'). Governs payment gateway configuration and PCI DSS compliance scope for this channel. Managed within Salesforce Commerce Cloud payment processor settings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay|gift_card|buy_now_pay_later|bank_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this storefront for sales tax, VAT, or GST calculation purposes (e.g., \'US-CA\', \'CA-ON\', \'GB\'). Used by the tax engine integrated with Salesforce Commerce Cloud to apply correct tax rates at checkout. Aligns with FASB ASC 606 revenue recognition and FTC pricing transparency requirements.']
  theme_code varchar [note: 'Identifier referencing the visual theme or template applied to this storefront within Salesforce Commerce Cloud (e.g., \'sfra-retail-v3\', \'pwa-kit-2024\'). Controls the storefront\'s UI/UX presentation layer, including layout, typography, and brand styling. Used by digital merchandising and visual merchandising teams for planogram-equivalent digital shelf configuration.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the storefront\'s primary operating time zone (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Used for scheduling promotional campaigns, flash sales, maintenance windows, and business-hours-based SLA calculations. Aligns with Salesforce Commerce Cloud site time zone configuration.']

  Note: 'Master record for each digital storefront or online channel operated by Retail, including the main e-commerce site, mobile app storefronts, and marketplace presences. Captures storefront identity, locale, currency, domain URL, platform configuration (Salesforce Commerce Cloud site ID, channel-specific settings, feature flags, theme/template references), launch date, status, supported payment methods, and supported fulfillment modes (BOPIS, ship-to-home, drop-ship). SSOT for digital channel identity and configuration within the e-commerce domain. Domain ownership principle: the ecommerce domain owns digital commerce operations from storefront configuration through checkout completion. Customer identity is owned by the customer domain; post-checkout order lifecycle is owned by the order domain; promotional campaign definitions are owned by the promotion domain; product master data is owned by the product domain.'
}

Table ecommerce.digital_catalog {
  digital_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each digital catalog record representing a channel-specific published view of a SKU on a given storefront. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a digital presentation asset managed and published via Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buyer. Business justification: Buyers manage online assortment, pricing strategy, and product content decisions for their assigned categories. Digital catalog accountability and performance reporting require buyer attribution for m']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary digital storefront category (navigation taxonomy node) under which this SKU is merchandised online. May differ from the master merchandise hierarchy in ORMS to reflect digital-specific category management and assortment breadth strategies.']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.demand_forecast. Business justification: Merchandising teams use demand forecasts to optimize online pricing, promotional timing, and product visibility. Direct link enables forecast-driven catalog management and dynamic pricing based on pre']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Online catalog displays store-level availability for BOPIS and ship-from-store fulfillment options. Enables "available at nearby stores" feature and store inventory lookup for omnichannel shoppers.']
  license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.license_permit. Business justification: Regulated products (alcohol, tobacco, pharmaceuticals, firearms) require valid licenses to sell. Retail must link catalog SKUs to permits to enforce sales restrictions, prevent unlicensed sales, and p']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Products displayed online show store-specific availability and fulfillment options. Real business process: "check store availability" feature, store-specific assortment display, local inventory querie']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Catalog items are assigned to profit centers for gross margin analysis, markdown accounting, channel profitability reporting, and product line P&L in retail management accounting systems.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Product pages display active promotions; linking enables merchandising teams to verify promotional pricing accuracy across catalog, SEO teams to optimize promotional landing pages, and compliance team']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Online catalog organized by retail seasons (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, Holiday). Seasonal merchandising drives publish/unpublish schedules, promotional calendars, and assortment planning. Critical fo']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Catalog publishing process requires authoritative link to product master for attributes, compliance flags, lifecycle status, vendor data, and pricing rules. Retail operations depend on single source o']
  sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Digital catalog product pages must display authoritative online prices from sku_price, which maintains channel-specific and zone-specific pricing. Core business process: e-commerce price display and c']
  age_restriction varchar [note: 'Age-gating requirement for this SKU on the digital storefront, applicable to regulated product categories such as alcohol, tobacco, and certain pharmaceuticals. Drives age verification workflows at checkout. Regulated by FTC and FDA.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Consumer-facing brand name associated with this SKU as displayed on the digital storefront. Used for brand-level filtering, faceted navigation, and brand page attribution. Includes private label (store brand) designations.']
  canonical_url varchar [note: 'The canonical URL of the product detail page (PDP) used to prevent duplicate content issues across multiple storefront URLs or faceted navigation paths. Declared in the HTML link rel=canonical tag. Managed in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  content_completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage score (0.00–100.00) measuring how complete the digital content is for this catalog record, based on presence of required fields such as title, description, images, SEO metadata, and rich media. Used to prioritize content enrichment efforts and improve Conversion Rate (CR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this digital catalog record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and content age analysis. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the online_price for this digital catalog record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency storefronts in international e-commerce operations.']
  has_video boolean [note: 'Indicates whether at least one product video (e.g., demonstration, unboxing, lifestyle) is published for this SKU on the storefront. Rich media presence is a key driver of Conversion Rate (CR) improvement in digital commerce.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU contains hazardous materials subject to special shipping, handling, or labeling requirements. Affects last-mile delivery eligibility and carrier selection in the Order Management System (OMS). Regulated by CPSC and OSHA.']
  image_count varchar [note: 'Total number of product images published for this SKU on the storefront, including hero, alternate angles, lifestyle, and swatch images. Used to measure digital content completeness and identify SKUs requiring additional imagery.']
  is_drop_ship_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is fulfilled via Drop Ship, where the vendor ships directly to the customer without passing through a Distribution Center (DC). Relevant for extended aisle and marketplace assortment strategies.']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU has been manually designated as a featured product by digital merchandisers, qualifying it for placement in featured product carousels, homepage spotlights, or category hero positions on the storefront.']
  is_online_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is currently available for purchase on the digital storefront. Distinct from publication_status — a product may be published but temporarily unavailable due to inventory depletion or fulfillment constraints. Drives add-to-cart eligibility.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is a private label (store brand) product owned and sold exclusively by the retailer. Private label products typically carry higher gross margins and are a key strategic differentiator in assortment planning.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is indexed and returned in on-site search results on the digital storefront. A product may be published but excluded from search (e.g., exclusive landing page products or clearance items managed via direct URL only).']
  last_published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when this digital catalog record was most recently published or re-published to the live storefront. Used for content freshness tracking, cache invalidation, and audit trails in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 locale code specifying the language and regional variant for this digital catalog record (e.g., en-US, fr-CA, es-MX). Supports multi-locale storefront content management and international e-commerce operations in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  long_description varchar [note: 'Full consumer-facing product description displayed on the product detail page (PDP), including features, benefits, materials, and usage instructions. Supports rich HTML formatting. Managed in Salesforce Commerce Cloud content management.']
  primary_image_url varchar [note: 'URL of the primary hero product image displayed on the product detail page (PDP) and product listing pages (PLP). Hosted on the digital asset management (DAM) system or CDN. Critical for digital content completeness scoring.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The consumer-facing product title as displayed on the digital storefront. May differ from the master product name in PIM to reflect channel-specific merchandising copy or A/B tested titles. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  publication_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the digital catalog record on the storefront. Controls whether the SKU is visible and purchasable by online shoppers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|published|unpublished|scheduled|archived|suspended — promote to reference product]']
  publish_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this digital catalog record is scheduled to be or was deactivated and removed from the storefront. Used for seasonal assortment management, limited-time offers, and clearance markdown lifecycle management.']
  publish_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this digital catalog record is scheduled to become or became active and visible on the storefront. Used for pre-launch product scheduling and promotional launch coordination in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  rating_average decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average customer review rating for this SKU on the digital storefront, on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Aggregated from verified purchase reviews. Used in product ranking algorithms, content completeness scoring, and conversion rate optimization analytics.']
  review_count varchar [note: 'Total number of published customer reviews for this SKU on the digital storefront. Used alongside rating_average to assess review volume credibility and identify SKUs requiring review generation campaigns.']
  seo_meta_description varchar [note: 'The HTML meta description tag for the product detail page (PDP), summarizing the product for search engine result snippets. Typically 150-160 characters. Drives organic Click-Through Rate (CTR) from search engine results pages.']
  seo_title varchar [note: 'The HTML page title tag for the product detail page (PDP), optimized for search engine indexing. Typically limited to 60 characters per SEO best practices. Managed in Salesforce Commerce Cloud content management.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief consumer-facing product description displayed in product listing pages (PLP) and search results. Typically 100-200 characters. Sourced from Salesforce Commerce Cloud product content and may differ from the master description in PIM.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric position used to control the default display sequence of this SKU within its assigned digital category on the storefront. Lower values appear first. Used by merchandisers to manually curate product listing page (PLP) rankings.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this digital catalog record originated or was last updated. Supports data lineage tracking across Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC), Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS), Informatica MDM, and Oracle Retail Price Management (RPM).']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital storefront or e-commerce channel on which this catalog record is published (e.g., main website, mobile app storefront, marketplace channel). Enables channel-specific catalog management across Salesforce Commerce Cloud storefronts.']
  tax_category_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code assigned to this SKU for digital commerce tax calculation purposes, determining applicable sales tax rates at checkout. Mapped to tax engine rules in Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Relevant for FDA-regulated food items with tax exemptions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this digital catalog record was most recently modified, including content updates, price changes, status transitions, or SEO edits. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']
  url_slug varchar [note: 'The human-readable URL path segment for the product detail page (PDP), used in constructing the storefront URL. Must be lowercase, hyphen-separated, and unique per storefront. Example: \'mens-slim-fit-chino-pants-navy\'.']

  Note: 'E-commerce product catalog record representing the published, channel-specific view of merchandise available on a given storefront. Tracks which SKUs are active online, digital content completeness (images, descriptions, rich media), SEO metadata (title, meta description, canonical URL), online availability flags, digital-only pricing overrides, and publication status. This is the digital presentation layer — distinct from the master product catalog in the product domain which owns canonical SKU/UPC definitions and the master merchandise hierarchy.'
}

Table ecommerce.web_session {
  web_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual visitor session on the digital storefront. Primary key for the web_session data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Employee purchase programs and associate product training require tracking which associates browse storefronts. Enables employee discount validation, internal traffic filtering from customer analytics']
  cart_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shopping cart created or interacted with during this session. Enables abandoned cart analysis and cart-level GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) attribution even when no order is placed.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order placed during this session, if is_order_placed is True. Enables direct linkage between digital session analytics and order-level GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) and AOV (Average Order Value) reporting.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program membership associated with this session, if the visitor is an authenticated loyalty member. Supports Customer Loyalty and Engagement Program analytics and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) attribution.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the authenticated customer associated with this session. Null for anonymous/guest sessions. Enables online-to-offline attribution and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) analysis.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign.
