  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proof of delivery record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver or delivery associate who completed the delivery. Used for driver performance tracking and accountability.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or logistics provider who executed the delivery. Used for carrier performance tracking and compliance.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order associated with this delivery. Enables tracing POD back to the original order.']
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.outbound_shipment. Business justification: POD for DC-to-store shipments. When DC ships to store, proof_of_delivery captures store receipt signature/timestamp, linking to outbound_shipment. Critical for store replenishment confirmation, DC-to-']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment that was delivered. Links to the shipment record for which proof of delivery was captured.']
  age_verification_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether age verification was successfully completed. True if verification passed, False otherwise.']
  age_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether age verification was required for this delivery (e.g., alcohol, tobacco). True if verification was required, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this proof of delivery record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer was notified of the delivery completion. True if notification was sent, False otherwise.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The calendar date of delivery. Used for daily delivery reporting and performance analytics.']
  delivery_exception_code varchar [note: 'The code representing any exception or issue encountered during delivery (e.g., customer not home, address incorrect, access denied). Used for root cause analysis.']
  delivery_instructions_followed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the driver followed the customer-provided delivery instructions. True if instructions were followed, False otherwise.']
  delivery_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The GPS latitude coordinate where the delivery was completed. Used for route optimization and delivery location verification.']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'The specific location where the package was left at the delivery address. Critical for customer satisfaction and package security. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_door|back_door|side_door|garage|porch|mailbox|parcel_locker|reception|neighbor|building_office|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The GPS longitude coordinate where the delivery was completed. Used for route optimization and delivery location verification.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the driver about the delivery. Captures additional context not covered by structured fields.']
  delivery_photo_url varchar [note: 'The URL or storage path to the photo taken at the delivery location. Provides visual proof of package placement and condition.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'The final status of the delivery attempt. Indicates whether the delivery was successful or encountered issues.']
  delivery_stop_reference varchar [note: 'The unique reference number for the delivery stop on the carrier route. Used for route optimization and last-mile logistics tracking.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the delivery was completed and POD was captured. Critical for SLA compliance and dispute resolution.']
  dispute_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer filed a dispute regarding this delivery (e.g., not received, damaged, incorrect). True if dispute exists, False otherwise.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'The current status of any delivery dispute. Used for dispute management and resolution tracking.']
  gps_accuracy_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'The accuracy radius of the GPS coordinates in meters. Indicates the precision of the location capture.']
  id_verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify the recipient\'s identity or age. Used for compliance with age-restricted product delivery regulations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this proof of delivery record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the customer of delivery completion. Used for channel effectiveness analysis.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the delivery completion notification was sent to the customer.']
  package_condition varchar [note: 'The physical condition of the package as observed and recorded at the time of delivery. Critical for damage claims and carrier accountability.']
  pin_confirmation_code varchar [note: 'The personal identification number (PIN) or confirmation code provided by the recipient to authorize delivery. Used for high-value or secure deliveries.']
  pod_capture_method varchar [note: 'The technology or device used to capture the proof of delivery. Used for process improvement and technology adoption tracking.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'The name of the person who received the delivery. May differ from the original customer if delivery was accepted by an alternate recipient.']
  recipient_relationship varchar [note: 'The relationship of the recipient to the original customer. Used to validate authorized delivery acceptance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer|household_member|neighbor|building_staff|reception|security|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical or electronic signature was captured at delivery. True if signature was obtained, False otherwise.']
  signature_image_url varchar [note: 'The URL or storage path to the digital signature image file. Used for dispute resolution and carrier compliance verification.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery met the promised service level agreement (delivery time commitment). True if SLA was met, False if breached.']
  temperature_at_delivery_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'The recorded temperature of the package or delivery environment at the time of delivery, measured in Celsius. Used for cold chain compliance.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-sensitive shipments remained within required temperature range during delivery. True if compliant, False if breach occurred. Applicable to grocery and pharmaceutical deliveries.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Used for carrier reconciliation and customer inquiry resolution.']

  Note: 'Records proof-of-delivery (POD) evidence captured at the point of customer delivery. Captures delivery stop reference, delivery timestamp, recipient name, signature image URL, delivery photo URL, PIN confirmation code, GPS coordinates at delivery, delivery location type (front door, mailbox, neighbor, locker), and POD capture method (mobile app, handheld scanner, kiosk). Critical for dispute resolution, carrier compliance, and customer delivery confirmation.'
}

Table fulfillment.carrier_facility_contract {
  carrier_facility_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this carrier-facility service contract record. Primary key.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution center facility receiving carrier service']
  fulfillment_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the carrier master record providing service to this facility']
  assigned_dock_doors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of dock door numbers or identifiers assigned to this carrier at this facility for loading operations.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned contract or agreement number for this carrier-facility relationship, used for legal and procurement reference.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this carrier-facility contract indicating whether it is operationally active.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier-facility contract record was created in the system.']
  daily_pickup_schedule varchar [note: 'Scheduled pickup times for this carrier at this facility, typically in format HH:MM or multiple times (e.g., 10:00,14:00,18:00), defining when the carrier collects outbound shipments.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this carrier-facility service agreement became active and rates/terms took effect.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this carrier-facility service agreement expires and requires renewal or renegotiation.']
  facility_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional volume-based discount percentage negotiated specifically for this facility based on shipment volume commitments.']
  facility_specific_base_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Negotiated base shipping rate per pound for this specific carrier-facility combination, potentially different from the carrier\'s standard base rate due to facility volume or regional pricing.']
  max_daily_volume_packages varchar [note: 'Maximum number of packages this carrier commits to pick up daily from this facility under the service agreement.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this carrier at this facility used in carrier selection logic (1=highest priority), allowing facilities to prefer certain carriers based on performance or cost.']
  rate_tier varchar [note: 'Negotiated rate tier or pricing level for this specific facility, which may differ from the carrier\'s standard rates based on facility volume commitments.']
  service_levels_enabled varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or JSON array of service levels enabled for this carrier-facility combination (e.g., ground, two_day, overnight), as not all carrier services may be available at all facilities.']
  sla_on_time_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Service level agreement percentage for on-time delivery performance that this carrier commits to for shipments originating from this facility.']
  updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier-facility contract record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual service agreement between a carrier and a distribution center facility. It captures facility-specific carrier terms including negotiated rates, service level commitments, pickup schedules, and dock door assignments. Each record links one carrier to one DC facility with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific carrier-facility relationship, enabling multi-carrier operations at each DC and multi-facility service coverage by each carrier.. Existence Justification: In retail distribution operations, carriers provide service to multiple DC facilities across the network, and each DC facility contracts with multiple carriers to ensure service redundancy, cost optimization, and capacity flexibility. The carrier-facility relationship is contractual and operational, with facility-specific negotiated rates, pickup schedules, dock door assignments, service level agreements, and volume commitments that vary by facility based on regional pricing, facility volume, and operational constraints.'
}

Table returns.rma {
  rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rma']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: RMAs must trace back to the specific fulfillment that generated the return for carrier performance analysis, fulfillment quality metrics by node/method, and root-cause analysis of returns. Essential f']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order from which the items are being returned.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who is initiating the return.']
  return_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store or distribution center where the returned merchandise will be received and processed.']
  rma_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or system user who authorized the RMA.']
  rma_processed_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who physically processed and inspected the returned merchandise.']
  service_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case associated with this return, if the return was initiated through customer service interaction.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'The date when the return merchandise authorization was approved and issued to the customer.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the RMA was authorized, capturing the exact moment of approval for audit and SLA tracking purposes.']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date when the RMA was closed and all return processing activities were completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the expected return value amount.']
  customer_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments provided by the customer explaining the reason for return or providing additional details about the issue.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the final disposition of the returned merchandise: restock to inventory, liquidate through clearance channel, rework/refurbish, scrap/destroy, RTV (Return to Vendor), or donate to charity.']
  exchange_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new order created for an exchange transaction, linking the return to the replacement order.']
  expected_return_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of merchandise expected to be returned under this RMA, calculated from the original order line items.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer must return the merchandise. After this date, the RMA becomes invalid and the return may not be accepted.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) representing the likelihood that this return is fraudulent, generated by fraud detection algorithms.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The date when the returned merchandise was inspected to determine its condition and disposition.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the merchandise inspection indicating whether items meet return acceptance criteria.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this return has been identified as potentially fraudulent based on fraud detection rules or manual review.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to this RMA based on customer tier, return value, or business rules.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date when the returned merchandise was physically received at the return location.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the returned merchandise was received, used for SLA tracking and operational metrics.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual refund amount to be issued to the customer after inspection and approval, which may differ from expected return value due to restocking fees or partial returns.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the refund is issued to the customer.']
  refund_processed_date date [note: 'The date when the refund payment was processed and issued to the customer.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return, typically applied to non-defective returns or opened merchandise.']
  return_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer initiated the return request. BOPIS = Buy Online Pick Up In Store, ROPIS = Reserve Online Pick Up In Store.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'The logistics method by which the customer will return the merchandise (ship to distribution center, drop at store, carrier pickup from customer location, or direct return to vendor).']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the primary reason for the return (e.g., defective, wrong item, size issue, customer remorse, damaged in transit).']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the reason for return, providing additional context beyond the standardized reason code.']
  return_shipping_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of shipping the returned merchandise back to the retailer, which may be borne by customer or retailer depending on return reason and policy.']
  return_shipping_paid_by varchar [note: 'Indicates which party is responsible for paying the return shipping cost.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'The type of resolution requested or authorized for this return: full refund to original payment method, exchange for different item, store credit, or replacement of defective item.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the return authorization issued to the customer. Used in customer communications and tracking.']
  rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA. Tracks progression from initial request through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|received|inspected|approved|denied|closed|cancelled|expired — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this RMA record (e.g., Salesforce Service Cloud, Oracle Retail, Store POS system).']
  store_credit_issued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of store credit issued to the customer as part of the return resolution.']

  Note: 'Return Merchandise Authorization master record representing the formal authorization issued to a customer to return one or more items. Captures the RMA number, originating order reference, customer identity, return channel (in-store, online, BOPIS), authorization date, expiry date, return reason code, return type (refund, exchange, store credit), total expected return value, and current RMA status (pending, authorized, received, closed, denied). Primary master entity for the returns domain and the anchor for all downstream return processing workflows.'
}

Table returns.rma_line {
  rma_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rma_line']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the warehouse associate who inspected and assessed the condition of the returned item.']
  buying_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buying_order_line. Business justification: Enables vendor quality scorecarding and defect-driven cost recovery. Retail operations track defective returns back to the original buying order (not just customer order) to support vendor chargebacks']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Returns processing requires original landed cost for refund calculations, margin impact analysis, vendor credit determination (RTV), and financial reconciliation. Core operational link between returns']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Returns processing at DC facilities requires tracking which DC received and inspected returned items for disposition routing, inventory restocking, and warehouse operations. Essential for RTV shipment']
  expiry_tracking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.expiry_tracking. Business justification: Returns of perishable/dated goods must link to expiry records for shelf-life validation, markdown policy enforcement, and vendor return eligibility determination. Critical for food, pharma, cosmetics ']
  fulfillment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_line. Business justification: Line-level traceability from return to original fulfillment line enables picker/packer quality scoring, defect tracking by associate, lot/serial reconciliation for recalls, and substitution error anal']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_receipt. Business justification: Connects returned item to original receipt for cost basis validation, vendor chargeback processing, and defect tracking to specific inbound shipments. Critical for vendor performance management and qu']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order from which this item is being returned. Links the return to the originating transaction.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Lot-tracked returns (food, pharma, cosmetics) require linking to original lot for recall traceability, FEFO compliance, and quality root-cause analysis. Regulatory requirement for perishable goods. En']
  original_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific line item on the original sales order. Enables precise traceability from return to original purchase.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent RMA header. Links this line item to the overall return authorization.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Return lines must reference the product SKU being returned. The product_description column becomes redundant as it can be joined from product.sku. This links returns processing to the product catalog.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Supplier identifier for the vendor who originally supplied this product. Used when disposition is Return to Vendor (RTV).']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the item upon receipt at the returns facility. Determines restocking eligibility and disposition path.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RMA line record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Ensures proper currency handling in multi-currency operations.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final disposition instruction for the returned merchandise. Determines the next step in the reverse logistics workflow (restock to inventory, return to vendor, liquidate, destroy, repair, or donate).']
  extended_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost value for this line (quantity × unit cost). Used for inventory and cost of goods sold adjustments.']
  extended_retail_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total retail value for this line (quantity × unit retail price). Represents the total refund exposure for this line item.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit Global Trade Item Number for the returned product. International standard for product identification across supply chains.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the returned item was physically inspected and condition assessed. Key milestone in the returns processing workflow.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RMA line record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to return line details.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the RMA. Determines the ordering and position of this item in the multi-item return authorization.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this RMA line item. Tracks the line through the returns workflow from authorization through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AUTHORIZED|RECEIVED|INSPECTED|PROCESSED|RESTOCKED|COMPLETED|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  liquidation_sale_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered from selling the returned item through liquidation channels (discount outlets, third-party liquidators, online auctions). Used to calculate net return loss.']
  quantity_authorized varchar [note: 'Number of units authorized for return on this RMA line. Represents the maximum quantity the customer is permitted to return.']
  quantity_received varchar [note: 'Actual number of units physically received back from the customer for this line item. May differ from quantity authorized.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual amount refunded to the customer for this line item. May differ from extended retail amount due to restocking fees, partial returns, or prorated refunds.']
  restocking_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item is in suitable condition to be restocked and resold. True if item can return to sellable inventory.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return of this line item. May be waived based on return reason or customer status.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the customer is returning this item. Used for root cause analysis and quality improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEFECTIVE|DAMAGED|WRONG_ITEM|NOT_AS_DESCRIBED|CHANGED_MIND|SIZE_FIT|DUPLICATE_ORDER|LATE_DELIVERY|OTHER — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the return reason provided by the customer or service representative. Provides additional context beyond the standardized code.']
  rtv_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-issued authorization number permitting the return of defective or excess merchandise back to the supplier. Required for vendor chargeback processing.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit identifier for the product being returned. Used for inventory tracking and product identification.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of goods sold per unit. Used for inventory valuation adjustments and margin analysis on returned merchandise.']
  unit_retail_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original retail selling price per unit at the time of purchase. Used to calculate refund amounts and revenue adjustments.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code for the returned item. Standard barcode identifier used in North American retail.']
  vendor_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Credit amount expected or received from the vendor for this returned item. Used in accounts payable adjustments and vendor chargeback reconciliation.']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the distribution center or warehouse location where the returned item was received and processed.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within an RMA, representing a single SKU being returned. Captures the SKU, UPC, GTIN, product description, quantity authorized versus quantity received, unit retail price, unit cost, return reason at the line level, condition code upon receipt (new, opened, damaged, defective), restocking eligibility flag, and disposition instruction. Enables granular tracking of each item in a multi-item return authorization.'
}

Table returns.return_request {
  return_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return request. Primary key for the return request entity.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Return requests reference specific fulfillment to identify shipping damage, wrong item picked, or carrier issues during pre-authorization. Enables fraud detection based on fulfillment method patterns ']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original order from which items are being returned.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the customer intends to return the items, if in-store return method is selected.']
  pickup_address_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the address from which the return items should be picked up, if pickup was requested.']
  primary_return_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service representative or returns specialist assigned to review and process this return request.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the return request.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to returns.rma. Business justification: Return request is the customer-initiated intent to return merchandise before formal RMA authorization. Once approved, a return_request results in an RMA (formal authorization). Currently, return_reque']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the return request was approved, triggering the generation of an RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return request record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated refund amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address provided by the customer for communication regarding this return request.']
  customer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number provided by the customer for communication regarding this return request.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for denial if the return request was rejected (e.g., outside return window, final sale item, missing proof of purchase, item condition not acceptable).']
  denial_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided to the customer explaining why the return request was denied.']
  denial_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the return request was denied, if applicable.']
  estimated_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the refund if the return request is approved, calculated based on original purchase price and applicable return policy rules.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this return request has been flagged for potential fraud and requires additional verification before approval.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (0-100) indicating the likelihood that this return request is fraudulent, based on customer history, return patterns, and fraud detection algorithms.']
  is_within_return_window boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the return request was submitted within the eligible return window defined by the applicable return policy.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return request record was last modified.']
  original_purchase_date date [note: 'Date when the original order was placed, used to validate return eligibility against return window policies.']
  pickup_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has requested carrier pickup of the return items from their location.']
  preferred_resolution_type varchar [note: 'Customer-indicated preference for how they would like the return to be resolved (refund to original payment method, exchange for different item, store credit, or replacement of same item).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for processing this return request, typically based on customer tier, order value, or issue severity.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer submitted the return request (e-commerce portal, mobile app, call center, in-store associate, chatbot, email).']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the return request, typically displayed to customers and service representatives. Format: RR followed by 10 digits.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return request in the approval workflow.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer initiated the return request. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer will return the merchandise (carrier pickup, drop-off at designated location, in-store return, mail).']
  return_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific return policy applicable to this request (e.g., standard 30-day, extended holiday, final sale, private label policy).']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the customer-stated reason for the return (e.g., defective, wrong item, size issue, changed mind, damaged in transit).']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description provided by the customer explaining the reason for the return.']
  return_window_expiry_date date [note: 'Last date by which the return request must be submitted to be eligible under the applicable return policy (typically 30, 60, or 90 days from purchase).']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes added by the returns specialist during the review process, documenting decision rationale, policy exceptions, or special handling instructions.']
  sla_response_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target date and time by which the return request must be reviewed and responded to, based on applicable SLA (Service Level Agreement) commitments.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this return request originated (e.g., Salesforce Service Cloud, e-commerce platform, store POS system).']
  total_items_requested varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct line items included in this return request.']
  total_quantity_requested varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units across all line items requested for return.']

  Note: 'Customer-initiated return request capturing the initial intent to return merchandise before formal RMA authorization. Records the request channel (e-commerce portal, store associate, call center), request timestamp, customer-stated reason, items and quantities requested for return, preferred resolution type (refund, exchange, store credit), and request status (submitted, under review, approved, denied). Serves as the intake record that triggers the RMA authorization workflow.'
}

Table returns.return_receipt {
  return_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return receipt record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping carrier that delivered the returned merchandise. Applicable for mail-in and shipped returns.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Return receipts at DC facilities (vs store locations) require facility tracking for receiving dock scheduling, inspection workflow routing, quality hold processing, and disposition decision execution.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Return receipts must link to original fulfillment for closed-loop cycle time analysis (order→fulfill→return→receipt), reverse logistics SLA measurement, and carrier damage claim validation. Enables co']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_receipt. Business justification: Links return receipt to original inbound receipt for discrepancy analysis, vendor performance scoring, and cost basis validation. Retail operations use this for vendor chargeback processing and qualit']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sales order from which the returned merchandise originated. Links return to original transaction.']
  inspector_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associate who performed the detailed quality inspection. Null if inspection not required or not yet performed.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Return receipts are physically received at specific nodes. Required for receiving workflow orchestration, location-level return volume metrics, staffing planning, and dock door scheduling. Retail oper']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the return. Used for return analytics, fraud detection, and customer service.']
  receiving_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or associate who physically received and logged the returned merchandise.']
  receiving_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro-fulfillment center where the returned merchandise was received.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RMA that authorized this return. Links the physical receipt event to the return authorization.']
  authorized_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of items authorized for return in the original RMA. Used to detect over-returns and under-returns.']
  carrier_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number from the shipping carrier for mail-in or shipped returns. Used to reconcile carrier delivery events with physical receipt.']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the returned merchandise at the time of receipt. Drives disposition decision and restocking eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_unopened|like_new|good|fair|poor|damaged|defective|unsellable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return receipt record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a discrepancy was detected between authorized and received items. Triggers exception handling workflows.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the nature of the discrepancy. Provides context for exception resolution and audit trail.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discrepancy detected at receipt. Determines resolution workflow and financial adjustments.']
  disposition_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommended disposition action for the returned merchandise based on condition, value, and business rules. May be overridden by final disposition decision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restock|liquidate|donate|destroy|rtv|repair|pending — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_recovery_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial recovery value of the returned merchandise based on condition and disposition recommendation. Used for financial planning and shrinkage analysis.']
  inspection_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when detailed quality inspection was completed. Null if inspection not required or not yet performed.']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed quality inspection is required before disposition. Triggered by high-value items, electronics, or condition concerns.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return receipt record was last modified in the data platform. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  packaging_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the product packaging at receipt. Impacts resale value and disposition options.']
  quantity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity fields. Ensures consistent interpretation of quantity values across different product types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique receipt number generated when returned merchandise is physically received. Used for tracking and customer communication.']
  receipt_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that captured the return receipt event. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  receipt_source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this receipt record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return receipt. Tracks progression from initial receipt through inspection and final disposition decision.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the returned merchandise was physically received at the receiving location. Primary business event timestamp for the receipt.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of items physically received at the receiving location. Compared against authorized quantity to flag discrepancies.']
  receiving_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility that received the returned merchandise. Determines downstream processing workflows and disposition options.']
  recovery_value_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated recovery value. Supports multi-currency operations.']
  refund_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why customer refund is being held pending resolution. Null or none if refund processed immediately upon receipt.']
  refund_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receipt event triggered a customer refund workflow. Links physical receipt to financial refund processing.']
  restocking_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned merchandise is eligible for restocking and resale. Based on condition assessment and business rules.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer returned the merchandise. Determines receipt workflow and processing requirements.']

  Note: 'Physical or digital receipt record generated when returned merchandise is received at a store, DC, or MFC. Captures the receiving location, receiving associate, receipt timestamp, carrier tracking number (for mail-in returns), actual items and quantities received versus authorized, condition assessment at receipt, discrepancy flags (over-return, under-return, wrong item), and receipt status. Links the physical goods receipt event to the RMA and triggers downstream disposition and refund workflows.'
}

Table returns.disposition {
  disposition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the disposition record. Primary key for the disposition entity.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Batch identifier grouping multiple disposition records processed together. Used for bulk RTV shipments, liquidation lots, or mass restocking operations.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action. Business justification: Audit findings identifying improper disposition practices (hazmat disposal violations, vendor return processing errors) generate corrective actions that mandate disposition procedure changes. Real bus']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Disposition decisions (restock vs liquidate vs RTV) require comparing estimated recovery value to original landed cost. Drives financial optimization of returned inventory. Removes denormalized origin']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Disposition decisions (restock/liquidate/RTV/scrap) execute at specific DC facilities. Tracking the facility is required for inventory movements, warehouse task generation, and GL posting. Replaces de']
  environmental_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_event. Business justification: Retail operations track environmental compliance events (hazmat disposal, spills, waste manifests) linked to disposition decisions for returned merchandise, especially electronics, chemicals, and regu']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Disposition decisions execute at specific locations (restock at DC, liquidate at warehouse, RTV from store). Needed for location-level recovery rate tracking, disposition capacity planning, and networ']
  inventory_stock_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_ledger. Business justification: Return dispositions (restock, liquidate, destroy) generate inventory transactions that should be tracked in the stock ledger. This links returns disposition decisions to inventory accounting. No visib']
  liquidation_partner_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third-party liquidation partner if disposition type is liquidation. Tracks which liquidator handled the item for recovery value reconciliation.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Lot-tracked dispositions enable batch-level quality analysis, vendor chargeback validation, and compliance documentation for perishable/regulated goods. Retail operations require lot linkage for vendo']
  responsible_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate or returns processor who made or approved the disposition decision. Used for accountability and quality tracking.']
  rma_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific return line item that this disposition decision applies to. Links disposition to the originating return merchandise authorization line.']
  supplier_rtv_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.rtv_request. Business justification: Disposition decisions (restock/RTV/liquidate) directly trigger supplier RTV requests. Loss prevention teams measure recovery rates by linking disposition outcomes to vendor credit receipts. Enables cl']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor to whom the item will be returned if disposition type is return_to_vendor. Used for vendor compliance and credit processing.']
  actual_recovery_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Realized financial recovery value after disposition execution. Populated after liquidation sale, vendor credit, or restock completion. Used for variance analysis against estimates.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the disposition action was fully executed (item restocked, shipped to vendor, transferred to liquidator, or destroyed). Used for cycle time measurement.']
  condition_grade varchar [note: 'Quality assessment grade assigned during inspection. A=New/Unopened, B=Like New/Minor Packaging Damage, C=Good/Visible Wear, D=Fair/Functional Defects, F=Unsellable/Damaged. Drives disposition type and recovery value estimation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this disposition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for recovery value amounts. Supports multi-currency operations for global retail operations.']
  decision_date date [note: 'The date when the disposition decision was made by the returns processing team or system. Key timestamp for cycle time analytics.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the disposition decision was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail and process timing analysis.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific defect or damage type found during inspection. Used for quality analytics and vendor chargeback justification.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the disposition action. Tracks progress from decision through execution.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'The final fate decision for the returned item. Determines the next step in the reverse logistics workflow and impacts inventory valuation and recovery value.']
  estimated_recovery_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected financial recovery value for the item based on disposition type and condition grade. Used for shrinkage forecasting and reverse logistics ROI analysis.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the returned item was physically inspected and condition grade was assigned. Precedes disposition decision date.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the returned item is classified as hazardous material requiring special disposition handling and compliance with EPA and DOT regulations.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the item has a unique serial number requiring individual tracking through disposition process. Common for electronics and high-value goods.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this disposition record was last updated. Tracks changes to disposition decisions or status updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the returns processor providing additional context about the disposition decision, item condition, or special handling requirements.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU being dispositioned under this record. Typically 1 for individual item returns, may be greater for bulk returns.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the disposition decision. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  restock_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific inventory location code where the item will be restocked if disposition type is restock_sellable or restock_clearance. May be a bin, aisle, or zone identifier within the facility.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of restocking fee charged if restocking_fee_applied is true. Contributes to reverse logistics cost recovery.']
  restocking_fee_applied boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a restocking fee was charged to the customer for this return. Impacts refund amount and customer satisfaction metrics.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'The return merchandise authorization number associated with this disposition. Links to the customer-facing return authorization.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the returned item if is_serialized is true. Used for warranty tracking, theft prevention, and individual item lifecycle management.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The stock keeping unit identifier for the returned item being dispositioned. Used for inventory tracking and product identification.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that created this disposition record. WMS=Manhattan WMS, OMS=Order Management System, RMS=Returns Management System, SFSC=Salesforce Service Cloud.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the disposition quantity. EA=Each, CS=Case, PK=Pack, BX=Box. Aligns with inventory management standards.']

  Note: 'Disposition record governing the final fate of each returned item after receipt and inspection. Captures disposition type (restock to sellable inventory, restock to clearance, return to vendor via RTV, liquidation, donation, destruction/write-off), decision date, disposition location, responsible associate, estimated and actual recovery value, and disposition status. Central to reverse logistics cost recovery, shrinkage management, and inventory reintegration decisions. Now also carries condition grade assessment (A-New through F-Unsellable) previously in a separate reference table.'
}

Table returns.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for refund']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Refund processing costs (labor, payment processing fees, fraud review) are allocated to cost centers for departmental P&L and budget variance analysis. Essential for retail operations financial manage']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Refunds post directly to GL accounts for merchandise returns, tax adjustments, and restocking fees. Required for daily financial close, reconciliation, and audit trail. Replaces denormalized gl_accoun']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order that is being refunded. Enables traceability to the original purchase transaction.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store location where the refund was processed, if applicable. Used for store-level financial reconciliation and performance analysis.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receiving the refund. Links refund to customer master data for reconciliation and customer lifetime value analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Refunds reduce profit center revenue and margin metrics. Required for accurate P&L reporting by channel, region, or store. Enables management reporting on return rates and profitability impact by busi']
  promo_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_redemption. Business justification: Refund processing must reference original promotional redemption to calculate correct refund amount (excluding promotional discount), update vendor funding obligations, and enforce promotional terms. ']
  refund_approved_by_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or authorized employee who approved the refund, if approval was required. Used for compliance and authorization audit trail.']
  refund_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or customer service representative who processed the refund transaction. Used for audit trail and performance tracking.']
  return_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent return transaction that triggered this refund. Links refund to the originating return merchandise authorization (RMA).']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'FK to returns.rma.rma_id — Refund to RMA linkage is required for return financial reconciliation — every refund must trace back to its authorizing RMA for audit and financial controls.']
  actual_settlement_date date [note: 'Actual date when the refund funds were confirmed as settled and available to the customer. Used for SLA compliance measurement and financial reconciliation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional positive or negative adjustment applied to the refund for reasons such as damage, missing components, promotional credits, or goodwill gestures.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was approved by authorized personnel or automated business rules. Represents authorization milestone in the refund workflow. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Unique authorization or approval code issued by the payment processor confirming the refund transaction was approved. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The customer-facing channel or touchpoint through which the refund was initiated and processed. Enables omnichannel analytics and channel performance measurement.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was successfully completed and funds were transferred to the customer. Represents final settlement of the refund transaction. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this refund record was first created in the database. Used for data lineage and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this refund transaction. Ensures proper financial reconciliation in multi-currency operations.']
  expected_settlement_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the refund funds will be available to the customer in their account. Used for customer communication and service level agreement (SLA) tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score assigned by fraud detection system, typically ranging from 0 to 100. Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk. Used for risk-based decisioning.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund transaction was first created or initiated in the system. Marks the beginning of the refund lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the refund was identified as fraudulent or suspicious. Used for fraud analytics and loss prevention reporting.']
  merchandise_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The gross value of returned merchandise being refunded, before any fees, taxes, or adjustments. Represents the base product value from the original transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by customer service representatives or system users providing additional context about the refund transaction.']
  payment_processor varchar [note: 'Name of the payment gateway or financial institution processing the refund transaction. Used for reconciliation and troubleshooting payment failures.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was submitted to the payment processor for execution. Indicates when the financial transaction was initiated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  processor_response_code varchar [note: 'Standardized response code returned by the payment processor indicating success, failure, or specific error condition. Used for operational monitoring and troubleshooting.']
  processor_response_message varchar [note: 'Human-readable message from the payment processor providing additional context about the transaction result. Used for customer service and technical support.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for issuing the refund. Used for root cause analysis, quality management, and vendor chargeback processing. Format: AAA999.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of why the refund was issued. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code for customer service and quality analysis.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to issue the refund to the customer. Determines processing workflow and reconciliation requirements.']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the refund transaction. Used for customer communication, customer service lookup, and external reporting. Format: RFN-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction. Tracks progression from initiation through final settlement or failure. Used for operational monitoring and customer service inquiries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|processing|completed|failed|reversed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund based on the portion of the original transaction being refunded and any adjustments applied.']
  restocking_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fee deducted from the refund amount to cover costs of processing, inspecting, and restocking returned merchandise. Applied per return policy terms.']
  shipping_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The shipping or delivery fee amount being refunded, if applicable per return policy. May be zero if shipping is non-refundable.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The sales tax or value-added tax (VAT) amount being refunded to the customer. Must align with original tax calculation and jurisdiction requirements.']
  total_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The final net amount refunded to the customer after all fees, taxes, and adjustments. This is the actual monetary value transferred back to the customer.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External transaction identifier or reference number from the payment processor. Links internal refund record to external payment system for reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this refund record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Financial refund transaction record representing the monetary reimbursement issued to a customer as resolution of a return. Captures refund method (original payment method, store credit, gift card, check), refund amount, restocking fee deducted, original transaction reference, refund authorization code, processing timestamp, refund status (pending, processed, failed, reversed), and payment processor response. Integrates with financial accounting for accounts payable and revenue reversal under ASC 606.'
}

Table returns.exchange_order {
  exchange_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the exchange order. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service representative who processed or approved the exchange order.']
  fulfillment_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center, store, or fulfillment location from which the replacement item will be shipped.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store location where the exchange was initiated or will be fulfilled, if applicable.']
  primary_exchange_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original order from which the returned item originated.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who initiated the exchange.']
  replacement_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Exchange fulfillment requires direct replacement SKU reference for inventory allocation, ATP (available-to-promise) checking, and like-for-like exchange policy enforcement. Essential for retail exchan']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating RMA that triggered this exchange order.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Exchange orders require a shipping address for replacement item delivery. Fulfillment operations depend on this link to route exchange shipments correctly. While exchange_order.profile_id provides ind']
  additional_payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment method used by the customer to cover any price differential for an upgrade exchange.']
  additional_payment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must provide additional payment due to a price differential (upgrade scenario).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exchange order record was first created in the system.']
  exchange_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exchange request was approved by the returns management system or customer service representative.']
  exchange_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the entire exchange transaction was finalized, including receipt of returned item and delivery of replacement.']
  exchange_delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replacement item was successfully delivered to the customer.']
  exchange_initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the customer first requested the exchange, typically when the RMA was created.']
  exchange_order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the exchange order, used for customer communication and tracking.']
  exchange_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exchange order in the fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|processing|shipped|delivered|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exchange_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason the customer requested an exchange rather than a refund.']
  exchange_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about why the customer requested an exchange.']
  exchange_shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replacement item was shipped to the customer.']
  exchange_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer initiated the exchange request.']
  exchange_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the exchange based on the relationship between returned and replacement items.']
  expedited_shipping_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether expedited shipping was used for the replacement item delivery.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'Method by which the replacement item will be delivered to the customer.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exchange order record was most recently modified.']
  price_differential_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net price difference between the replacement and returned items. Positive indicates customer owes additional payment (upgrade), negative indicates refund due (downgrade).']
  price_differential_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the price differential amount.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount to be refunded to the customer if the replacement item costs less than the returned item (downgrade scenario).']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which any refund amount will be returned to the customer.']
  replacement_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the replacement SKU being sent to the customer.']
  returned_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the returned SKU being sent back by the customer.']
  returned_sku varchar [note: 'SKU of the product being returned by the customer as part of the exchange.']
  shipping_cost_waived_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shipping costs for the replacement item have been waived as a customer service gesture.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special handling instructions or notes related to the exchange order.']

  Note: 'Exchange order created when a return is resolved via product replacement rather than monetary refund. Captures the originating RMA reference, replacement SKU(s) and quantities, price differential (upgrade or downgrade), exchange order status, fulfillment channel (ship-to-home, in-store pickup, curbside), and the linked replacement order identifier. Enables tracking of exchange fulfillment as a distinct resolution path separate from standard order flow.'
}

Table returns.rtv_line {
  rtv_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RTV line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Return-to-vendor shipments originate from DC facilities. Tracking the source facility is required for outbound dock scheduling, carrier coordination, vendor credit reconciliation, and RTV performance ']
  product_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the master product record. Links to the product master data for detailed product attributes.']
  rtv_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent RTV header document. Links this line item to the overall RTV transaction.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: RTV (Return to Vendor) processing requires direct SKU reference for vendor chargeback reconciliation, defective product tracking, and cost recovery analysis. Critical for AP credit memo matching and v']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the vendor receiving the returned merchandise. Links to the supplier master data.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the warehouse or distribution center location where the returned merchandise is staged. Links to the facility master data.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Vendor compliance penalty or chargeback amount applied to this line. Represents deductions for vendor non-compliance with quality, packaging, or delivery standards.']
  chargeback_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the vendor chargeback. Used for vendor performance tracking and compliance enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality_defect|packaging_violation|labeling_error|late_delivery|quantity_shortage|unauthorized_substitution|compliance_violation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Condition of the merchandise being returned. Indicates whether items are damaged, defective, expired, obsolete, or simply overstock in good condition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RTV line record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  credit_issued_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor issued the credit for this returned merchandise. Marks completion of the financial settlement for this line.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-issued credit memo document number for this return. Links the RTV line to the vendor\'s accounts payable credit transaction.']
  credit_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between expected and actual vendor credit amounts. Calculated as actual credit total minus expected credit total. Used for vendor credit reconciliation and dispute management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amounts. Indicates the currency in which unit cost and extended cost are denominated.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final disposition action for the returned merchandise. Indicates whether items will be returned to vendor, destroyed, liquidated, donated, or restocked. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: return_to_vendor|destroy|liquidate|donate|rework|restock|quarantine — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date for perishable or time-sensitive items. Relevant for returns due to short shelf life or expired merchandise.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost for this line item calculated as return quantity multiplied by unit cost. Represents the total value of merchandise being returned.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RTV line record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the RTV document. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this RTV line item. Tracks progression from pending through shipment, vendor receipt, and credit issuance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|shipped|received_by_vendor|credited|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot or batch number for the returned product. Critical for traceability in recalls and quality investigations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments related to this RTV line item. Captures additional context, special handling instructions, or dispute details.']
  original_po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number under which this item was originally received. Links the return to the original procurement transaction for traceability.']
  original_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when this merchandise was originally received from the vendor. Used to calculate return window compliance and aging analysis.']
  return_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units being returned to the vendor. Represents the number of items shipped back in this line.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for returning this item to the vendor. Used for vendor performance tracking and root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|damaged_in_transit|quality_issue|expired|slow_moving|discontinued|overstock|vendor_error|recall — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation of why this item is being returned. Provides additional context beyond the standardized reason code.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized items being returned. Provides item-level traceability for high-value or regulated products.']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for the shipment containing this returned item. Enables tracking of merchandise in transit back to vendor.']
  shipped_date date [note: 'Date when this line item was physically shipped back to the vendor. Marks the beginning of the return transit period.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for the returned item. Represents the original purchase cost or agreed-upon return credit value per unit.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the return quantity. Indicates whether quantity is in eaches, cases, pallets, or other packaging units.']
  vendor_credit_actual_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Actual credit amount per unit that the vendor issued. Represents the confirmed reimbursement value per item received from vendor.']
  vendor_credit_actual_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual credit amount for this line received from the vendor. Represents the confirmed total vendor reimbursement for this line item.']
  vendor_credit_expected_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Expected credit amount per unit that the vendor should issue. Represents the anticipated reimbursement value per item based on return agreement terms.']
  vendor_credit_expected_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total expected credit amount for this line calculated as return quantity multiplied by expected credit per unit. Represents the total anticipated vendor reimbursement.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s own product identifier for this item. Used for cross-referencing with vendor systems and purchase orders.']
  vendor_received_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor confirmed receipt of the returned merchandise. Used to calculate transit time and trigger credit processing.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for a Return to Vendor (RTV) request, capturing each individual SKU being returned to the supplier. Records the SKU, UPC, GTIN, quantity shipped back, unit cost, extended cost, condition code, vendor credit expected per unit, actual credit received, and variance amount. Enables granular vendor credit reconciliation and chargeback tracking at the item level.'
}

Table returns.return_policy {
  return_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return policy record. Primary key.']
  compliance_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Return policies must align with corporate compliance policies governing consumer protection laws, FTC regulations, and state-specific return rights. Real business process: policy governance ensures re']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Return policies vary by merchandise category (electronics 30-day vs apparel 90-day vs grocery no-return). Essential for POS policy enforcement, return window calculation, restocking fee application, a']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_program. Business justification: Return policy compliance training programs are mandatory for customer-facing associates to ensure adherence to consumer protection laws and corporate policies. Real business process: policy training r']
  accepted_condition varchar [note: 'Required condition of merchandise for return acceptance. Defines whether items must be unopened, unused, or can be returned in any condition.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether returns under this policy require manager or supervisor approval before processing. Common for high-value items or out-of-window returns.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this return policy record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return policy record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and policy lifecycle tracking.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment or loyalty tier to which this return policy applies. Premium members may receive extended return windows.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this return policy expires and is no longer applicable to new transactions. Nullable for open-ended policies.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this return policy becomes active and applicable to transactions. Policies are not enforced before this date.']
  exchange_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct product exchanges (size, color, model) are permitted under this policy without requiring a full return and repurchase.']
  extended_window_days varchar [note: 'Extended return window duration for premium loyalty tier members or special promotional periods. Nullable if no extension applies.']
  holiday_extension_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy includes extended return windows for holiday season purchases (e.g., purchases made in November-December returnable through mid-January).']
  inspection_sla_hours varchar [note: 'Target time in hours for completing merchandise inspection and condition assessment after return receipt. Used for operational SLA measurement.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this return policy record. Used for audit and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return policy record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  liquidation_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether returned merchandise under this policy can be sent to liquidation channels (discount outlets, bulk resellers) for recovery.']
  non_returnable_item_exceptions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of item types or SKU patterns that are explicitly excluded from returns under this policy (e.g., perishables, intimate apparel, custom orders, clearance items).']
  original_packaging_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item must be returned in its original packaging for acceptance. Common for electronics and high-value items.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the return policy, used in operational systems and customer communications. Unique alphanumeric code.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed text description of the return policy terms and conditions, including any special provisions, exclusions, or customer-facing language.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the return policy (e.g., \'Standard 30-Day Return\', \'Electronics Extended Return\', \'Final Sale No Return\').']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return policy indicating the policy category and applicability scope.']
  policy_url varchar [note: 'Web URL where the full return policy terms are published for customer reference. Required for e-commerce compliance.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level for processing returns under this policy. Premium and VIP customers may receive expedited processing.']
  proof_of_purchase_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proof of purchase (receipt, order confirmation, transaction lookup) is mandatory for return processing.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which refunds are issued under this policy. May restrict refunds to store credit or exchange for certain categories or conditions.']
  refund_processing_sla_hours varchar [note: 'Target time in hours for completing refund processing from return acceptance to funds disbursement. Used for operational SLA measurement.']
  restocking_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a restocking fee is charged for returns under this policy. Common for high-value electronics and special-order items.']
  restocking_fee_flat_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount charged as a restocking fee, regardless of purchase price. Nullable if percentage-based or no fee applies.']
  restocking_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the original purchase price charged as a restocking fee. Nullable if no restocking fee applies. Typical range is 10-25%.']
  restocking_sla_hours varchar [note: 'Target time in hours for restocking accepted returned merchandise back into available inventory. Used for operational SLA measurement.']
