  return_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return policy. Only active policies are applied to new transactions.']
  return_shipping_paid_by varchar [note: 'Party responsible for paying return shipping costs. Retailer-paid shipping is common for defective items or retailer errors; customer-paid for buyer remorse.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from purchase date during which a return is accepted under this policy. Standard retail windows range from 14 to 90 days.']
  rma_auto_approval_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RMA requests are automatically approved based on policy rules without manual review. Drives automated return authorization logic.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) to which this return policy applies. Policies may vary by channel (e.g., online purchases may have different return windows than in-store). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store|online|mobile_app|bopis|ropis|call_center|all_channels — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  store_credit_expiry_days varchar [note: 'Number of days until store credit issued for returns expires. Nullable if store credit does not expire. Subject to state-specific gift card laws.']
  vendor_return_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether merchandise returned under this policy is eligible for Return to Vendor (RTV) processing to recover costs from suppliers.']

  Note: 'Master record defining return policy rules by merchandise category, sales channel, and customer segment. Captures return window duration, restocking fee rules, accepted return conditions, proof-of-purchase requirements, non-returnable item exceptions, channel applicability (in-store, online, BOPIS), loyalty tier overrides (extended windows for premium members), SLA processing targets by return stage, and policy effective/expiry dates. Drives automated RMA authorization logic and operational SLA measurement.'
}

Table returns.restock_event {
  restock_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the restock event. Primary key for this operational record capturing the restocking of a returned item back into sellable or clearance inventory.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Restocking returned merchandise into DC inventory requires linking to the specific facility for putaway task routing, bin assignment, inventory ledger posting, and cycle count reconciliation. Critical']
  disposition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the disposition decision record that determined this item should be restocked. Links to the disposition workflow that evaluated the returned merchandise condition and routing.']
  inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Restock events occur at specific nodes (DC, store, warehouse). Essential for location-level inventory reconciliation, return processing KPIs by facility, and capacity planning. Retail operations track']
  restock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific store, distribution center, or fulfillment node where the item was restocked into inventory. Links to the location master data.']
  restocked_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or store associate who performed the restocking action. Links to the workforce master data for accountability and productivity tracking.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating return merchandise authorization that triggered this restock event. Links the restock back to the customer return or vendor return transaction.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Restock operations require SKU reference for inventory accuracy, markdown decision workflows, and return-to-stock cycle time measurement. Critical for retail inventory management, condition-based pric']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Restocking returned merchandise updates specific inventory positions at bin/location level. Critical for real-time ATP calculation, inventory accuracy, and reconciling physical stock after return proc']
  aisle_number varchar [note: 'The aisle identifier where the restocked item was placed on the sales floor or in the warehouse. Supports store operations and inventory locating.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'The specific bin, shelf, or storage location identifier within the warehouse or store where the item was placed during restocking. Used for precise inventory tracking and picking operations.']
  condition_grade varchar [note: 'The assessed condition grade of the returned item at the time of restocking. Determines whether the item goes back to full-price inventory, clearance, or requires further disposition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this restock event record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this restock event. Supports multi-currency operations for international retail.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-by date for perishable or time-sensitive products being restocked. Critical for food safety, pharmaceutical compliance, and inventory rotation (FIFO/FEFO).']
  inspection_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether required quality inspection has been completed for the restocked item. True when inspection is finished and item is cleared for sale.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the restocked item requires additional quality inspection before being made available for sale. True for items with condition concerns or regulatory requirements.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when quality inspection was completed for the restocked item. Null if no inspection was required or inspection is still pending.']
  inventory_adjustment_posted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inventory adjustment transaction has been successfully posted to the warehouse management system and inventory ledger. True when the on-hand quantity has been updated.']
  inventory_adjustment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the inventory adjustment transaction was posted to the WMS and inventory systems. May differ from restock_timestamp if there is a processing delay.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'The inventory status assigned to the restocked item. Determines whether the item is available for sale at full price, marked for clearance, held for inspection, or quarantined.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer lot or batch number for the restocked item. Critical for traceability of food, pharmaceutical, and regulated products subject to recall management.']
  markdown_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of price reduction applied to the restocked item if it was moved to clearance or discounted inventory. Zero if restocked at full price.']
  markdown_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the restocked item relative to its original retail price. Zero if no markdown was applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the restock event. Captures special handling instructions, condition observations, or operational exceptions noted by the restocking associate.']
  original_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'The original cost of goods sold (COGS) for the restocked item. Used for inventory valuation and financial reconciliation of returned merchandise.']
  quantity_restocked decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of units restocked back into inventory in this event. Supports fractional quantities for items sold by weight or volume.']
  restock_location_type varchar [note: 'The type of facility where the item was restocked. Indicates whether restocking occurred at a retail store, distribution center (DC), micro-fulfillment center (MFC), dark store, or was returned to vendor.']
  restock_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code explaining why this item was restocked. Distinguishes between customer returns that passed inspection, vendor returns, warranty exchanges, and other restock scenarios.']
  restock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the restocking action was completed and the item was placed back into available inventory. This is the principal business event timestamp for the restock transaction.']
  restocked_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The inventory value assigned to the restocked item after any markdown or condition adjustment. Represents the expected recoverable value of the returned merchandise.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number for serialized items such as electronics, appliances, or high-value goods. Enables individual unit tracking and warranty management.']
  shelf_position varchar [note: 'The specific shelf or position identifier within the aisle where the item was restocked. Enables precise planogram compliance and inventory locating.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this restock event record. Identifies whether the event was captured by the warehouse management system (WMS), order management system (OMS), retail merchandising system (RMS), point of sale (POS), or entered manually.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier from the source system that generated this restock event. Enables traceability back to the originating system record for audit and reconciliation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the restocked quantity. Defines whether the quantity represents individual units, cases, pallets, or weight/volume measures.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this restock event record was last modified in the data platform. Audit field for tracking record changes and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing the restocking of a returned item back into sellable or clearance inventory. Records the SKU, quantity restocked, restocking location (store, DC, MFC), bin/shelf location, restocking timestamp, condition grade at restock, restocking associate, markdown applied (if any), and the originating RMA and disposition references. Triggers inventory adjustment transactions to update on-hand quantities in the warehouse management and inventory systems.'
}

Table returns.liquidation_batch {
  liquidation_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the liquidation batch record. Primary key for the liquidation batch entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Liquidation operations costs (handling, transportation, partner fees) allocated to reverse logistics or loss prevention cost centers. Required for departmental expense tracking, budget variance analys']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or warehouse location from which the liquidation batch merchandise originated and was consolidated.']
  environmental_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_event. Business justification: Liquidation batches containing hazmat, electronics, or regulated materials trigger environmental compliance obligations (manifest tracking, disposal documentation, EPA reporting). Required for regulat']
  liquidation_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Liquidation batches require GL postings for inventory write-offs, loss recognition, and recovery proceeds. The existing gl_posting_reference indicates financial posting but lacks proper FK. Essential ']
  primary_liquidation_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the liquidation batch record, supporting audit trail and accountability requirements.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Large-scale liquidation of regulated products (electronics with WEEE obligations, hazmat, recalled items) triggers regulatory filings (EPA manifests, state environmental reports). Real business proces']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party liquidation partner, bulk buyer, auction house, or marketplace handling the sale of this batch.']
  batch_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the liquidation batch was approved for release to the liquidation partner, marking the transition from internal review to external sale process.']
  batch_creation_date date [note: 'Date when the liquidation batch record was initially created in the system and merchandise consolidation began.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the liquidation batch, used for tracking and reference in operational systems and communications with liquidation partners.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the liquidation batch indicating its position in the reverse logistics workflow from creation through final sale completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_transit|received|sold|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  buyer_feedback varchar [note: 'Feedback provided by the liquidation partner or buyer regarding the batch quality, accuracy, and condition upon receipt, used for continuous improvement.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the liquidation batch was cancelled if status is cancelled, documenting the business rationale for not proceeding with the sale.']
  category_mix varchar [note: 'Summary description of the product categories and merchandise types included in this liquidation batch, helping buyers assess assortment fit.']
  condition_grade_mix varchar [note: 'Summary description of the mix of merchandise condition grades (A-New through F-Unsellable) included in this batch, providing buyers with quality expectations.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this liquidation batch record.']
  final_sale_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual price at which the liquidation batch was sold to the liquidation partner or buyer, representing the gross recovery amount before fees and expenses.']
  gl_posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger journal entry or posting that recorded the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) write-off and recovery accounting for this liquidation batch.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from quality inspection and consolidation activities, documenting any issues, discrepancies, or special handling requirements for the batch.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the liquidation batch contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, transportation, and disposal compliance.']
  liquidation_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel or method through which the returned merchandise in this batch is being liquidated, determining the sales approach and partner type.']
  liquidation_fees decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total fees charged by the liquidation partner, auction house, or marketplace for handling the sale, including commission, listing fees, and transaction charges.']
  liquidation_partner_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier provided by the liquidation partner for tracking this batch in their system.']
  manifest_document_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the detailed item-level manifest document listing all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units), quantities, and condition grades in the liquidation batch.']
  net_recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net proceeds from the liquidation sale after deducting all fees, transportation costs, and other expenses from the final sale price. Used for shrinkage and loss accounting.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets used to consolidate and ship the liquidation batch merchandise, supporting logistics planning and handling requirements.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the liquidation partner or buyer confirmed receipt of the batch merchandise, triggering inspection and sale preparation activities.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this liquidation batch record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this liquidation batch record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  recovery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of original cost value recovered through the liquidation sale, calculated as (net_recovery_amount / total_cost_value) * 100. Key performance indicator for reverse logistics efficiency.']
  requires_data_destruction boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the batch contains electronic devices or media requiring certified data destruction before liquidation to protect customer privacy and comply with data protection regulations.']
  reserve_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable sale price set for the liquidation batch, below which the merchandise will not be sold. Used primarily in auction and negotiated sale scenarios.']
  sale_date date [note: 'Date when the liquidation batch was sold to the final buyer, marking the completion of the liquidation transaction and triggering financial settlement.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when payment for the liquidation sale was received and financial settlement was completed, finalizing the recovery accounting.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Date when the liquidation batch merchandise was shipped from the originating distribution center to the liquidation partner or buyer.']
  tax_write_off_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount eligible for tax deduction related to the liquidation loss, calculated as the difference between cost value and recovery amount, supporting tax accounting and compliance.']
  total_cost_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) value for all merchandise units in the batch, representing the original acquisition cost basis for accounting and write-off calculations.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total count of individual merchandise units included in this liquidation batch across all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) and condition grades.']
  transportation_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost incurred for shipping and transporting the liquidation batch from the originating distribution center to the liquidation partner or buyer location.']
  volume_cubic_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total volume of the liquidation batch merchandise in cubic meters, used for warehouse space planning and transportation capacity requirements.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total weight of the liquidation batch merchandise in kilograms, used for transportation planning and cost calculation.']

  Note: 'Batch record grouping returned merchandise designated for liquidation sale to third-party buyers or auction channels. Captures batch number, liquidation channel (auction, bulk buyer, online marketplace), total units, total cost value, reserve and final sale price, liquidation partner reference, batch and sale dates, and net recovery amount. Includes item-level detail: individual SKU, UPC, quantity, condition grade (A-New through F-Unsellable), estimated and actual recovery value per unit, and originating RMA and disposition references. Supports reverse logistics cost recovery tracking, COGS write-off calculations, and shrinkage accounting at both batch and item granularity.'
}

Table returns.liquidation_item {
  liquidation_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the liquidation item line within a liquidation batch. Primary key.']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: Liquidation recovery rate calculation and loss accounting require linking to original landed cost basis. Essential for financial reporting and liquidation partner negotiations. Removes denormalized un']
  liquidation_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent liquidation batch that this item belongs to. Groups items sold together in a single liquidation event.']
  liquidation_partner_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the third-party liquidation partner or buyer. Used for partner performance tracking and relationship management.']
  lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot. Business justification: Liquidation of lot-tracked items requires lot linkage for vendor credit negotiation, compliance documentation, and batch-level recovery analysis. Retail operations need lot traceability for liquidatio']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Liquidation batch processing tracks SKU-level recovery values for financial loss accounting, category liquidation performance analysis, and disposition strategy optimization. Essential for retail shri']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original supplier or vendor of this product. Used for vendor chargeback analysis and supplier quality tracking.']
  actual_recovery_value_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual amount recovered per unit after liquidation sale completion. Used for variance analysis and recovery rate tracking.']
  actual_total_recovery_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total actual recovery amount for this line item (quantity × actual recovery value per unit). Used for final P&L impact calculation.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Brand or manufacturer name of the liquidated product. Used for brand-level recovery rate analysis and vendor negotiations.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Product category or merchandise hierarchy classification. Enables category-level liquidation and recovery analysis.']
  condition_grade varchar [note: 'Physical condition assessment of the returned merchandise. Drives disposition decisions and recovery value expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|like_new|good|fair|poor|damaged|defective|unsellable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this liquidation item record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record. Supports multi-currency liquidation operations.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision that led to liquidation. Indicates why this item was routed to liquidation rather than restocking or vendor return.']
  estimated_recovery_value_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected recovery amount per unit based on condition grade and market conditions at time of liquidation planning. Used for financial forecasting.']
  estimated_total_recovery_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total projected recovery amount for this line item (quantity × estimated recovery value per unit). Used for P&L forecasting.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this item within the liquidation batch. Used for ordering and reference.']
  liquidation_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel or method used to liquidate this item. Enables channel performance analysis and optimization.']
  loss_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net loss on this line item (total cost minus actual total recovery value). Used for COGS write-off and shrinkage reporting.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of this SKU included in the liquidation batch. Used for inventory reconciliation and recovery calculations.']
  recovery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of original cost recovered through liquidation (actual recovery ÷ total cost × 100). Key performance indicator for reverse logistics efficiency.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Original reason code from the customer return that led to this liquidation. Used for root cause analysis of liquidation drivers.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Originating RMA number that authorized the return of this merchandise. Provides traceability back to the customer return event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this liquidation item record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'Distribution center or warehouse location where the item was held prior to liquidation. Used for facility-level liquidation tracking.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of this line item in kilograms. Used for logistics cost allocation and freight calculations.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a liquidation batch, capturing each individual SKU included in the liquidation sale. Records the SKU, UPC, quantity, unit cost, condition grade, estimated recovery value per unit, actual recovery value per unit, and the originating RMA and disposition references. Enables item-level recovery tracking and COGS write-off calculations.'
}

Table returns.return_shipment {
  return_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return shipment record. Primary key for the return shipment entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier handling the return shipment. Links to carrier master data for service level and rate information.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier_service. Business justification: Return shipments track carrier service level (ground, 2-day, overnight) for cost allocation, carrier performance analysis by service tier, and reverse logistics SLA compliance. Denormalized carrier_se']
  destination_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific location where the return shipment is delivered. May link to store, DC, vendor, or liquidation center depending on destination_type.']
  origin_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Return shipments require customer\'s origin address for carrier pickup scheduling and routing in reverse logistics. While return_shipment has origin_location_id for facilities, customer-initiated retur']
  origin_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific location where the return shipment originates. May link to store, DC, or customer address depending on origin_type.']
  return_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent return order that this shipment fulfills. Links the physical shipment to the return authorization.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'FK to fulfillment.shipment.shipment_id — Return shipment tracking requires joining return_shipment to the carrier/tracking data in fulfillment.shipment. Customer service agents need this for return status inquiries.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the return shipment was confirmed delivered. Used for RTV confirmation, refund processing triggers, and carrier SLA measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return shipment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  delivery_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signature is required upon delivery. Typically true for high-value RTV shipments to vendors.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment destination location. Identifies the final disposition point for the returned merchandise.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Carrier-provided expected delivery date for the return shipment. Used for receipt planning and exception management.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of shipment exceptions or special handling instructions. Used for exception resolution and audit trail.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of delivery exception if shipment encountered issues. Examples include damaged, lost, refused, address invalid.']
  insurance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared value for shipment insurance coverage. Used for high-value RTV shipments to protect against loss or damage.']
  insurance_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the insurance amount. Enables multi-currency insurance value tracking.']
  label_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of the prepaid return shipping label. Used for customer return cost analysis and vendor RTV cost allocation.']
  label_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the return label cost. Enables multi-currency label cost tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return shipment record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  origin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment origin location. Identifies whether the return is initiated from customer, store, distribution center, or vendor.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of packages in this return shipment. Used for multi-box shipment tracking and receipt verification.']
  proof_of_delivery_url varchar [note: 'Link to the carrier-provided proof of delivery document. Used for RTV confirmation and dispute resolution.']
  return_label_number varchar [note: 'Prepaid return shipping label identifier. Used to match customer-initiated returns to the original order.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-issued RMA number for RTV shipments. Required for vendor acceptance and chargeback processing.']
  rtv_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for returning merchandise to the vendor. Used for vendor performance tracking and chargeback justification.']
  ship_date date [note: 'The date the return shipment was tendered to the carrier. Used for transit time calculation and carrier performance measurement.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return shipment. Tracks progression from label generation through final delivery.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return shipment flow. Identifies the origin and destination pattern for reverse logistics routing.']
  shipping_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the carrier for this return shipment. Used for reverse logistics cost allocation and vendor chargeback calculation.']
  shipping_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the shipping cost. Enables multi-currency return shipment cost tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that created this return shipment record. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total volumetric measurement of the return shipment in cubic meters. Used for dimensional weight pricing and warehouse space planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the return shipment in kilograms. Used for carrier rating and capacity planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier assigned by the carrier. Used for shipment visibility and proof of delivery.']

  Note: 'Shipment record for the physical movement of returned merchandise across all return flows: customer mail-in returns (with prepaid label tracking), store-to-DC consolidation shipments, and store/DC-to-vendor RTV shipments. Captures carrier, service level, tracking number, ship and delivery dates, package dimensions, shipping label cost, and shipment status. Essential for return receipt matching, RTV delivery confirmation, and carrier cost allocation in reverse logistics.'
}

Table returns.store_credit {
  store_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the store credit instrument. Primary key.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original order that was returned and resulted in this store credit issuance. Provides full traceability from purchase through return to credit.']
  liability_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Store credits create deferred revenue liability requiring specific GL account tracking for balance sheet reporting, breakage estimation, and escheatment compliance. Replaces denormalized liability_acc']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical store location where the store credit was issued. Null for credits issued through digital channels.']
  primary_store_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who processed the store credit issuance. Supports audit trail and employee performance tracking.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer to whom this store credit was issued. Enables customer-level credit balance tracking and redemption history.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating RMA that triggered this store credit issuance. Links the credit back to the return transaction.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Machine-readable barcode representation of the store credit code for POS scanning. Supports omnichannel redemption workflows.']
  breakage_estimate_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a breakage revenue estimate (expected non-redemption) has been applied to this store credit for financial reporting purposes under ASC 606.']
  combinable_with_promotions_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store credit can be combined with other promotional discounts during redemption. Supports pricing and promotion policy enforcement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the store credit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique alphanumeric code printed on the store credit instrument or provided digitally. Used for redemption at POS or e-commerce checkout.']
  credit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason or business context for issuing this store credit. Distinguishes between return-driven credits, promotional incentives, and service recovery gestures.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the store credit monetary values. Supports multi-currency retail operations.']
  escheatment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the unredeemed balance must be reported and remitted to the state as unclaimed property. Null if not subject to escheatment.']
  escheatment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unredeemed balance is subject to state escheatment (unclaimed property) laws. Supports regulatory compliance for abandoned store credits.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the store credit expires and can no longer be redeemed. Nullable for credits with no expiration policy.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the store credit was issued to the customer. Marks the start of the credit\'s validity period.']
  issued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original monetary value of the store credit at the time of issuance. Represents the full stored-value balance before any redemptions.']
  issuing_channel varchar [note: 'The customer-facing channel through which the store credit was issued. Supports omnichannel returns and credit issuance analytics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the store credit record was most recently updated. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_redemption_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the most recent partial or full redemption transaction. Null if the credit has never been redeemed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or customer service notes related to the store credit issuance or redemption.']
  pin_code varchar [note: 'Optional security PIN required for redemption of the store credit. Protects against unauthorized use if the credit code is compromised.']
  redemption_channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Policy constraint on where the store credit can be redeemed. Supports business rules for channel-specific credits.']
  remaining_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current unredeemed monetary value available on the store credit. Decreases with each partial redemption until fully redeemed or expired.']
  store_credit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the store credit. Tracks progression from issuance through redemption, expiration, or voiding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: issued|active|partially_redeemed|fully_redeemed|expired|voided|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store credit can be transferred or gifted to another customer. False means the credit is locked to the original recipient.']
  void_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the store credit was voided. Null if the credit has not been voided.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason the store credit was voided. Null if the credit has not been voided.']

  Note: 'Store credit instrument issued as return resolution in lieu of cash or card refund. Functions as a stored-value financial instrument with its own lifecycle: issuance, partial redemption, full redemption, expiration, or voiding. Captures credit code, issued and remaining balance, issue/expiry dates, issuing channel and location, customer and originating RMA references. Integrates with POS and e-commerce systems for omnichannel redemption.'
}

Table returns.vendor_credit {
  vendor_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor credit memo record. Primary key for the vendor credit entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor credit processing and dispute resolution costs allocated to procurement or supply chain cost centers. Required for operational expense tracking, budget management, and supplier relationship cos']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor credits post to AP GL accounts for RTV (Return to Vendor) transactions. Critical for month-end close, AP reconciliation, and three-way match validation. Replaces denormalized gl_account_code wi']
  primary_vendor_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer or procurement employee responsible for managing this vendor credit and RTV process. Identifies the business owner of the credit transaction.']
  quaternary_vendor_last_modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the system user who last modified this vendor credit record. Provides audit trail for record changes.']
  rtv_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RTV request that triggered this vendor credit. Links the credit memo to the original return authorization.']
  tertiary_vendor_created_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the system user who created this vendor credit record. Provides audit trail for record creation.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who issued this credit memo. Identifies the party providing the credit.']
  ap_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounts payable document or transaction where this vendor credit was applied. Links the credit to the financial system posting.']
  chargeback_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to any vendor compliance chargeback that this credit is offsetting or related to. Links credits to vendor penalty reversals.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor credit record was first created in the system. Represents the initial capture of the credit memo information.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the credit issued by the vendor. Represents the amount to be credited to the retailer\'s accounts payable or applied against future purchases.']
  credit_applied_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit was applied to the retailer\'s accounts payable ledger or offset against vendor invoices.']
  credit_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor issued the credit memo. This is the official credit issuance date from the vendor\'s perspective.']
  credit_expiration_date date [note: 'The date by which the vendor credit must be utilized or applied. After this date, the credit may no longer be valid per vendor terms.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'The unique credit memo number issued by the vendor. This is the vendor\'s external reference for the credit document.']
  credit_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the retailer received notification or documentation of the vendor credit. May differ from the credit issuance date.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor credit in the credit lifecycle. Tracks whether the credit has been received from the vendor, applied to accounts payable, or is under dispute.']
  credit_type varchar [note: 'The type of credit being issued by the vendor. Indicates whether the vendor is providing full monetary credit, partial credit, replacement goods in lieu of credit, or other credit arrangements.']
  credit_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between expected credit amount and actual credit amount. Positive variance indicates the retailer received more credit than expected; negative indicates less.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount. Identifies the currency in which the vendor credit is denominated.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for dispute if the credit status is disputed. Captures why the retailer is contesting the vendor\'s credit amount or terms.']
  dispute_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed text description of the dispute reason. Provides additional context for credit disputes requiring vendor negotiation or resolution.']
  expected_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit amount that the retailer expected to receive based on the RTV request and vendor agreement. Used for variance analysis when actual credit differs from expected.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor credit record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to credit status, amounts, or other attributes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text notes regarding this vendor credit. Captures additional context, special arrangements, dispute details, or processing instructions.']
  original_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The vendor invoice number that this credit is being applied against. Provides traceability to the original purchase transaction.']
  original_po_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number associated with the original merchandise that was returned to the vendor. Links the credit to the original procurement transaction.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms associated with this credit, such as net 30, net 60, or immediate application. Defines when and how the credit can be utilized.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The restocking fee charged by the vendor and deducted from the credit amount. Represents the vendor\'s cost for processing and restocking returned merchandise.']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the vendor contact for this credit memo. Used for communication regarding credit status, disputes, or reconciliation.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor representative who issued or authorized this credit memo. Provides a point of contact for credit inquiries or disputes.']
  vendor_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the vendor contact for this credit memo. Provides an alternative communication channel for urgent credit issues.']

  Note: 'Financial credit memo record issued by a supplier/vendor in response to an approved RTV request. Captures the vendor reference, RTV request reference, credit memo number issued by the vendor, credit amount, credit date, credit type (full credit, partial credit, replacement goods), credit status (pending, received, applied, disputed), and the accounts payable document reference for credit application. Enables vendor credit reconciliation and chargeback management.'
}

Table returns.return_fraud_case {
  return_fraud_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return fraud investigation case.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Compliance audits discovering patterns of fraudulent returns generate audit findings that spawn fraud investigations. Real business process: audit-driven fraud detection where findings trigger case cr']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fraud investigation costs (labor, third-party services, legal fees) and civil recovery proceeds allocated to loss prevention cost center. Required for shrink expense tracking, ROI analysis on fraud pr']
  detection_location_id varchar [note: 'Store or fulfillment center where the suspected fraud was detected or where the fraudulent return was attempted.']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.pos_transaction. Business justification: Retail fraud investigation teams analyze receipt-based return fraud (wardrobing, receipt fraud, serial returners) requiring direct access to original POS transaction details - tender type, associate, ']
  primary_return_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loss prevention or fraud investigation employee assigned to this case.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the suspected fraudulent return activity.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Fraud investigations track promotion abuse patterns including serial returners exploiting promotional pricing, coupon fraud rings, and policy violations. Critical for fraud pattern analysis, promotion']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Primary RMA record linked to this fraud case. A single fraud case may involve multiple RMAs tracked separately.']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Organized retail crime or regulatory violations (e.g., gift card fraud triggering state AG investigation) result in violation notices linked to fraud cases. Real business process: regulatory enforceme']
  case_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud investigation case was formally closed with a final disposition.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the fraud case, used for tracking and reference in loss prevention operations.']
  case_opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the formal fraud investigation case was opened and assigned for review.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fraud investigation case. Open indicates initial detection; under investigation means active review is in progress; pending review awaits management decision; closed confirmed means fraud was verified; closed unsubstantiated means insufficient evidence; escalated indicates senior management involvement; law enforcement referred means case was submitted to authorities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_investigation|pending_review|closed_confirmed|closed_unsubstantiated|escalated|law_enforcement_referred — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  civil_recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount sought or recovered through civil demand letter or restitution process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fraud case record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated fraud value amount.']
  customer_return_count_90d varchar [note: 'Total number of returns initiated by this customer in the 90 days prior to case detection, used to identify serial returner patterns.']
  customer_return_value_90d decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of returns initiated by this customer in the 90 days prior to case detection.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the suspected fraud was initially identified. Associate flag indicates store employee reported suspicious activity; automated rule means system-based detection triggered; loss prevention review indicates proactive audit; customer service escalation means case originated from service interaction; analytics model indicates AI/ML detection; third party alert means external fraud service notification.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the suspected fraud was first detected or flagged in the system.']
  enforcement_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken by the retailer in response to confirmed fraud. None indicates no action; RMA denial means return was rejected; account restriction limits future returns; account suspension temporarily blocks account; account termination permanently closes account; law enforcement referral involves police; civil demand letter seeks restitution; store ban prohibits physical store access. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|rma_denial|account_restriction|account_suspension|account_termination|law_enforcement_referral|civil_demand_letter|store_ban — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_fraud_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the fraudulent activity in the transaction currency, used for shrinkage reporting and loss quantification.']
  evidence_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of evidence collected during the investigation, including witness statements, video surveillance references, receipt analysis, product inspection findings, and other supporting documentation.']
  fraud_indicators varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific fraud indicators or red flags that triggered the investigation, such as high return frequency, missing tags, altered receipts, mismatched serial numbers, or suspicious timing patterns.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score (0-100) calculated by fraud detection algorithms based on customer return history, transaction patterns, and behavioral indicators. Higher scores indicate greater fraud likelihood.']
  fraud_typology varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of return fraud detected. Wardrobing refers to purchasing items for temporary use and returning them; receipt fraud involves fraudulent or altered receipts; empty box is returning packaging without merchandise; stolen merchandise return is attempting to return stolen goods; serial returner pattern indicates abuse through excessive returns; refund abuse involves exploiting return policies; price switching is manipulating price tags; counterfeit return is returning fake merchandise. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wardrobing|receipt_fraud|empty_box|stolen_merchandise_return|serial_returner_pattern|refund_abuse|price_switching|counterfeit_return — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  investigation_outcome varchar [note: 'Final determination of the fraud investigation. Fraud confirmed means evidence supports fraudulent intent; fraud unsubstantiated means insufficient evidence; customer error indicates unintentional mistake; policy misunderstanding means customer misinterpreted return policy; system error indicates operational or technical issue; pending means investigation incomplete.']
  investigation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the fraud case based on estimated value, customer history, and fraud typology. Critical cases involve high-value fraud or organized retail crime; high priority involves significant loss or repeat offenders; medium priority is standard investigation; low priority is minor suspected abuse.']
  investigator_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and observations recorded by the assigned investigator during the case review process.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fraud case record was most recently updated.']
  law_enforcement_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case number assigned by law enforcement agency if the fraud case was referred for criminal prosecution.']
  linked_rma_count varchar [note: 'Total number of RMA records associated with this fraud investigation, used to identify patterns across multiple return transactions.']
  management_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case requires senior management or legal review before enforcement action, typically true for high-value cases or law enforcement referrals.']
  recovery_status varchar [note: 'Status of civil recovery efforts. Not applicable means no recovery attempted; demand sent means civil demand letter issued; partial recovery indicates some payment received; full recovery means complete restitution; uncollectible means recovery efforts abandoned.']
  related_case_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of other fraud case IDs that are related to this case, used to track organized retail crime rings or coordinated fraud patterns across multiple customers or locations.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when management review was completed and case disposition was approved.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this fraud case record, such as loss prevention management system, fraud detection platform, or customer service case management system.']
  third_party_fraud_service_code varchar [note: 'External fraud detection service case identifier if the case was flagged or validated by a third-party fraud prevention vendor.']

  Note: 'Investigation record for suspected or confirmed return fraud and abuse. Captures fraud typology (wardrobing, receipt fraud, empty box, stolen merchandise return, serial returner pattern), detection method (associate flag, automated rule, loss prevention review), linked RMA(s), investigation status and outcome, enforcement action taken (RMA denial, account restriction, law enforcement referral), and estimated fraud value. Supports loss prevention operations, shrinkage reporting, and customer risk scoring.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cost center record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key generated by the lakehouse ingestion pipeline from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center (DC) associated with this cost center, when the cost center represents a DC or fulfillment location. Supports supply chain cost allocation and COGS tracking.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store associated with this cost center, when the cost center represents a store-level organizational unit. Enables store-level P&L, comp sales (SSS), and footfall-to-cost analysis.']
  primary_cost_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee designated as the responsible manager (cost center owner) for this cost center in SAP S/4HANA (field: VERAK). Accountable for budget adherence, expense approvals, and financial close sign-off.']
  actual_ytd_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual expenses posted to this cost center from the start of the fiscal year to the current reporting period. Sourced from SAP FI/CO actuals. Used for budget variance, EBITDA tracking, and financial close reporting.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate or distribute costs from this cost center to other cost objects (e.g., direct posting, SAP assessment cycle, distribution cycle, activity-based costing). Governs how overhead is spread across stores, DCs, and product categories.']
  annual_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost center for the current fiscal year, expressed in the cost center currency. Used for Open to Buy (OTB) tracking, budget vs. actuals variance analysis, and financial planning.']
  budget_fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (four-digit integer, e.g., 2025) to which the annual budget amount applies. Aligns with the SAP fiscal year variant for period-based budget vs. actuals comparison.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP Business Area (GSBER) to which this cost center is assigned. Enables cross-company-code P&L reporting by business segment (e.g., Grocery, Apparel, E-Commerce). Supports FASB ASC 280 segment disclosures.']
  commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total open purchase order and contract commitment amounts encumbered against this cost center\'s budget but not yet invoiced. Supports available budget calculation and OTB management in SAP CO-OM.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP Company Code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity to which this cost center is assigned. Used for statutory financial reporting, P&L consolidation, and FASB-compliant revenue and expense recognition.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling Area (KOKRS) to which this cost center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed. All cost centers within a controlling area share the same chart of accounts and fiscal year variant.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'SAP cost center category (KOSAR) that controls which business transactions are allowed for this cost center (e.g., \'A\' for general cost centers, \'E\' for production cost centers). Governs posting rules in FI/CO.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the cost center within the controlling area, as defined in SAP S/4HANA CO module (field: KOSTL). Used in P&L reporting, budget allocation, and expense posting.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Long-form description of the cost center\'s business purpose, scope of expenses, and organizational context. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA field LTEXT.']
  cost_center_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this cost center within the enterprise cost center hierarchy (1 = top/enterprise, higher numbers = more granular). Enables hierarchical roll-up queries for consolidated P&L and EBITDA reporting.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the cost center as maintained in SAP S/4HANA (field: KTEXT). Describes the organizational unit, e.g., \'Store 042 - Chicago Loop\', \'DC Atlanta\', \'Corporate Finance\'.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center. \'active\' = open for postings; \'locked\' = temporarily blocked for new postings (SAP lock indicator); \'inactive\' = no longer in use; \'pending\' = awaiting activation; \'archived\' = closed and retained for historical reporting.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by organizational unit type. Drives expense allocation rules, budget templates, and P&L reporting hierarchies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store|distribution_center|corporate|regional_office|ecommerce|shared_services|manufacturing|logistics — promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this cost center\'s organizational unit is physically located. Used for multi-country P&L reporting, tax jurisdiction assignment, and GDPR/CCPA data residency compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost center record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA CO). Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost center\'s controlling area currency (SAP field: WAERS). All budget, actual, and commitment amounts for this cost center are expressed in this currency.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant (PERIV) assigned to this cost center\'s controlling area. Defines the fiscal calendar (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April–March). Critical for period-based budget vs. actuals comparison and FASB-compliant financial close.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP Functional Area (FKBER) classifying the cost center by business function (e.g., Sales, Administration, Production, Logistics). Used for cost-of-sales accounting and functional P&L reporting per FASB ASC 220.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Primary General Ledger account code associated with this cost center for default expense postings. Links cost center activity to the chart of accounts in SAP FI for P&L and EBITDA reporting.']
  headcount_budget varchar [note: 'Approved headcount (number of full-time equivalent employees) budgeted for this cost center in the current fiscal year. Used for workforce cost planning, Workday HCM integration, and labor cost as a percentage of sales analysis.']
  hierarchy_area varchar [note: 'Node within the standard cost center hierarchy (SAP field: KOSAR/KHINR) that positions this cost center in the enterprise organizational tree. Used for roll-up reporting in P&L, EBITDA tracking, and budget consolidation.']
  internal_order_code varchar [note: 'SAP Internal Order (AUFNR) code linked to this cost center for detailed cost monitoring of specific activities or projects (e.g., marketing campaigns, store remodels). Enables granular expense tracking beyond standard cost center reporting.']
  is_locked_for_posting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center is currently locked against new financial postings (SAP lock indicator). True = no new postings allowed; False = postings permitted. Used during period-end close and cost center restructuring.']
  is_statistical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center is a statistical cost center (SAP field: STAT). Statistical cost centers receive postings for informational/reporting purposes only and do not carry real costs. True = statistical; False = real cost center.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this cost center record in the source system (SAP S/4HANA CO). Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and financial close reconciliation.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP Profit Center (PRCTR) to which this cost center is assigned. Enables P&L reporting at the profit center level, supporting GMROI analysis and segment-level financial performance tracking.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal regional classification code for the cost center\'s geographic location (e.g., \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST\', \'EMEA\'). Supports regional P&L roll-up, comp sales (SSS) analysis, and regional budget allocation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this cost center master record was sourced (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA\'). Supports data lineage, MDM golden record management via Informatica MDM, and audit traceability.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code assigned to this cost center for tax calculation and reporting purposes (SAP field: TXJCD). Determines applicable sales tax, VAT, and local tax rates for expense postings. Required for FTC pricing transparency and FDA compliance for grocery cost centers.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'SAP Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element code associated with this cost center for project-based cost tracking. Used when the cost center is linked to a capital project (e.g., store renovation, DC expansion) for CAPEX vs. OPEX classification per FASB ASC 842.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Start date of the cost center\'s validity period (SAP field: DATAB). The cost center is open for expense postings from this date. Supports fiscal year-aligned budget and actuals reporting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'End date of the cost center\'s validity period (SAP field: DATBI). After this date, no new postings are permitted. Null or 9999-12-31 indicates an open-ended cost center with no planned closure.']

  Note: 'Master record for organizational cost centers used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for expense allocation, P&L reporting, and budget tracking. Each cost center represents a distinct organizational unit (store, DC, corporate department) responsible for incurring costs. Includes cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area, profit center assignment, and validity periods.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the profit center master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the profit_center data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  annual_cogs_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned annual Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) budget for this profit center. Used to calculate budgeted gross margin and GMROI targets. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  annual_opex_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned annual operating expense (OPEX) budget for this profit center, excluding COGS. Includes store labor, occupancy, marketing, and overhead. Used for EBITDA planning and P&L variance reporting. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  annual_revenue_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned annual revenue target for this profit center in the profit center currency. Used for P&L variance analysis, comp sales (SSS) benchmarking, and EBITDA forecasting. Sourced from SAP CO-PCA budget planning. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  asc280_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center qualifies as a reportable segment under FASB ASC 280 (Segment Reporting) based on the 10% quantitative thresholds for revenue, profit/loss, or assets (True = reportable segment). Drives external financial disclosure requirements.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal year or fiscal year-period (YYYY or YYYY-MM) for which the current budget is active for this profit center. Used in Open to Buy (OTB) planning, financial forecasting, and variance analysis against actuals.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP business area code representing a distinct area of operations for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be drawn up (e.g., \'RETAIL\', \'WHOLESALE\', \'ECOM\'). Supports cross-company-code reporting and segment disclosures.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Retail channel classification for this profit center indicating how revenue is generated: \'physical_store\' (brick-and-mortar), \'ecommerce\' (digital), \'omnichannel\' (BOPIS/ROPIS), \'wholesale\', \'marketplace\' (third-party platform), \'dark_store\' (fulfillment-only). Enables channel-level P&L and comp sales (SSS) analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI company code representing the legal entity to which this profit center belongs. Determines the chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, and currency for financial postings. Required for intercompany transfer pricing and legal entity P&L.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling Area code to which this profit center belongs (e.g., \'US01\', \'EU01\'). The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost and profit center accounting is performed. Required for all CO-PCA postings.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center code linked to this profit center for overhead cost allocation and internal management accounting. Enables COGS and operating expense tracking at the profit center level for EBITDA calculation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which this profit center operates (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'CAN\'). Required for multi-national tax reporting, transfer pricing compliance, and GDPR/CCPA data residency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center master record was first created in the source system (SAP CO-PCA). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and financial close governance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the profit center\'s local management reporting currency (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). All revenue, COGS, gross margin, and EBITDA figures for this profit center are reported in this currency. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category (principal quantitative context).']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which this profit center was deactivated or archived. Null if the profit center is currently active. Used for historical P&L analysis, store closure reporting, and comp sales (SSS) exclusion logic.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intercompany transactions for this profit center are subject to elimination during consolidated financial reporting (True = elimination required). Used in group consolidation to remove internal P&L and balance sheet effects per FASB ASC 810.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal calendar for this profit center (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April–March). Determines period-end close dates, budget cycles, and financial reporting periods for EBITDA and P&L tracking.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP functional area code classifying the business function of this profit center (e.g., \'SALES\', \'PRODUCTION\', \'ADMIN\', \'DISTRIBUTION\'). Used for cost-of-sales accounting and P&L by function reporting per IFRS/GAAP.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Default General Ledger (GL) account code associated with this profit center for automatic account determination in SAP FI/CO. Used to route revenue and cost postings to the correct GL account during financial close.']
  hierarchy_node_code varchar [note: 'Code of the standard hierarchy node to which this profit center is assigned in SAP CO-PCA. Enables rollup P&L reporting across organizational levels (e.g., store → region → banner → enterprise). Used for segment reporting per ASC 280.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this profit center master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), MDM synchronization, and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lease_accounting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center has active lease obligations subject to ASC 842 lease accounting (True = lease obligations exist). Triggers right-of-use (ROU) asset and lease liability recognition in the financial close process for store and DC profit centers.']
  mdm_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the authoritative source system from which this profit center master record was originated or last mastered (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA\', \'INFORMATICA_MDM\'). Supports data lineage, MDM golden record management, and audit traceability.']
  posting_block_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial postings to this profit center are currently blocked (True = blocked, False = open). Set during financial close periods, audits, or when a profit center is being restructured. Prevents erroneous postings during sensitive periods.']
  profit_center_category varchar [note: 'Management accounting classification indicating the primary financial role of the profit center: \'revenue\' (generates external revenue), \'cost\' (internal cost allocation only), \'investment\' (capital-intensive with ROI tracking), \'service\' (shared service provider). Drives transfer pricing rules and internal P&L treatment.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the profit center within the controlling area, as defined in SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (e.g., \'PC_STORE_0042\', \'PC_ECOM_US\'). Used in journal entries, cost allocations, and management reports. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the profit center\'s purpose, scope, and the business unit it represents. Provides context for financial analysts, auditors, and management reporting consumers.']
  profit_center_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this profit center within the management accounting hierarchy (e.g., 1 = enterprise, 2 = banner, 3 = region, 4 = store). Used for hierarchical P&L rollup, drill-down reporting, and segment aggregation.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the profit center (e.g., \'Chicago Flagship Store\', \'US E-Commerce Channel\', \'Private Label Apparel\'). Used in P&L reports, dashboards, and management presentations. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the profit center master record. \'active\' — open for postings; \'inactive\' — temporarily closed; \'locked\' — blocked for new postings (e.g., during financial close); \'pending\' — awaiting activation; \'archived\' — historical record, no postings allowed. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the autonomous business unit represented by this profit center. Determines the nature of P&L reporting: \'store\' (physical retail location), \'channel\' (e-commerce, BOPIS, DSD), \'product_line\' (private label, electronics, apparel), \'banner\' (hypermarket, discount outlet), \'region\' (geographic grouping), \'corporate\' (shared services). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code to which this profit center belongs (e.g., \'NORTHEAST\', \'MIDWEST\', \'WEST_COAST\', \'EMEA\'). Used for regional P&L rollup, comp sales analysis, and management reporting by geography.']
  responsible_manager varchar [note: 'Full name or employee ID of the manager accountable for this profit center\'s financial performance. Used in management reporting, budget approval workflows, and P&L accountability assignments in SAP CO-PCA.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Financial reporting segment assigned to this profit center per FASB ASC 280 (Segment Reporting). Maps profit centers to reportable segments (e.g., \'GROCERY\', \'APPAREL\', \'ECOMMERCE\', \'INTERNATIONAL\') for external financial disclosures and SEC filings.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated display name for the profit center used in space-constrained reports, POS system displays, and financial dashboards (e.g., \'CHI-FLAGSHIP\', \'ECOM-US\').']
  store_number varchar [note: 'Physical store identifier associated with this profit center when profit_center_type is \'store\'. Links to the store master for footfall, planogram, and comp sales (SSS) reporting. Null for non-store profit centers.']
  transfer_price_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transfer pricing rules apply to transactions involving this profit center (True = transfer pricing active). Relevant for intercompany transactions between profit centers, particularly for cross-border operations subject to OECD transfer pricing guidelines.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this profit center is valid and open for financial postings in SAP CO-PCA. Defines the start of the profit center\'s active period for time-dependent master data management and fiscal year planning.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this profit center is valid for financial postings. After this date, the profit center is locked for new transactions. Null indicates an open-ended validity period. Used for profit center lifecycle management and financial close.']

  Note: 'Master record for profit centers representing autonomous business units (store, product line, channel, banner) for internal management accounting in SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA. Enables internal P&L reporting tracking revenue, COGS, gross margin, operating expenses, and EBITDA at the profit center level. Includes profit center hierarchy, controlling area, segment assignment, and responsible manager. Supports segment reporting per ASC 280, transfer pricing, and management decision-making.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the general ledger account record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the gl_account master data product.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'SAP account group (KTOKS) that controls the number range and field selection for the GL account. Groups accounts by functional category such as cash accounts, receivables, payables, revenue accounts, COGS accounts, and operating expense accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|receivables|payables|revenue|cogs|opex|assets|liabilities|equity|statistical — promote to reference product]']
  account_long_name varchar [note: 'Extended descriptive name of the GL account providing full business context beyond the short name. Used in detailed financial reporting, audit documentation, and regulatory disclosures.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short description of the GL account as maintained in SAP S/4HANA (SKAT table). Used in financial statements, P&L reports, and EBITDA dashboards for display and labeling purposes.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known GL account number as defined in the SAP S/4HANA chart of accounts (SKA1/SKB1 tables). Used in all financial postings, journal entries, and regulatory reporting. Typically a 6–10 digit numeric code aligned to the company\'s account numbering scheme.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account. \'active\' permits financial postings; \'blocked\' prevents new postings while retaining history; \'marked_for_deletion\' flags the account for archival after period-end close; \'inactive\' denotes accounts no longer in use. Maps to SAP field XSPEB (posting block) and XLOEV (deletion flag).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Fundamental classification of the GL account per GAAP balance sheet and income statement structure. Drives financial statement placement: asset/liability/equity for balance sheet; revenue/expense for P&L. Maps to SAP field KTOKS (account group) and XBILK (balance sheet indicator).']
  alternative_account_number varchar [note: 'Country-specific or group-level alternative account number used for local statutory reporting or group consolidation mapping. Enables cross-mapping between the operational chart of accounts and a group/statutory chart of accounts. Maps to SAP field ALTKT in SKB1.']
  balance_sheet_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is a balance sheet account (True) or a P&L income statement account (False). Balance sheet accounts carry forward balances at year-end; P&L accounts are closed to retained earnings. Maps to SAP field XBILK in SKA1.']
  chart_of_accounts_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the chart of accounts to which this GL account belongs in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., \'INT\' for international, \'CAUS\' for US GAAP). A single enterprise may maintain multiple charts of accounts for group vs. local reporting.']
  commitment_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether commitment management (budget encumbrance) is active (True) for this GL account. When enabled, purchase orders and purchase requisitions create budget commitments against this account, supporting Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget control in merchandise planning.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which this GL account is active. Drives financial statement segregation, tax reporting, and intercompany reconciliation.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is linked to a CO (Controlling) cost element and its category. \'primary\' cost elements correspond to P&L accounts (e.g., COGS, operating expenses); \'secondary\' are internal allocation accounts; \'not_applicable\' for balance sheet accounts. Drives EBITDA and P&L cost center reporting.']
