  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GL account master record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and financial close process governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account currency in which balances are maintained (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). If set, postings to this account must be made in this currency. Maps to SAP field WAERS in SKB1.']
  deferred_revenue_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is used to record deferred revenue (True), such as gift card liabilities, loyalty point deferrals, or advance payments not yet recognized under ASC 606. Supports balance sheet classification and revenue recognition scheduling.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this GL account became active and available for financial postings. Supports temporal validity tracking for chart of accounts changes during fiscal year transitions and regulatory reporting periods.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date after which this GL account is no longer valid for new financial postings. Null indicates an open-ended account with no planned expiry. Used to manage account lifecycle during chart of accounts restructuring and fiscal year-end close.']
  fasb_classification varchar [note: 'FASB Accounting Standards Codification (ASC) topic or subtopic to which this GL account is primarily aligned (e.g., \'ASC 606\' for revenue recognition accounts, \'ASC 842\' for lease liability/ROU asset accounts, \'ASC 330\' for inventory/COGS accounts). Supports FASB-compliant regulatory reporting and audit readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ASC_606|ASC_842|ASC_330|ASC_350|ASC_360|ASC_420 — promote to reference product]']
  field_status_group varchar [note: 'SAP field status group code (e.g., G001, G004) assigned to this GL account that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry. Enforces data quality and completeness rules at the point of financial posting.']
  financial_statement_version varchar [note: 'SAP Financial Statement Version (FSV) code that determines how this account is grouped and displayed in statutory financial statements (balance sheet, P&L). Supports GAAP, IFRS, and management reporting views. Maps to SAP table T011.']
  fs_item_code varchar [note: 'The financial statement line item code to which this GL account is assigned within the Financial Statement Version. Enables aggregation of multiple GL accounts into a single P&L or balance sheet line for external reporting and FASB-compliant disclosures.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area classification that segments costs by business function for cost-of-sales P&L reporting (e.g., \'SALES\', \'ADMIN\', \'LOGISTICS\', \'MARKETING\', \'STORE_OPS\'). Enables functional income statement presentation per IFRS IAS 1 and management reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales|administration|logistics|marketing|store_operations|ecommerce|supply_chain — promote to reference product]']
  house_bank_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is a bank account (True) linked to a house bank in SAP FI-TR (Treasury). Bank GL accounts are used for cash management, bank reconciliation, and PCI DSS-compliant payment processing.']
  intercompany_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is designated for intercompany transactions (True) between legal entities within the retail group. Intercompany accounts are eliminated during group consolidation. Maps to SAP intercompany account configuration.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this GL account master record in SAP S/4HANA. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit compliance, and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control documentation.']
  lease_account_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the GL account\'s role under FASB ASC 842 lease accounting. \'rou_asset\' identifies Right-of-Use asset accounts for operating and finance leases; \'lease_liability\' identifies corresponding liability accounts; \'lease_expense\' identifies amortization/interest expense accounts; \'not_applicable\' for non-lease accounts. Critical for ASC 842 compliance reporting in retail store lease portfolios.']
  line_item_display boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual line items posted to this account are stored and displayable (True) in the FI line item report. Required for accounts needing detailed transaction-level audit trails. Maps to SAP field XKRES in SKB1.']
  open_item_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether open item management is active (True) for this account, enabling individual line items to be matched and cleared (e.g., for clearing accounts, GR/IR accounts, bank clearing). Maps to SAP field XOPVW in SKB1.']
  planning_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is included in financial planning and budgeting processes (True). Planning-relevant accounts are available for budget entry in SAP BPC or S/4HANA Financial Planning, supporting annual budgeting and rolling forecast cycles.']
  posting_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether financial postings to this GL account are currently blocked (True) or permitted (False). A blocked account cannot receive new journal entries but retains its historical balance. Maps to SAP field XSPEB in SKA1/SKB1.']
  profit_center_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether postings to this GL account are distributed to profit centers (True) for segment and store-level P&L reporting. Profit center-relevant accounts enable comparable store sales (Comp Sales/SSS) analysis and GMROI reporting by retail location.']
  profit_loss_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the GL account within the P&L statement structure. Enables EBITDA tracking, gross margin analysis, and COGS reporting. \'revenue\' includes net sales and other income; \'cogs\' covers Cost of Goods Sold; \'gross_margin\' is a subtotal indicator; \'operating_expense\' covers SG&A and store operations; \'ebitda\' marks EBITDA-relevant accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue|cogs|gross_margin|operating_expense|ebitda|depreciation|interest|tax|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  reconciliation_account_type varchar [note: 'Identifies whether this GL account serves as a reconciliation (control) account for a subledger. \'customer\' links to accounts receivable subledger; \'vendor\' links to accounts payable subledger; \'asset\' links to fixed asset subledger; \'none\' indicates a standalone GL account. Maps to SAP field MITKZ in SKB1.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Specifies the ASC 606 revenue recognition pattern applicable to this revenue GL account. \'point_in_time\' applies to standard retail POS and e-commerce sales; \'over_time\' applies to gift card breakage, loyalty program deferrals, and subscription revenues; \'not_applicable\' for non-revenue accounts. Drives automated revenue recognition scheduling in SAP FI.']
  sort_key varchar [note: 'Three-digit SAP sort key that determines which document field is used to populate the allocation field in line items posted to this account (e.g., \'001\' = posting date, \'012\' = document number). Drives line item sorting in account statements and aging reports.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational source system from which this GL account master record was ingested (e.g., \'SAP_S4HANA_PROD\', \'SAP_S4HANA_QA\'). Supports multi-system data lineage and Informatica MDM golden record traceability.']
  statistical_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a statistical GL account (True) used for management reporting and KPI tracking without affecting the legal financial statements. Statistical accounts capture quantities or values for internal analytics such as footfall, units sold, or headcount.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Defines the tax relevance of the GL account for input/output tax determination. Controls whether tax codes are required, optional, or not applicable during posting. Maps to SAP field MWSKZ in SKB1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: input_tax|output_tax|not_relevant|tax_only|deferred_tax|use_tax — promote to reference product]']
  tax_posting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tax code is mandatory (True) when posting to this GL account. Enforces tax compliance controls at the point of financial entry, ensuring all taxable transactions are properly coded for regulatory reporting.']
  tolerance_group varchar [note: 'SAP tolerance group assigned to this GL account that defines acceptable variance thresholds for payment differences, rounding, and clearing. Controls automatic clearing of small differences during accounts payable and receivable processing.']

  Note: 'General ledger chart of accounts master data aligned to SAP S/4HANA FI-GL. Defines every account used in financial postings including P&L accounts (revenue, COGS, operating expenses), balance sheet accounts (assets, liabilities, equity), and statistical accounts. Includes account type, financial statement version assignment, tax category, and FASB/GAAP classification.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Primary key for the journal_entry data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which the journal entry is assigned for management accounting and internal P&L reporting. Enables cost allocation across store operations, distribution centers, and corporate functions.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to specific fiscal periods. The journal_entry table has fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) but no FK to financial_period master. This FK enables joining to get period']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary General Ledger account associated with this journal entry header. In SAP FI-GL, the header-level GL account is used for document-level classification and routing. Supports P&L, balance sheet, and EBITDA reporting.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA ledger identifier specifying which accounting ledger the entry is posted to (e.g., \'0L\' = Leading Ledger for GAAP/FASB, \'N1\' = Non-Leading Ledger for IFRS parallel accounting). Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP plant (store, distribution center, or warehouse) associated with this journal entry. Links financial postings to physical retail locations for store-level P&L, COGS, and inventory accounting (e.g., COGS postings from goods issue, DSD receipts).']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO profit center for segment-level P&L reporting. Enables gross margin, EBITDA, and revenue tracking by business unit, store, or product category in retail financial reporting.']
  violation_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Retail violation notices (health dept fines, OSHA penalties, environmental citations) trigger financial postings for penalty accruals and payments. Finance links journal entries to notices for audit t']
  accrual_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an accrual posting (True) — i.e., an expense or revenue recognized in the current period but not yet cash-settled. Supports accrual accounting under FASB ASC 606 and period-end close processes.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the journal entry was approved for posting in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL. Distinct from entry_timestamp (creation) and posting_date (ledger date). Supports SOX audit trail and financial close process monitoring.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the approver who authorized the journal entry posting, particularly for parked documents and manual adjustments. Supports dual-control requirements under SOX Section 302/404 and internal audit controls.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI assignment field (ZUONR) used for sorting and matching open items in accounts payable and receivable clearing. Typically populated with the purchase order number, sales order number, or payment reference for automated clearing and reconciliation.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI business area code representing an organizational unit for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be created. Used in retail for segment reporting across hypermarkets, department stores, discount outlets, and e-commerce channels.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA four-character company code identifying the legal entity or organizational unit for which the journal entry is posted. Aligns with the FI-GL company code configuration and supports intercompany elimination and entity-level P&L reporting.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the journal entry header represents a debit (D) or credit (C) posting at the document level. Fundamental double-entry bookkeeping indicator used in SAP FI-GL for balance validation and financial statement preparation.']
  deferred_revenue_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry records deferred revenue (True) — i.e., cash received but revenue not yet recognized per FASB ASC 606 performance obligation criteria. Relevant for gift cards, loyalty points, subscription services, and BOPIS/ROPIS prepayments in retail.']
  document_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross total amount of the journal entry in the document (transaction) currency. Represents the sum of all debit or credit line items before netting. Used for financial reporting, reconciliation, and audit trail in retail P&L and balance sheet analysis.']
  document_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the transaction currency in which the journal entry amounts are originally recorded (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Distinct from local/company code currency. Required for multi-currency retail operations and FX reporting.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract, etc.) that triggered the journal entry. May differ from the posting date and is used for aging analysis, vendor/customer reconciliation, and audit trail purposes.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI accounting document number uniquely identifying the journal entry within a company code and fiscal year. This is the externally-known business identifier used in audit trails, reconciliation, and cross-system references (e.g., EDI, ERP).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP FI two-character document type code classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=General Ledger, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AA=Asset Posting, AB=Accounting Document, ZP=Payment Clearing, RV=Revenue). Drives number range assignment and account type determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|AA|AB|ZP|RV|KZ|DZ|WA|WE|RE — promote to reference product]']
  entered_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person or system process that created the journal entry in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL. Required for FASB-compliant audit trail, SOX internal controls, and financial close accountability.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the journal entry record was first created and saved in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for audit trail and FASB-compliant financial close documentation.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry by its accounting purpose: \'standard\' = normal business transaction; \'accrual\' = expense/revenue accrual; \'reversal\' = reversal of a prior entry; \'adjustment\' = period-end correction; \'intercompany\' = elimination entry; \'depreciation\' = fixed asset depreciation; \'lease\' = ASC 842 lease expense; \'reclassification\' = account reclassification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|accrual|reversal|adjustment|intercompany|depreciation|lease|reclassification|deferred_revenue|cogs — promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (1–16 in SAP, including special periods 13–16 for year-end adjustments) within the fiscal year in which the journal entry is posted. Critical for period-end close, accrual management, and financial reporting cycles.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the journal entry is recorded (e.g., 2024). Used for period-end financial close, P&L reporting, and EBITDA tracking aligned with the retail fiscal calendar.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI functional area code classifying the journal entry by business function for cost-of-sales P&L reporting (e.g., COGS, Selling, Administration, Distribution, Marketing). Supports income statement presentation under FASB ASC 606.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to translate document currency amounts to local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables. Critical for multi-currency retail operations, FX gain/loss accounting, and FASB ASC 830 compliance.']
  header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description or business justification entered at the journal entry header level in SAP FI-GL. Provides context for the posting (e.g., \'Q3 Rent Accrual\', \'COGS Adjustment - Store 1042\', \'Intercompany Elimination - FY2024 Q4\'). Required for audit trail and financial close documentation.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry involves a transaction between two legal entities within the same corporate group (True). Used for intercompany elimination in consolidated financial statements under FASB ASC 810.']
  last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the journal entry record was last modified in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL. Supports audit trail requirements, change tracking, and financial close process monitoring.']
  local_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the journal entry translated into the company code\'s local (functional) currency using the FX rate at posting date. Used for statutory P&L, balance sheet, and EBITDA reporting under FASB standards.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the company code\'s local (functional) currency. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance. Amounts are translated from document currency to local currency using the FX rate.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger and determines the fiscal period assignment. Distinct from the document date and entry date. Governs period-end P&L, balance sheet, and EBITDA reporting.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP FI two-digit posting key controlling the debit/credit assignment, account type, and field selection for the journal entry (e.g., 40=Debit G/L, 50=Credit G/L, 01=Customer Invoice, 31=Vendor Invoice). Fundamental SAP FI-GL configuration element.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the journal entry in the SAP FI-GL workflow. \'draft\' = entry in preparation; \'posted\' = fully posted to ledger; \'parked\' = saved but not yet posted (pending approval); \'held\' = temporarily saved; \'reversed\' = entry has been reversed; \'error\' = posting failed validation.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External or cross-system reference document number associated with the journal entry (e.g., vendor invoice number, purchase order number, sales order number, EDI transaction reference). Enables cross-module and cross-system reconciliation in SAP FI-GL.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previously posted document (True) or an original posting (False). Used in SAP FI-GL for accrual reversal management and period-end close processes.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP accounting document number of the original entry that this journal entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Provides the audit trail link between reversal and original posting for FASB-compliant financial close.']
  scheduled_reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which SAP S/4HANA is scheduled to automatically reverse this journal entry (typically the first day of the following period for accrual entries). Populated when accrual_indicator is True. Supports automated period-end close and accrual management.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA segment identifier for FASB ASC 280 segment reporting. Classifies the journal entry by reportable business segment (e.g., Grocery, Apparel, Electronics, E-Commerce, Private Label). Enables segment-level P&L and EBITDA disclosure.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA module that originated the journal entry posting (e.g., FI-AP=Accounts Payable, FI-AR=Accounts Receivable, FI-AA=Asset Accounting, FI-TX=Tax Accounting, MM=Materials Management, SD=Sales & Distribution, CO=Controlling). Enables source-system traceability and cross-module reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FI-AP|FI-AR|FI-AA|FI-TX|FI-GL|MM|SD|CO|PP|WM — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount (sales tax, VAT, or use tax) included in or associated with the journal entry in document currency. Relevant for FI-TX (Tax Accounting) postings and compliance with FTC pricing transparency and FDA labeling requirements for grocery retail.']
  trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the intercompany trading partner involved in this journal entry. Populated only when intercompany_indicator is True. Required for intercompany reconciliation and elimination in consolidated P&L and balance sheet reporting.']

  Note: 'Core general ledger journal entry header capturing all financial postings in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL, including accrual entries, scheduled reversals, and period-end closing entries. Records accounting documents for sales revenue, COGS, expense accruals, revenue accruals, deferred revenue, adjustments, intercompany eliminations, depreciation, lease expense, and reclassifications. Includes document type, posting date, fiscal period, company code, document/local currency, FX rate, reference document, posting status, accrual/reversal indicators, source module (FI-AP, FI-AR, FI-AA, FI-TX), and business justification. FASB-compliant audit trail for all financial transactions supporting accurate period-end P&L, balance sheet, and EBITDA reporting.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry line item in the general ledger. Primary key for this silver-layer data product, aligned to SAP FI-GL line item table (BSEG).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for this line item posting, corresponding to SAP BSEG-KOSTL. Enables cost allocation and departmental P&L reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL account to which this line item is posted, corresponding to SAP BSEG-HKONT. Drives P&L, balance sheet, and EBITDA reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent journal entry header (BKPF in SAP FI) to which this line item belongs. Enables drill-down from financial statements to individual debit/credit postings.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the profit center associated with this line item, corresponding to SAP BSEG-PRCTR. Supports segment-level P&L and EBITDA reporting by business unit or store.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.purchase_order. Business justification: Line-level detail for PO-related postings including freight charges, import duties, and purchase price variances. Supports detailed cost accounting and variance analysis required in retail operations.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'SAP account type indicator (KOART): A=Asset, D=Customer (Debtor), K=Vendor (Creditor), M=Material, S=GL Account. Determines the sub-ledger reconciliation account and financial statement classification.']
  amount_doc_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount in the document (transaction) currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-WRBTR. Used for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange analysis.']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount translated to the group (consolidation) currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-KURSF-derived group currency amount. Supports consolidated P&L and EBITDA reporting across all legal entities.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount translated to the company code local currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-DMBTR. Used for statutory financial reporting and local GAAP compliance.']
  assignment varchar [note: 'Free-form assignment field used for line item sorting and allocation, corresponding to SAP BSEG-ZUONR. Typically populated with a reference number (e.g., purchase order, cost object) to facilitate reconciliation and reporting.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'SAP business area code (GSBER) representing an organizational unit for which an internal balance sheet and P&L can be created. Used for segment reporting across store formats (hypermarket, e-commerce, discount).']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which this open item was cleared (AUGDT in SAP BSEG). Null for uncleared items. Used for days-payable-outstanding (DPO) and days-sales-outstanding (DSO) calculations.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP clearing document number (AUGBL) assigned when this open item is cleared against a payment or offsetting entry. Null for uncleared items. Used in accounts payable/receivable reconciliation and aging analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity for which this journal entry line is posted. Enables multi-entity financial consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this journal entry line record was first created in the system, corresponding to SAP BKPF-CPUDT and CPUTM combined. Used for audit trail, financial close monitoring, and SOX compliance.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting, corresponding to SAP BSEG-SHKZG. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and trial balance validation.']
  doc_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the document (transaction) currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-WAERS. Enables multi-currency P&L and balance sheet reporting.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date of the originating source document (invoice, receipt, etc.), corresponding to SAP BSEG-BLDAT. May differ from posting date; used for accrual and cutoff analysis.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type code (e.g., SA=GL posting, KR=vendor invoice, DR=customer invoice, AB=accounting document), corresponding to SAP BKPF-BLART. Classifies the nature of the journal entry for reporting and audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|KR|DR|AB|KZ|DZ|WA|WE|RE|RV — promote to reference product]']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign currency exchange rate applied to translate the document currency amount to local currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-KURSF. Used for FX gain/loss analysis and multi-currency reconciliation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal posting period (1–16, including special periods) within the fiscal year, corresponding to SAP BSEG-MONAT. Drives monthly financial close and period-end reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which this journal entry line is posted, corresponding to SAP BSEG-GJAHR. Used for annual financial close, budgeting, and year-over-year P&L comparisons.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area (FKBER) used for cost-of-sales accounting, classifying expenses by business function (e.g., COGS, selling, administration, distribution). Supports P&L by function reporting.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP internal order (AUFNR) to which this line item is assigned. Used for project-level cost tracking, capital expenditure monitoring, and marketing campaign cost allocation.']
  is_reversed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this journal entry line has been reversed (True) or is active (False), derived from SAP BKPF-STBLG. Reversed entries must be excluded from financial statement totals to prevent double-counting.']
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'SAP ledger group (LDGRP) identifying the accounting principle under which this line is posted (e.g., leading ledger for GAAP, non-leading ledger for IFRS). Supports parallel accounting for multi-GAAP reporting.']
  line_item_text varchar [note: 'Descriptive text for this journal entry line item, corresponding to SAP BSEG-SGTXT. Provides narrative context for auditors, financial analysts, and during financial close review.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent journal entry, corresponding to SAP BSEG-BUZEI. Used to order and uniquely identify each debit or credit posting within a document.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the company code local currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-PSWSL. Required for statutory reporting and local GAAP financial statements.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (MATNR in BSEG) corresponding to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) associated with this line item. Populated for inventory valuation, COGS, and goods movement postings. Enables drill-down from GL to product-level cost analysis.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which this line item is posted to the general ledger, corresponding to SAP BSEG-BUDAT. Determines the fiscal period and year for financial reporting.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Two-digit SAP posting key (BSCHL) that determines the account type, debit/credit indicator, and field selection for this line item. Examples: 40=GL debit, 50=GL credit, 31=vendor credit, 01=customer debit.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity associated with this line item (MENGE in SAP BSEG), applicable for material-related postings such as goods receipts, inventory adjustments, and COGS entries. Enables unit-level cost analysis.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number (XBLNR from BKPF) such as a vendor invoice number, purchase order number, or customer order number. Enables cross-system reconciliation between ERP and source operational systems.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP document number of the reversing entry (STBLG from BKPF), if this line has been reversed. Enables audit trail tracing from original posting to reversal for financial close and SOX compliance.']
  special_gl_indicator varchar [note: 'SAP special GL indicator (UMSKZ) used for down payments, guarantees, and other special transactions that are posted to alternative reconciliation accounts. Blank for standard postings.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount (VAT/sales tax) associated with this line item in document currency, corresponding to SAP BSEG-WMWST. Required for tax compliance reporting and accounts payable/receivable reconciliation.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code assigned to this line item for VAT/sales tax determination, corresponding to SAP BSEG-MWSKZ. Drives tax reporting, compliance, and accounts payable/receivable tax calculations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field (MEINS in SAP BSEG), such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), or CS (case). Required for material-related GL postings to support COGS and inventory valuation analysis.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date for cash flow and bank reconciliation purposes, corresponding to SAP BSEG-VALUT. Used in treasury and accounts payable/receivable aging analysis.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP WBS element (PROJK) for project-based cost assignment, corresponding to capital expenditure projects such as store openings, DC expansions, or IT implementations. Supports CAPEX tracking and ASC 842 lease accounting.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each general ledger journal entry, representing individual debit and credit postings to GL accounts. Captures GL account, cost center, profit center, amount in document and local currency, tax code, assignment field, and line item text. Enables drill-down from financial statements to source transactions. Aligns to SAP FI-GL line item table (BSEG).'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the AP invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Assigned by the data platform upon ingestion from SAP S/4HANA FI-AP.']

This FK enables cost allocation and reporting. The cost_center table is the SSOT for cost center master data in SAP S/4HANA ']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: AP invoices are posted to fiscal periods. The ap_invoice table has fiscal_year (STRING) and fiscal_period (STRING) but no FK to the financial_period master. This FK enables period-level controls (post']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AP invoices have gl_account_code (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK enables account validation, financial statement mapping, and ensures postings use valid GL accounts from the chart of ']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Freight and logistics invoices reference specific inbound shipments for accrual matching, payment processing, and freight cost allocation. Standard AP three-way match process in retail supply chain.']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'SAP automatic payment program run identifier (F110 run ID combining run date and identification key) that processed this invoice for payment. Used to trace payment execution and remittance details.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AP invoices have profit_center_code (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This FK enables P&L allocation and profit center reporting.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched during three-way match (PO/GR/Invoice) processing in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (supplier) master record who issued this invoice. Links to the supplier/vendor master in SAP S/4HANA MM and Informatica MDM golden record.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from the invoice payment as a vendor compliance penalty (chargeback) for violations such as late delivery, labeling errors, or EDI non-compliance. Reduces net payment to vendor.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the open AP invoice item was cleared (settled) in the SAP FI-AP subledger, either by payment, credit memo offset, or manual clearing. Marks the end of the invoice lifecycle.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number of the clearing transaction (AUGBL) that settled this invoice item. Enables full audit trail from invoice receipt through payment clearing for SOX compliance.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity or organizational unit responsible for this AP invoice. Determines the chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, and currency for financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AP invoice record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA FI-AP) or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage for period-end close.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP processing and foreign exchange revaluation in SAP FI.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash discount amount available if payment is made by the discount due date. Represents the financial benefit of early payment and is tracked for cash discount capture rate KPI reporting.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'Last date by which payment must be made to capture the early payment cash discount offered by the vendor (e.g., 2/10 Net 30 — discount expires on day 10). Critical for cash discount capture optimization.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for invoices placed in disputed status during three-way match or AP review. Drives dispute resolution workflow and vendor communication in SAP FI-AP and Salesforce Service Cloud. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_discrepancy|quantity_discrepancy|duplicate_invoice|missing_po|quality_issue|chargeback|other — promote to reference product]']
  due_date date [note: 'Calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date plus payment terms net days in SAP FI-AP.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company code local currency at the time of posting. Used for foreign currency AP revaluation and P&L reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal posting period (MONAT) within the fiscal year (01–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special closing periods). Determines the accounting period for AP subledger and GL reconciliation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year (GJAHR) in which the invoice is posted. Used for period-end AP close, financial reporting, and year-over-year AP aging and spend analysis.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number (MBLNR) for the goods receipt that is matched against this invoice during three-way match. Confirms that goods or services were received before payment is authorized.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross invoice amount before any deductions, discounts, or tax adjustments as stated on the vendor invoice. Represents the full obligation prior to netting. Corresponds to WRBTR in SAP FI.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date printed on the vendor invoice document. Used as the baseline date for payment terms calculation (e.g., Net 30 from invoice date) and cash discount eligibility. Corresponds to BLDAT in SAP FI.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned invoice number as provided by the vendor on the invoice document. Used for vendor reconciliation, EDI matching, and dispute resolution. Corresponds to BELNR field in SAP FI-AP.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the AP invoice through the invoice-to-payment cycle. Drives AP aging analysis, payment run eligibility, and period-end close. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|under_review|approved|posted|scheduled|paid|disputed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type as defined in SAP FI-AP document type configuration. Determines posting logic, payment terms applicability, and GL account determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|credit_memo|debit_memo|recurring|intercompany|proforma — promote to reference product]']
  is_edi_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice was received electronically via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transmission from the vendor, as opposed to paper or email. EDI invoices (e.g., ANSI X12 810) enable straight-through processing.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is part of a recurring payment schedule (e.g., monthly rent, lease payments under ASC 842, or standing service contracts). Recurring invoices are auto-generated in SAP FI-AP.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net payable amount after applying all applicable discounts, deductions, and chargebacks. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP aging analysis.']
  payment_block varchar [note: 'Indicator specifying whether and why the invoice is blocked from automatic payment processing. Corresponds to ZLSPR in SAP FI-AP. A value of \'none\' means the invoice is eligible for the next payment run.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which payment was executed and disbursed to the vendor via the selected payment method (ACH, wire, check). Populated upon successful payment run completion in SAP FI-AP.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number generated for the payment transaction that clears this invoice. Used for bank reconciliation, remittance advice, and audit trail of the payment-to-invoice clearing.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the invoice will be or was settled (e.g., ACH electronic transfer, wire transfer, check, virtual card, EFT). Corresponds to ZLSCH in SAP FI-AP and determines payment run processing logic.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (ZTERM) defining the agreed payment conditions with the vendor, including net days, discount percentages, and discount periods (e.g., ZB14 = 2% 10 days Net 30). Drives due date and discount date calculation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was posted to the SAP general ledger and AP subledger. Determines the accounting period for financial reporting and period-end close. Corresponds to BUDAT in SAP FI.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the vendor invoice was physically or electronically received by the AP department. Used to measure invoice processing cycle time and SLA compliance for vendor payment.']
  remittance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or text included in the payment remittance advice sent to the vendor, enabling the vendor to match the payment to their outstanding invoices. Supports vendor reconciliation and dispute prevention.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'Internal SAP FI document number (BELNR) assigned upon posting of the invoice in SAP S/4HANA. Used for cross-reference with the general ledger and FI-AP subledger.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) included in or applicable to the invoice as determined by SAP FI tax code configuration. Required for tax compliance reporting.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way matching process comparing the purchase order (PO), goods receipt (GR), and vendor invoice quantities and amounts in SAP FI-AP/MM. Exceptions require AP team resolution before payment approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AP invoice record, including status changes, payment updates, or dispute resolution actions. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit compliance.']
  vendor_bank_account varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the vendor bank account used for payment disbursement. Stored in compliance with PCI DSS requirements. Full account details are maintained in SAP FI-AP vendor master banking data.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable document lifecycle record capturing the full vendor invoice-to-payment cycle in SAP S/4HANA FI-AP. Encompasses invoice receipt, three-way match (PO/GR/Invoice), payment scheduling, payment execution (ACH, wire, check), discount capture, and settlement clearing. Includes vendor reference, purchase order linkage, payment terms, due dates, payment method, payment run details, bank account, remittance information, and clearing status. Supports vendor payment compliance, AP aging analysis, cash flow management, and period-end AP close.'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {
  ar_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the AR invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR invoices have cost_center_code (STRING) but no FK to cost_center master. This FK enables cost allocation for receivables.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: AR invoices are posted to fiscal periods. The ar_invoice table has fiscal_year (STRING) and fiscal_period (STRING) but no FK to financial_period master. This FK enables period controls and metadata. F']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR invoices have gl_account_code (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK enables account validation and financial statement mapping for receivables.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order that generated this invoice. Enables order-to-cash cycle tracing and revenue attribution back to the originating commercial transaction.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the B2B customer (wholesale account, franchise, or commercial counterparty) to whom this invoice is issued. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE minimum category.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AR invoices have profit_center_code (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This FK enables P&L allocation for revenue.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of payments and credits applied against this invoice to date. Used to calculate the outstanding open balance and support partial payment tracking in cash application.']
  bank_clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was cleared through the bank and matched to this invoice in SAP FI-AR. Null until payment is received and cleared. Used for cash flow reporting and bank reconciliation.']
  billing_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the receivable indicating the commercial relationship type. Supports segmented AR reporting across wholesale accounts, franchise fee billing, commercial receivables, intercompany settlements, and drop-ship vendor billing.']
  billing_document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known SAP FI-AR billing document number (BELNR) assigned at invoice creation. Used for customer remittance matching, EDI reconciliation, and dispute resolution. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER minimum category.']
  cash_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount amount available to the customer if payment is received within the discount period defined in payment terms (e.g., 2% if paid within 10 days on 2/10NET30 terms).']
  cash_discount_due_date date [note: 'Last date by which the customer must pay to qualify for the early payment cash discount. Calculated from invoice_date and payment_terms_code discount period.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP document number of the clearing transaction (payment, credit memo, or write-off) that fully or partially settled this invoice. Enables payment-to-invoice matching and audit trail.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code (BUKRS) representing the legal entity issuing the invoice. Required for entity-level P&L, EBITDA reporting, and FASB-compliant financial close.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AR invoice record was first created in the source system (SAP FI-AR). Used for audit trail and period-end close reconciliation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED minimum category.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AR reporting and FX translation. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized SAP reason code for the customer dispute (e.g., pricing discrepancy, short shipment, quality issue, chargeback). Used for dispute root-cause analysis and vendor chargeback management.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any customer dispute raised against this invoice. Drives collections workflow routing and dunning block management in SAP FI-AR and Salesforce Service Cloud.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which full payment must be received per the agreed payment terms. Drives AR aging bucket classification (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days) and dunning trigger logic.']
  dunning_block boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dunning notices are blocked for this invoice (True = blocked). Set when a dispute is open, a payment arrangement is in place, or the account is under review.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent dunning notice sent to the customer for this invoice. Used to track collections activity and enforce dunning interval rules.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning escalation level (0–4) assigned to this invoice in SAP FI-AR dunning program. Level 0 = no dunning; higher levels indicate escalating collection notices sent to the customer.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal accounting period (01–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special closing periods) within the fiscal year. Drives period-end AR close, aging bucket assignment, and monthly P&L reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the invoice is posted (e.g., 2024). Used for annual P&L reporting, EBITDA tracking, and year-end AR close reconciliation.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross billed amount before tax, discounts, or adjustments. Represents the face value of goods or services invoiced to the B2B customer. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The business date on which the invoice was issued to the customer. Represents the principal real-world event time for revenue recognition under ASC 606. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP minimum category.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the AR invoice. Drives AR aging analysis, dunning workflow, and period-end close. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS minimum category.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type in SAP FI-AR. Distinguishes standard customer invoices, credit memos for returns/adjustments, debit memos for chargebacks, proforma invoices, and intercompany billing.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total amount due after applying discounts and before tax. Represents the contractual obligation for payment. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET net total component.']
  open_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice (net_amount minus applied_amount). Drives AR aging analysis, dunning escalation, and collections workflow prioritization.']
  payment_block boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is blocked from automatic payment processing (True = blocked). Applied during dispute resolution, credit hold, or compliance review.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Agreed payment instrument for settling this invoice (e.g., ACH bank transfer, wire, check). Determines cash application routing and bank clearing workflow in SAP FI-AR.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30) defining the payment schedule, early payment discount percentage, and due date calculation rules for this invoice.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the invoice is posted to the general ledger in SAP FI-AR. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition and AR balance reporting. May differ from invoice_date for period-end accruals.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference number provided by the customer (e.g., purchase order number, contract number) for remittance matching and EDI reconciliation. Stored in SAP FI-AR XBLNR field.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this invoice was recognized under ASC 606 criteria (performance obligation satisfied). May differ from invoice_date for multi-element arrangements or deferred revenue scenarios.']
  revenue_recognition_status varchar [note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition status for this invoice. Indicates whether revenue has been recognized, is deferred pending performance obligation fulfillment, or has been reversed. Critical for FASB-compliant financial reporting.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization (VKORG) responsible for this invoice. Enables P&L reporting and revenue attribution by sales channel, region, or business unit.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice (sales tax, VAT, or applicable jurisdiction tax). Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AR invoice record in the source system. Supports change data capture and audit compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED minimum category.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount written off as uncollectible bad debt for this invoice. Used for bad debt expense reporting, allowance for doubtful accounts calculation, and FASB-compliant AR reserve management.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice balance was written off as bad debt in SAP FI-AR. Null until a write-off is executed. Required for bad debt aging and allowance for doubtful accounts reporting.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable document lifecycle record for B2B customer billing and cash collection in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR. Encompasses invoice creation, payment receipt, partial payment handling, cash discount processing, bank clearing, dunning activities, and collections management for wholesale accounts, franchise fees, and commercial receivables. Includes customer account, billing/due dates, payment terms, payment method, bank clearing date, applied amounts, dunning level, and ASC 606 revenue recognition flags. Supports AR aging analysis, cash application, collections workflow, and period-end AR close.'
}

Table finance.revenue_recognition_event {
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ASC 606 revenue recognition event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer contract or sales agreement under which this revenue recognition event is triggered. Aligns with ASC 606 Step 1: Identify the contract with a customer.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP cost center responsible for the revenue-generating activity, used for P&L reporting and EBITDA tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger (GL) account to which the recognized revenue amount is posted in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store or e-commerce channel location where the originating transaction occurred. Supports comp sales and SSS (Same-Store Sales) reporting.']
  original_event_revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [note: 'For reversal or adjustment events, the revenue_recognition_event_id of the original recognition event being reversed or corrected. Null for original (non-reversal) events. Supports audit trail and financial reconciliation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer party associated with the contract generating this revenue recognition event. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition events do not have a profit_center FK. In retail, revenue is tracked by profit center (store, channel, product line) for P&L reporting. This FK enables P&L allocation for ASC 606 r']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal accounting period (YYYY-MM) to which this revenue recognition event is posted. Used for period-end financial close, P&L reporting, and EBITDA tracking in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  allocated_transaction_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of the total transaction price allocated to this specific performance obligation under ASC 606 Step 4 (Allocate the transaction price to the performance obligations), based on relative standalone selling prices.']
  asc606_step varchar [note: 'Identifies which of the five ASC 606 revenue recognition steps this event record primarily documents: Step 1 (Identify contract), Step 2 (Identify performance obligations), Step 3 (Determine transaction price), Step 4 (Allocate transaction price), Step 5 (Recognize revenue). Supports compliance audit and FASB disclosure reporting.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The retail channel through which the originating transaction was executed. Supports omnichannel revenue disaggregation per ASC 606-10-50-5. BOPIS = Buy Online Pick Up In Store; ROPIS = Reserve Online Pick Up In Store.']
  cogs_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost of goods sold (COGS) associated with the recognized revenue event. Used to compute gross margin and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) at the transaction level for P&L reporting.']
  contract_modification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revenue recognition event was triggered by a contract modification (e.g., price change, scope change, return, exchange). True = event results from a contract modification per ASC 606-10-25-10 through 25-13.']
  contract_modification_type varchar [note: 'The accounting treatment applied to the contract modification per ASC 606. \'new_contract\' = treated as a separate contract; \'modification_existing\' = prospective adjustment to existing contract; \'cumulative_catchup\' = cumulative catch-up adjustment to revenue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this revenue recognition event record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and financial close process monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this recognition event (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency retail operations and FASB foreign currency translation.']
  deferred_revenue_balance decimal(18,4) [note: 'The remaining unrecognized revenue balance for this performance obligation as of the recognition event date. Represents the contract liability on the balance sheet per ASC 606. Applicable for gift cards, extended warranties, and loyalty programs.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this revenue recognition event, formatted as RRE- followed by a 10-digit sequence. Used for audit trails, financial close reconciliation, and FASB ASC 606 compliance reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this revenue recognition event belongs. Supports annual P&L reporting, EBITDA tracking, and year-over-year comp sales analysis.']
  gift_card_breakage_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The estimated breakage rate (as a decimal proportion, e.g., 0.0850 = 8.50%) applied to gift card balances to recognize breakage income proportionally as redemptions occur. Applicable only when revenue_category = \'gift_card_breakage\'. Based on historical redemption patterns per ASC 606-10-55-48.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross margin amount for this recognition event, calculated as recognized_amount minus cogs_amount. Supports P&L reporting and EBITDA tracking at the transaction level.']
  loyalty_point_fair_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The estimated standalone selling price (fair value) per loyalty point at the time of the originating transaction, used for transaction price allocation under ASC 606 Step 4. Expressed in the transaction currency.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed decimal(18,4) [note: 'The number of loyalty program points redeemed in this recognition event. Used to calculate the standalone selling price of the loyalty performance obligation and the allocated transaction price for loyalty redemptions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the finance team regarding this revenue recognition event, such as rationale for variable consideration estimates, contract modification details, or audit reviewer comments.']
  obligation_satisfaction_date date [note: 'The date on which the distinct performance obligation was fully satisfied (e.g., delivery date for product sale, activation date for extended warranty, redemption date for loyalty points). Aligns with ASC 606 Step 5.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the revenue recognition journal entry was posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. May differ from transaction_date due to period-end cut-off adjustments or delayed recognition.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the revenue recognition period for over-time performance obligations (e.g., extended warranty coverage end, franchise agreement expiry). Null for point-in-time recognition events.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'ASC 606 Step 5 recognition pattern. \'point_in_time\' = revenue recognized at a single moment (e.g., product sale, gift card redemption); \'over_time\' = revenue recognized progressively (e.g., extended warranty, franchise fee, subscription).']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the revenue recognition period for over-time performance obligations (e.g., extended warranty coverage start, franchise agreement commencement). Null for point-in-time recognition events.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revenue recognition event. \'pending\' = awaiting performance obligation satisfaction; \'recognized\' = revenue posted to GL; \'deferred\' = revenue deferred to future period; \'reversed\' = recognition reversed due to contract modification or return; \'cancelled\' = event voided.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the performance obligation was satisfied and revenue was recognized in the General Ledger. This is the principal business event timestamp for this entity.']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual revenue amount recognized in the current accounting period for this event. For point-in-time recognition, equals the allocated transaction price. For over-time recognition, represents the portion earned in the current period.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue stream type per ASC 606 disaggregation disclosure requirements. Covers retail-specific categories including gift card breakage (unredeemed gift card income), loyalty point redemptions, franchise fees, extended warranties, and standard product sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_sale|gift_card_breakage|loyalty_redemption|franchise_fee|extended_warranty|service_fee — promote to reference product]']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recognition event is a reversal of a previously posted revenue entry (e.g., due to a return, contract cancellation, or correction). True = this is a reversal entry with a negative recognized_amount.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The business document number of the originating transaction (e.g., SAP sales order number, POS transaction ID, invoice number) that generated this revenue recognition event. Used for end-to-end audit trail and financial reconciliation.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of originating source document that triggered this revenue recognition event. Supports audit trail and reconciliation back to SAP S/4HANA SD module or SAP CAR POS transaction data.']
  standalone_selling_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standalone selling price (SSP) of the performance obligation at contract inception, used as the basis for transaction price allocation under ASC 606 Step 4. Determined by observable prices, adjusted market assessment, or expected cost plus margin approach.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the originating retail transaction (POS sale, e-commerce order, gift card purchase, etc.) occurred. Used for period-end revenue cut-off and financial close.']
  transaction_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total transaction price allocated to the contract as determined under ASC 606 Step 3 (Determine the transaction price). Represents the gross amount before allocation to individual performance obligations. Includes variable consideration estimates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this revenue recognition event record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Tracks updates from contract modifications, reversals, or period-end adjustments.']
  variable_consideration_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The estimated variable consideration included in the transaction price (e.g., volume discounts, rebates, returns allowances, markdown adjustments). Constrained per ASC 606 variable consideration constraint guidance.']

  Note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition event capturing the five-step revenue recognition model for retail transactions including gift card breakage, loyalty point redemptions, franchise fees, and extended warranties. Records performance obligation identification, transaction price allocation, satisfaction date, recognized amount, deferred revenue balance, and contract modification events. Ensures FASB ASC 606 compliance.'
}

Table finance.finance_budget {
  finance_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each budget record in the financial planning system. Primary key for the budget data product in SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail business unit (e.g., hypermarket, e-commerce, department store) to which this budget is allocated, supporting channel-level P&L and EBITDA projections.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Retail compliance programs (food safety, PCI, OSHA) have dedicated budget allocations. Compliance managers track planned vs actual spending against programs for annual planning and variance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center or profit center responsible for this budget line. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA CO cost center master data for P&L and EBITDA reporting.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: DC operating budgets (labor, utilities, equipment) are planned and tracked by facility. Standard retail budgeting process requires facility-level budget allocation and variance reporting.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Budget records are tied to specific fiscal periods. The budget table has fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) as separate columns, but no FK to the financial_period master. This FK enables joinin']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger (GL) account associated with this budget line item, enabling line-item granularity for financial reporting and FASB-compliant disclosures.']
  plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the plan version record defining whether this budget entry belongs to the original budget, revised budget, rolling forecast, or annual plan. Maps to SAP CO plan version.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual financial amount posted to the GL account and cost center for this budget period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI actuals for budget vs. actual variance analysis.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget record received final approval from the authorized approver. Required for audit trail and financial governance compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the budget record within the financial planning approval cycle. Tracks progression from draft through submission, review, approval, rejection, or period lock. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|locked — promote to reference product]']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the financial authority (e.g., CFO, Finance Director, Cost Center Manager) who approved this budget record. Supports approval workflow audit trail.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level financial classification of the budget line: Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), Operating Expenditure (OPEX), Cost of Goods Sold (COGS), labor costs, or revenue. Drives P&L and EBITDA reporting segmentation.']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the budget record, used in financial planning communications, approval workflows, and audit trails. Format: BDG-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The retail channel (store, e-commerce, omnichannel, wholesale, marketplace) for which this budget is planned. Enables channel-level revenue, COGS, and gross margin projections.']
  cogs_projection decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the budget period, used to project gross margin and support merchandise planning and Open to Buy (OTB) calculations.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of the budget that has been formally committed through purchase orders or contracts but not yet expensed. Supports Open to Buy (OTB) integration and encumbrance accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget record was first created in the financial planning system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and financial close process tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this budget record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency financial planning and consolidation.']
  ebitda_projection decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) for the budget period. Key metric for investor financial guidance and enterprise P&L reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (1–13, where 13 is a special closing period) within the fiscal year to which this budget line applies. Supports monthly and period-level budget vs. actual variance analysis in SAP CO.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1–4) within the fiscal year for this budget record. Used for quarterly reforecast cycles, investor financial guidance, and quarterly P&L reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this budget record applies. Aligns with the retail fiscal calendar in SAP S/4HANA FI for annual financial planning and investor guidance.']
  forecast_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The latest forward-looking financial estimate for this budget line, updated during rolling forecast or reforecast cycles. Used for forecast vs. prior forecast variance analysis and investor guidance.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to derive the forecast or budget amounts: top-down (executive-driven allocation), bottom-up (cost center aggregation), driver-based (KPI-linked), statistical (model-driven), or zero-based budgeting.']
  gross_margin_projection decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned gross margin (revenue minus COGS) for the budget period and business unit. Supports Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis and financial close reporting.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record has been locked for the period, preventing further edits. True when the period is closed or the budget has been formally locked post-approval.']
  is_reforecast boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget record represents a reforecast (True) rather than the original budget plan (False). Distinguishes reforecast iterations from baseline budget entries for variance tracking.']
  lock_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget record was locked to prevent further modifications, typically at period-end close. Supports financial close process integrity and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for financial planners to document assumptions, rationale, or commentary associated with this budget record. Supports audit trail and management review during financial planning cycles.']
  otb_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The Open to Buy (OTB) amount representing the remaining purchasing budget available for merchandise procurement in this period. Calculated as planned inventory budget minus committed and received inventory costs.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the planning period covered by this budget record. Defines the effective close of the budget window for financial reporting and period-end close processes.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the planning period covered by this budget record. Defines the effective beginning of the budget window for financial close and reporting alignment.']
  plan_version_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the budget record by planning cycle type: original budget (initial annual plan), revised budget (approved amendment), rolling forecast (monthly update), quarterly reforecast, or annual plan. Maps to SAP CO plan version category.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original planned financial amount for this budget line item in the functional currency. Represents the baseline against which actuals and forecasts are compared in budget vs. actual reporting.']
  prior_forecast_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The forecast amount from the immediately preceding forecast cycle, retained for forecast vs. prior forecast variance analysis and trend tracking in financial planning.']
  revenue_projection decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forward-looking revenue estimate for the business unit and channel in this budget period. Supports top-line financial planning, investor guidance, and Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) tracking.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget record was formally submitted for approval in the financial planning workflow. Supports submission tracking and planning cycle compliance.']
  submitted_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the person who submitted this budget record for approval. Supports submission tracking and financial planning accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this budget record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The absolute difference between planned amount and actual amount (actual_amount minus planned_amount) for this budget line. Positive value indicates overspend; negative indicates underspend. Supports budget vs. actual reporting.']
  variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage variance between planned and actual amounts ((actual - planned) / planned * 100). Used in financial dashboards and P&L reporting to flag material variances requiring management action.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive financial planning record encompassing annual budgets, periodic forecasts, rolling reforecasts, and forward-looking financial projections for retail business units, cost centers, and profit centers in SAP S/4HANA CO. Captures plan version (original budget, revised budget, monthly rolling forecast, quarterly reforecast, annual plan), fiscal year/period, category (CAPEX, OPEX, COGS, labor), GL account detail with line-item granularity (planned/committed/actual amounts per account and cost center), forecast method, variance analysis (budget vs. actual, forecast vs. prior forecast), revenue/COGS/gross margin/EBITDA projections by channel and business unit, approval workflow, and submission tracking. Supports OTB (Open to Buy) integration, budget vs. actual reporting, financial planning cycles, and investor financial guidance.'
}

Table finance.financial_period {
  financial_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the financial period record in the retail fiscal calendar. Primary key for the financial_period data product.']
  comparable_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding financial period in the prior fiscal year used for same-store sales (SSS/Comp Sales) and year-over-year (YoY) financial comparisons. Accounts for 53-week year shifts in the NRF retail calendar.']
  prior_year_period_financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the exact same period number in the immediately preceding fiscal year. Used for year-over-year (YoY) financial variance analysis, P&L comparisons, and FASB comparative financial statement preparation. Distinct from comparable_period_id which accounts for 53-week calendar shifts.']
  ap_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts payable (AP) postings are permitted for this financial period. AP periods may be managed independently from GL periods in SAP S/4HANA to support vendor invoice processing timelines and COGS accruals.']
  ar_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts receivable (AR) postings are permitted for this financial period. AR periods may be managed independently to support customer payment processing, revenue recognition (ASC 606), and loyalty program financial settlements.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the budget or plan version associated with this financial period (e.g., \'ORIGINAL\', \'REVISED_Q2\', \'FORECAST_V3\'). Links the period to the corresponding financial plan in SAP CO for budget vs. actual variance analysis and financial planning cycles.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal calendar convention used for this period. The 4-5-4 and 4-4-5 calendars are NRF industry standards that divide the fiscal year into 4-week and 5-week periods to align comparable store sales (SSS) reporting across years.']
  close_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days allocated for completing the financial close process for this period (from period end date to scheduled close date). A KPI for finance operations efficiency; industry benchmark for retail is typically 5–10 business days for month-end close.']
  close_status varchar [note: 'Status of the financial close process for this period. Tracks progression through the month-end or year-end close cycle. \'not_started\' indicates close activities have not begun; \'in_progress\' means close tasks are underway; \'completed\' means the period is fully closed and reconciled; \'reopened\' means a closed period was reopened for corrections.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code (Buchungskreis) to which this financial period configuration applies. A company code represents an independent legal entity within the retail enterprise (e.g., a specific banner, subsidiary, or country operation).']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'SAP CO controlling area code associated with this financial period. The controlling area defines the organizational unit for cost accounting, P&L reporting, and EBITDA tracking within SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this financial period record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and financial close governance per ISO 27001 and FASB standards.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the functional currency of the company code during this financial period (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Governs currency translation for consolidated P&L and EBITDA reporting.']
