  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the financial period (inclusive). Defines the upper boundary for transaction posting, revenue recognition cutoff, and financial close. For 53-week fiscal years, the final period end date may extend one week beyond the standard calendar.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024). Retail fiscal years may not align with the calendar year; typically begins in late January or early February per the 4-5-4 or 4-4-5 retail calendar convention.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal year structure (e.g., \'K4\' for calendar year, \'V3\' for April–March, or a custom retail variant). Determines how posting periods map to calendar months in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  gl_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether general ledger (GL) journal entry postings are currently permitted for this financial period. Derived from posting_status but maintained as an explicit business flag for integration with downstream systems that check posting eligibility.']
  is_53_week_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fiscal year containing this period is a 53-week year. Under the NRF 4-5-4 calendar, a 53rd week occurs approximately every 5–6 years to realign the fiscal calendar with the Gregorian calendar. Impacts comparable store sales (SSS) comparisons and year-over-year financial analysis.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (periods 13–16 in SAP) used exclusively for year-end audit adjustments, accrual reversals, and FASB-required restatements. Adjustment periods do not correspond to calendar time.']
  is_quarter_end_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the final period of a fiscal quarter, triggering quarterly close procedures, quarterly P&L reporting, and SEC 10-Q filing preparation for publicly traded retail entities.']
  is_year_end_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the final period of the fiscal year requiring year-end close procedures, including balance sheet finalization, retained earnings posting, and annual financial statement preparation per FASB standards.']
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA ledger group identifier that this period applies to (e.g., \'0L\' for leading ledger, \'L1\' for local GAAP ledger, \'L2\' for IFRS ledger). Supports parallel accounting for multi-GAAP retail entities operating across jurisdictions.']
  mm_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether materials management (MM) postings — including goods receipts, inventory adjustments, and COGS postings — are permitted for this financial period in SAP S/4HANA MM module. Critical for inventory valuation and COGS accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary associated with this financial period (e.g., \'Holiday season period — extended close window\', \'Includes 53rd week adjustment\', \'Restatement applied per ASC 606 guidance\'). Used by finance teams for period-specific documentation.']
  period_close_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the financial period was officially closed in SAP S/4HANA FI, preventing further postings. Critical for financial close scheduling, audit compliance, and FASB revenue recognition cutoff enforcement.']
  period_length_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days in the financial period (typically 28 or 35 days for 4-week or 5-week NRF periods). Used for daily average calculations, proration of fixed costs, and lease accounting (ASC 842) period allocations.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the financial period (e.g., \'FY2024 Period 1\', \'Q1 FY2024 Week 1\', \'January 2024\'). Used in financial reports, P&L statements, and EBITDA dashboards for period labeling.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential period number within the fiscal year (1–12 for regular periods, 13–16 for special/adjustment periods). Maps to SAP S/4HANA posting period (MONAT) in table BKPF. Controls which periods are open for posting in the general ledger.']
  period_open_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the posting period was officially opened for journal entry postings in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for financial close audit trails and compliance with FASB reporting timelines.']
  period_short_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code uniquely identifying the financial period across systems (e.g., \'FY2024P01\', \'FY2024P13\'). Used as a business key for cross-system integration between SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORMS), and financial reporting tools.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period by its accounting purpose. \'regular\' periods are standard operating periods; \'adjustment\' periods are used for year-end adjustments; \'closing\' periods are used for financial close entries; \'opening\' periods for balance carryforward; \'special\' for audit or correction postings.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current open/close status of the posting period in the general ledger. \'open\' allows new journal entries; \'closed\' prevents further postings; \'blocked\' restricts posting pending review; \'future\' indicates the period is not yet active. Controls SAP S/4HANA FI posting period management (OB52).']
  quarter_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable fiscal quarter label (e.g., \'Q1 FY2024\', \'Q4 FY2023\'). Used in financial reporting dashboards, investor relations materials, and comparable store sales (SSS/Comp Sales) analysis.']
  quarter_number varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1–4) to which this period belongs within the fiscal year. Used for quarterly P&L reporting, EBITDA tracking, and SEC/FASB quarterly financial disclosures.']
  reopen_date date [note: 'The date on which a previously closed financial period was reopened for additional postings or corrections. Populated only when close_status is \'reopened\'. Supports audit trail requirements for FASB restatements.']
  reopen_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for reopening a previously closed financial period (e.g., \'Audit adjustment required\', \'Revenue recognition restatement per ASC 606\', \'Vendor chargeback correction\'). Required for SOX compliance and audit trail documentation.']
  scheduled_close_date date [note: 'The planned/target date for completing the financial close process for this period, as defined in the financial close calendar. Used to track close cycle performance and SLA adherence for the finance operations team.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the financial period. Defines the inclusive lower boundary for transaction posting, revenue recognition (ASC 606), and financial close scheduling.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this financial period record (e.g., status change, reopen event, close date update). Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail compliance.']
  week_number varchar [note: 'Fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1–52 or 1–53 for 53-week years). Critical for weekly retail performance reporting, same-store sales (SSS) comparisons, and Open-to-Buy (OTB) planning cycles.']

  Note: 'Fiscal calendar and financial period master defining the retail fiscal year structure (4-5-4 or 4-4-5 calendar), accounting periods, quarter boundaries, and year-end close dates. Includes period open/close status, period type (regular, adjustment, closing), and fiscal year variant. Controls posting periods in SAP S/4HANA FI and drives financial close scheduling.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager responsible for the custody and maintenance of the asset. Used for accountability and asset tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  created_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the fixed asset record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Material handling equipment, racking systems, and automation assets are assigned to specific distribution centers. Required for asset management, depreciation allocation, and facility-level P&L report']
  lease_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: Fixed assets have lease_indicator_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating whether the asset is leased, but no FK to lease_contract. This FK links leased assets to their governing lease contracts (ASC 842 compliance']
  modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the fixed asset record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  primary_fixed_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor from whom the asset was purchased. Links to accounts payable and procurement records.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative depreciation expense recognized since acquisition. Subtracted from acquisition cost to calculate net book value.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original purchase price or capitalized cost of the fixed asset including installation and setup costs. Basis for depreciation calculation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was acquired or placed in service. Marks the start of the depreciation lifecycle.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Classification code defining the type of fixed asset (e.g., store fixtures, POS terminals, warehouse equipment, vehicles, leasehold improvements). Determines depreciation rules and useful life.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the fixed asset including make, model, and distinguishing characteristics.']
  asset_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the physical location of the asset (store number, distribution center code, warehouse ID, or corporate office location).']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique asset identifier assigned by the enterprise asset management system. Used for physical tagging and tracking.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. Active assets are in service and depreciating; under construction assets are capitalized but not yet depreciating; retired/disposed assets are no longer in use.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date the asset was capitalized and added to the fixed asset register. May differ from acquisition date for assets under construction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts (acquisition cost, accumulated depreciation, net book value).']
  depreciation_area varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation area code distinguishing book depreciation (GAAP) from tax depreciation and other parallel valuations (e.g., 01=book, 15=tax).']
  depreciation_key varchar [note: 'SAP FI-AA depreciation key code that defines the calculation rule, useful life, and period control for depreciation runs.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense. Straight-line is most common for retail fixed assets.']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash or fair value received from sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal.']
  impairment_indicator_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the asset has been identified for impairment testing under FASB ASC 360. True if impairment indicators are present.']
  impairment_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative impairment loss recognized when the carrying value exceeds the recoverable amount. Reduces net book value and is reported in P&L.']
  last_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense amount posted in the most recent depreciation run. Used for monthly P&L reporting and EBITDA add-back calculation.']
  last_depreciation_run_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent periodic depreciation calculation run for this asset. Updated monthly during financial close.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturer or original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number identifying the specific asset configuration or variant.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the asset calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Represents the asset\'s value on the balance sheet.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number under which the asset was procured. Provides audit trail to procurement transaction.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date the asset was retired from service or disposed. Marks the end of the depreciation lifecycle.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Subtracted from acquisition cost to determine depreciable base.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number or unique equipment identifier for trackable assets such as POS terminals, vehicles, and warehouse equipment.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected useful life of the asset in years as determined by asset class and accounting policy. Used to calculate annual depreciation.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage expires. Used for maintenance planning and risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master and depreciation lifecycle record for retail capital assets including store fixtures, POS terminals, warehouse equipment, vehicles, and leasehold improvements in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA. Captures asset class, acquisition details, useful life, depreciation method and key, periodic depreciation run results (run date, depreciation amount, depreciation area), accumulated depreciation, net book value, and asset location. Supports CAPEX tracking, monthly depreciation close, GAAP vs. tax book reconciliation, EBITDA reporting (D&A add-back), and FASB ASC 360 impairment testing.'
}

Table finance.lease_contract {
  lease_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the lease contract record. Primary key for the lease_contract data product in the finance domain Silver layer.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lease contracts have gl_cost_center (STRING) but no FK to cost_center master. This FK enables cost allocation for lease expenses (ASC 842 compliance).']
  environmental_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.environmental_event. Business justification: Retail store leases involve environmental compliance (refrigerant leaks, hazmat storage, waste disposal). Environmental events at leased locations are tracked against leases for landlord notification,']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store or facility location covered by this lease. Links to the store master record for operational reporting, comp sales (SSS) analysis, and P&L attribution.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Retail store leases require regulatory filings (occupancy permits, zoning compliance, environmental disclosures). Lease administration tracks associated filings for renewal compliance verification and']
  rou_asset_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Lease contracts have gl_account_rou_asset (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK links to the GL account for right-of-use asset under ASC 842. This is the FIRST of two FKs from lease_contrac']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lessor\'s master supplier/vendor record in the MDM system (Informatica MDM). Enables linkage to supplier payment terms, banking details, and vendor compliance records.']
  base_rent_monthly decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly base rent payment amount as stipulated in the lease contract, excluding variable lease components (e.g., percentage rent, CAM charges). Used as the primary input for lease payment schedule generation and ASC 842 liability measurement.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease term begins and the lessee obtains the right to use the underlying asset. This is the measurement date for initial ROU asset and lease liability recognition under ASC 842.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the lease contract, used in correspondence with lessors, auditors, and legal teams. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO lease contract object (RE-FX module).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lease contract record was first created in the system (SAP S/4HANA RE-FX or finance data product). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and financial close process tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this lease contract (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency lease portfolios for international retail operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Contractual end date of the non-cancellable lease term. Used to calculate remaining lease term, schedule final payments, and trigger renewal option evaluation workflows.']
  gl_account_lease_liability varchar [note: 'SAP GL account number for the lease liability recognized on the balance sheet at lease commencement under ASC 842. Tracks the present value of future lease payments for balance sheet disclosure.']
  implicit_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The rate of interest implicit in the lease as defined by the lessor, used as the discount rate when readily determinable. Takes precedence over the incremental borrowing rate (IBR) for lease liability measurement under ASC 842. Expressed as a decimal.']
  incremental_borrowing_rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The interest rate the lessee would have to pay to borrow funds on a collateralized basis over a similar term and in a similar economic environment, used to discount future lease payments to present value when the implicit rate is not readily determinable. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.045000 = 4.5%). Required for ASC 842 lease liability measurement.']
  initial_direct_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental costs incurred by the lessee directly attributable to negotiating and arranging the lease (e.g., legal fees, broker commissions). Added to the initial ROU asset value at commencement under ASC 842.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent lease payment was made to the lessor. Used for payment reconciliation, delinquency monitoring, and lease liability roll-forward calculations during monthly close.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the lease contract record, including remeasurements, modifications, and status changes. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer and audit compliance.']
  lease_incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total lease incentives received from the lessor (e.g., tenant improvement allowances, rent-free periods, moving allowances). Reduces the initial ROU asset value at commencement under ASC 842 and is amortized over the lease term.']
  lease_liability_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current outstanding lease liability balance as of the last accounting close, reflecting initial recognition less principal payments made to date. Split into current and non-current portions for balance sheet classification per ASC 842.']
  lease_liability_initial decimal(18,2) [note: 'Present value of future lease payments discounted at the applicable rate (implicit rate or IBR) at lease commencement date. Represents the initial lease liability recognized on the balance sheet under ASC 842.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lease contract. Drives accounting treatment, payment processing, and disclosure eligibility. \'Modified\' indicates a lease modification event requiring remeasurement under ASC 842.']
  lease_term_months varchar [note: 'Total non-cancellable lease term expressed in months, including any renewal periods that are reasonably certain to be exercised. Used for amortization schedule generation and ASC 842 disclosure of weighted-average remaining lease term.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'ASC 842 classification of the lease as either an operating lease (expense recognized straight-line on P&L) or a finance lease (ROU asset amortized separately from interest expense). Drives balance sheet recognition and disclosure treatment.']
  leased_asset_category varchar [note: 'Category of the underlying leased asset. Retail-specific categories include store locations (hypermarkets, department stores, discount outlets), distribution centers (DC), dark stores, micro-fulfillment centers (MFC), equipment, and vehicles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store_location|distribution_center|office|equipment|vehicle|dark_store|micro_fulfillment_center — promote to reference product]']
  lessor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the lessor (landlord or equipment owner) as stated in the lease agreement. Used for counterparty identification in AP processing, EDI transactions, and disclosure reporting.']
  low_value_asset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease qualifies for the low-value asset practical expedient (typically assets with an underlying value below $5,000 when new). When True, lease payments are recognized as expense on a straight-line basis without balance sheet recognition.']
  modification_date date [note: 'Date on which a lease modification event occurred (e.g., extension of lease term, addition of leased space, change in rent). Triggers remeasurement of the ROU asset and lease liability at the modification date under ASC 842.']
  modification_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the most recent lease modification event. Determines whether the modification is accounted for as a separate new lease or a remeasurement of the existing lease under ASC 842.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled lease payment is due to the lessor. Used for AP cash management, payment run scheduling, and short-term lease liability classification on the balance sheet.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which lease payments are due under the contract terms. Drives payment schedule generation, cash flow forecasting, and AP payment run scheduling in SAP S/4HANA.']
  purchase_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract includes an option for the lessee to purchase the underlying asset. A purchase option that is reasonably certain to be exercised is a criterion for finance lease classification under ASC 842.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract contains one or more renewal options. When True, renewal_option_term_months and renewal_reasonably_certain_flag must be evaluated for inclusion in the lease term under ASC 842.']
  renewal_option_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the renewal option period(s) available under the lease contract. Included in the lease term measurement when the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise the option.']
  renewal_reasonably_certain_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lessee has determined it is reasonably certain to exercise the renewal option, requiring inclusion of the renewal period in the lease term and remeasurement of ROU asset and lease liability under ASC 842.']
  rou_asset_carrying_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current net book value of the ROU asset after accumulated amortization (finance lease) or straight-line adjustments (operating lease) as of the last accounting close date. Used for balance sheet presentation and ASC 842 disclosure.']
  rou_asset_initial_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Initial carrying amount of the Right-of-Use (ROU) asset recognized at lease commencement, equal to the initial lease liability plus any lease payments made at or before commencement, less any lease incentives received, plus initial direct costs. Expressed in functional currency (USD).']
  short_term_lease_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease qualifies for the short-term lease practical expedient under ASC 842 (lease term of 12 months or less at commencement). When True, the lessee is not required to recognize an ROU asset or lease liability on the balance sheet.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the lease was terminated early (if applicable). Triggers derecognition of the ROU asset and lease liability and recognition of any gain or loss on termination under ASC 842.']
  termination_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract contains an early termination option. When True, the termination option must be assessed for reasonable certainty of exercise, which may shorten the lease term used in ASC 842 measurement.']
  variable_lease_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease contract includes variable lease payments (e.g., percentage rent based on store sales, CPI-indexed escalations, or CAM charges). Variable payments not dependent on an index or rate are excluded from lease liability measurement but disclosed separately under ASC 842.']
  variable_payment_basis varchar [note: 'Describes the basis on which variable lease payments are calculated. \'sales_percentage\' is common in retail (percentage rent tied to store revenue). \'cpi_index\' indicates CPI-linked escalation. \'cam_charges\' covers common area maintenance. Drives variable payment disclosure under ASC 842.']
  weighted_avg_remaining_term decimal(6,2) [note: 'Weighted average remaining lease term in years as of the reporting date, calculated across the lease portfolio. Required ASC 842 disclosure for both operating and finance leases in the financial statement footnotes.']

  Note: 'Retail lease contract master record with full payment lifecycle for store locations, DC facilities, and equipment leases under FASB ASC 842. Captures lessor, lease term, classification (operating vs. finance), ROU asset value, lease liability, incremental borrowing rate, payment schedule with individual payment records (payment date, amount, principal/interest split, variable lease payments), ROU amortization, lease liability reduction, renewal options, and GL posting references. Supports monthly lease accounting close, ASC 842 balance sheet recognition, payment tracking, and disclosure requirements.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record. Primary key for cross-entity financial postings between retail subsidiaries, international entities, and corporate holding companies.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Intercompany transactions are posted to fiscal periods. The table has fiscal_year (INT) and fiscal_period (INT) but no FK to financial_period master. This FK enables period-level reconciliation and cl']
  netting_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the netting run in which this intercompany transaction was included for multilateral settlement optimization.']
  sending_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Intercompany transactions have gl_account_sending (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This is the FIRST of two FKs from intercompany_transaction to gl_account, so the FK column name has a disting']
  sending_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions have sending_profit_center (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This is the FIRST of two FKs from intercompany_transaction to profit_center, so the FK column name has ']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this intercompany transaction record was first created in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  document_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the source document or invoice that originated this intercompany transaction. May differ from posting date for timing differences.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known SAP FI document number for this intercompany transaction. Used for audit trail and reconciliation across legal entities.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the intercompany transaction amount is due for settlement between the sending and receiving entities.']
  elimination_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the consolidation elimination journal entry document that offsets this intercompany transaction in group reporting.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) marking whether this intercompany transaction requires elimination entries during consolidation to remove intra-group transactions from consolidated financial statements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate used to convert the document currency to local currencies. Critical for multi-currency intercompany settlement and consolidation.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the exchange rate used (spot rate, monthly average, budget rate, or fixed rate) for currency conversion.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this intercompany transaction is assigned.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this intercompany transaction is assigned for financial reporting and consolidation purposes.']
  gl_account_receiving varchar [note: 'General ledger account number in the receiving company code chart of accounts where the intercompany transaction is posted.']
  local_currency_amount_receiving decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount converted to the local currency of the receiving company code for statutory reporting and consolidation.']
  local_currency_amount_sending decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount converted to the local currency of the sending company code for statutory reporting and consolidation.']
  local_currency_receiving varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the receiving company code\'s local currency for statutory books.']
  local_currency_sending varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the sending company code\'s local currency for statutory books.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this intercompany transaction record was last modified. Tracks changes for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  netting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this intercompany transaction has been included in a multilateral netting process to reduce settlement volumes and FX exposure.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms code defining the due date calculation and settlement terms for this intercompany transaction (e.g., NET30, NET60).']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date when the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger. Used for period assignment and financial reporting.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the legal entity receiving the intercompany transaction (creditor side in most cases).']
  receiving_profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center within the receiving company code that is charged or credited for this intercompany transaction. Used for internal P&L reporting.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the intercompany transaction was successfully reconciled and confirmed by both sending and receiving entities.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the intercompany reconciliation process indicating whether sending and receiving entries match and have been confirmed by both entities.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference to the originating business document (purchase order, invoice, contract) that triggered this intercompany transaction.']
  sending_company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code of the legal entity originating the intercompany transaction (debtor side in most cases).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Actual date when the intercompany transaction was settled through cash transfer, netting, or other settlement mechanism.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism used to settle the intercompany transaction (cash transfer, multilateral netting, bilateral offset, or in-kind settlement).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the document currency. Represents the gross amount before any adjustments.']
  transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional business context and details about the nature and purpose of this intercompany transaction.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intercompany transaction in the financial close and consolidation workflow.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the cross-entity posting (management fee, shared service allocation, inventory transfer, loan, dividend, royalty).']
  transfer_pricing_documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the supporting transfer pricing documentation, study, or agreement that justifies the pricing of this intercompany transaction.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'The OECD-compliant transfer pricing methodology applied to determine the arm\'s length price for this intercompany transaction for tax compliance.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date for cash flow and treasury purposes, particularly relevant for intercompany loans and settlements.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary difference identified during intercompany reconciliation between sending and receiving entity records. Zero indicates perfect match.']

  Note: 'Intercompany financial transaction record capturing cross-entity postings between retail subsidiaries, international entities, and corporate holding companies in SAP S/4HANA FI. Includes sending/receiving company codes, transaction type (management fee, shared service allocation, inventory transfer, loan, dividend), amount, document and local currency, FX conversion rate, netting status, elimination flag, and reconciliation status. Supports legal entity consolidation, intercompany elimination entries, transfer pricing documentation, and multi-currency settlement.'
}

Table finance.tax_posting {
  tax_posting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax posting transaction record in SAP S/4HANA FI-TX.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax postings have cost_center (STRING) but no FK to cost_center master. This FK enables cost allocation for tax expenses.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.dc_facility. Business justification: Sales and use tax postings by facility location for nexus determination, tax jurisdiction assignment, and compliance reporting. Required for multi-state retail tax compliance and audit defense.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax postings have gl_account_number (STRING) but no FK to gl_account master. This FK enables account validation for tax postings.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail store location where the taxable transaction occurred (for POS sales tax postings).']
  original_posting_tax_posting_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax posting ID that this record reverses (populated only when reversal_flag is true).']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer for output tax postings on sales transactions.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Tax postings have profit_center (STRING) but no FK to profit_center master. This FK enables P&L allocation for tax expenses.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier/vendor for input tax postings on purchase transactions.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to supporting documentation for tax audit defense (original invoice, receipt, exemption certificate, tax calculation log).']
  business_area varchar [note: 'The business area code for cross-company-code financial reporting and segment analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for this tax posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax posting record was first created in the SAP S/4HANA FI-TX system.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known accounting document number from which this tax posting originated (invoice, receipt, credit memo).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of source accounting document that generated this tax posting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert transaction currency to local currency for GL posting.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the company\'s local reporting currency for financial consolidation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this tax posting record was last modified (for correction postings or adjustments).']
  nexus_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the retailer has established tax nexus (physical or economic presence) in this jurisdiction, triggering tax collection obligation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the tax transaction was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) to which this tax posting is assigned for tax return preparation and regulatory reporting.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this posting is a reversal of a previously posted tax transaction (for corrections or cancellations).']
  reverse_charge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a reverse charge VAT transaction where the customer is responsible for self-assessing and remitting VAT.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount posted to the tax GL account, in transaction currency.']
  tax_amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount converted to local reporting currency for financial statement consolidation.']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'The name of the government tax authority to which this tax will be remitted (e.g., California Department of Tax and Fee Administration, IRS).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The SAP tax code that defines the tax type, rate, and GL account assignment for this posting.']
  tax_determination_date date [note: 'The business event date used to determine applicable tax rates and jurisdiction (typically transaction date or delivery date).']
  tax_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is input tax (paid to suppliers) or output tax (collected from customers).']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction qualified for tax exemption (e.g., resale certificate, government entity, non-profit exemption).']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The exemption certificate number provided by the customer to support tax-exempt status (resale certificate, government ID, non-profit certificate).']
  tax_jurisdiction_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name for city-level sales tax jurisdictions (applicable in US multi-jurisdiction tax scenarios).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Composite jurisdiction code uniquely identifying the complete tax authority (country-state-county-city combination).']
  tax_jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the tax jurisdiction where the tax obligation arises.']
  tax_jurisdiction_county varchar [note: 'County or district name for county-level sales tax jurisdictions (applicable in US multi-jurisdiction tax scenarios).']
  tax_jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code within the country for state-level sales tax (US) or provincial tax (Canada).']
  tax_point_date date [note: 'The date when the tax liability arises for VAT purposes (typically invoice date or goods delivery date per VAT regulations).']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(7,5) [note: 'The effective tax rate percentage applied to the taxable base (e.g., 8.25000 for 8.25% sales tax).']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'The retailer\'s tax registration number with the jurisdiction\'s tax authority (sales tax permit number, VAT registration number, GST number).']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax being posted (sales tax, VAT, use tax, excise duty, withholding tax, customs duty).']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross transaction amount subject to tax before tax calculation, in transaction currency.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the tax transaction was recorded.']

  Note: 'Tax transaction record capturing sales tax, VAT, use tax, and excise duty postings on retail transactions in SAP S/4HANA FI-TX. Includes tax code, tax jurisdiction (state/county/city/country), taxable base amount, tax amount, tax rate, tax type (input/output), tax determination date, reporting period, nexus indicator, exempt flag, and source document reference. Supports multi-jurisdiction sales tax compliance, Streamlined Sales Tax (SST) reporting, tax return preparation, tax audit defense, and regulatory reporting to federal, state, and local tax authorities.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corporate bank account record. Primary key for the bank account entity.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bank accounts have gl_account_code (STRING) representing the GL account for bank reconciliation, but no FK to gl_account master. This FK links the bank account to its corresponding GL account in the c']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'The date when the bank account was permanently closed, if applicable. Null for active accounts.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The legal name or business description of the bank account as registered with the financial institution.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The financial institution account number used for AP disbursements, AR collections, payroll funding, and treasury operations. Confidential financial identifier.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'The date when the bank account was originally opened with the financial institution.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the bank account indicating whether it is available for transactions, temporarily suspended, permanently closed, or awaiting activation.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'The functional classification of the bank account indicating its primary business purpose: operating (general business transactions), payroll (employee compensation), concentration (cash pooling), lockbox (AR collections), disbursement (AP payments), or collection (customer payments).']
  ach_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this bank account is enabled for ACH electronic funds transfer processing for domestic US transactions.']
  authorized_signatories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of employee names or identifiers authorized to approve transactions and sign on behalf of the company for this bank account.']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The available balance for withdrawal or payment processing, accounting for pending transactions, holds, and minimum balance requirements.']
  bank_key varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the bank in the SAP system, used for payment processing and bank statement reconciliation.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the financial institution where the account is held.']
  branch_address varchar [note: 'The physical street address of the bank branch where the account is held, used for correspondence and regulatory reporting.']
  branch_city varchar [note: 'The city where the bank branch is located.']
  branch_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166 country code representing the country where the bank branch is located.']
  branch_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific bank branch where the account is domiciled.']
  branch_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the bank branch location.']
  branch_state varchar [note: 'The state or province where the bank branch is located.']
  check_printing_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this bank account is enabled for physical check printing and disbursement.']
  collection_method_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this bank account is enabled for incoming payment collection (AR receipts, customer payments, lockbox processing).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code representing the legal entity that owns this bank account, used for financial consolidation and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the SAP S/4HANA system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the denomination of the bank account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD).']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current available balance in the bank account as of the last bank statement processing or real-time update, used for cash position reporting and treasury management.']
  iban varchar [note: 'The International Bank Account Number used for identifying bank accounts across international borders, primarily in European and other international transactions.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The annual interest rate applied to the account balance, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%), used for interest income calculation.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when the last successful bank reconciliation was completed for this account, aligning bank statement balances with GL balances.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent bank statement processed for this account, used for reconciliation tracking and audit purposes.']
  minimum_balance_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum balance that must be maintained in the account per the bank agreement to avoid fees or maintain account status.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or employee identifier of the person who last modified this bank account record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this bank account record was last modified in the SAP S/4HANA system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments related to the bank account management and usage.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial balance of the bank account when it was first opened or migrated into the system, used as the baseline for reconciliation.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum overdraft or line of credit limit authorized by the bank for this account, used for short-term liquidity management.']
  payment_method_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this bank account is enabled for outgoing payment processing (AP disbursements, payroll, vendor payments).']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit American Bankers Association (ABA) routing transit number for domestic US bank transfers and ACH transactions.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'The Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) Bank Identifier Code (BIC) used for international wire transfers and cross-border payments.']
  wire_transfer_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this bank account is enabled for domestic and international wire transfer processing.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID or employee identifier of the person who created this bank account record.']

  Note: 'Corporate bank account master record for retail entity bank accounts used for AP disbursements, AR collections, payroll funding, and treasury operations in SAP S/4HANA FI-TR. Captures bank name, account number, routing/SWIFT/IBAN, account type (operating, payroll, concentration, lockbox), currency, bank key, daily balance positions, authorized signatories, account status, and GL reconciliation account. Supports cash position reporting, daily bank statement processing, payment method determination, and bank reconciliation workflows.'
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  approved_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'The user identifier of the authorized approver who approved this payment run for execution.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'The user identifier of the person who created this payment run in the system.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account from which funds are disbursed for this payment run.']
  financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Payment runs occur within specific financial periods for accounting close and reconciliation purposes. Currently payment_run has fiscal_year: INT and fiscal_period: INT as separate attributes. Adding ']
  parent_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent payment run if this run is a retry, correction, or child run of a previous payment run.']
  reversal_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reversal_payment_run_id)']
  approval_workflow_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the approval workflow instance that governs the authorization process for this payment run.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run was approved by an authorized approver.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code within the enterprise that is executing this payment run, aligned with SAP S/4HANA company code structure.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the payment run was fully completed and all payments successfully processed.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center code to which the payment run expenses are allocated for management accounting and budgeting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment run, indicating the currency in which payments are denominated.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context or notes about the purpose and scope of this payment run.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed error message or failure reason if the payment run encountered processing errors or was rejected by the payment system.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment run was executed and submitted to the payment processor or banking system.']
  failed_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payments that failed to process or were rejected during this payment run.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the payment run transactions are posted to the general ledger for financial reporting.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payment run is part of a recurring payment schedule (e.g., monthly payroll, quarterly vendor payments).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment run record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes or comments related to this payment run for internal reference and audit purposes.']
  payment_batch_code varchar [note: 'External batch identifier assigned by the payment processor or banking system for tracking and reconciliation.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The system or interface through which the payment run is processed and transmitted to financial institutions.']
  payment_file_format varchar [note: 'The technical file format standard used for the payment file transmission (e.g., NACHA for ACH, ISO 20022 for international payments).']
  payment_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the payment file generated for this payment run and transmitted to the bank or payment processor.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or mechanism used to disburse funds in this payment run.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The standard payment terms applied to the invoices or obligations being paid in this payment run.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The priority classification of this payment run, determining processing sequence and urgency.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center code associated with this payment run for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment run was successfully reconciled with bank statements.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the bank reconciliation process for this payment run, tracking whether payments have been matched to bank statements.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the payment run, formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment run based on the business purpose and payment category.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which payments in this run are scheduled to be executed and funds disbursed.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run in the approval and execution workflow.']
  successful_payment_count varchar [note: 'The number of payments that were successfully processed and completed within this payment run.']
  total_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of all payments included in this payment run before any adjustments or fees.']
  total_payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual payment transactions included in this payment run.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

Table finance.plan_version {
  plan_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plan_version']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this plan version.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit that this plan version applies to.']
  created_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who originally created this plan version.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or organizational unit that owns or is responsible for this plan version.']
  last_modified_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this plan version.']
  parent_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent plan version from which this version was derived or copied. Null for original versions.']
  prior_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on plan_version (prior_plan_version_id)']
  scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planning scenario (e.g., best case, worst case, most likely) that this version represents.']
  submitted_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who submitted this plan version for approval.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this plan version was formally approved by authorized stakeholders.']
  assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying this plan version, such as growth rates, inflation rates, market conditions, or strategic initiatives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this plan version record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values in this plan version (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the plan version, including its purpose, key assumptions, and any special considerations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when this plan version becomes effective and operational for financial planning purposes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date when this plan version is no longer valid or has been superseded by a newer version. Null for versions that remain active.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year that this plan version applies to (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version serves as the baseline or reference version for variance analysis and performance tracking.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan version is locked from further edits, typically after approval or finalization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this plan version record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or annotations related to this plan version, including change rationale or special instructions.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period covered by this plan version.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period covered by this plan version.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan version indicating its approval state and usability.']
  submitted_date date [note: 'The date when this plan version was submitted for review and approval.']
  version_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for this plan version (e.g., \'Initial Budget\', \'Mid-Year Reforecast\', \'Board Approved Version\').']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version identifier or number for this plan iteration (e.g., V1.0, V2.1, 2024-Q1-Rev3). Used to distinguish between different iterations of the same plan.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan version type indicating its purpose in the planning cycle.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plan_version. Referenced by plan_version_id.'
}

Table finance.netting_run {
  netting_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for netting_run']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this netting run, supporting segregation of duties and audit requirements.']
  initiated_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who initiated this netting run, supporting accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity for which this netting run is executed, supporting multi-entity financial consolidation.']
  reversal_netting_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on netting_run (reversal_netting_run_id)']
  reversed_by_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subsequent netting run that reversed this run, maintaining the audit trail of corrections.']
  account_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct accounts (vendors, customers, intercompany entities) included in this netting run.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) to which this netting run applies, aligning with the general ledger posting period.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this netting run requires formal approval before posting to the general ledger, based on materiality thresholds or policy rules.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the netting run was formally approved, establishing the audit trail for financial close controls.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the netting run processing completed successfully or terminated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this netting run record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency for this netting run.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the purpose, scope, or special circumstances of this netting run.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'System-generated error message if the netting run failed, supporting troubleshooting and operational monitoring.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The business date on which the netting run was executed or is scheduled to execute.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the netting run processing began, capturing the exact moment of execution for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1-4) to which this netting run applies, supporting quarterly financial close and reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this netting run belongs, supporting year-end close and multi-year reporting.']
  gl_batch_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger batch that contains the journal entries generated by this netting run.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the netting run entries were posted to the general ledger.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this netting run record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  net_position_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the netting run resulted in a net payable, net receivable, or balanced position.']
  netting_method varchar [note: 'The netting methodology applied in this run, determining how offsetting balances are calculated and settled.']
  netting_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of accounts and transactions included in this netting run (e.g., intercompany balances, vendor payables, customer receivables).']
  posted_to_gl_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the netting run results have been posted to the general ledger, confirming financial statement impact.']
  processing_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds taken to complete the netting run processing, supporting performance monitoring and optimization.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this netting run has been reversed, supporting error correction and period adjustments.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for reversing this netting run, supporting audit and compliance documentation requirements.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the netting run, formatted as NR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the netting run based on its purpose and timing within the financial close cycle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system or module that generated or triggered this netting run (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Oracle Financials).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the netting run within the financial close workflow.']
  total_gross_payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of payables included in the netting run before netting adjustments are applied.']
  total_gross_receivable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of receivables included in the netting run before netting adjustments are applied.']
  total_netted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount after offsetting payables and receivables, representing the actual settlement obligation or receivable.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual transactions included in this netting run for processing and reconciliation tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for netting_run. Referenced by netting_run_id.'
}

Table finance.ledger {
  ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ledger']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts structure that this ledger uses for account classification and reporting.']
  parent_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ledger (parent_ledger_id)']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'The accounting standard or framework that governs this ledger (e.g., US GAAP, IFRS).']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity or company code to which this ledger belongs in the enterprise structure.']
  consolidation_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for consolidation scenarios when this ledger is used for group reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'The controlling area code for management accounting and cost center reporting associated with this ledger.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency of the ledger.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the ledger purpose, scope, and usage within the financial reporting framework.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ledger ceases to be active for new postings. Null indicates an open-ended ledger.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this ledger becomes active and available for financial postings.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Code representing the fiscal calendar variant used by this ledger (e.g., calendar year, 4-4-5 retail calendar).']
  is_consolidation_ledger boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger is used for group consolidation and intercompany elimination purposes.']
  is_leading_ledger boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary leading ledger for the organization (true) or a secondary/parallel ledger (false).']
  last_closed_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent period close was completed for this ledger.']
  last_closed_period varchar [note: 'The most recent fiscal period (YYYYMM format) that has been closed in this ledger.']
  ledger_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the ledger based on reporting purpose and accounting framework alignment.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the ledger used in financial reporting and system integration.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the ledger for human-readable identification.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger based on its functional purpose in the financial accounting system.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this ledger record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ledger record was last modified in the system.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'Code defining the number and structure of posting periods (e.g., 12 regular periods plus special periods) for this ledger.']
  profit_loss_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number used for profit and loss statement aggregation.']
  retained_earnings_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number used for retained earnings carry-forward during year-end closing.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ledger in the financial system lifecycle.']
  supports_cost_center boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports cost center accounting for overhead allocation and analysis.']
  supports_profit_center boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports profit center accounting for internal management reporting.']
  supports_segment_reporting boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ledger supports segment-level financial reporting as required by FASB ASC 280.']
  valuation_view varchar [note: 'The valuation perspective or view that this ledger represents for parallel accounting purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this ledger record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ledger. Referenced by ledger_id.'
}

Table finance.scenario {
  scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for scenario']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this scenario was formally approved. Critical for establishing the official baseline for financial planning and reporting.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the scenario in the financial governance workflow. Pending indicates awaiting review; Approved indicates formally accepted; Rejected indicates not accepted; Under Review indicates in approval process.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or committee who approved this scenario. Used for audit trail and governance compliance.']
  assumptions_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key financial and business assumptions underlying this scenario (e.g., revenue growth rates, cost inflation, market conditions, strategic initiatives). Critical for scenario interpretation and sensitivity analysis.']
  baseline_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the baseline scenario used for variance analysis and comparison. Typically points to the approved budget or prior year actual scenario.']
  business_unit_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of business units, divisions, or legal entities included in this scenario. Defines the organizational scope of the scenario for segmented reporting and analysis.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for consolidating financial data in this scenario. Full for 100% consolidation of subsidiaries; Proportional for consolidation based on ownership percentage; Equity for equity method accounting; None for standalone entity reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this scenario record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the reporting currency for this scenario (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All financial amounts in this scenario are expressed in this currency.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary system or process that generated or maintains this scenario (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Hyperion Planning, Excel-based planning model). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scenario, including its purpose, assumptions, key drivers, and intended use cases. Provides business context for financial analysts and planners.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date through which this scenario remains effective. Nullable for scenarios with indefinite validity (e.g., rolling forecasts).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this scenario becomes effective and can be used for financial planning, reporting, and analysis.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period within the year to which this scenario applies. Format: Q1-Q4 for quarters, M01-M12 for months, H1-H2 for half-years, FY for full year.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this scenario applies (e.g., 2024). Used for period-based financial planning and reporting alignment.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Description of geographic regions or countries included in this scenario (e.g., North America, EMEA, Global). Defines the geographic coverage for regional financial planning and reporting.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this scenario represents consolidated financial data across multiple entities or business units. True for enterprise-wide consolidated scenarios; False for standalone entity scenarios.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the default scenario for reporting and analysis. True if this scenario should be used as the primary reference; False otherwise. Only one scenario per fiscal period should be marked as default.']
  is_public boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this scenario is used for external public reporting and disclosure. True for scenarios used in SEC filings, earnings releases, and investor communications; False for internal management scenarios.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this scenario record. Used for audit trail and accountability in financial data governance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this scenario record was last updated. Critical for change tracking and ensuring users are working with the most current scenario version.']
  lock_date date [note: 'Date on which this scenario was locked to prevent further modifications. Ensures data integrity for finalized scenarios used in reporting and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or documentation related to this scenario. Used for capturing ad-hoc information, special instructions, or context that does not fit in structured fields.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team responsible for maintaining and updating this scenario. Typically a financial planning analyst, FP&A manager, or controller.']
  parent_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent scenario from which this scenario was derived or versioned. Nullable for original scenarios. Enables hierarchical scenario relationships and version lineage tracking.']
  planning_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Number of months into the future that this scenario covers. Used for forecasting and planning scenarios to define the projection period (e.g., 12 for annual budget, 36 for strategic plan).']
  scenario_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the scenario indicating its primary use case. Operational for day-to-day financial management; Strategic for long-term planning; Regulatory for compliance reporting; Management for internal decision-making; Consolidated for enterprise-wide aggregation.']
  scenario_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the scenario for external reference and reporting (e.g., ACTUAL, BUDGET_2024, FORECAST_Q1).']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the financial scenario (e.g., Actual Results, Annual Budget 2024, Rolling Forecast Q1 2024).']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scenario indicating its purpose in financial planning and analysis. Actual represents realized results; Budget represents approved annual plan; Forecast represents updated projections; Plan represents strategic multi-year plan; Projection represents long-term estimates; Baseline represents reference point for variance analysis.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scenario. Draft indicates scenario is being prepared; Active indicates scenario is in use for reporting and analysis; Locked indicates scenario is finalized and no further changes allowed; Archived indicates scenario is retained for historical reference; Superseded indicates scenario has been replaced by a newer version; Cancelled indicates scenario was abandoned.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for scenarios that undergo revisions. Enables tracking of scenario evolution and comparison across versions (e.g., Budget v1, Budget v2).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for scenario. Referenced by scenario_id.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for legal_entity']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'The accounting method used to consolidate this legal entity into group financial statements (e.g., full consolidation, equity method, cost method).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this legal entity record was first created in the system.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the legal entity was officially dissolved, liquidated, or ceased operations.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or fictitious business name under which the legal entity operates, if different from the legal name.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit DUNS number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for business identification and credit reporting.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this legal entity record becomes effective for financial reporting and operational purposes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this legal entity record is effective; null indicates the record is currently active.']
  entity_status varchar [note: 'Current operational and legal status of the entity (e.g., active, inactive, dissolved, suspended, merged, acquired).']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'The legal structure and classification of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC, partnership, sole proprietorship, branch, subsidiary).']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'The month in which the legal entity\'s fiscal year ends (1-12, where 1=January, 12=December).']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'The primary currency in which the legal entity conducts its business operations and maintains its books, using ISO 4217 three-letter code.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'The date on which the legal entity was officially incorporated or registered with the governing authority.']
  incorporation_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The country, state, or province where the legal entity is incorporated or registered.']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'The standard industry classification code for the entity\'s primary business activity (e.g., NAICS, SIC, GICS).']
  is_public_company boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the legal entity is a publicly traded company with securities listed on a stock exchange.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this legal entity record was last updated or modified.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The official registered legal name of the entity as it appears on incorporation documents and regulatory filings.']
  lei_code varchar [note: 'The 20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier code used for financial transaction reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ownership interest held by the parent entity, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 100.00 for wholly-owned, 51.00 for majority-owned).']
  parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent legal entity if this entity is a subsidiary or branch within a corporate hierarchy.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the primary contact person or registered agent.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary contact person or registered agent for the legal entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the primary contact person or registered agent.']
  registered_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the official registered address of the legal entity as filed with regulatory authorities.']
  registered_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the official registered address (suite, floor, building name, etc.).']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'The city of the official registered address.']
  registered_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the official registered address.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the official registered address.']
