  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the merit budget (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP).']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the merit cycle for external reference and reporting (e.g., \'MC-2024-01\', \'FY24-MERIT\').']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the merit cycle (e.g., \'FY2024 Annual Merit Review\', \'Q2 2024 Performance Cycle\').']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the merit cycle based on frequency and purpose (annual, mid-year, quarterly, off-cycle, special).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the merit cycle, including objectives, scope, and any special considerations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when the merit cycle period ends. Nullable for open-ended cycles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when the merit cycle becomes effective and merit increases take effect.']
  eligible_employee_count varchar [note: 'The number of employees eligible to receive merit increases in this cycle.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this merit cycle applies (e.g., 2024).']
  guideline_increase_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The recommended or guideline merit increase percentage for standard performers (e.g., 3.0 represents 3%).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merit cycle is currently active (True) or inactive (False).']
  maximum_increase_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum merit increase percentage allowed for any individual employee (e.g., 10.0 represents 10%).']
  minimum_increase_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum merit increase percentage allowed for eligible employees (e.g., 0.0 for no minimum).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the merit cycle record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the merit cycle record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the merit cycle for internal reference.']
  performance_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the performance period being evaluated for this merit cycle.']
  performance_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the performance period being evaluated for this merit cycle.']
  planning_start_date date [note: 'The date when planning and preparation for the merit cycle begins.']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method used to prorate merit increases for employees hired mid-cycle (none, hire_date, service_months, custom).']
  review_end_date date [note: 'The date by which all performance reviews and merit recommendations must be completed.']
  review_start_date date [note: 'The date when performance reviews and merit recommendations begin.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merit cycle (draft, planned, active, in_review, approved, closed, cancelled).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the merit cycle record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for merit_cycle. Referenced by merit_cycle_id.'
}

Table workforce.candidate {
  candidate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for candidate']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the internal employee who referred this candidate, used for referral bonus tracking.']
  job_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job requisition or job posting that the candidate applied to.']
  referred_by_candidate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on candidate (referred_by_candidate_id)']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the candidate\'s residential address, typically including street number and street name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the candidate\'s residential address, typically including apartment, suite, or unit number.']
  alternate_phone_number varchar [note: 'Secondary or alternate contact phone number for the candidate, if provided.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate submitted their application for the position.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the candidate\'s application, used for tracking and reference throughout the recruitment process.']
  availability_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate is available to start employment if an offer is extended.']
  background_check_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the background check process was completed for the candidate.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the candidate\'s background verification and screening process.']
  candidate_source varchar [note: 'Channel or source through which the candidate was identified or applied, used for recruitment effectiveness analysis.']
  candidate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the candidate in the recruitment lifecycle, indicating their progression through the hiring process.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the candidate\'s residential address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the candidate\'s country of residence.']
  cover_letter_file_path varchar [note: 'Storage location or file path reference to the candidate\'s uploaded cover letter document, if provided.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate record was first created in the system.']
  desired_salary decimal(15,2) [note: 'Salary expectation or desired compensation amount indicated by the candidate during the application process.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for candidate communication and correspondence throughout the recruitment process.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'The first name or given name of the candidate as provided in the application.']
  highest_education_level varchar [note: 'Highest level of education completed by the candidate, used for qualification assessment.']
  interview_completed_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate\'s interview was completed.']
  interview_scheduled_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate\'s interview is scheduled to take place.']
  is_eligible_for_rehire boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is a former employee who is eligible for rehire based on previous employment records.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate record was last updated or modified.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'The last name or family name of the candidate as provided in the application.']
  location_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred work location or store location indicated by the candidate.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'The middle name or middle initial of the candidate, if provided.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate accepted the employment offer.']
  offer_extended_date date [note: 'Date when a formal employment offer was extended to the candidate.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the candidate, used for interview scheduling and recruitment communication.']
  position_applied_for varchar [note: 'Job title or position name that the candidate has applied for.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code or ZIP code of the candidate\'s residential address.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the employee or contact who referred the candidate, if applicable.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the candidate was notified of rejection or when the application was closed as unsuccessful.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or category explaining why the candidate was not selected for the position.']
  resume_file_path varchar [note: 'Storage location or file path reference to the candidate\'s uploaded resume or curriculum vitae document.']
  salary_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the desired salary amount.']
  screening_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned during initial candidate screening or assessment, used for ranking and filtering candidates.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the candidate\'s residential address.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total number of years of relevant work experience reported by the candidate.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for candidate. Referenced by candidate_id.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_run {
  payroll_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_run']
  approved_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this payroll run for processing.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initiated or created this payroll run.']
  payroll_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll calendar that defines the schedule and timing for this payroll run.']
  reversal_of_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payroll run that this run reverses or corrects, if applicable.']
  reversal_payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_run (reversal_payroll_run_id)']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run was approved for processing.']
  check_date date [note: 'The date printed on physical checks or used as the effective date for electronic payments.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the legal entity or company for which this payroll run is executed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll run.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees included in this payroll run.']
  employer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total employer-paid payroll taxes including FICA, FUTA, SUTA, and other statutory employer contributions.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'The recurring schedule on which this payroll run is executed.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which payroll transactions from this run are posted to the general ledger.']
  gl_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether payroll transactions have been posted to the general ledger.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross wages and salaries before any deductions for all employees in this payroll run.']
  integration_batch_code varchar [note: 'External batch identifier used for integration with Workday HCM or other payroll systems.']
  is_final_run boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a final payroll run for terminated employees or end-of-year processing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run record was last modified.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net pay amount distributed to employees after all deductions for this payroll run.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this payroll run for documentation and audit purposes.']
  pay_date date [note: 'The date on which employees will receive payment for this payroll run.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the pay period covered by this payroll run.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the pay period covered by this payroll run.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment method used for disbursing funds to employees in this payroll run.']
  processing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when payroll processing completed for this run.']
  processing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when payroll processing began for this run.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the payroll run, externally visible and used for reference in communications and reporting.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run indicating the purpose and nature of the payroll processing cycle.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll run indicating its position in the processing workflow.']
  tax_filing_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter for tax reporting purposes to which this payroll run belongs.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year for tax reporting purposes to which this payroll run belongs.']
  total_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost to the employer including gross pay, employer taxes, and employer-paid benefits for this payroll run.']
  total_deductions_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all deductions including taxes, benefits, garnishments, and other withholdings for this payroll run.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_run. Referenced by payroll_run_id.'
}

Table workforce.shift_swap_request {
  shift_swap_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift_swap_request']
  approving_manager_associate_id varchar [note: 'The manager or supervisor who approved or rejected the shift swap request.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'The employee who is initiating the shift swap request and seeking to exchange their assigned shift.']
  counterpart_shift_swap_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shift_swap_request (counterpart_shift_swap_request_id)']
  department_id varchar [note: 'The department within the store or facility where the shift swap is occurring (e.g., grocery, electronics, apparel).']
  proposed_shift_shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'The shift assignment that the requesting employee is willing to take in exchange. May be null for one-way swap requests.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'The shift assignment that the requesting employee wants to swap away from.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'The retail store or distribution center location where the shift swap is taking place.']
  target_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'The employee who is being asked to swap shifts with the requesting employee. May be null if request is open to any qualified employee.']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the approving manager regarding their decision on the shift swap request.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shift swap request was officially approved by the authorized manager or system.']
  auto_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shift swap request was automatically approved by the system without manual manager intervention.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shift swap was fully executed and the schedule was updated to reflect the swap.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of automated compliance validation checking labor laws, overtime rules, rest period requirements, and qualification matching.']
  compliance_violation_details varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any labor law or policy violations detected during compliance checking of the shift swap request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this shift swap request record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time after which the shift swap request is no longer valid and will automatically expire if not acted upon.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this shift swap request record was most recently updated or modified.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification has been sent to the target employee or manager about this shift swap request.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when notification was sent to relevant parties about the shift swap request.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency or priority assigned to the shift swap request, influencing processing order and approval speed.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when a shift swap request is rejected, detailing why the request could not be approved.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the shift swap request, formatted as SSR-YYYYMMDD sequence.']
  request_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the requesting employee for why they need to swap shifts.']
  request_reason_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the reason for the shift swap request for reporting and analytics purposes.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift swap request in the approval workflow.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the swap request: direct (specific employee targeted), open (any qualified employee), or partial (portion of shift).']
  requires_manager_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift swap request requires explicit manager approval or can be auto-approved based on business rules.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the target employee or manager responded to the shift swap request.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shift swap request was originally submitted by the requesting employee.']
  swap_effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the shift swap will take effect and the new schedule assignments become active.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shift_swap_request. Referenced by shift_swap_request_id.'
}

Table workforce.coverage_request {
  coverage_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for coverage_request']
  approver_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved or rejected the coverage request.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who is requesting coverage or whose shift needs coverage.']
  covering_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who has been assigned or volunteered to provide coverage.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department within the store where coverage is required.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store location where coverage is needed.']
  requesting_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initiated the coverage request, if different from the employee needing coverage.']
  superseded_coverage_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on coverage_request (superseded_coverage_request_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the coverage request was approved or rejected by the manager.']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Comments or notes provided by the approver explaining the approval or rejection decision.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether managerial approval is required before coverage can be assigned.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the coverage request approval or rejection decision was recorded.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the coverage request was cancelled by the requester or system.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided for why the coverage request was cancelled.']
  coverage_date date [note: 'Date for which coverage is being requested.']
  coverage_end_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end date and time of the shift or period requiring coverage.']
  coverage_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start date and time of the shift or period requiring coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this coverage request record was first created in the data platform.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours for which coverage is needed, calculated from start and end times.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the coverage request is no longer valid if not fulfilled.']
  fulfillment_date date [note: 'Date when a covering employee was successfully assigned to fulfill the coverage request.']
  fulfillment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the coverage request was marked as fulfilled.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this coverage request record is currently active in the system.']
  labor_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code to which labor costs for this coverage will be allocated.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this coverage request record was last updated in the data platform.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification has been sent to eligible employees about this coverage opportunity.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when notification was sent to eligible employees.']
  premium_pay_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the covering employee is eligible for premium pay or incentive compensation for this coverage.']
  premium_pay_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base pay rate for coverage shifts, such as 1.5 for time-and-a-half.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the coverage request based on operational impact.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason why coverage is needed.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the employee or manager describing why coverage is needed.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the coverage request was submitted by the employee or manager.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the coverage request, typically displayed to employees and managers.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the coverage request was created in the system.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coverage request indicating the reason for coverage need.']
  response_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees who responded to the coverage request notification.']
  shift_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific shift that requires coverage.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system from which this coverage request originated, such as Workday HCM or internal scheduling system.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this coverage request in the originating source system.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the coverage request in the approval and fulfillment workflow.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for coverage_request. Referenced by coverage_request_id.'
}

Table workforce.pay_period {
  pay_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pay_period']
  prior_pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pay_period (prior_pay_period_id)']
  approval_deadline timestamp [note: 'The date and time by which managers must approve timesheets and time entries for this pay period.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month in which this pay period starts (1-12).']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this pay period starts (e.g., 2024).']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period was officially closed for time entry and moved to processing status.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date included in this pay period. Effective-until date for the pay period.']
  finalized_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when payroll processing was completed and the pay period was finalized.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs (1, 2, 3, or 4).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this pay period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period allows for payroll adjustments or corrections from prior periods.']
  is_holiday_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period includes major holidays that may affect scheduling or payroll processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pay period record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about this pay period, such as special processing instructions or exceptions.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overtime hours can be recorded and compensated during this pay period.']
  pay_date date [note: 'The date on which employees are paid for work performed during this pay period.']
  payroll_cycle_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific payroll cycle or schedule this pay period belongs to (e.g., \'RETAIL-HOURLY\', \'CORPORATE-SALARY\').']
  payroll_processing_date date [note: 'The date on which payroll calculations and processing are executed for this pay period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the pay period (e.g., \'PP01-2024\', \'January 2024 - Period 1\').']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the pay period within the fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 1, 2, 3...26 for bi-weekly).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay period frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly).']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this pay period. Effective-from date for the pay period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pay period indicating whether it is open for time entry, closed for processing, finalized, or archived.']
  time_entry_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for time and attendance tracking during this pay period.']
  timesheet_submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'The date and time by which employees must submit their timesheets for this pay period.']
  total_days_count varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days in this pay period.']
  working_days_count varchar [note: 'The number of standard working days (excluding weekends and holidays) within this pay period.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pay_period. Referenced by pay_period_id.'
}

Table workforce.union {
  union_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for union']
  parent_union_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on union (parent_union_id)']
  affiliation varchar [note: 'Parent federation or umbrella organization the union is affiliated with (e.g., AFL-CIO, Change to Win).']
  bargaining_unit_description varchar [note: 'Description of the employee group or job classifications covered by this union for collective bargaining purposes.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the union was certified by the National Labor Relations Board or equivalent authority.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for union correspondence and official communications.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary union representative or business agent for communication and coordination.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the union representative or office.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement with this union became effective.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement with this union expires.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this union record was first created in the system.']
  decertification_date date [note: 'Date when the union was decertified or lost its bargaining rights, if applicable.']
  dues_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard union dues amount charged per payment period, in the organization\'s base currency.']
  dues_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate union dues for member employees.']
  dues_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which union dues are collected from member employees.']
  grievance_procedure_description varchar [note: 'Summary of the formal grievance and dispute resolution process established in the collective bargaining agreement.']
  initiation_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time fee charged to new union members upon joining, in the organization\'s base currency.']
  is_right_to_work_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether right-to-work laws apply to this union\'s jurisdiction, affecting mandatory membership requirements.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational scope of the union\'s authority (e.g., regional, national, store-specific).']
  local_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the local chapter or branch of the union, if applicable.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current number of employees who are members of this union within the organization.']
  national_union_name varchar [note: 'Name of the national or international union body that the local union is part of.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special considerations related to this union relationship.']
  office_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the union office street address including building number and street name.']
  office_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the union office address for suite, floor, or additional location details.']
  office_city varchar [note: 'City where the union office is located.']
  office_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the union office location.']
  office_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the union office location.']
  office_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the union office is located.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'Date when the union was officially recognized as the collective bargaining representative for employees.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the union relationship with the organization.']
  union_abbreviation varchar [note: 'Commonly used abbreviation or acronym for the union name (e.g., UFCW, SEIU, Teamsters).']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the union, used for external identification and reporting purposes.']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the labor union or collective bargaining organization.']
  union_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the union based on its organizational structure and membership scope.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this union record was last modified in the system.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website address for the union organization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for union. Referenced by union_id.'
}

Table workforce.bargaining_unit {
  bargaining_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bargaining_unit']
  parent_bargaining_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bargaining_unit (parent_bargaining_unit_id)']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the bargaining unit was officially certified by the National Labor Relations Board or equivalent regulatory body.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Official certification or registration number issued by the labor relations board for this bargaining unit.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the official collective bargaining agreement document.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement became effective for this bargaining unit.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement expires and requires renegotiation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bargaining unit record was first created in the system.']
  dues_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard union dues amount deducted per pay period for members of this bargaining unit.']
  dues_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which union dues are deducted from member paychecks.']
  eligible_employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees eligible for membership in this bargaining unit based on job classification and location.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage area of the bargaining unit indicating whether it spans multiple locations or is site-specific.']
  grievance_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the documented grievance and dispute resolution procedure applicable to this bargaining unit.']
  health_benefits_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific health insurance plan available to members of this bargaining unit.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Primary state or jurisdiction under which this bargaining unit operates and is regulated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bargaining unit record was most recently updated.']
  last_negotiation_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent collective bargaining negotiation session was completed for this unit.']
  last_strike_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent strike or work stoppage action by this bargaining unit.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current number of employees who are members of this bargaining unit.']
  next_negotiation_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next collective bargaining negotiation session or contract renewal discussion.']
  nlrb_region varchar [note: 'NLRB regional office jurisdiction responsible for oversight of this bargaining unit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special conditions, or historical context related to this bargaining unit.']
  overtime_rules_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific overtime calculation and eligibility rules applicable to this bargaining unit.']
  pension_plan_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pension or retirement plan applicable to members of this bargaining unit.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary union representative for official communications regarding this bargaining unit.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary union representative or steward for this bargaining unit.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary union representative for this bargaining unit.']
  seniority_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of seniority system used for this bargaining unit to determine benefits, layoffs, and promotions.']
  shift_differential_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shift differential pay applies to members of this bargaining unit for non-standard shifts.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bargaining unit indicating whether it is actively recognized and operational.']
  strike_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this bargaining unit has a history of strike actions or work stoppages.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union or labor organization representing this bargaining unit.']
  union_local_number varchar [note: 'Local chapter or branch number of the union representing this bargaining unit.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the bargaining unit in operational systems and payroll processing.']
  unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the bargaining unit including scope, coverage, and purpose.']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the bargaining unit as recognized in collective bargaining agreements.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bargaining unit based on the type of workforce or operational area it represents.']
  wage_scale_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the wage scale or pay grade structure defined in the collective bargaining agreement for this unit.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for bargaining_unit. Referenced by bargaining_unit_id.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_calendar {
  payroll_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_calendar']
  prior_payroll_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_calendar (prior_payroll_calendar_id)']
  adjustment_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period allows payroll adjustments or corrections from prior periods.']
  approval_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which payroll must be approved by authorized personnel before processing.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit or division that uses this payroll calendar.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the payroll calendar in external systems and reports.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1-12) to which this payroll calendar period primarily belongs.']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the payroll calendar (e.g., \'Bi-Weekly US Hourly\', \'Monthly Salaried\', \'Weekly Store Associates\').']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Frequency type of the payroll calendar indicating how often payroll is processed.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which this payroll calendar period belongs.']
  check_date date [note: 'Date printed on physical paychecks or used as the transaction date for direct deposits.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country jurisdiction for this payroll calendar.']
  created_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that created this payroll calendar record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll calendar record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the payroll calendar including its purpose, coverage, and any special processing rules.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this payroll calendar ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended calendars.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this payroll calendar becomes effective and can be used for payroll processing.']
  employee_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the employee group or classification that uses this payroll calendar (e.g., hourly, salaried, union, non-union).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1-4) to which this payroll calendar period belongs.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this payroll calendar period belongs.']
  last_modified_by_user_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that last modified this payroll calendar record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll calendar record was last updated or modified.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity that owns and operates this payroll calendar.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about this payroll calendar period, including any exceptions or special circumstances.']
  off_cycle_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is an off-cycle payroll run (true) or a regular scheduled payroll (false).']
  pay_date date [note: 'Date when employees are paid for the corresponding pay period.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the pay period covered by this payroll calendar entry.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the pay period covered by this payroll calendar entry.']
  payroll_processing_date date [note: 'Date when payroll processing is scheduled to run for this pay period.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the pay period within the fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 1-26 for bi-weekly, 1-12 for monthly).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll calendar indicating whether it is actively used for payroll processing.']
  timesheet_submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which employees must submit timesheets for this pay period.']
  total_days_count varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in the pay period.']
  workday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for this payroll calendar in Workday HCM system for integration and synchronization.']
  working_days_count varchar [note: 'Number of working days within the pay period, excluding weekends and holidays.']
  year_end_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the final payroll period of the fiscal or calendar year requiring special year-end processing.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_calendar. Referenced by payroll_calendar_id.'
}

Table marketing.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing campaign. Primary key.']
  approved_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the campaign for execution.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the campaign record.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Campaigns must comply with regulatory frameworks (GDPR consent, CAN-SPAM, TCPA, FTC advertising standards). Retail marketing requires compliance verification before launch and audit trail for regulato']
  demand_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.demand_forecast. Business justification: Marketing campaigns directly impact demand forecasts; demand planners incorporate campaign timing, spend, and expected lift into statistical models. Real retail process: planner adjusts forecast when ']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail brand or banner that owns or sponsors this campaign.']
  modified_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last modified the campaign record.']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign if this campaign is part of a hierarchical campaign structure (e.g., a sub-campaign or wave within a master campaign).']
  price_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_strategy. Business justification: Retail campaigns execute specific pricing strategies (EDLP, Hi-Lo, promotional). Campaign planning requires knowing which price strategy governs promoted SKUs. Real business process: campaign brief cr']
  primary_kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Campaigns are measured against specific KPIs (ROAS, conversion rate, CTR) defined in analytics. Marketing teams set primary success metrics at campaign launch; this link enables performance tracking, ']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.replenishment_plan. Business justification: Promotional campaigns drive dedicated replenishment plans for campaign-specific inventory. Retail buyers create replenishment plans tied to Black Friday, back-to-school campaigns. Essential for promot']
  required_training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_program. Business justification: Marketing associates require mandatory compliance training (data privacy, advertising standards, FTC disclosure rules) before executing campaigns. Real business need: ensuring campaign creators are ce']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.purchase_order. Business justification: Large promotional campaigns trigger dedicated purchase orders for promotional merchandise (e.g., Super Bowl campaign inventory, holiday gift sets). Buyers create POs specifically tagged to campaigns f']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign execution concluded, may differ from planned end date.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred for the campaign across all channels and activities.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign execution began, may differ from planned start date due to operational adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of campaign approval workflow indicating whether the campaign has been approved for execution.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign was approved for execution.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget for the campaign across all channels and activities.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the campaign budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the campaign, often used in external systems and tracking URLs.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the campaign including its purpose, key messages, and tactical approach.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the marketing campaign used for identification and reporting.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign indicating its operational state.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign based on its strategic purpose (e.g., brand awareness, customer acquisition, retention, win-back, seasonal promotion, product launch).']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of marketing channels used in this campaign (e.g., email, SMS, social media, paid search, display, in-store, direct mail).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  creative_theme varchar [note: 'The overarching creative theme, messaging concept, or visual identity used in campaign assets.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the campaign indicating whether it is national, regional, local, or international.']
  is_omnichannel boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the campaign is executed across multiple channels in an integrated omnichannel approach.']
  is_personalized boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the campaign uses personalized messaging or offers tailored to individual customers.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campaign record was last modified.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective or goal of the campaign (e.g., increase store traffic, drive online sales, build brand awareness, grow loyalty membership).']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the campaign is planned to conclude.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the campaign is planned to begin execution.']
  primary_channel varchar [note: 'The primary or lead marketing channel for this campaign. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|social_media|paid_search|display|in_store|direct_mail|out_of_home — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_audience_definition varchar [note: 'Description of the target audience segment for this campaign, including demographic, behavioral, and geographic criteria.']
  target_audience_size varchar [note: 'Estimated or actual number of customers or prospects targeted by this campaign.']
  target_conversion_goal varchar [note: 'Target number of conversions (e.g., purchases, sign-ups, store visits) expected from this campaign.']
  target_country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the campaign is executed (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  target_revenue_goal decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue target or goal set for this campaign, used to measure campaign success.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter used for digital campaign tracking, typically matches the campaign code or name.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter used for digital campaign tracking to identify the marketing medium (e.g., email, cpc, social, display).']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter used for digital campaign tracking to identify the traffic source (e.g., google, facebook, newsletter).']

  Note: 'Master record for every marketing campaign executed across all retail channels (digital, in-store, email, SMS, social, paid media, OOH). Captures campaign name, type (brand awareness, acquisition, retention, win-back, seasonal), objective, status (draft, active, paused, completed), planned and actual start/end dates, target audience definition, owning brand or banner, campaign owner, budget allocation, channel mix, creative theme, UTM parameters, and campaign hierarchy (parent/child). Distinct from promotion.campaign which owns deal mechanics and redemption — this entity owns upstream campaign strategy and channel execution.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_brief {
  campaign_brief_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign brief. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign that this brief governs.']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary customer segment being targeted by this campaign.']
  item_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_hierarchy. Business justification: Campaign briefs in retail specify target product categories/hierarchies for category-specific marketing strategies, assortment planning, and merchandising alignment. Category managers and marketing te']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_brand. Business justification: Campaign briefs are brand-specific strategic documents. While campaign_brief links to campaign, and campaign links to marketing_brand, the brief itself should have a direct FK to marketing_brand becau']
  privacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_assessment. Business justification: Campaign briefs triggering new data collection/processing require Data Protection Impact Assessment under GDPR Article 35 and CCPA. Real business need: privacy-by-design workflow, regulatory mandate. ']
  revised_from_campaign_brief_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign_brief (revised_from_campaign_brief_id)']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the executive or stakeholder who provided final approval sign-off on the brief.']
  approver_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the approver who signed off on the campaign brief.']
  assigned_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external creative or media agency assigned to execute this campaign brief.']
  brief_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign brief received final approval and sign-off.']
  brief_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the campaign brief for easy identification and reference.']
  brief_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the campaign brief, used for external reference and tracking.']
  brief_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign brief in the approval workflow.']
  brief_submitted_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign brief was formally submitted for review and approval.']
  brief_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the brief by campaign execution scope and channel mix. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: integrated|digital_only|print_only|social_media|email|in_store|omnichannel — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget amount allocated for executing this campaign brief across all channels and activities.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget allocated amount.']
  call_to_action varchar [note: 'Primary call-to-action that the campaign should drive (e.g., Shop Now, Sign Up, Learn More, Visit Store).']
  campaign_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for campaign execution as specified in the brief.']
  campaign_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for campaign execution as specified in the brief.']
  channel_mix_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic allocation and prioritization of marketing channels (email, social, paid search, display, in-store, etc.) for campaign execution.']
  competitive_context varchar [note: 'Analysis of competitive landscape, competitor campaigns, and market positioning considerations relevant to this brief.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created the campaign brief.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign brief record was first created in the system.']
  creative_assets_required varchar [note: 'List of creative deliverables required (e.g., video, display ads, email templates, social posts, in-store signage).']
  creative_lead_name varchar [note: 'Name of the creative lead or art director responsible for creative development.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic markets, regions, or store locations targeted by this campaign brief.']
  internal_team_assignment varchar [note: 'Internal marketing team or department responsible for managing and executing this brief.']
  key_message varchar [note: 'Core message or value proposition that must be communicated across all campaign creative and channels.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the campaign landing page or destination where customers will be directed.']
  legal_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Legal and regulatory compliance considerations, disclaimers, and requirements that must be adhered to in campaign execution.']
  mandatory_brand_elements varchar [note: 'List of required brand assets, logos, taglines, legal disclaimers, and visual elements that must appear in all campaign materials.']
  product_category_focus varchar [note: 'Primary product categories or merchandise assortments featured in the campaign.']
  revision_notes varchar [note: 'Notes documenting changes, feedback, and revisions made to the brief during the review process.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number tracking the number of revisions made to the campaign brief.']
  success_kpi_definition varchar [note: 'Definition of key performance indicators and success metrics that will be used to measure campaign effectiveness.']
  target_audience_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the target audience personas, demographics, psychographics, and behavioral characteristics.']
  target_conversion_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target conversion rate percentage that the campaign aims to achieve.']
  target_reach varchar [note: 'Target number of unique customers or impressions the campaign aims to achieve.']
  target_roi_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target return on investment percentage expected from the campaign spend.']
  tone_of_voice varchar [note: 'Prescribed tone and style for campaign communications (e.g., friendly, authoritative, playful, urgent).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last updated the campaign brief.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign brief record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Creative and strategic brief document associated with a marketing campaign. Captures campaign objectives, target audience personas, key messages, tone of voice, mandatory brand elements, competitive context, success KPIs, agency or internal team assignments, brief approval status, revision history, and sign-off dates. Serves as the authoritative upstream strategy document that governs all downstream creative and channel execution for the campaign.'
}

Table marketing.audience_segment {
  audience_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing audience segment. Primary key.']
  parent_audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audience_segment (parent_audience_segment_id)']
  parent_segment_audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent segment if this segment is a refinement or sub-segment of a broader audience definition. Null for top-level segments.']
  privacy_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_assessment. Business justification: Audience segmentation using personal data requires privacy impact assessment under GDPR Article 35. Real business need: data subject rights protection, legal basis validation. Retail segmentation (beh']
  activation_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of marketing channels where this segment is activated (e.g., email, SMS, social media, display advertising, search, in-store personalization).']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment has been activated for use in live marketing campaigns and media buying platforms.']
  actual_reach varchar [note: 'Actual count of unique customers or contacts currently assigned to this segment after the last refresh execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the marketing manager or compliance officer who approved this segment for activation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment was approved for use in marketing campaigns.']
  average_aov decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average Order Value for customers in this segment, calculated from historical transaction data.']
  average_cltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average Customer Lifetime Value across all members of this segment, used for ROI forecasting and media spend allocation decisions.']
  average_purchase_frequency decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of purchases per customer per year within this segment, used for campaign frequency planning.']
  business_objective varchar [note: 'Strategic marketing objective or campaign goal this segment supports (e.g., customer acquisition, retention, cross-sell, win-back, loyalty tier upgrade).']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical confidence or quality score for modeled segments, representing the predicted accuracy of segment membership (0.0000 to 1.0000). Null for non-modeled segments.']
  consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit customer consent is required before activating this segment for marketing communications, per privacy regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment record was first created in the system.']
  creation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to create the segment: manual selection, rule-based logic, machine learning model, lookalike modeling, imported from external system, or cloned from existing segment.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days segment membership data is retained before automatic purge, aligned with data privacy and retention policies.']
  data_sources varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of source systems and data domains used to build this segment (e.g., CRM, loyalty program, web analytics, POS transaction data, email engagement).']
  definition_logic varchar [note: 'SQL predicate, rule set, or business logic defining the segment membership criteria. May reference customer attributes, transaction history, loyalty tier, web behavior, or other data sources.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition expires or is scheduled for deactivation. Null for open-ended segments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became active and available for campaign targeting.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'Estimated number of customers or contacts that qualify for this segment based on the most recent evaluation of the definition logic.']
  exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a suppression segment used to exclude customers from campaigns (e.g., unsubscribed, opted-out, recent purchasers to avoid over-messaging).']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the segment membership was last recalculated and updated.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the machine learning or lookalike model used to generate this segment, if applicable. Null for non-modeled segments.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version number or timestamp of the ML model used to generate this segment, enabling model lineage tracking and reproducibility.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this segment definition or configuration.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this segment record was last updated.']
  next_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the next segment membership refresh based on the defined refresh frequency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, campaign history, performance observations, or special handling instructions related to this segment.']
  owning_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the marketing team, business unit, or campaign manager responsible for this segment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this segment for resource allocation, campaign sequencing, and media spend decisions.']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which the segment membership is recalculated and updated to reflect current customer behavior and attributes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: real_time|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|on_demand|static — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code or abbreviation for the segment, used in campaign tagging and reporting systems.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment purpose, target audience characteristics, and intended use cases for marketing activation.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the audience segment used for campaign targeting and identification by marketing teams.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audience segment indicating whether it is available for campaign activation.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment methodology: behavioral (purchase patterns, browsing), demographic (age, income, location), psychographic (lifestyle, values), RFM-based (Recency Frequency Monetary analysis), lookalike (modeled similarity to high-value customers), or suppression (exclusion list).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of user-defined tags or labels for segment categorization, search, and filtering (e.g., seasonal, promotional, loyalty, acquisition).']
  target_persona varchar [note: 'Marketing persona or customer archetype this segment represents (e.g., high-value shoppers, bargain hunters, omnichannel enthusiasts, lapsed customers).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the marketing analyst or system that created this segment definition.']

  Note: 'Marketing-owned audience segment definitions used for campaign targeting, media buying, and personalization. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral, demographic, psychographic, RFM-based, lookalike, suppression), definition logic (SQL predicate or rule set), estimated reach, data sources used (CRM, loyalty, web analytics), refresh frequency, activation status, and owning marketing team. Distinct from customer.segment which is the customer-domain SSOT for customer classification — this entity represents marketing-specific targeting constructs built on top of customer segments for campaign activation.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_audience {
  campaign_audience_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign audience association record.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audience segment being targeted by this campaign.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that is targeting this audience segment.']
  split_from_campaign_audience_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign_audience (split_from_campaign_audience_id)']
  activation_end_date date [note: 'The date when this audience segment targeting ends or expires within the campaign. Used for time-bound targeting windows or campaign flight schedules.']
  activation_start_date date [note: 'The date when this audience segment targeting becomes active within the campaign. Used for scheduled or phased campaign rollouts.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audience segment was activated for the campaign and messages were sent or ads were served.']
  actual_reached_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of customers or contacts successfully reached after campaign execution. Populated post-execution for performance measurement.']
  budget_allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the campaign budget allocated specifically to this audience segment for media spend, creative production, or channel costs.']
  budget_allocation_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget allocation amount.']
  campaign_audience_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this campaign-audience association. Tracks whether the targeting configuration is in planning, execution, or post-campaign state.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'The marketing channel(s) for which this audience segment is applicable within the campaign. Determines where this segment will be activated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|push|display|social|search|direct_mail|in_store|all_channels — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_verification_date date [note: 'The date when opt-in consent was last verified for this audience segment. Used for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'The number of customers in this audience segment who completed the campaign conversion goal (purchase, sign-up, store visit, etc.). Populated post-execution for ROI measurement.']
  conversion_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of reached customers who converted (conversion_count / actual_reached_count * 100). Primary metric for campaign return on investment.']
  cost_per_acquisition decimal(15,2) [note: 'The average cost to acquire one conversion from this audience segment (budget_allocation_amount / conversion_count). Key efficiency metric for campaign optimization.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the marketing user who created this campaign-audience association.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign-audience association record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the estimated reach that was successfully delivered (actual_reached_count / estimated_reach * 100). Calculated post-execution to measure targeting accuracy and deliverability.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'The estimated number of customers or contacts in this audience segment at the time of campaign planning and activation setup.']
  frequency_cap varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times a customer in this audience segment should be contacted or exposed to campaign messages within the campaign period. Used to prevent over-messaging.']
  frequency_cap_period_days varchar [note: 'The time period in days over which the frequency cap is enforced. For example, a frequency cap of 3 over 7 days means no more than 3 messages in any 7-day window.']
  lookalike_model_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the lookalike modeling algorithm or configuration used when this audience segment serves as a seed for lookalike expansion. Only populated when targeting_role is lookalike_seed.']
  lookalike_similarity_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The minimum similarity score (0.0000 to 1.0000) required for a customer to be included in the lookalike expansion audience. Higher thresholds produce smaller, more similar audiences.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this campaign-audience association, including targeting rationale, special instructions, or post-campaign observations.']
  opt_in_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether opt-in consent has been verified for all customers in this audience segment for the specified channel. Required for GDPR, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM compliance.']
  personalization_strategy varchar [note: 'The personalization or dynamic content strategy applied to this audience segment within the campaign (e.g., product recommendations, location-based offers, behavioral triggers, demographic customization).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority ranking of this audience segment within the campaign when multiple segments are targeted. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for message delivery or budget allocation.']
  response_count varchar [note: 'The number of customers in this audience segment who responded to the campaign (clicked, opened, converted, or took the desired action). Populated post-execution for performance measurement.']
  response_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of reached customers who responded to the campaign (response_count / actual_reached_count * 100). Key performance indicator for campaign effectiveness.']
  return_on_ad_spend decimal(10,4) [note: 'The ratio of attributed revenue to budget allocation for this audience segment (revenue_attributed_amount / budget_allocation_amount). Measures campaign profitability per segment.']
  revenue_attributed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total revenue attributed to this audience segment from the campaign based on the attribution model. Used for ROI calculation and segment performance comparison.']
  revenue_attributed_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the attributed revenue amount.']
  segment_refresh_date date [note: 'The date when the audience segment membership was last refreshed or recalculated prior to campaign activation. Important for understanding data recency.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason why this audience segment is being excluded or suppressed from the campaign (e.g., recent purchasers, unsubscribed, high-value customers to protect, regulatory exclusion). Only populated when targeting_role is exclude or suppress.']
  targeting_role varchar [note: 'The role this audience segment plays in the campaign targeting strategy (include for primary targeting, exclude/suppress for opt-outs or suppression lists, lookalike_seed for expansion modeling, control_group for A/B testing, holdout for measurement).']
  test_group_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this audience segment is part of an A/B test or experimental group within the campaign. True if this is a test variant, false if control or standard execution.']
  test_variant_name varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the test variant applied to this audience segment (e.g., Variant A, Creative B, Offer 20% Off). Only populated when test_group_indicator is true.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the marketing user who last modified this campaign-audience association.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this campaign-audience association record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a marketing campaign to one or more audience segments, defining the targeting configuration for each campaign execution. Captures campaign reference, audience segment reference, targeting role (include, exclude/suppress, lookalike seed), estimated reach at time of activation, actual reached count post-execution, channel applicability, and activation timestamp. Enables multi-segment targeting strategies and suppression list management per campaign.'
}

Table marketing.media_plan {
  media_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the media plan. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this media plan record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent marketing campaign this media plan supports.']
  marketing_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_budget. Business justification: Media plans are funded by marketing budgets and represent the allocation of budget to media channels. The media_plan should link to marketing_budget to track which budget allocation is funding the pla']
  modified_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this media plan record.']
  owner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing manager or media planner responsible for this media plan.']
  primary_media_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or manager who approved this media plan.']
  revised_from_media_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on media_plan (revised_from_media_plan_id)']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the advertising agency or media buying agency responsible for executing this media plan.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the media plan by marketing leadership or finance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media plan was approved.']
  audience_segment_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of audience segment identifiers targeted by this media plan.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the planned budget amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media plan record was first created in the system.']
  display_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to display advertising (banner ads, programmatic display, retargeting).']
  dsp_platform_name varchar [note: 'Name of the demand-side platform used for programmatic media buying (e.g., Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Amazon DSP).']
  email_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to email marketing campaigns and automation.']
  flight_end_date date [note: 'End date for the media flight or campaign execution period.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'Start date for the media flight or campaign execution period.']
  geographic_targeting varchar [note: 'Geographic scope for media placement, including countries, regions, DMAs, or postal codes targeted.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media plan record was last modified.']
  ooh_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to out-of-home advertising (billboards, transit ads, digital signage).']
  paid_search_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to paid search advertising channels (Google Ads, Bing Ads, etc.).']
  paid_social_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to paid social media advertising channels (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, etc.).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code for the media plan, used for external reference and integration with media buying platforms.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the media plan strategy, objectives, and tactical approach.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name for the media plan, used for identification and reporting.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the media plan indicating its approval and execution state.']
  planned_frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of times each individual in the target audience is expected to be exposed to the campaign.']
  planned_reach varchar [note: 'Estimated number of unique individuals or households expected to be exposed to the campaign at least once.']
  planned_total_impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of ad impressions planned across all channels for this media plan.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning period for which this media plan is designed.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning period for which this media plan is designed.']
  print_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to print advertising (newspapers, magazines, circulars).']
  radio_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to radio advertising (terrestrial and streaming radio).']
  sms_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to SMS marketing campaigns and automation.']
  target_cpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cost per customer acquisition or conversion for performance marketing channels.']
  target_cpc decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cost per click for paid search and paid social channels.']
  target_cpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cost per thousand impressions for media buying efficiency.']
  target_roas decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target return on ad spend ratio, expressed as revenue generated per dollar spent on advertising.']
  total_planned_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for this media plan across all channels and placements.']
  tv_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to television advertising (broadcast, cable, connected TV).']
  video_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget allocated to video advertising channels (YouTube, OTT, streaming platforms).']

  Note: 'Master record for a media plan defining the planned allocation of marketing spend across channels, placements, and time periods for a campaign. Captures media plan name, campaign reference, planning period, total planned budget, channel breakdown (paid search, paid social, display, video, OOH, print, radio, TV, email, SMS), agency or DSP used, flight dates per channel, planned impressions, planned reach and frequency targets, and approval status. Serves as the financial and tactical blueprint for media buying execution.'
}

Table marketing.media_buy {
  media_buy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the media buy transaction. Primary key for the media buy record.']
  advertised_sku_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.sku_price. Business justification: Media buys feature specific products at specific advertised prices ("$9.99 sale" in display ads). Creative approval and legal compliance require verifying advertised prices match current authorized re']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or manager who approved this media buy. Used for governance and audit trail.']
  buyer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user responsible for executing and managing this media buy. Accountability and performance tracking.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign this media buy supports. Enables campaign-level performance rollup and attribution.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_channel. Business justification: Media buys are executed via specific marketing channels and should reference the authoritative marketing_channel master. Currently, media_buy has a denormalized channel_type STRING attribute. Adding m']
  created_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this media buy record. Audit trail for data governance.']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset (ad copy, banner, video) assigned to this media buy. Links buy to creative execution for performance analysis.']
  featured_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Retail media buys (circulars, digital ads, TV spots, display) frequently feature specific hero SKUs or promotional items. Essential for tracking promotional effectiveness, ad-to-sale attribution, and ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Media buys require GL account assignment for proper expense classification (advertising expense accounts) and financial reporting. Accounting teams need this for month-end close, accruals, and varianc']
  make_good_for_media_buy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on media_buy (make_good_for_media_buy_id)']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail brand or product line this media buy promotes. Enables brand-level spend and performance analysis.']
  media_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent media plan under which this buy was executed. Links the buy to strategic planning and budget allocation.']
  modified_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this media buy record. Audit trail for change management.']
  publisher_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor master record for the publisher. Links to vendor management and payment systems.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual spend incurred for this media buy based on delivered impressions, clicks, or contracted terms. Updated as invoices are received and reconciled.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the media buy was approved. Audit trail for governance and compliance.']
  booking_date date [note: 'The date when the media buy was officially booked or reserved with the publisher. Key milestone in the buy lifecycle.']
