  output_format varchar [note: 'File format in which the report or dashboard will be rendered and delivered to the subscriber.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this subscription for delivery queue management and resource allocation.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription expires and report deliveries should cease. Nullable for open-ended subscriptions.']
  subscription_name varchar [note: 'User-defined name or label for this subscription to help identify it among multiple subscriptions.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription becomes active and report deliveries should begin.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription indicating whether it is actively delivering reports or has been paused, cancelled, or expired.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a business users or teams subscription to a scheduled report or dashboard. Stores subscriber identity reference, report or dashboard reference, delivery channel (email, Slack, Teams, in-app), delivery schedule (cron expression or named cadence), output format, filter parameters applied, subscription status, and last delivery timestamp. Enables governed self-service report distribution across retail business units.'
}

Table analytics.access_policy {
  access_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the analytics access policy record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Individual user identifier for user-specific policy assignments. Used for exceptions and personalized access grants.']
  parent_access_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on access_policy (parent_access_policy_id)']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Permission level granted by this policy: view (read-only), export (download capability), edit (modify), admin (full control), or deny (explicit block).']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy requires explicit approval workflow before activation. True for high-risk or sensitive data access policies.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this policy was approved. Part of the governance audit trail.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the data steward or governance officer who approved this policy. Required for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  assigned_group varchar [note: 'User group identifier to which this policy is assigned. Enables group-based access management for analytics assets.']
  assigned_role varchar [note: 'Role identifier to which this policy is assigned. Supports role-based access control (RBAC) for analytics consumption.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business rationale for granting or restricting access. Documents the business case, regulatory requirement, or operational need driving this policy.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework this policy supports (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, SOX, PCI-DSS, HIPAA). Enables compliance reporting and audit readiness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this policy record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and policy lifecycle tracking.']
  data_classification_constraint varchar [note: 'Data classification level constraint enforced by this policy. Restricts access based on data sensitivity: restricted (PII/PHI/PCI), confidential (business-sensitive), internal (employee-only), public (unrestricted).']
  department_scope_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of department codes to which this policy restricts data visibility. Enables departmental data segmentation (e.g., merchandising, finance, supply chain).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this access policy expires and is no longer enforced. Null indicates indefinite policy duration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this access policy becomes active and enforceable. Supports time-bound access grants for temporary projects or seasonal roles.']
  enforcement_mode varchar [note: 'Policy enforcement behavior: enforce (block unauthorized access), audit (log violations but allow access), warn (notify user but allow access). Supports phased rollout and testing.']
  filter_expression varchar [note: 'SQL WHERE clause or filter predicate applied for row-level security. Dynamically filters data based on user context, role, or attribute values.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this policy is currently active and being enforced. False indicates the policy is disabled or archived.']
  last_access_review_date date [note: 'Date when this policy was last reviewed for continued appropriateness and accuracy. Supports periodic access recertification processes.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this policy record. Tracks accountability for policy changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this policy record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  masking_rule varchar [note: 'Data masking technique applied for column-level security: none (no masking), full_mask (complete obfuscation), partial_mask (show last 4 digits), hash (one-way hash), tokenize (reversible token), redact (replace with placeholder), null (return null). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|full_mask|partial_mask|hash|tokenize|redact|null — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory policy review. Ensures policies remain current and aligned with business needs and regulatory requirements.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the access policy, used for system integration and reference.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business justification and purpose of this access policy. Explains why the policy exists and what business need it serves.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the access policy for identification and management purposes.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the access policy: draft (under review), active (enforced), suspended (temporarily disabled), expired (past end date), revoked (permanently cancelled).']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the access control mechanism: row-level (filters rows), column-level (masks columns), object-level (entire object access), attribute-level (specific field access), cell-level (individual cell masking), or dynamic (context-based).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for policy evaluation when multiple policies apply to the same user and object. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for conflict resolution.']
  region_scope_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of region codes or geographic identifiers to which this policy restricts data visibility. Supports regional business unit data isolation.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the data or analytics asset governed by this policy. Drives approval workflows and monitoring intensity.']
  store_scope_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of store identifiers or store codes to which this policy restricts data visibility. Enables store-level data segmentation for regional managers and store operations.']
  target_object_name varchar [note: 'Fully qualified name or identifier of the specific analytics object (dashboard, report, metric, data product) governed by this policy.']
  target_object_type varchar [note: 'Type of analytics object this policy governs: dashboard, report, semantic metric, data product, dataset, view, or table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dashboard|report|semantic_metric|data_product|dataset|view|table — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_schema_name varchar [note: 'Database schema or catalog namespace where the target object resides, supporting multi-tenant and domain-segregated analytics environments.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this policy record. Part of the audit trail for governance and compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record defining row-level, column-level, and object-level access policies governing who can consume which analytics products, dashboards, and semantic layer entities. Captures policy name, target object type (dashboard, report, semantic metric, data product), access level (view, export, edit), assigned role or group, data classification constraint, store/region scope restriction, and effective date range. Enforces data governance and privacy controls on analytics consumption.'
}

Table analytics.retail_calendar {
  retail_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each calendar date record in the retail fiscal calendar. Primary key for the retail calendar dimension.']
  prior_year_retail_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on retail_calendar (prior_year_retail_calendar_id)']
  calendar_date date [note: 'The Gregorian calendar date represented by this record. Serves as the natural business key for date-based joins and filtering.']
  calendar_day_of_month varchar [note: 'The day of the month in the Gregorian calendar (1-31). Used for day-of-month pattern analysis and billing cycle alignment.']
  calendar_day_of_year varchar [note: 'The day number within the Gregorian calendar year (1-365 or 1-366). Supports calendar year-to-date calculations.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The Gregorian calendar month number (1-12). Enables calendar-month aggregations and seasonality analysis.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The Gregorian calendar quarter (1-4). Supports calendar-based quarterly analysis and external benchmarking.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The Gregorian calendar year (YYYY). Used for calendar-year reporting and external regulatory filings.']
  comparable_date_prior_year date [note: 'The corresponding date in the prior fiscal year for year-over-year comparable sales (comp sales) analysis. Accounts for fiscal calendar shifts.']
  comparable_week_prior_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal week number in the prior year that corresponds to this date for week-over-week year-over-year analysis.']
  day_abbreviation varchar [note: 'The three-letter abbreviation for the day of the week. Used for compact display in dashboards and reports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Mon|Tue|Wed|Thu|Fri|Sat|Sun — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  day_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the day of the week. Provides human-readable day identification for reporting and visualization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_day_of_period varchar [note: 'The day number within the current fiscal period. Supports daily sales tracking and period-to-date performance analysis.']
  fiscal_day_of_week varchar [note: 'The day number within the fiscal week (1-7). Used for day-of-week sales pattern analysis and staffing optimization.']
  fiscal_day_of_year varchar [note: 'The day number within the fiscal year (1-364 or 1-371). Enables year-to-date calculations and trend analysis.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) number within the fiscal year (1-12). Aligns with monthly financial close and reporting cycles.']
  fiscal_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal period (month) to which this date belongs. Supports monthly close processes and period-end reporting.']
  fiscal_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal period (month) to which this date belongs. Enables period boundary identification and period-to-date calculations.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter number within the fiscal year (1-4). Used for quarterly financial reporting and planning cycles.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'The fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1-52 or 1-53). Follows the retail 4-5-4 or 4-4-5 week structure for consistent period comparisons.']
  fiscal_week_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal week to which this date belongs. Supports weekly reporting cycles and week-end inventory snapshots.']
  fiscal_week_of_period varchar [note: 'The week number within the current fiscal period (1-4 or 1-5). Enables week-level analysis within monthly periods following 4-5-4 structure.']
  fiscal_week_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal week to which this date belongs. Enables week boundary identification and week-to-date calculations.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The retail fiscal year to which this date belongs. Fiscal year may differ from calendar year based on company fiscal calendar definition.']
  fiscal_year_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the fiscal year to which this date belongs. Supports fiscal year-end reporting and planning cycles.']
  fiscal_year_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the fiscal year to which this date belongs. Enables fiscal year boundary identification and year-to-date calculations.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'The name of the holiday if the date is a recognized retail holiday. Null for non-holiday dates. Enables holiday-specific performance tracking.']
  is_53_week_year boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fiscal year contains 53 weeks instead of the standard 52. Occurs approximately every 5-6 years in 4-5-4 calendars.']
  is_back_to_school_period boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date falls within the back-to-school shopping period. Drives seasonal merchandising and promotional planning.']
  is_black_friday_week boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date falls within the Black Friday promotional week. Critical for peak season planning and analysis.']
  is_current_date boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this record represents today\'s date. Updated daily to support current-date filtering in reports.']
  is_cyber_monday_week boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date falls within the Cyber Monday promotional week. Supports digital commerce peak event analysis.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date is a recognized retail holiday. Supports holiday sales analysis and staffing planning.']
  is_holiday_shopping_period boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date falls within the November-December holiday shopping season. Critical for peak season resource planning.']
  is_leap_year boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the calendar year is a leap year (366 days). Used for year-over-year normalization in analytics.']
  is_weekday boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date falls on a weekday (Monday through Friday). Used for weekday traffic and sales pattern analysis.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Used for weekend vs weekday sales analysis.']
  iso_week varchar [note: 'The ISO 8601 week number (1-53). Provides standardized week numbering for international reporting and cross-industry comparisons.']
  iso_year varchar [note: 'The ISO 8601 week-numbering year. May differ from Gregorian year for dates in the first or last week of the year.']
  month_abbreviation varchar [note: 'The three-letter abbreviation for the calendar month. Used for compact display in dashboards and reports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Jan|Feb|Mar|Apr|May|Jun|Jul|Aug|Sep|Oct|Nov|Dec — promote to reference product]']
  month_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the calendar month. Provides human-readable month identification for reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December — promote to reference product]']
  season varchar [note: 'The retail merchandising season designation (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter). Drives seasonal assortment planning and promotional strategies.']

  Note: 'Authoritative retail fiscal calendar master defining the 4-5-4 or 4-4-5 fiscal week structure used across all retail planning, reporting, and analytics. Captures fiscal year, fiscal quarter, fiscal period, fiscal week number, ISO week number, Gregorian date range, season designation (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter), key retail event flags (Black Friday week, Back-to-School, Holiday), and comparable period mapping. Serves as the conformed time dimension for all retail analytics and reporting.'
}

Table analytics.reporting_hierarchy {
  reporting_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reporting hierarchy node. Primary key for the reporting hierarchy master record.']
  parent_reporting_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on reporting_hierarchy (parent_reporting_hierarchy_id)']
  attributes_json varchar [note: 'JSON-formatted string containing hierarchy-type-specific attributes that do not warrant dedicated columns. For example, geographic hierarchies may include latitude/longitude; merchandise hierarchies may include buyer name; organizational hierarchies may include square footage. Provides extensibility without schema changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial metrics reported at this hierarchy node. Used for multi-currency reporting and consolidation (e.g., USD for US stores, CAD for Canadian stores, EUR for European operations).']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or data feed that provided this hierarchy node record (e.g., Informatica MDM, SAP S/4HANA FI/CO, Oracle Retail Merchandising System). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node becomes inactive. Null for currently active nodes. Supports historical reporting and enables point-in-time hierarchy reconstruction for trend analysis and comp sales calculations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this hierarchy node becomes active and valid for reporting. Supports time-variant hierarchy structures (e.g., store reorganizations, category restructuring). Used for as-of-date reporting and historical trend analysis.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this hierarchy node in the source operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA cost center, Oracle Retail location code, MDM golden record ID). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  external_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that owns this hierarchy node (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Retail Merchandising System, Informatica MDM). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  hierarchy_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the reporting hierarchy structure (e.g., Store Geographic Hierarchy, Merchandise Category Hierarchy, Channel Hierarchy).']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hierarchy structure. Organizational hierarchies represent corporate structure (banner/division/region/district/store). Geographic hierarchies represent location rollups (country/state/city/postal). Merchandise hierarchies represent product categorization (department/category/subcategory/class). Channel hierarchies represent sales channel groupings (omnichannel/online/store/mobile). Financial hierarchies represent cost center and profit center rollups. Customer segment hierarchies represent customer classification structures.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this hierarchy node is currently active and available for use in reporting and analytics. True for active nodes, False for inactive/historical nodes. Used for filtering current vs historical hierarchies.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this hierarchy node record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change data capture, and incremental processing.']
  leaf_node_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is a leaf (terminal) node with no children. True for leaf nodes (e.g., individual stores, SKUs), False for parent/aggregate nodes. Used to control drill-down behavior in dashboards.']
  level_name varchar [note: 'Business name for this hierarchy level (e.g., Banner, Region, District, Store for organizational hierarchy; Department, Category, Subcategory, Class for merchandise hierarchy). Provides semantic meaning to level_number.']
  level_number varchar [note: 'Numeric position of this node within the hierarchy depth. Root level is 1, immediate children are 2, etc. Used for level-based aggregation and drill-down control in BI tools.']
  node_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the hierarchy node, including scope, coverage, and business purpose. Provides context for reporting users.']
  node_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this specific node within the hierarchy. May be a store number, department code, region code, or other business key depending on hierarchy type. Used for drill-down and roll-up navigation in OLAP reporting.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for this hierarchy node (e.g., Northeast Region, Electronics Department, Store #1234, E-Commerce Channel).']
  node_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or responsible party for this hierarchy node (e.g., Regional VP, Category Manager, Channel Director). Used for governance and accountability in KPI reporting.']
  node_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path from root to this node, typically delimited (e.g., /Banner/Region/District/Store or Department>Category>Subcategory). Enables fast ancestor and descendant queries without recursive joins.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hierarchy node. Active nodes are used in current reporting. Inactive nodes are historical. Pending nodes are scheduled for future activation. Deprecated nodes are being phased out. Merged nodes have been consolidated into another node.']
  parent_node_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the parent node in the hierarchy. Null for root nodes. Enables recursive roll-up aggregation of KPIs and metrics from child to parent levels.']
  reporting_calendar_type varchar [note: 'Calendar convention used for period-based reporting at this hierarchy node. Gregorian for standard calendar. Retail 4-5-4 and 4-4-5 for NRF retail calendar. Fiscal for custom fiscal periods. Enables consistent period comparisons and comp sales calculations.']
  rollup_method varchar [note: 'Default aggregation method for rolling up metrics from child nodes to this parent node. Sum for additive metrics (sales, units). Average for rates and percentages. Weighted_average for metrics requiring weighting (average selling price). None for non-aggregatable dimensions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sum|average|weighted_average|min|max|count|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_node_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this node is the root (top-level) node of the hierarchy. True for root nodes (e.g., Total Company, All Products), False for all other nodes.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value controlling the display sequence of sibling nodes at the same hierarchy level. Lower values appear first. Used to control presentation order in reports and dashboards.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this hierarchy node (e.g., America/New_York, America/Los_Angeles). Used for time-based reporting and comp sales calculations across geographic regions.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number for this hierarchy node record. Incremented with each modification. Supports optimistic locking and change history tracking for hierarchy restructuring events.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this hierarchy node record. Used for audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'Master record defining organizational and merchandise reporting hierarchies used to roll up KPIs and metrics across the retail enterprise. Captures hierarchy name, hierarchy type (organizational, geographic, merchandise, channel), node identifier, node name, parent node reference, level number, level name, and effective date range. Supports drill-down and roll-up navigation in dashboards and OLAP reporting for dimensions such as store region/district/store, department/category/subcategory, and banner/channel.'
}

Business justification: Sets performance targets at PO level for vendor scorecards (e.g., 95% on-time delivery target for specific PO). Used in supplier performance management and contract compliance tracking.']
  target_financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.financial_period. Business justification: Annual/quarterly targets must align to fiscal calendar for budget vs actual reporting, performance reviews, and incentive compensation calculations. Distinct from measurement period; targets are set p']

  tertiary_analytics_last_updated_by_user_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who most recently modified the target record. Supports accountability and audit trail.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the target within the governance workflow. Ensures targets are formally reviewed and authorized before becoming active.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the target was formally approved. Provides audit trail for governance compliance.']
  business_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of business entity for which the target is set. Determines the scope and granularity of performance measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the target record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for monetary targets (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for non-monetary KPIs.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or process that originated or last updated the target record (e.g., SAP BPC, Blue Yonder Demand Planning, Manual Entry, Excel Upload). Supports data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date until which this target version remains effective. Null for currently active targets. Supports temporal validity and target change management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this target version becomes effective. Supports temporal validity and target change management.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period within the year (e.g., Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, M01, M02, W01, FY) for which the target is defined. Supports quarterly, monthly, weekly, and annual target setting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the target is set, following the organization\'s fiscal calendar (e.g., 2024, 2025).']
  floor_threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable performance level below which corrective action or escalation is required. Represents the lower bound of acceptable performance.']
  incentive_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether achievement of this target is tied to compensation, bonuses, or other incentive programs. True if target performance impacts financial rewards.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this target is currently active and in use for performance measurement. False for superseded, cancelled, or historical targets.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the target record was most recently modified. Provides audit trail for record changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, constraints, or commentary related to the target. Supports collaborative planning and knowledge capture.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance measurement period for this target. Defines the conclusion of the evaluation window.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance measurement period for this target. Defines the beginning of the evaluation window.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the target was revised after initial approval (e.g., market conditions changed, strategic pivot, acquisition impact). Provides context for mid-period adjustments.']
  stretch_target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Aspirational or stretch goal value that exceeds the baseline target. Used to incentivize exceptional performance and drive continuous improvement.']
  target_basis varchar [note: 'Descriptive explanation of the rationale, assumptions, or data sources used to derive the target value. Provides transparency and auditability for target-setting decisions.']
  target_owner_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role or job title of the target owner (e.g., Store Manager, Category Manager, Regional VP, E-Commerce Director). Provides context for accountability assignment.']
  target_setting_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to establish the target value. Top-down: cascaded from corporate goals; Bottom-up: aggregated from operational plans; Algorithmic: calculated by forecasting models; Historical: based on prior period performance; Benchmark: derived from industry or peer comparisons.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned or expected value for the KPI during the specified period. Represents the baseline performance goal that the business entity is expected to achieve.']
  target_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the target record. Increments when targets are revised or reforecasted during the performance period. Supports target change history and audit.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the target value is expressed (e.g., USD, percent, units, score, days, ratio). Ensures consistent interpretation of target metrics.']
  variance_alert_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deviation from target that triggers an alert or notification (e.g., 10.00 means alert if actual is 10% or more below target). Enables proactive performance management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining planned KPI targets for a specific business entity (store, category, region, channel) and time period. Captures KPI definition reference, target entity type and identifier, fiscal period, target value, stretch target value, floor threshold, target setting method (top-down, bottom-up, algorithmic), approval status, and target owner. Enables variance-to-target reporting and performance management across all retail KPIs including sales, margin, shrink, and NPS.'
}

  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the alert: new when first triggered, acknowledged when reviewed, investigating during analysis, resolved when addressed, dismissed if false positive, escalated if requiring higher attention.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alert trigger mechanism: threshold breach when a KPI crosses a defined limit, anomaly detection for statistical outliers, trend deviation for directional changes, forecast variance for prediction misses, data quality issues, or target misses.']
  business_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of business entity affected by this alert (e.g., store, product, customer segment, supplier, category, region, sales channel). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store|product|customer|supplier|category|region|channel|campaign|employee|distribution_center — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this alert record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for monetary KPIs (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for non-monetary metrics.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or data product that provided the KPI measurement triggering this alert (e.g., SAP CAR, Oracle Retail, Blue Yonder).']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Number of times the alert has been escalated to higher management levels. Zero for no escalation, increments with each escalation.']
  false_positive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alert was determined to be a false positive after investigation. True if false alarm, False if legitimate alert.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this alert record was last modified, tracking status changes, acknowledgments, and resolution updates.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the measurement period for which the KPI was calculated and the alert triggered.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the measurement period for which the KPI was calculated and the alert triggered.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used to deliver the alert notification (email, SMS, push notification, Slack, Microsoft Teams, dashboard, webhook). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|push|slack|teams|dashboard|webhook — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification was successfully sent to recipients. True if sent, False if pending or failed.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Type of time period for the measurement: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, yearly, rolling window, or custom date range. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|yearly|rolling|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_score varchar [note: 'Calculated priority score (0-100) combining severity, business impact, and urgency to support alert queue management and triage.']
  recipient_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of notification recipients (email addresses, user IDs, or distribution group names) who received or should receive this alert.']
  related_alert_count varchar [note: 'Number of related or correlated alerts triggered in the same time window for the same or related business entities, supporting root cause analysis.']
  resolution_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Time elapsed in minutes from alert trigger to resolution, used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking and performance analysis.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the investigation findings, root cause analysis, and actions taken to resolve the alert.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was marked as resolved. Null if still open or investigating.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the alert rule configuration that was active when this alert triggered, supporting rule change tracking and audit.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the alert: critical requires immediate action, high needs urgent attention, medium for monitoring, low for awareness, informational for FYI.']
  suppression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this alert was suppressed from notification due to alert fatigue rules, maintenance windows, or duplicate detection. True if suppressed, False if active.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the alert was suppressed (e.g., maintenance window, duplicate within 1 hour, recipient opt-out). Null if not suppressed.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The threshold or boundary value that was breached to trigger the alert. Null for non-threshold alert types.']
  trigger_condition varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the condition that triggered the alert, including the rule logic and comparison operators used.']
  triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert condition was detected and the alert was triggered.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measurement for the KPI value (e.g., USD, units, percentage, days, count).']
  variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute difference between actual value and threshold value, calculated as actual minus threshold.']
  variance_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual and threshold, calculated as (actual - threshold) / threshold * 100.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing a triggered analytics alert when a KPI value breaches a configured threshold or anomaly is detected. Stores alert name, associated KPI definition, triggering condition (threshold breach, anomaly, trend deviation), actual value at trigger, threshold value, severity level (critical, warning, informational), affected business entity, notification recipients, acknowledgment status, and resolution notes. Supports proactive retail performance management.'
}

  session_id varchar [note: 'The user session identifier associated with the query execution, used to group queries within a single user session.']

  access_granted_by_policy varchar [note: 'The data access policy or permission rule that granted the user access to execute this query.']
  aggregation_functions varchar [note: 'The aggregation functions used in the query (e.g., SUM, AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN), captured for complexity analysis.']
  bi_tool varchar [note: 'The BI or analytics tool used to execute the query (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Databricks SQL, Looker). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tableau|power_bi|databricks_sql|looker|qlik|thoughtspot|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cache_hit boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the query results were served from cache rather than re-executed.']
  compute_cost_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'The estimated compute cost in USD for executing the query, used for cost attribution and chargeback.']
  contains_pii boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the query accessed data containing PII.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this query execution record was created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'The highest data classification level of the objects accessed by the query (restricted, confidential, internal, public).']
  data_scanned_bytes varchar [note: 'The total volume of data scanned during query execution, measured in bytes.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'The error message or exception details if the query failed or encountered issues during execution.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the query was executed by the user.']
  export_format varchar [note: 'The file format used if the query results were exported: CSV, Excel, PDF, JSON, Parquet, or none if not exported.']
  filter_criteria varchar [note: 'The filter or WHERE clause criteria applied by the user in the query, captured for usage pattern analysis.']
  join_count varchar [note: 'The number of table joins performed in the query, used to assess query complexity.']
  query_complexity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated score representing the complexity of the query based on joins, aggregations, subqueries, and data volume.']
  query_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total execution time of the query in seconds, from submission to completion.']
  query_name varchar [note: 'User-assigned name or title for the query, used for identification and reuse.']
  query_optimization_applied boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether automatic query optimization or rewrite was applied by the query engine.']
  query_purpose varchar [note: 'The business purpose or use case for the query: ad-hoc analysis, reporting, dashboard refresh, data exploration, model training, or data quality check.']
  query_status varchar [note: 'The execution status of the query: success, failed, timeout, cancelled by user, or partial results.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'The full SQL or semantic query definition executed by the user. May be SQL, natural language, or semantic layer query syntax.']
  query_type varchar [note: 'The type or format of the query executed: SQL, semantic layer query, natural language, visual query builder, or saved query reference.']
  result_set_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'The size of the query result set in megabytes, used for performance and storage analysis.']
  rows_returned varchar [note: 'The number of rows returned by the query execution.']
  saved_query_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the user saved this query for future reuse.']
  shared_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the query or its results were shared with other users.']
  target_data_product varchar [note: 'The specific data product or table accessed by the query within the target domain.']
  target_domain varchar [note: 'The business domain or data domain accessed by the query (e.g., Customer, Product, Inventory, Order, Finance).']
  user_department varchar [note: 'The department or business unit of the user who executed the query, used for usage segmentation and cost allocation.']
  user_role varchar [note: 'The role or job function of the user who executed the query (e.g., analyst, manager, data scientist, executive).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing ad-hoc self-service queries executed by business users against the retail semantic layer or data catalog. Stores user reference, query text or semantic query definition, target domain and product, execution timestamp, query duration, rows returned, BI tool used (Tableau, Power BI, Databricks SQL, Looker), query status, and data classification of accessed objects. Enables analytics usage governance, cost attribution, and popular query identification.'
}

  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Business terms for financial metrics reference specific GL accounts (e.g., \'Net Sales\' = accounts 4000-4999). Enables business-to-technical mapping for self-service analytics and data literacy.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.kpi_definition. Business justification: Formalizes the relationship between glossary terms and KPI definitions. Currently glossary_term has associated_kpi_code (string) which is a soft reference. Replacing this with a proper FK to kpi_defin']

This FK links the term to the dimension, enabling business users to understand the business meaning of dimensions used to slice and filter KPIs']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Link provides regulatory traceability for business glossary and ensures co']

This FK links the term to the entity, enabling business users to navigate from terminology definitions to the actual seman']

This FK links the term to the metric, enabling business users to navigate from terminology definitions to the actual certified me']

  associated_report_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of report names or dashboard titles where this term is prominently used. Helps users understand term application.']

  calculation_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical or logical formula used to calculate or derive this term if it represents a metric or KPI. Example: GMROI = Gross Margin / Average Inventory Cost.']

  contains_financial_data boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this term represents financial data subject to accounting standards or financial reporting requirements.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this glossary term record was first created in the system.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Business criticality rating indicating the importance of this term to enterprise decision-making and regulatory compliance.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Security classification level for data represented by this term. Indicates sensitivity and access control requirements.']
  data_steward_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the data steward responsible for this glossary term.']
  data_steward_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or role responsible for stewarding this glossary term, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and governance compliance.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'Expected data type for this term when represented in data products or analytics. Helps ensure consistent implementation across systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: string|numeric|decimal|integer|date|timestamp|boolean — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this glossary term definition is no longer active. Null indicates the term is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this glossary term definition becomes active and should be used across the enterprise.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this glossary term is currently active and available for use. False indicates the term is deprecated or retired.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this glossary term record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or context about the glossary term that do not fit in other structured fields.']
  owning_domain varchar [note: 'The primary data domain responsible for defining and maintaining this glossary term. Aligns with enterprise data domain taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer|product|inventory|order|store|supply_chain|pricing|promotion|workforce|supplier|finance|loyalty|ecommerce|distribution|merchandising|analytics — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of regulatory standards, compliance frameworks, or industry guidelines that govern the definition or usage of this term. Example: GDPR Article 4, FASB ASC 606, PCI DSS.']
  related_terms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of related glossary term codes or names that have semantic relationships with this term. Helps users discover connected concepts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Primary operational system or application where this term originates or is authoritatively maintained. Example: SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Retail, Salesforce.']
  synonyms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of alternative terms, aliases, or synonyms used across the organization that refer to the same concept. Example: Item, Product, Article for SKU.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of user-defined tags or labels for categorization, search, and discovery. Example: retail, sales, inventory, compliance.']
  technical_definition varchar [note: 'Technical or data-centric definition describing how the term is calculated, derived, or represented in data systems.']
  term_abbreviation varchar [note: 'Standard abbreviation or acronym for the term. Example: SKU, SSS, GMROI, BOPIS, EDLP.']
  term_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the glossary term type. Indicates whether the term represents master data, transactional data, a metric, dimension, KPI, business rule, process, or system concept. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: master_data|transactional_data|reference_data|metric|dimension|kpi|business_rule|process|system — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Short, unique alphanumeric code for the glossary term used for system references and lookups. Example: SKU_DEF, COMP_SALES, GMROI.']
  term_name varchar [note: 'The official business term or phrase as used across the enterprise. Example: Stock Keeping Unit, Comparable Store Sales, Gross Margin Return on Investment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for this term if it represents a quantitative metric. Example: USD, Percent, Units, Days, Weeks.']
  usage_example varchar [note: 'Concrete example demonstrating how the term is used in business context, reports, or analytics. Provides clarity for end users.']
  usage_frequency varchar [note: 'Indicator of how frequently this term is used across reports, dashboards, and analytics products. Helps prioritize governance efforts.']
  valid_values varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of valid values, ranges, or constraints for this term. Example: Active, Inactive, Suspended for Status terms.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this glossary term definition. Incremented when the definition is updated. Example: 1.0, 2.1.']

  Note: 'Master record for the enterprise business glossary defining standardized retail terminology used across analytics, reporting, and data products. Captures term name, business definition, synonyms, related terms, owning domain, data steward, usage examples, associated KPI or semantic metric references, approval status, and effective date. Ensures consistent interpretation of retail business concepts (e.g., Net Sales vs Gross Sales, Comp Store vs Non-Comp, Shrink vs Waste) across all analytics consumers.'
}

Table analytics.kpi_dimensionality {
  kpi_dimensionality_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this KPI-dimension configuration record. Primary key.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the KPI definition being configured for dimensional analysis']
  metric_dimension_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the metric dimension available for slicing this KPI']
  aggregation_level varchar [note: 'The aggregation method to apply when this KPI is sliced by this dimension (e.g., sum for additive metrics, average for ratios, none for pre-aggregated). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  configuration_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes explaining why this dimension is configured for this KPI, any special handling, or business context.']
  configured_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the BI team member or data steward who configured this KPI-dimension pairing.']
  default_filter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Optional default filter value to apply for this dimension when this KPI is first loaded (e.g., current_year for time dimension, flagship_stores for store dimension). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  dimension_value_filter varchar [note: 'Optional filter expression limiting which dimension members are valid for this KPI (e.g., only active stores, only product categories with inventory). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  dimensionality varchar [note: 'The business dimensions by which this KPI can be sliced and analyzed (e.g., Store, Product Category, Time Period, Customer Segment, Channel). Comma-separated list of applicable dimensions. [Moved from kpi_definition: The dimensionality attribute in kpi_definition is a text description of which dimensions apply to the KPI. This should be replaced by the explicit M:N relationship captured in kpi_dimensionality, where each valid dimension is a separate record with its own configuration.]']
  display_format varchar [note: 'Optional display format override for this dimension when used with this specific KPI (e.g., abbreviated store names for space-constrained dashboards). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  drill_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether drill-down operations are enabled for this KPI along this dimension hierarchy. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this KPI-dimension configuration ceased to be effective. Null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this KPI-dimension configuration became effective and available for use.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this KPI-dimension configuration is currently active and available in BI tools.']
  is_required_dimension boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this dimension MUST be included when querying this KPI (e.g., time period is required for all temporal KPIs).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'The display order of this dimension when presenting this KPI in BI tools and dashboards. Lower numbers appear first. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the configuration relationship between KPI definitions and the metric dimensions by which they can be sliced and analyzed. It captures the explicit business rules governing which dimensions are available for each KPI, how they aggregate, and their display behavior in BI tools. Each record links one KPI definition to one metric dimension with configuration attributes that exist only in the context of this specific KPI-dimension pairing.. Existence Justification: In retail analytics operations, BI teams actively configure which dimensions are available for each KPI, how they aggregate, their display order, and drill-down behavior. This is not a derived analytical correlation but an explicit configuration that semantic layer architects manage as part of OLAP cube design and BI tool setup. The business recognizes this as \'KPI dimensionality configuration\' - a managed artifact that determines how business users can slice and filter metrics.'
}

Table analytics.report_composition {
  report_composition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this report-KPI composition record. Primary key.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the KPI definition that is included in this report composition']
  report_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the report definition that contains this KPI']
  added_date date [note: 'Date when this KPI was added to this report definition. Used for tracking report evolution and composition history.']
  composition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this KPI within this report: active (currently displayed), deprecated (scheduled for removal), testing (under evaluation). Supports report composition lifecycle management.']
  conditional_formatting_rules varchar [note: 'JSON or structured string defining the conditional formatting logic for this KPI in this report (e.g., color coding rules, threshold-based highlighting). Allows report-specific visual treatment of the same KPI.']
  configured_by varchar [note: 'Username or email of the BI analyst or report owner who configured this KPI for inclusion in this report. Used for governance and change tracking.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'The sequential position in which this KPI appears within the report layout. Used by BI tools to render KPIs in the intended business sequence.']
  drill_down_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether drill-down functionality is enabled for this KPI in this report context. The same KPI may allow drill-down in some reports but not others based on audience and use case.']
  filter_defaults varchar [note: 'JSON or structured string defining the default filter values applied to this KPI when the report is opened (e.g., {"region": "Northeast", "time_period": "last_30_days"}). Enables report-specific pre-filtering of KPI data.']
  is_primary_metric boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this KPI is designated as a primary or headline metric for this report. Primary metrics may receive special visual treatment or positioning.']
  removed_date date [note: 'Date when this KPI was removed from this report definition. Nullable for active KPIs. Enables historical tracking of report composition changes.']
  section_name varchar [note: 'The named section or tab within the report where this KPI is displayed (e.g., "Executive Summary", "Store Performance", "Financial Metrics"). Used to organize KPIs into logical groupings within multi-section reports.']
  target_threshold_override decimal(18,4) [note: 'Report-specific override of the KPI\'s default target threshold value. Allows different reports to apply different performance targets for the same KPI based on audience or business context. Nullable if using the KPI\'s default target.']
  visualization_type varchar [note: 'The chart or visualization format used to display this KPI in this specific report context (e.g., table, line_chart, bar_chart, gauge, scorecard). The same KPI may be visualized differently across different reports.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the configuration relationship between KPI definitions and report definitions in the retail analytics catalog. It captures how KPIs are composed into reports with report-specific presentation rules, display ordering, visualization preferences, and threshold overrides. Each record links one KPI definition to one report definition with attributes that control how that specific KPI appears and behaves within that specific report context.. Existence Justification: In retail analytics operations, BI teams actively manage the composition of reports by selecting which KPIs to include, configuring how each KPI is visualized, setting display order, and defining report-specific presentation rules. A single KPI (e.g., Comparable Store Sales) appears in multiple reports (Executive Dashboard, Store Operations Report, Regional Performance Report) with different visualizations, thresholds, and filters. Conversely, each report contains multiple KPIs arranged in a specific sequence with report-specific configuration. This is an operational configuration activity managed through BI catalog tools, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table analytics.metric_entity_dependency {
  metric_entity_dependency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this metric-entity dependency record. Primary key.']
  semantic_layer_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the semantic layer entity that this metric depends on for calculation']
  semantic_metric_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the semantic metric that consumes this entity in its calculation']
  aggregation_role varchar [note: 'The role this entity plays in the metric calculation: BASE_MEASURE (provides the fact to aggregate), DIMENSION (provides grouping/slicing attributes), FILTER (used only for filtering), GROUPING (defines the grain of aggregation).']
  calculation_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence indicating the order in which this entity is joined or processed in the metric calculation. Lower numbers are processed first. Used for complex multi-step metric calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this metric-entity dependency was first defined in the semantic layer platform. Tracks when the relationship was established.']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dependency relationship: DIRECT (entity is explicitly referenced in metric definition), INDIRECT (entity is required through transitive dependency), DERIVED (entity is computed from other entities for this metric).']
  entity_alias varchar [note: 'The alias or reference name used for this entity within the metric calculation expression. Used when the same entity is referenced multiple times with different join paths or filters.']
  filter_expression varchar [note: 'SQL WHERE clause or semantic layer filter logic applied specifically to this entity when used in this metric calculation. Defines the subset of entity data included in the metric.']
  is_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this entity dependency is mandatory for the metric calculation. True = metric cannot be calculated without this entity; False = entity is optional or conditional.']
  join_condition varchar [note: 'SQL join condition or semantic layer join expression that defines how this entity is joined to other entities in the metric calculation. Captures the ON clause logic.']
  join_type varchar [note: 'SQL join type used to connect this entity to the metric calculation (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL, CROSS). Defines how the entity participates in the metric query generation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this dependency configuration (join logic, filter, role). Used for change tracking and impact analysis.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analytics engineer or data steward who last modified this dependency configuration. Used for governance and accountability.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the metadata lineage relationship between semantic metrics and the semantic layer entities they depend on for calculation. It captures the technical join configuration, filter logic, and aggregation behavior required to compute each metric from its constituent entities. Each record links one semantic metric to one semantic layer entity with attributes that define how the entity participates in the metric calculation within the BI semantic layer platform.. Existence Justification: In retail semantic layer platforms (Looker, dbt Semantic Layer, Databricks Unity Catalog), metrics explicitly depend on multiple entities for calculation (e.g., a Gross Margin % metric requires sales_fact, product_dim, and cost_dim entities), and entities are reused across many metrics. The business actively manages these dependencies as metadata configurations that drive BI query generation, defining join paths, filter logic, and aggregation roles for each metric-entity pair.'
}

Table analytics.sla_kpi_measurement {
  sla_kpi_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this SLA-KPI measurement binding record. Primary key.']
  kpi_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enterprise KPI definition used to measure this SLA']
  sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supply chain SLA being measured']
  alert_threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'An early-warning threshold value that triggers alerts before the breach threshold is reached. Allows proactive intervention when KPI performance is trending toward SLA breach.']
  breach_penalty_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'The penalty rate applied when this specific KPI breaches the threshold for this specific SLA. Expressed as a percentage or per-unit rate depending on the SLA\'s penalty structure type. Allows different KPIs measuring the same SLA to have different penalty consequences.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this measurement binding was created in the SLA management system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this KPI-SLA measurement binding ceases to be active. Nullable for ongoing measurements. Supports historical tracking of which KPIs were used to measure which SLAs at different points in time.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this KPI-SLA measurement binding becomes active. Allows the measurement framework to evolve over time as KPIs are added or removed from SLA scorecards.']
  is_primary_kpi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this KPI is the primary/lead metric for this SLA (true) or a supporting/secondary metric (false). Used to distinguish the main performance indicator from supplementary metrics.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or email of the user who last modified this measurement binding configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this measurement binding.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this specific KPI is calculated and evaluated against this specific SLA. May differ from the KPI\'s default reporting frequency or the SLA\'s default measurement frequency when this combination requires special handling.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this KPI-SLA measurement binding. Active means currently in use; Suspended means temporarily disabled; Under Review means being evaluated for changes; Deprecated means no longer used but retained for historical reference.']
  measurement_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'The relative weight or importance of this KPI in the overall SLA performance scorecard, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.30 for 30%). Used when multiple KPIs contribute to a composite SLA score.']
  measurement_window_days varchar [note: 'Rolling number of days over which this KPI is aggregated when evaluating this SLA. May differ from the SLA\'s default measurement window when this specific KPI requires different temporal aggregation.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The target threshold value for this KPI when measuring this specific SLA. This is the SLA-KPI-specific threshold that may differ from the KPI\'s enterprise-wide target_threshold_value or the SLA\'s generic target_threshold_value, allowing customization per measurement context.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or email of the supply chain analyst or SLA manager who created this KPI-SLA measurement binding.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the measurement contract between supply chain SLA definitions and enterprise KPI definitions. It captures the specific measurement parameters, thresholds, and penalty structures that apply when a particular KPI is used to evaluate a particular SLA. Each record links one SLA definition to one KPI definition with measurement-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this monitoring relationship.. Existence Justification: In retail supply chain operations, a single SLA (e.g., Supplier Fill Rate SLA) is measured by multiple KPIs (fill rate percentage, order completeness count, line item accuracy), and each enterprise KPI (e.g., On-Time Delivery %) is used to measure multiple SLAs across different suppliers, product categories, and distribution centers. The business actively manages SLA performance scorecards where each KPI-SLA pairing has its own measurement frequency, threshold, penalty rate, and effective dates.'
}

Table analytics.workspace {
  workspace_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for workspace']
  access_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.access_policy. Business justification: Workspaces require access control to govern who can access workspace resources. This FK links workspace to its governing access_policy, consistent with the pattern where dashboard_config and report_su']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing account responsible for workspace-related charges.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit to which this workspace is assigned.']
  owner_department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department that owns and manages this workspace.']
  parent_workspace_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on workspace (parent_workspace_id)']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Security classification level that determines who can access this workspace.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the workspace was activated and made available for use.']
  archive_after_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of inactivity after which workspace content is automatically archived.']
  archived_date date [note: 'Date when the workspace was archived and moved to inactive status.']
  asset_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data assets, reports, dashboards, or other objects contained within this workspace.']
  auto_archive_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic archiving of inactive workspace content is enabled.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance framework that governs this workspace, such as GDPR, CCPA, SOX, or HIPAA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|CCPA|SOX|HIPAA|PCI-DSS|ISO-27001|NIST — promote to reference product]']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code to which workspace expenses are allocated.']
  created_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this workspace.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the workspace was initially created in the system.']
  data_residency_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code indicating where workspace data must be stored for compliance purposes.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the workspace purpose, scope, and intended use cases.']
  environment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workspace based on its intended environment usage.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the workspace is scheduled to expire or be decommissioned.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workspace is currently active and available for use.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workspace is the default workspace for new users or specific user groups.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workspace serves as a template for creating new workspaces.']
  last_accessed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the workspace was last accessed by any user.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the workspace configuration or content was last modified.']
  max_member_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of users allowed to be members of this workspace.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of users who have access to this workspace.']
  modified_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this workspace configuration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the workspace.']
  owner_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who owns and is responsible for this workspace.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this workspace for resource allocation and support purposes.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter code representing the geographic region where this workspace data is stored.']
  retention_policy_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that data within this workspace must be retained before it can be purged.']
  storage_quota_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity allocated to this workspace measured in gigabytes.']
  storage_used_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current amount of storage consumed by this workspace measured in gigabytes.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or labels applied to this workspace for categorization and search purposes.']
  template_source_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the template workspace from which this workspace was created, if applicable.']
  visibility_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the organizational scope within which this workspace is visible and discoverable.']
  workspace_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the workspace, used for external references and integrations.']
  workspace_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the workspace used for identification and display purposes.']
  workspace_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the workspace indicating its operational state.']
  workspace_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workspace based on its scope and intended use within the organization.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for workspace. Referenced by workspace_id.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_program {
  compliance_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance program. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_AGREEMENT (long-running compliance framework container).']
  parent_compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compliance_program (parent_compliance_program_id)']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Required or planned frequency of compliance audits for this program. Continuous: real-time monitoring. Ad_hoc: event-driven or unscheduled audits.']
  certification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current certification or accreditation expires and must be renewed. Null if not applicable or perpetual.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Official certification or accreditation number issued by the governing body or third-party auditor (e.g., ISO 27001 certificate number, PCI-DSS Attestation of Compliance number). Null if program does not issue certifications.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of external certification or accreditation for this program. Not_applicable: program does not require external certification.']
  compliance_program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the compliance program purpose, objectives, key requirements, and business rationale.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance program record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance program became or will become binding and enforceable within the organization.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance program ends or requires renewal. Null for perpetual programs without fixed term.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the external regulatory authority, standards organization, or certification body that oversees this compliance program (e.g., Federal Trade Commission, Consumer Product Safety Commission, PCI Security Standards Council).']
  incident_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of compliance incidents, violations, or breaches recorded for this program in the current calendar year.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit or assessment conducted for this program.']
  last_audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent compliance audit. Passed: full compliance. Passed_with_findings: compliant but with minor issues noted. Failed: non-compliant. Not_applicable: audit not yet conducted or program not subject to audit.']
  manager varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual responsible for day-to-day program management and coordination. Business-confidential organizational information.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this compliance program record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance program record was last updated or modified.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance audit or assessment for this program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, historical notes, or program-specific considerations not captured in structured fields.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the business unit, division, or department responsible for managing and executing this compliance program (e.g., Legal & Compliance, Food Safety, Information Security, Store Operations).']
  penalty_amount_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum potential financial penalty or fine (in USD) for non-compliance with this program, based on regulatory framework or historical precedent. Business-confidential risk assessment data.']
  policy_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or document management system reference to the official compliance program policy document, standard operating procedures, or program charter.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the compliance program (e.g., FDA-FSMA-2024, PCI-DSS-V4, GDPR-EU). Used in regulatory filings and audit documentation.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the compliance program (e.g., Food and Drug Administration Food Safety Modernization Act Program, Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard Program).']
  program_scope varchar [note: 'Organizational scope of the compliance program. Enterprise_wide: applies to all Retail operations. Banner_specific: applies to specific retail banners. Channel_specific: applies to e-commerce, stores, or distribution only. Region_specific: applies to specific geographic regions. Store_specific: applies to individual stores. DC_specific: applies to distribution centers.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the compliance program. Active: program is operational and enforced. Suspended: temporarily paused. Under_review: being evaluated for changes. Retired: no longer applicable. Pending_activation: approved but not yet effective. Non_compliant: program exists but organization is currently out of compliance.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compliance program by its nature: regulatory (government-mandated), industry_standard (voluntary best practice), internal_policy (company-specific), certification (third-party accreditation), environmental (sustainability/emissions), safety (workplace/product), privacy (data protection), security (information/payment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory|industry_standard|internal_policy|certification|environmental|safety|privacy|security — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or industry standard framework(s) that this program addresses (e.g., FDA/FSMA, OSHA, PCI-DSS, GDPR, CCPA, ISO 27001, GS1). Multiple frameworks may be listed comma-separated.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for program recertification, re-assessment, or formal renewal review. Applicable for certification-based and time-bound programs.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk severity if the organization fails to maintain compliance with this program. Critical: regulatory shutdown or major financial penalty. High: significant fines or reputational damage. Medium: moderate penalties. Low: minor impact.']
  sponsor varchar [note: 'Name or title of the executive sponsor accountable for the compliance program (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, VP Food Safety, Chief Information Security Officer). Business-confidential organizational information.']
  training_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of compliance training for employees covered by this program. Not_applicable: training not required.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employee training is mandatory for this compliance program. True: training required. False: no training requirement.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this compliance program record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each formal compliance program operated by Retail, such as the FDA/FSMA Food Safety Program, OSHA Safety Program, PCI-DSS Payment Security Program, GDPR Privacy Program, CCPA Privacy Program, and Environmental Compliance Program. Captures program name, owning business unit, program sponsor, regulatory framework(s) covered, program status (active, suspended, under review), effective date, renewal date, and program scope (enterprise-wide, banner-specific, channel-specific). Acts as the top-level organizing entity for all compliance activities.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance obligation record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific employee or user assigned as the current owner of this obligation. Links to workforce or user entity.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance program under which this obligation is managed. Links to the compliance program entity.']
  parent_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on obligation (parent_obligation_id)']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory requirement that mandates this obligation. Links to the regulatory requirement catalog.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this obligation is subject to internal or external audit review. May differ from fulfillment cadence.']
  cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the obligation must be fulfilled. Determines scheduling and recurrence rules for compliance tasks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|semi_annual|quarterly|monthly|weekly|event_driven|one_time — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The specific compliance framework or standard under which this obligation is classified. Examples include PCI-DSS, GDPR, CCPA, FSMA, ISO 27001.']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'The high-level control objective this obligation supports. Examples include data privacy protection, payment security, food safety, workplace safety.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this obligation record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the obligation must be fulfilled to maintain compliance. Used for scheduling and alerting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the obligation becomes active and enforceable. Aligns with regulatory effective dates.']
