  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier party against whom this SLA performance is being tracked. Supports supplier fill rate compliance monitoring and scorecarding.']
  actual_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The measured actual metric value achieved during the measurement period. For fill rate SLAs this is the actual fill rate percentage achieved. Compared against target_value to determine breach status.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual performance value failed to meet the contractual SLA target for this measurement period. True = SLA breach occurred; False = SLA target met or exceeded. Primary trigger for breach alerting workflows.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the SLA breach based on the magnitude of variance from target. Critical indicates severe underperformance requiring immediate escalation; none indicates no breach. Used to prioritize corrective action and chargeback processing.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the vendor compliance chargeback assessed against the supplier for this SLA breach, expressed in the operating currency. Calculated per the contractual chargeback schedule. Applicable only when chargeback_eligible_flag is True.']
  chargeback_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA breach qualifies for a vendor compliance chargeback (Chargeback) under the supplier agreement terms. True = chargeback may be issued; False = breach is not chargeback-eligible (e.g., force majeure, waived). SSOT for chargeback processing workflows.']
  chargeback_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the vendor compliance chargeback associated with this SLA breach. Pending = not yet issued; issued = chargeback sent to supplier; disputed = supplier has contested; resolved = settled; waived = chargeback forgiven.']
  consecutive_breach_count varchar [note: 'The number of consecutive measurement periods in which this SLA has been breached by the same supplier, as of this record. Used to identify chronic underperformers and trigger escalation or contract review.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the supplier\'s corrective action plan was confirmed as completed and accepted. Null if corrective action is still in progress or not required.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The contractually or operationally agreed deadline by which the supplier must submit or complete the corrective action plan for the SLA breach. Used for escalation tracking.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Status of the corrective action plan (CAP) initiated in response to an SLA breach. Not_required = no breach or waived; pending = CAP requested but not started; in_progress = CAP underway; completed = CAP closed; overdue = CAP past due date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA performance record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage and compliance requirements.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which the SLA performance data was sourced. Supports data lineage, audit, and reconciliation. SAP_S4HANA = SAP S/4HANA Retail; ORMS = Oracle Retail Merchandising System; MANHATTAN_WMS = Manhattan Associates WMS; BLUE_YONDER = Blue Yonder Demand Planning; EDI = Electronic Data Interchange feed.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA breach has been escalated to senior management or a formal supplier review process due to severity, recurrence, or overdue corrective action. True = escalated; False = not escalated.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The supplier fill rate expressed as a decimal percentage, calculated as shipped_quantity divided by ordered_quantity. Represents the Order Completion Percentage (Fill Rate) for this measurement period. Applicable only for fill_rate SLA type.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this SLA is measured and reported. Determines the granularity of the measurement_period_start_date and measurement_period_end_date window.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the measurement window for which this SLA performance record is calculated. Together with measurement_period_start_date defines the SLA evaluation window.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the measurement window for which this SLA performance record is calculated. Used to define the reporting period for scorecarding and compliance monitoring.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this SLA performance measurement was captured or calculated. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for this record.']
  metric_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the SLA metric values (target_value, actual_value, variance_value). Percentage applies to fill rate and accuracy SLAs; days/hours apply to lead time and on-time delivery SLAs; units/cases apply to quantity-based SLAs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, dispute details, exception justifications, or operational commentary related to this SLA performance record. Used by supply chain analysts and vendor managers.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of units or cases ordered on the associated purchase order(s) within the measurement period. Used as the denominator in fill rate percentage calculation for fill rate SLA types.']
  performance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this SLA performance record. Compliant = target met; breached = target missed; at_risk = approaching threshold; under_review = disputed or under investigation; waived = breach formally excused by agreement.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of units or cases physically received and confirmed at the distribution center within the measurement period. May differ from shipped_quantity due to transit damage or discrepancies.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the SLA breach was formally resolved, including completion of corrective actions and/or chargeback settlement. Null for open or compliant records. Used for resolution cycle time reporting.']
  scorecard_period varchar [note: 'The standardized scorecard period label for this SLA measurement, used in supplier performance scorecarding and reporting. Format: YYYY-Qn for quarterly, YYYY-Wnn for weekly, or YYYY-MM for monthly periods.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of units or cases shipped by the supplier against the ordered quantity within the measurement period. Used in fill rate calculation and short-ship analysis.']
  short_ship_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity shortfall between ordered_quantity and shipped_quantity. Represents units not fulfilled by the supplier. Used for chargeback calculation and supplier compliance reporting.']
  short_ship_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the supplier shipped less than the ordered quantity. Used for root cause analysis, chargeback eligibility determination, and supplier performance improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|allocation_shortage|production_delay|transportation_delay|order_error|weather_event|force_majeure — promote to reference product]']
  sla_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this SLA performance record, used in supplier communications, chargeback documentation, and audit trails. Format: SLA-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA commitment being measured. Fill rate tracks order completion percentage; on_time_delivery tracks delivery punctuality; lead_time tracks supplier delivery duration; order_accuracy tracks shipment correctness; invoice_accuracy tracks billing correctness; chargeback_compliance tracks vendor compliance penalty adherence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fill_rate|on_time_delivery|lead_time|order_accuracy|invoice_accuracy|chargeback_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The originating transaction identifier from the source system of record (e.g., SAP delivery document number, EDI 856 ASN number, WMS receipt number) that this SLA performance record is derived from. Supports audit trail and reconciliation.']
  target_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The contractually agreed target metric value for this SLA commitment. For fill rate SLAs this is the target fill rate percentage (e.g., 0.9500 = 95%). For lead time SLAs this is the target number of days. Unit of measure is defined by sla_type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA performance record was last modified. Audit trail field used to track corrections, status changes, and chargeback updates.']
  variance_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between actual_value and target_value (actual minus target). Negative values indicate underperformance against the SLA commitment. Used for trend analysis and breach severity assessment.']

  Note: 'Operational records tracking actual vs. target SLA performance for each supply chain SLA commitment. Captures measurement period, actual and target metric values, variance, breach flag and severity, corrective action status, and resolution tracking. For fill rate SLAs: ordered/shipped/received quantities, fill rate percentage, short-ship reason codes, and chargeback eligibility. SSOT for SLA breach alerting, supplier fill rate compliance monitoring, scorecarding, and chargeback processing.'
}

Table supplychain.inbound_appointment {
  inbound_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each dock appointment scheduling record for inbound shipments at distribution centers and stores.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for delivering the inbound shipment to the facility.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center (DC) or store location where the inbound appointment is scheduled. Links to the facility master.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment record expected at this appointment. Enables traceability from appointment to physical shipment.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order associated with this inbound appointment. Links the appointment to the procurement lifecycle.']
  scheduled_by_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Receiving coordinators schedule inbound appointments for operational accountability and performance tracking. Operations managers track coordinator productivity, appointment accuracy, and dock utiliza']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor originating the inbound shipment associated with this appointment.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the carrier checked in or arrived at the facility gate or dock. Used to calculate on-time performance and dwell time against the scheduled start.']
  actual_carton_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of cartons or cases physically received during unloading. Discrepancies against expected carton count trigger supplier chargeback or shortage claims.']
  actual_pallet_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of pallets received and counted during the unloading process. Compared against expected pallet count to identify discrepancies and supplier compliance issues.']
  actual_unload_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when physical unloading was completed and the dock door was cleared. Used to compute actual unload duration and dock utilization.']
  actual_unload_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when physical unloading of the trailer or container began at the assigned dock door.']
  advance_ship_notice_number varchar [note: 'The Advance Ship Notice (ASN) number transmitted by the supplier via EDI 856 prior to shipment arrival. Enables pre-receiving, labor planning, and automated appointment creation.']
  appointment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The planned duration of the dock appointment in minutes, calculated based on expected volume (pallets/cartons) and appointment type. Drives dock door time slot blocking in the WMS scheduler.']
  appointment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the appointment, entered by DC staff, carriers, or the scheduling system. May include special handling requirements, access instructions, or exception details.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable appointment reference number used by carriers, suppliers, and DC staff to identify and communicate about the appointment. Typically generated by the WMS or carrier portal.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dock appointment. Tracks progression from initial scheduling through completion or exception. Values: scheduled (booked but not confirmed), confirmed (carrier acknowledged), arrived (carrier checked in at gate), completed (unload finished), no_show (carrier did not arrive), cancelled (appointment voided).']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inbound appointment by unloading method. Live unload: carrier waits while freight is unloaded. Drop trailer: carrier leaves trailer at dock for later unloading. Cross-dock (cross-docking): freight transferred directly to outbound without storage. Floor load: loose cartons loaded directly on trailer floor.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading (BOL) number issued by the carrier as a receipt of freight and contract of carriage. Key document for inbound receiving reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'The Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) to uniquely identify the transportation carrier. Used in EDI transactions and freight audit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the appointment record was first created in the system, regardless of scheduling source. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'The specific dock door or bay number at the facility assigned to this inbound appointment. Used for physical routing of labor and equipment to the correct location.']
  dock_door_zone varchar [note: 'The physical zone or section of the facility where the assigned dock door is located (e.g., North Dock, Receiving Zone A). Supports labor planning and traffic flow management.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'The commercial driver\'s license (CDL) number of the driver presenting at the facility. Captured for security verification and regulatory compliance at gate check-in.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the truck driver who checked in at the facility for this appointment. Captured at gate check-in for security and compliance purposes.']
  expected_carton_count varchar [note: 'The total number of cartons or cases expected in the inbound shipment per the ASN or purchase order. Used for receiving accuracy validation.']
  expected_pallet_count varchar [note: 'The number of pallets expected to be unloaded during this appointment as communicated via ASN or purchase order. Used for dock labor planning and time slot duration calculation.']
  expected_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The total expected gross weight of the inbound shipment in kilograms as declared on the Bill of Lading or ASN. Used for dock equipment planning and freight audit.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inbound shipment contains hazardous materials (Hazmat) requiring special handling, dock equipment, or regulatory compliance procedures at the facility.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inbound shipment requires temperature-controlled handling (refrigerated or frozen). Triggers assignment to a temperature-controlled dock door and cold chain receiving procedures.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the appointment record was most recently modified. Tracks status changes, reschedules, and data corrections for audit and change management purposes.']
  late_arrival_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason captured when a carrier arrives outside the scheduled appointment window. Used for root cause analysis of inbound logistics delays and carrier performance management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: traffic|weather|mechanical_breakdown|driver_hours|customs_delay|shipper_delay|other — promote to reference product]']
  on_time_arrival_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier arrived within the scheduled appointment window. Derived from comparison of actual arrival timestamp to scheduled start timestamp. Used for carrier on-time performance (OTP) reporting and SLA compliance tracking.']
  original_scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The original planned appointment start timestamp before any rescheduling occurred. Preserved for compliance reporting and to measure the impact of rescheduling on DC operations.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number, which is the freight bill number used to track the shipment through the carrier\'s system. Standard identifier for LTL and TL freight.']
  reschedule_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this appointment has been rescheduled. High reschedule counts indicate carrier reliability issues or supply chain disruptions and are used in supplier/carrier compliance scoring.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when the unloading activity is expected to be completed and the dock door released for the next appointment.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when the carrier is expected to arrive at the dock door and unloading is scheduled to begin. Principal business event timestamp for the appointment.']
  scheduling_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the appointment was originally created. Carrier portal: self-service web portal. EDI: Electronic Data Interchange automated scheduling. Manual: DC staff entered directly. API: programmatic integration from TMS or OMS.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number affixed to the trailer or container door. Verified at arrival to confirm shipment integrity and detect tampering during transit.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'The specific temperature handling requirement for the inbound shipment. Ambient: room temperature. Chilled: 0–4°C. Frozen: -18°C or below. Ultra-frozen: -40°C or below (e.g., pharmaceutical or specialty frozen goods).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned trailer or container identification number for the inbound shipment. Used for physical asset tracking, seal verification, and RFID scanning at the dock.']

  Note: 'Dock appointment scheduling records for inbound shipments at DCs and stores. Captures appointment date/time, dock door assignment, carrier, expected shipment, appointment status (scheduled, confirmed, arrived, completed, no-show), appointment type (live unload, drop trailer), and scheduling source (carrier portal, EDI, manual). Coordinates inbound logistics flow.'
}

Table supplychain.cross_dock_plan {
  cross_dock_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the cross-dock execution plan record. Primary key for this entity.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Distribution Center (DC) facility where the cross-dock operation is executed. Links to the DC master record.']
  inbound_asn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inbound Advance Shipment Notice (ASN) from the supplier, confirming the shipment details and expected arrival of merchandise to be cross-docked.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination retail store to which the cross-docked merchandise is allocated and shipped. For multi-store plans, this represents the primary or single destination store.']
  merchandise_category_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandise category (e.g., grocery, apparel, electronics) to which the cross-docked SKU belongs. Used for category-level cross-dock performance reporting and assortment planning alignment.']
  outbound_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier or Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider responsible for delivering cross-docked merchandise from the DC to the destination store.']
  planner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Cross-dock planners create plans for planning accountability and performance tracking. Supply chain tracks plan accuracy, dwell time, and execution success by planner for operational efficiency and KP']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional event driving this cross-dock plan, if applicable. High-velocity promotional merchandise is a primary use case for cross-docking to accelerate store replenishment.']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the replenishment plan that triggered this cross-dock execution. Links to the demand-driven replenishment plan generated by the forecasting and replenishment system.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) being cross-docked. Links to the product master record for item-level tracking and allocation.']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inbound Purchase Order (PO) from which merchandise is being cross-docked. Links to the purchase order header record in the procurement system.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor delivering the inbound merchandise for cross-docking. Used for supplier performance tracking and EDI coordination.']
  actual_carton_qty varchar [note: 'The actual number of cartons processed through the cross-dock operation. Used to reconcile against planned carton count and measure DC handling accuracy.']
  actual_inbound_date date [note: 'The actual date on which inbound merchandise arrived at the Distribution Center (DC). Compared against planned inbound date to measure supplier on-time delivery performance.']
  actual_inbound_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when inbound merchandise was received at the DC dock door. Used for dwell time calculation and cross-dock cycle time analytics.']
  actual_outbound_date date [note: 'The actual date on which cross-docked merchandise departed the Distribution Center (DC) for the destination store. Used to measure DC throughput speed and cross-dock execution efficiency.']
  actual_outbound_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when cross-docked merchandise departed the DC on the outbound carrier. Used to calculate total DC dwell time and cross-dock cycle time.']
  actual_received_unit_qty varchar [note: 'The actual number of units received from the supplier at the DC inbound dock. Compared against planned quantity to identify short shipments, overages, or discrepancies.']
  actual_shipped_unit_qty varchar [note: 'The actual number of units shipped outbound to the destination store after cross-docking. May differ from received quantity due to damage, quality holds, or allocation adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cross-dock plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and operational reporting.']
  cross_dock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cross-docking method. Pre-allocated means merchandise is pre-sorted for specific stores before arrival; opportunistic (flow-through) means merchandise is sorted upon arrival based on real-time demand signals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_allocated|opportunistic|flow_through|merge_in_transit|pool_distribution — promote to reference product]']
  dwell_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The total number of hours merchandise spent at the Distribution Center (DC) between inbound receipt and outbound shipment. A key cross-dock efficiency metric; target is typically under 24 hours for true cross-docking.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the planned unit quantity that was actually fulfilled and shipped to the destination store through this cross-dock plan. Calculated as (actual_shipped_unit_qty / planned_unit_qty) * 100. Key supply chain KPI for cross-dock execution performance.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text operational instructions for DC associates handling the cross-dock merchandise, such as fragile handling, temperature requirements, sequencing rules, or special labeling requirements.']
  inbound_dock_door varchar [note: 'The assigned inbound dock door number or identifier at the Distribution Center where the supplier\'s truck will unload merchandise for cross-docking.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cross-docked merchandise contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transport compliance per regulatory standards.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cross-docked merchandise is a Private Label (store brand) product. Private label merchandise may have distinct handling, labeling, and compliance requirements.']
  is_promotional_merchandise boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchandise in this cross-dock plan is associated with a promotional event. Promotional merchandise is a primary driver of cross-docking to accelerate store replenishment and reduce DC handling costs.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this cross-dock plan record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  outbound_dock_door varchar [note: 'The assigned outbound dock door number or identifier at the Distribution Center from which cross-docked merchandise will be loaded onto the outbound carrier for store delivery.']
  outbound_trailer_number varchar [note: 'The trailer or container number assigned for the outbound shipment of cross-docked merchandise to the destination store. Used for load tracking and carrier coordination.']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments added by DC planners or operations managers regarding exceptions, adjustments, or special circumstances related to this cross-dock plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the cross-dock plan, used in operational communications between DC teams, suppliers, and store operations. Follows the format CDP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the cross-dock execution plan. Tracks progression from initial planning through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|planned|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  planned_carton_qty varchar [note: 'The planned number of cartons (cases) of the SKU scheduled for cross-docking. Used for dock door capacity planning, pallet build, and outbound trailer loading.']
  planned_inbound_date date [note: 'The planned date on which inbound merchandise from the supplier is expected to arrive at the Distribution Center (DC) for cross-docking. Used for dock scheduling and labor planning.']
  planned_inbound_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise planned date and time for inbound merchandise arrival at the DC dock door. Used for granular dock door scheduling and labor allocation within the WMS.']
  planned_outbound_date date [note: 'The planned date on which cross-docked merchandise is scheduled to depart the Distribution Center (DC) for the destination store. Drives outbound dock scheduling and carrier booking.']
  planned_pallet_qty varchar [note: 'The planned number of pallets expected for this cross-dock plan. Used for outbound trailer capacity planning and dock door assignment.']
  planned_unit_qty varchar [note: 'The planned number of individual units (eaches) of the SKU scheduled for cross-docking under this plan. Drives inbound receiving expectations and outbound load planning.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Temperature handling requirement for the cross-docked merchandise. Critical for grocery and perishable categories to ensure cold chain compliance during DC cross-dock operations.']
  variance_unit_qty varchar [note: 'The difference between planned unit quantity and actual shipped unit quantity for this cross-dock plan. Positive values indicate a shortfall; negative values indicate an overage. Used for supplier compliance and chargeback management.']

  Note: 'Cross-docking execution plans for merchandise flowing directly from inbound receiving to outbound shipping without DC storage. Captures source PO, destination store allocations, SKU-level quantities, planned and actual cross-dock dates, cross-dock type (pre-allocated, opportunistic flow-through), execution status, and handling instructions. Reduces DC handling costs and accelerates store replenishment for high-velocity and promotional merchandise.'
}

Table supplychain.supplychain_edi_transaction {
  supplychain_edi_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for edi_transaction']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'The EDI interchange receiver identifier (ISA08) as defined in the interchange control header. Identifies the intended recipient of the EDI transmission. May be the trading partner\'s EDI ID (outbound) or the retailer\'s EDI ID (inbound).']
  item_cross_reference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.item_cross_reference. Business justification: EDI document processing (850 PO, 856 ASN) requires mapping trading partner item numbers to internal SKUs. Cross-reference table provides this translation during inbound EDI transaction parsing, enabli']
  sender_vendor_id varchar [note: 'The EDI interchange sender identifier (ISA06) as defined in the interchange control header. Identifies the originating party of the EDI transmission. May be the retailer\'s EDI ID (outbound) or the trading partner\'s EDI ID (inbound).']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'The shipment or ASN reference number extracted from the EDI document payload (e.g., BSN02 on 856 ASN). Links the EDI transaction to the physical shipment record in Manhattan Associates WMS for inbound receiving and cross-docking workflows.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate identifier referencing the trading partner (supplier, 3PL, or logistics provider) involved in this EDI transaction. Links to the supplier/trading partner master record for onboarding validation and compliance scoring.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the degree of compliance of this EDI transaction against the retailer\'s routing guide requirements and EDI standards. Calculated by the VAN or integration platform based on segment completeness, data element accuracy, and timing adherence. Drives trading partner scorecards and chargeback assessments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this EDI transaction record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the EDI document was received from a trading partner (INBOUND) or sent to a trading partner (OUTBOUND) by the retailer. Critical for routing guide compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  document_reference_number varchar [note: 'The business document reference number embedded within the EDI transaction, such as the Purchase Order number (850), Invoice number (810), or ASN shipment reference (856). Enables cross-referencing with source documents in SAP S/4HANA MM, Oracle RMS, or Manhattan WMS.']
  document_size_bytes varchar [note: 'The size of the raw EDI document payload in bytes. Used for VAN transmission cost allocation, integration platform capacity planning, and anomaly detection (e.g., unusually large or empty documents).']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'The EDI messaging standard used for this transaction (e.g., ANSI X12 for North American retail, EDIFACT for international trading partners, TRADACOMS for UK retail). Determines parsing rules and compliance validation logic.']
  edi_version varchar [note: 'The version of the EDI standard used for this transaction (e.g., \'004010\', \'005010\', \'006020\' for ANSI X12; \'D96A\', \'D01B\' for EDIFACT). Version determines segment and element specifications for validation.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'The primary error code returned when an EDI transaction fails validation or processing (e.g., ANSI X12 AK304 error codes: 1=Unrecognized segment, 2=Unexpected segment, 3=Mandatory segment missing, 8=Segment has data element errors). Supports error triage and trading partner remediation.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the error or validation failure for this EDI transaction. Provides context for error resolution, trading partner notification, and chargeback dispute management.']
  functional_ack_status varchar [note: 'The acknowledgment status returned in the 997 Functional Acknowledgment or 999 Implementation Acknowledgment for this transaction. Indicates whether the trading partner accepted, accepted with errors, or rejected the EDI document. Critical for EDI compliance monitoring and chargeback prevention.']
  functional_ack_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the 997 Functional Acknowledgment was received for this transaction. Used to calculate acknowledgment turnaround time and detect SLA breaches for late acknowledgments per retailer routing guide requirements.']
  group_control_number varchar [note: 'The control number assigned to the functional group (GS06 segment) within the EDI interchange. Groups related transaction sets of the same type and enables functional acknowledgment (997) matching at the group level.']
  interchange_control_number varchar [note: 'The unique 9-digit control number assigned to the EDI interchange envelope (ISA13 segment). Used to uniquely identify and track each interchange transmission for deduplication, acknowledgment matching, and audit purposes.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The supplier invoice number extracted from the EDI 810 Invoice transaction payload (BIG02 segment). Used to match EDI invoices against purchase orders and receipts in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for three-way match and accounts payable processing.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the EDI transaction was fully processed and accepted or rejected by the downstream system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM, Oracle RMS). Used to measure end-to-end processing latency and SLA adherence.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing lifecycle state of the EDI transaction within the VAN/integration platform. Drives SLA breach detection, retry workflows, and integration health dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIVED|QUEUED|PROCESSING|ACCEPTED|REJECTED|ERROR|RETRYING|ACKNOWLEDGED — promote to reference product]']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The retailer\'s Purchase Order number associated with this EDI transaction, extracted from the document payload (e.g., BEG02 on 850, BIG04 on 810, BSN02 on 856). Enables cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA MM purchase orders and Oracle RMS procurement records.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the EDI document was received and ingested by the retailer\'s integration platform or VAN. Used to calculate transmission latency and inbound SLA compliance windows.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this EDI transaction has been retried after an initial failure or rejection. Used to identify persistently failing transactions, trigger escalation workflows, and monitor integration platform health.']
  routing_guide_version varchar [note: 'The version of the retailer\'s EDI routing guide against which this transaction was validated (e.g., \'v2024.1\'). Enables compliance scoring to be evaluated against the correct version of requirements, supporting trading partner onboarding and annual routing guide updates.']
  segment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of segments in the EDI transaction set as reported in the SE02 trailer segment. Used for document integrity validation — the received segment count must match the SE02 value to confirm complete transmission.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EDI transaction breached the retailer\'s routing guide SLA requirement (True = SLA breached, False = within SLA). Populated based on comparison of transmission_timestamp against sla_target_hours. Drives SLA breach alerts and chargeback workflows.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The contractual SLA target in hours within which this EDI transaction type must be transmitted and acknowledged per the retailer\'s routing guide (e.g., ASN must be sent within 24 hours of shipment). Used as the benchmark for SLA breach detection.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated or will consume this EDI transaction (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for PO/Invoice, MANHATTAN_WMS for ASN/receiving, ORACLE_RMS for merchandise orders). Supports integration health monitoring and system-level error attribution.']
  test_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the EDI interchange is a test transmission (T) or a production transmission (P) as specified in the ISA15 segment. Test transactions must be excluded from operational processing and compliance scoring.']
  trading_partner_duns varchar [note: 'The Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number of the trading partner, a 9-digit identifier used for supplier identity verification, credit risk assessment, and EDI partner onboarding validation.']
  trading_partner_gln varchar [note: 'The GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) of the trading partner (supplier, 3PL, or DC) involved in this EDI exchange. A 13-digit GS1 identifier used to uniquely identify legal entities and physical locations in the supply chain. Required for GS1-compliant EDI routing.']
  trading_partner_name varchar [note: 'The registered business name of the trading partner (supplier, 3PL, or logistics provider) as maintained in the EDI partner profile. Supports human-readable reporting and onboarding dashboards.']
  trading_partner_qualifier varchar [note: 'The ANSI X12 ISA qualifier code indicating the type of trading partner identifier used in the interchange (e.g., 01=DUNS, 08=UCC EDI Comm ID, 12=Phone, 14=DUNS+4, 20=Health Industry Number, ZZ=Mutually Defined). Required for EDI interchange routing.']
  transaction_control_number varchar [note: 'The control number assigned to the individual transaction set (ST02 segment) within the functional group. Uniquely identifies a single transaction set for acknowledgment and error tracking.']
  transaction_set_code varchar [note: 'The ANSI X12 or EDIFACT transaction set number identifying the document type exchanged (e.g., 850 Purchase Order, 855 PO Acknowledgment, 856 Advance Ship Notice, 810 Invoice, 860 PO Change Order, 997 Functional Acknowledgment, 832 Price/Sales Catalog, 846 Inventory Inquiry). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 850|855|856|810|860|997|832|846|753|754 — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_set_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the EDI transaction set type (e.g., \'Purchase Order\', \'Advance Ship Notice (ASN)\', \'Invoice\', \'Functional Acknowledgment\') corresponding to the transaction_set_id. Supports dashboards and reporting without requiring code lookups.']
  transmission_protocol varchar [note: 'The communication protocol used to transmit the EDI document (e.g., AS2, SFTP, FTP, HTTPS, VAN mailbox, AS4). Relevant for integration health dashboards and security compliance audits per ISO 27001.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the EDI document was transmitted over the VAN or direct connection. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement and audit trails.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this EDI transaction record was last modified in the Silver layer data product, such as when processing status changed or retry count incremented.']
  van_provider varchar [note: 'The Value-Added Network (VAN) or direct EDI integration provider used to transmit this transaction (e.g., SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, Sterling Commerce, OpenText, Direct AS2/SFTP). Supports integration health monitoring and VAN cost allocation.']

  Note: 'EDI transaction log for supply chain document exchanges with suppliers, 3PLs, and trading partners via SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, or similar VAN. Captures transaction set type (850 PO, 855 PO Ack, 856 ASN, 810 Invoice, 860 PO Change, 997 Functional Ack), trading partner GLN/DUNS, transmission timestamp, direction (inbound/outbound), document reference, processing status, error codes, retry count, and compliance score. Enables EDI compliance monitoring against retailer routing guide requirements, SLA breach detection for late ASN/invoice, integration health dashboards, and trading partner onboarding validation.'
}

Table supplychain.dc_facility {
  dc_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center facility. Primary key for the DC facility master record.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the facility (street number, street name, building identifier).']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details (suite, floor, building name) if applicable.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility\'s warehouse automation maturity: manual (labor-intensive), semi-automated (some mechanization), highly automated (extensive robotics and conveyors), or fully automated (lights-out operations).']
  bonded_warehouse_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is a customs-bonded warehouse authorized to store imported goods before duties and taxes are paid, enabling deferred duty payment and re-export options.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the facility is located.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the facility was permanently closed or decommissioned. Null for active facilities.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the facility is located (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'MEX\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility record was first created in the master data system.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Total number of loading dock doors available for inbound receiving and outbound shipping operations.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique alphanumeric code for the facility used in operational systems, shipping documents, and cross-system integration. Typically 4-12 characters.']
  facility_email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the facility used for operational communications, appointment confirmations, and document exchange.']
  facility_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the facility manager or site director responsible for day-to-day operations and performance.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the distribution center or fulfillment facility (e.g., \'Northeast Regional DC\', \'Manhattan MFC-01\').']
  facility_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the facility used for carrier coordination, supplier communication, and operational inquiries.']
  facility_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the facility. Active facilities are fully operational; planned facilities are approved but not yet built; under construction facilities are being built; decommissioned facilities are permanently closed; temporarily closed facilities are inactive but may reopen; seasonal facilities operate only during peak periods.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility based on its primary operational purpose: regional DC (large-scale distribution), micro-fulfillment center (MFC for rapid e-commerce), dark store (fulfillment-only, no retail), cross-dock hub (transshipment), returns center (reverse logistics), or forward stocking location (FSL).']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1-issued 13-digit Global Location Number uniquely identifying this facility in global supply chain transactions and EDI exchanges.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified and equipped to handle, store, and ship hazardous materials in compliance with DOT and OSHA regulations.']
  inbound_dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors dedicated to inbound receiving operations from suppliers and carriers.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility record was most recently modified in the master data system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees for mapping, routing, and geospatial analytics.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current facility lease agreement expires. Null for owned facilities. Used for lease renewal planning and financial forecasting.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees for mapping, routing, and geospatial analytics.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the facility first became operational and began processing inventory and orders.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the facility on weekdays (Monday-Friday) in format \'HH:MM-HH:MM\' (e.g., \'06:00-22:00\'). Used for appointment scheduling and labor planning.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the facility on weekends (Saturday-Sunday) in format \'HH:MM-HH:MM\'. May differ from weekday hours or indicate closed status.']
  outbound_dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors dedicated to outbound shipping operations to stores, customers, or other facilities.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is owned by the company, leased from a landlord, or operated by a third-party logistics provider (3PL). Impacts financial reporting and operational control.']
  owning_region_code varchar [note: 'Business region or geographic division code that owns and manages this facility (e.g., \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST\', \'CENTRAL\'). Used for organizational hierarchy and P&L reporting.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the facility address used for mail delivery and geographic routing.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the state, province, or region (e.g., \'CA\' for California, \'ON\' for Ontario).']
  storage_capacity_cubic_feet decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total volumetric storage capacity of the facility measured in cubic feet, accounting for vertical storage utilization.']
  storage_capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of standard pallet positions available for inventory storage in racking and floor storage.']
  temperature_zone_ambient_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has ambient temperature storage zones (room temperature, non-climate-controlled) for general merchandise.']
  temperature_zone_chilled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has refrigerated (chilled) storage zones typically maintained at 32-40°F for perishable goods like dairy and produce.']
  temperature_zone_frozen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has frozen storage zones typically maintained at 0°F or below for frozen foods and ice cream.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the facility location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Chicago\') used for scheduling and timestamp normalization.']
  total_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total building square footage of the facility including warehouse space, office space, and support areas. Measured in square feet.']
  twenty_four_seven_operation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility operates continuously 24 hours per day, 7 days per week to support high-volume or time-sensitive fulfillment requirements.']
  warehouse_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage dedicated to warehouse operations including storage, picking, packing, and staging areas. Measured in square feet.']
  wms_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the Manhattan WMS instance or system deployment that manages this facility\'s warehouse operations. Used for system integration and data routing.']

  Note: 'Master record for each distribution center (DC) and micro-fulfillment center (MFC) operated by Retail. Captures facility type (regional DC, MFC, dark store, cross-dock hub, returns center), physical address, square footage, storage capacity (pallet positions, cubic feet), temperature zones (ambient, chilled, frozen), dock door count, operating hours, facility status (active, planned, decommissioned), owning region, and Manhattan WMS instance assignment. SSOT for DC/MFC identity referenced by all distribution operations including zone layout, dock scheduling, carrier assignments, and performance tracking.'
}

Table supplychain.warehouse_zone {
  warehouse_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the warehouse zone. Primary key for the warehouse zone entity.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse supervisor or zone manager responsible for operations within this zone.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent distribution center or micro-fulfillment center (MFC) that contains this zone.']
  aisle_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending aisle identifier for the zone, defining the end of the physical aisle range contained within this zone for pick path optimization.']
  aisle_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting aisle identifier for the zone, defining the beginning of the physical aisle range contained within this zone for pick path optimization.']
  automation_type varchar [note: 'Level and type of automation deployed in the zone. ASRS = Automated Storage and Retrieval System, AMR = Autonomous Mobile Robot, AGV = Automated Guided Vehicle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manual|conveyor|asrs|shuttle|amr|agv|robotic_pick — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  barcode_scanning_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether barcode scanning is mandatory for all inventory movements within the zone to ensure location accuracy and inventory integrity.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for tracking labor, equipment, and operational expenses associated with the zone for profit and loss (P&L) reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was first created in the warehouse management system, used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  current_occupancy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current space utilization percentage of the zone, calculated as occupied capacity divided by total capacity, used for capacity management and slotting decisions.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for physical inventory cycle counting within the zone, used for inventory accuracy management and shrinkage control.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the zone configuration was deactivated or retired from warehouse operations. Null indicates the zone is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the zone configuration became active and available for warehouse operations.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone is certified and equipped for storage of hazardous materials per OSHA and DOT regulations.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory cycle count performed in the zone, used for audit trail and inventory accuracy monitoring.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was most recently modified, used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  location_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual storage locations (bins, slots, shelves, pallet positions) contained within this zone for granular inventory tracking.']
  next_scheduled_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next physical inventory cycle count in the zone, used for operational planning and resource scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or configuration details relevant to the zone.']
  pick_method varchar [note: 'Primary order picking methodology used in the zone, determining workflow design and labor productivity metrics.']
  rack_configuration_type varchar [note: 'Type of racking system installed in the zone, determining storage density, accessibility, and material handling equipment requirements.']
  replenishment_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for zone replenishment tasks, with lower numbers indicating higher priority for maintaining forward pick face availability.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone is equipped with RFID infrastructure for automated inventory tracking and real-time location visibility.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the zone determining access control requirements, used for high-value merchandise, controlled substances, or restricted inventory.']
  slotting_strategy varchar [note: 'Inventory slotting methodology applied to the zone for optimizing pick efficiency, labor productivity, and space utilization.']
  temperature_classification varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for the zone, critical for grocery retail operations managing perishable goods and food safety compliance.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature threshold for temperature-controlled zones, measured in degrees Celsius. Used for cold chain monitoring and food safety compliance.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature threshold for temperature-controlled zones, measured in degrees Celsius. Used for cold chain monitoring and food safety compliance.']
  total_capacity_cubic_meters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volumetric storage capacity of the zone measured in cubic meters, used for space utilization analysis and capacity planning.']
  total_capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Total number of standard pallet positions available in the zone, critical for slotting optimization and inventory capacity management.']
  weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the zone in kilograms, constrained by floor loading limits and rack weight ratings for safety compliance.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the zone, used for operational reference and pick path planning within the warehouse management system (WMS).']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the zone describing its purpose or location within the facility (e.g., Receiving Dock A, Bulk Storage North, Forward Pick Zone 1, Packing Station B).']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the zone indicating availability for warehouse operations and inventory placement.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the zone indicating its primary operational purpose within the distribution center workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receiving|bulk_storage|forward_pick|reserve|packing|staging|dispatch|returns|cross_dock — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Defines the complete physical layout hierarchy within a DC or MFC, from zones (receiving, bulk storage, forward pick, packing, staging, dispatch, returns) down to individual storage locations (bin, slot, shelf, pallet position, floor stack). Zone-level attributes include zone code, type, temperature classification (ambient, chilled, frozen), aisle range, rack configuration (selective, drive-in, push-back, flow), automation type (manual, conveyor, AS/RS, shuttle, AMR), and zone capacity metrics. Location-level attributes include location code, aisle, bay, level, position, location type (bulk, pick face, reserve, floor, overflow), weight capacity, cubic capacity, RFID/barcode tag, and current occupancy status. SSOT for all physical space definitions within a facility — supports slotting optimization, pick path planning, capacity management, and location-level inventory tracking within Manhattan WMS.'
}

Table supplychain.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inbound_shipment']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for delivering this shipment. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight audit.']
  origin_facility_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating facility for inter-facility transfers. Null for supplier-direct shipments.']
  primary_inbound_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center receiving this shipment. Determines facility-specific receiving workflows and capacity planning.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor originating this shipment. Links to supplier master data for performance tracking and compliance monitoring.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date the shipment arrived at the distribution center. Used for on-time delivery performance measurement and carrier scorecarding.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the shipment actually arrived at the distribution center. Captured by yard management system or gate check-in.']
  actual_carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons actually received and verified during receiving operations. Compared against expected count for discrepancy resolution.']
  actual_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets actually received during receiving operations. Compared against expected count for variance analysis.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total actual weight of the shipment in kilograms as measured during receiving. Used for freight cost verification and variance analysis.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from the EDI 856 Advance Ship Notice document sent by the supplier prior to shipment arrival. Critical for pre-receiving planning and dock scheduling.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment. Serves as contract of carriage and receipt.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter code uniquely identifying the transportation carrier. Standard identifier used in EDI transactions and freight documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment is designated for cross-docking operations, bypassing putaway and moving directly to outbound staging.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific dock door assigned for receiving this shipment. Used for yard management and receiving workflow coordination.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date the shipment is expected to arrive at the distribution center. Used for dock scheduling and labor planning.']
  expected_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the shipment is expected to arrive at the distribution center. Used for appointment scheduling and dock door allocation.']
  expected_carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons expected in this shipment as communicated in the ASN. Used for receiving planning and variance detection.']
  expected_cube_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total expected volume of the shipment in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation capacity optimization.']
  expected_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets expected in this shipment as communicated in the ASN. Used for dock space allocation and material handling equipment planning.']
  expected_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total expected weight of the shipment in kilograms as communicated in the ASN. Used for freight audit and capacity planning.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight transportation cost for this inbound shipment. Used for landed cost calculation and freight spend analysis.']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the freight cost amount. Enables multi-currency freight cost tracking and consolidation.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and regulatory compliance. True triggers HAZMAT receiving protocols.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shipment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  late_arrival_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining the reason for late arrival when on-time arrival flag is false. Used for root cause analysis and carrier accountability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional information, special instructions, or exceptions related to this inbound shipment.']
  on_time_arrival_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment arrived within the scheduled appointment window. Used for carrier performance scorecarding and SLA compliance.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Used for freight tracking and proof of delivery.']
  receiving_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all receiving operations were completed for this shipment. Marks closure of the receiving process and inventory availability.']
  receiving_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when receiving operations began for this shipment. Marks the start of unloading and inspection activities.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the trailer or container. Verified at receiving to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inbound shipment, typically referenced in communications with suppliers and carriers. May be system-generated or supplier-provided.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound shipment. Tracks progression from in-transit through receiving completion or cancellation.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inbound shipment based on origin and purpose. Determines receiving workflow and handling requirements.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment maintained required temperature throughout transit. False triggers quality inspection and potential rejection.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment requires temperature-controlled handling for cold chain compliance. True for refrigerated or frozen goods.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific temperature range requirement for this shipment. Determines receiving dock assignment and storage location.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identification number of the trailer or container used to transport the shipment. Critical for yard management and dock door assignment.']

  Note: 'Tracks inbound shipments arriving at a DC or MFC from suppliers, vendors, or inter-facility transfers. Captures ASN (Advance Ship Notice) reference, carrier, trailer number, expected and actual arrival date/time, PO references, total carton and pallet counts, shipment status (in-transit, arrived, receiving, closed), and temperature compliance flag for cold chain shipments. Integrates with EDI 856 ASN feeds and Manhattan WMS receiving workflows. SSOT for all inbound freight visibility at the distribution center level.'
}

Table supplychain.receiving_event {
  receiving_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the receiving event. Primary key for this product.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier that delivered the inbound shipment.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center where the receiving event occurred.']
  food_safety_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_log. Business justification: Receiving temperature checks are logged in food_safety_log. Receiving events must reference specific temperature monitoring records for HACCP verification, regulatory audits, and product disposition d']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment being received at the dock door.']
  receiving_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse employee who performed the receiving operation.']
  receiving_supervisor_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse supervisor who approved or oversaw the receiving event, particularly for exception handling.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who shipped the goods being received.']
  actual_carton_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of cartons physically received and verified during the receiving event.']
  actual_pallet_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of pallets physically received and verified during the receiving event.']
  actual_unit_quantity varchar [note: 'The total number of individual units (eaches) physically received and verified during the receiving event.']
  advance_ship_notice_number varchar [note: 'The ASN number provided by the supplier, used to match expected shipment details with actual received goods.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The bill of lading number associated with the inbound shipment, used for carrier reconciliation.']
  carton_variance_count varchar [note: 'The difference between expected and actual carton count (actual minus expected). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the receiving event record was first created in the warehouse management system.']
  damage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any damaged goods were identified during the receiving event.']
  damaged_unit_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of units identified as damaged during the receiving inspection.']
  discrepancy_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any discrepancy between expected and actual quantities (e.g., supplier short ship, damaged in transit, incorrect ASN).']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'The specific dock door assignment where the inbound shipment was unloaded.']
  expected_carton_count varchar [note: 'The number of cartons expected to be received based on the advance shipment notice or purchase order.']
  expected_pallet_count varchar [note: 'The number of pallets expected to be received based on the advance shipment notice or purchase order.']
  expected_unit_quantity varchar [note: 'The total number of individual units (eaches) expected to be received based on the advance shipment notice or purchase order.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the receiving event record was last modified in the warehouse management system.']
  overage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receiving event resulted in an overage (more units received than expected).']
  pallet_variance_count varchar [note: 'The difference between expected and actual pallet count (actual minus expected). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number associated with the received goods, used for procurement reconciliation.']
  putaway_task_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether putaway tasks have been automatically generated in the WMS following the receiving event.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods require quality inspection before being made available for putaway or sale.']
  receiving_event_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the receiving event, used for tracking and reporting.']
  receiving_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the receiving clerk documenting any observations, issues, or special handling instructions.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receiving event.']
  receiving_type varchar [note: 'Type of receiving operation: blind receiving (no advance shipment notice), directed receiving (with ASN), cross-dock, return to vendor, or inter-facility transfer.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the security seal was found intact upon arrival. False indicates potential tampering or security breach.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number on the trailer or container, verified during receiving to ensure shipment integrity.']
  shortage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receiving event resulted in a shortage (fewer units received than expected).']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-controlled shipments arrived within the required temperature range.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual temperature reading (in Celsius) recorded upon arrival for temperature-controlled shipments.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'The trailer or container number of the vehicle that delivered the shipment.']
  unit_variance_quantity varchar [note: 'The difference between expected and actual unit quantity (actual minus expected). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.']
  unload_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes taken to unload the shipment, calculated from start to end timestamp.']
  unload_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the unloading of the inbound shipment was completed.']
  unload_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the unloading of the inbound shipment began at the dock door.']

  Note: 'Captures the physical receiving activity at a DC dock door when inbound shipments are unloaded and verified. Records dock door assignment, unload start/end timestamps, cartons received vs. expected, discrepancy flags (overage, shortage, damage), receiver employee ID, pallet count, and blind vs. directed receiving mode. Each receiving event is tied to an inbound shipment and drives put-away task generation in Manhattan WMS.'
}

Table supplychain.warehouse_task {
  warehouse_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the warehouse task. Primary key for the warehouse task entity.']
  asn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the inbound Advanced Ship Notice associated with putaway tasks. Links receiving tasks to expected inbound shipments.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Employee or operator identifier to whom this warehouse task has been assigned. Used for labor tracking and productivity measurement.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the distribution center or warehouse facility where this task is being executed.']
  handling_unit_id varchar [note: 'License plate number (LPN) or container identifier for the physical tote, carton, or pallet being handled in this task. Used for container-based tracking.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the customer order or replenishment order driving this warehouse task. Applicable for pick and pack tasks.']
  receiving_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplychain.receiving_event. Business justification: Putaway tasks are generated from receiving events. WMS orchestration requires linking task to originating receipt for labor tracking, exception handling, and cycle time measurement. Standard DC workfl']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the outbound shipment associated with this task. Links pick tasks to their destination shipment.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the master product entity associated with this warehouse task.']
  target_location_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse location identifier where the task terminates or inventory is placed. For putaway tasks, this is the storage bin. For pick tasks, this is the staging or packing location.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Wave planning identifier grouping multiple tasks released together for coordinated execution. Used in wave-based picking strategies.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of units completed or processed by the warehouse operator for this task. May differ from planned quantity in case of shorts or overages.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this warehouse task record was first inserted into the data warehouse or lakehouse. System-generated for data lineage tracking.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of material handling equipment required or used for this task: forklift (counterbalance lift), reach_truck (narrow aisle reach), pallet_jack (manual or powered), order_picker (vertical picker), conveyor (automated transport), AMR (Autonomous Mobile Robot), AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle), manual (no equipment), tugger (tow tractor), or clamp_truck (specialized forklift). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: forklift|reach_truck|pallet_jack|order_picker|conveyor|amr|agv|manual|tugger|clamp_truck — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this task encountered an exception condition requiring intervention or special handling.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the warehouse operator or supervisor providing additional context about the exception or task variance.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for task exception: short_pick (insufficient inventory), damaged_product, location_blocked, equipment_failure, quality_hold, mispick, system_error, or other predefined exception types.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date for perishable goods. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking logic and inventory rotation.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-14, GTIN-13, GTIN-12, or GTIN-8) for the product being handled. Used for barcode scanning and EDI integration.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this warehouse task record was most recently modified in the data warehouse or lakehouse. System-generated for change tracking.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot or batch number for traceability and recall management. Critical for food, pharmaceutical, and regulated products.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of units originally planned or requested for this warehouse task as generated by the WMS.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the product involved in this warehouse task. Represents the specific item variant being handled.']
  source_zone varchar [note: 'Logical warehouse zone classification for the source location (e.g., reserve storage, forward pick, receiving dock, returns area). Used for zone-based task assignment.']
  standard_labor_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Engineered labor standard time in minutes expected for this task type and quantity. Used for productivity variance analysis and incentive pay calculation.']
  substituted_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU that was actually picked or used if a substitution occurred, different from the originally planned SKU.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether a product substitution occurred during task execution due to stockout or allocation rules.']
  target_zone varchar [note: 'Logical warehouse zone classification for the target location. Used for labor planning and travel time optimization.']
  task_assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the task was assigned to a warehouse operator. Used for measuring task queue time and labor allocation efficiency.']
  task_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the task was successfully completed or closed. Used for cycle time analysis and throughput measurement.']
  task_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warehouse task was originally generated by the WMS. Represents the business event timestamp for task creation.']
  task_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from task start to completion. Used for labor standards validation and engineered labor standards comparison.']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the warehouse task, typically generated by Manhattan WMS for tracking and reference purposes.']
  task_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for task execution sequencing within Manhattan WMS labor optimization engine. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for task interleaving and dynamic work assignment.']
  task_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operator began actively working on the task. Used for calculating actual labor time and productivity metrics.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warehouse task: pending (created but not assigned), assigned (allocated to operator), in_progress (operator actively working), completed (successfully finished), short (completed with quantity variance), cancelled (voided before completion), suspended (temporarily paused), or error (system exception encountered). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|assigned|in_progress|completed|short|cancelled|suspended|error — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the type of warehouse task: putaway (inbound receiving to storage), discrete_pick (single order pick), cluster_pick (multiple orders picked simultaneously), batch_pick (multiple SKUs for multiple orders), zone_pick (picking within assigned zone), replenishment (moving inventory from reserve to forward pick location), move (internal location transfer), cycle_count (inventory accuracy verification), consolidation (combining picked items), or packing (preparing items for shipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: putaway|discrete_pick|cluster_pick|batch_pick|zone_pick|replenishment|move|cycle_count|consolidation|packing — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  travel_distance_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Calculated or measured travel distance in feet between source and target locations. Used for slotting optimization and labor planning.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity values: each (individual item), case (shipping carton), pallet (full pallet load), inner_pack (intermediate packaging), display_unit (shelf-ready packaging), or weight/volume measures for bulk goods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|inner_pack|display_unit|kilogram|pound|liter|gallon — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
