  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between planned quantity and actual quantity (actual minus planned). Positive values indicate overage, negative values indicate shortage.']

  Note: 'Represents any directed warehouse task generated within Manhattan WMS, covering putaway, picking, replenishment, cycle count, and internal movement task types. Captures task type discriminator (putaway, discrete_pick, cluster_pick, batch_pick, zone_pick, replenishment, move), SKU, quantity, lot/expiry, source location, target location, assigned operator, equipment type (forklift, reach truck, conveyor, AMR), task priority, task status (pending, assigned, in-progress, completed, short, cancelled), creation timestamp, completion timestamp, substitution flag, and exception reason. Unified SSOT for all directed work within the DC — eliminates separate putaway and pick task entities in favor of a single task management model consistent with Manhattan WMS task interleaving and labor optimization.'
}

Table supplychain.outbound_order {
  outbound_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution center outbound order. Primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to deliver this outbound order.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center (MFC) fulfilling this outbound order.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand planning forecast or consensus demand plan that triggered this replenishment order. Links outbound execution to upstream planning.']
  destination_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination node (store, customer, DC, 3PL facility) receiving this shipment. Polymorphic reference interpreted based on destination_type.']
  merchandise_category_id varchar [note: 'Primary merchandise category for this outbound order. Used for category-level supply chain performance analysis.']
  planner_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Replenishment planners create outbound store replenishment orders for planning accountability and performance tracking. Supply chain tracks planner accuracy, fill rates, and forecast vs. actual by pla']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional event or campaign driving this outbound order, if applicable. Links fulfillment to promotional planning.']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the replenishment plan that generated this outbound order. Links execution to planning layer.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse wave to which this order has been assigned for picking. Wave assignment groups orders for efficient batch picking.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the order was confirmed delivered to the destination. Null until delivery confirmation is received.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the order was actually shipped from the facility. Null until shipment occurs.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of lading number issued for this shipment. Legal document acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for order cancellation: inventory unavailable, destination closed, carrier unavailable, customer request, etc. Null if order not cancelled.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Transportation service level selected for this shipment: ground (standard), express (expedited), next-day, two-day, same-day, LTL (Less Than Truckload), or FTL (Full Truckload). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground|express|next_day|two_day|same_day|ltl|ftl — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this outbound order record was first created in the warehouse management system. Audit trail for record inception.']
  destination_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the shipment destination. Required for customer and store deliveries.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City name for the shipment destination address.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the shipment destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the shipment destination address.']
  destination_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the shipment destination address.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Type of destination node receiving this outbound shipment: store (retail location), customer (direct-to-consumer), distribution center (inter-DC transfer), 3PL node (third-party logistics partner), or supplier (return to vendor).']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Outbound dock door number from which this order was loaded for shipment.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered units successfully fulfilled and shipped. Calculated as (shipped units / ordered units) * 100. Key supply chain KPI.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this order was fulfilled via cross-docking (direct transfer from inbound to outbound without storage).']
  is_drop_ship boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this order is fulfilled via drop ship (vendor ships directly to customer, bypassing DC).']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this order requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or frozen goods).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this outbound order record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions for this outbound order. May include handling instructions, delivery requirements, or operational comments.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the outbound order was created in the warehouse management system. Business event timestamp representing order inception.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique order number assigned to this outbound shipment. Used for tracking and communication with destination nodes.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound order in the warehouse management workflow. Tracks progression from order creation through final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|released|waved|picking|picked|packing|packed|shipped|in_transit|delivered|closed|cancelled — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outbound order indicating the fulfillment purpose: store replenishment (push inventory to stores), e-commerce fulfillment (direct-to-customer), BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ROPIS (Reserve Online Pick Up In Store), inter-DC transfer (distribution center to distribution center), or DSD bypass (Direct Store Delivery bypass).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for order fulfillment. Critical orders (e.g., stockout replenishment, promotional merchandise) are processed ahead of normal flow.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Progressive number assigned by LTL carriers for freight tracking and billing.']
  replenishment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for this replenishment order: stockout prevention, promotional support, seasonal refresh, new item introduction, etc.']
  replenishment_type varchar [note: 'For store replenishment orders: method used to determine replenishment quantities. Push (DC-initiated based on forecast), pull (store-requested), min-max (reorder point triggered), VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory), promotional (event-driven), seasonal (seasonal assortment), or new store (initial stock). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: push|pull|min_max|vmi|promotional|seasonal|new_store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'Date by which the order is requested to ship from the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center. Used for planning and wave assignment.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the order must be delivered to the destination node to meet business commitments or replenishment schedules.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for this shipment: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (below -18°C), or climate-controlled (specific range).']
  total_cartons varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or cases packed for this outbound order.']
  total_cube_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of the outbound order in cubic meters. Used for trailer capacity planning and freight optimization.']
  total_lines varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKU line items included in this outbound order.']
  total_pallets varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets loaded for this outbound order.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units (eaches) across all lines in this outbound order.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the outbound order in kilograms. Used for carrier selection and freight cost calculation.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for this shipment. Used for shipment visibility and proof of delivery.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trailer or container loaded with this outbound order.']

  Note: 'Represents a distribution outbound order directing the DC or MFC to fulfill and ship goods to a destination (store, customer, 3PL node). Captures order type (store replenishment, e-commerce, BOPIS, DSD bypass, inter-DC transfer), destination node, requested ship date, required delivery date, priority level, total lines/units/weight/cube, wave assignment, and order status lifecycle (released, waved, picking, packed, shipped, closed). For store replenishment: captures replenishment type (push, pull, min-max, VMI), replenishment reason, demand planning source reference, and replenishment frequency. Central transactional entity for all DC outbound execution including store replenishment, e-commerce fulfillment, and inter-facility transfers.'
}

Table supplychain.outbound_order_line {
  outbound_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each line item within an outbound distribution order. Primary key for the outbound order line entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Transportation carrier assigned to ship this line item. Links to carrier master for service levels, rates, and performance metrics.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Distribution center fulfilling this outbound order line. Links to DC master for facility capacity, operating hours, and carrier relationships.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outbound distribution order header. Links this line to its containing order for fulfillment tracking and shipment consolidation.']
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Shipment consolidation record containing this line item. Links to shipment header for tracking number, carrier, and delivery status.']
  pack_task_id varchar [note: 'Packing task record for this line item. Links to packing station, carton assignment, and packing material usage.']
  pick_task_id varchar [note: 'Specific pick task assigned to a warehouse associate for this line. Links to task execution for labor tracking and productivity measurement.']
  primary_outbound_location_id varchar [note: 'Retail store or fulfillment node receiving this line item. Links to store master for address, capacity, and operational attributes.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master product record for this line item. Links to product master data for attributes, pricing, and merchandising information.']
  source_location_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location from which this line item was picked. Links to warehouse location master for zone, aisle, and bin tracking.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse wave or batch that included this line for picking. Links to wave planning for labor optimization and throughput analysis.']
  allocated_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units allocated from available inventory to fulfill this line. May differ from ordered quantity due to stock availability constraints.']
  carton_number varchar [note: 'Shipping carton or container identifier containing this line item. Used for package-level tracking and multi-carton shipment management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this outbound order line record was first created in the warehouse management system. Audit trail for order entry and system integration.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Enables multi-currency operations and financial consolidation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-before date for perishable goods. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking logic and shelf-life management.']
  extended_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost for this line (unit cost multiplied by shipped quantity). Represents inventory value transferred from DC to destination.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global trade item number (UPC, EAN, or GTIN-14) for the product. Enables cross-system product identification and EDI transaction processing.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling requirements or notes for warehouse and carrier personnel. Includes fragile, orientation, stacking, and security instructions.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and carrier certification per DOT regulations.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line requires refrigerated or climate-controlled transportation and storage to maintain product integrity.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to this line record. Tracks status changes, quantity adjustments, and data corrections throughout fulfillment lifecycle.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the outbound order. Establishes ordering and uniqueness of line items within a single order document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this order line. Tracks progression through warehouse workflow from allocation through shipment or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|cancelled|short_shipped|backordered — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number for traceability. Critical for product recalls, quality control, and regulatory compliance in food and pharmaceutical retail.']
  ordered_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units originally requested for this line item. Represents the demand signal from the destination location or customer.']
  original_sku varchar [note: 'Original SKU requested if a substitution was made. Enables demand signal accuracy and substitution pattern analysis for assortment planning.']
  packed_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units packed into shipping containers for this line. Confirms units staged for outbound shipment and ready for carrier pickup.']
  picked_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of units physically picked from warehouse storage locations. Reflects warehouse execution accuracy and inventory availability.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized items such as electronics, appliances, or high-value goods. Enables item-level tracking and warranty management.']
  shipped_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Final quantity of units shipped to the destination. Used for fill rate calculation, short-ship identification, and invoice reconciliation.']
  short_ship_qty decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity variance between ordered and shipped units. Represents unfulfilled demand due to stockouts, damage, or allocation constraints.']
  short_ship_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this line was short-shipped. Used for root cause analysis, supplier performance tracking, and inventory planning improvement.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal stock keeping unit code identifying the specific product variant being shipped. Used for inventory tracking and warehouse operations.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a substitute product was shipped in place of the originally ordered SKU due to stockout or allocation constraints.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific temperature control requirement for this line item. Drives carrier selection, routing, and cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for this line item at time of shipment. Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and profitability analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields on this line. Standardizes quantity interpretation across systems and trading partners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|GAL|LTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  volume_cubic_meters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of this line item. Used for trailer utilization, storage capacity planning, and dimensional weight pricing.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of this line item in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier capacity planning, and load optimization.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU within an outbound distribution order. Captures SKU/UPC, ordered quantity, allocated quantity, picked quantity, packed quantity, shipped quantity, unit of measure, lot number, expiry date, storage location sourced from, and line status. Enables granular fulfillment tracking and short-ship identification at the SKU level.'
}

Table supplychain.wave {
  wave_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wave batch. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse supervisor or shift manager responsible for overseeing execution of this wave.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Shipping carrier assigned to transport the outbound shipments generated from this wave.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Distribution center where this wave is being executed.']
  merchandise_category_id varchar [note: 'Primary product category for items in this wave, used for category-based wave planning and zone assignment.']
  actual_pick_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the last pick task in this wave was completed.']
  actual_pick_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the first pick task in this wave was started by warehouse associates.']
  assigned_pick_zones varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of warehouse pick zone identifiers assigned to this wave for zone-based picking strategies.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Shipping service level for this wave (e.g., ground, two-day, next-day, same-day) determining delivery speed and cost.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Destination channel for orders in this wave: store_replenishment (DC to store), ecommerce (DC to customer), BOPIS (buy online pick up in store), ship_from_store, wholesale (B2B), marketplace (third-party platform).']
  consolidation_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse staging area or dock door where picked items from this wave are consolidated for packing and shipping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wave record was first created in the WMS system.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Primary material handling equipment or picking technology allocated for this wave execution.']
  fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered units successfully picked, calculated as (picked_units / total_units) * 100, measuring wave execution effectiveness.']
  generation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to create this wave: scheduled (time-based automatic), manual (planner-initiated), demand_triggered (order volume threshold), threshold_based (inventory or capacity rule).']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wave contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and compliance documentation per DOT regulations.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wave contains orders driven by promotional campaigns or special marketing events requiring expedited handling.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wave requires temperature-controlled handling and storage (refrigerated or frozen goods).']
  labor_hours_actual decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual total labor hours consumed by warehouse associates to complete this wave, measured from task start to completion.']
  labor_hours_planned decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor hours required to complete picking, packing, and staging for this wave, used for workforce scheduling.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this wave record was most recently modified, tracking status changes and execution progress.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions for warehouse associates executing this wave (e.g., gift wrap required, fragile items, rush processing).']
  picked_units varchar [note: 'Actual quantity of units successfully picked and confirmed for this wave.']
  planned_pick_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for picking operations to complete for this wave.']
  planned_pick_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for picking operations to begin for this wave.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for wave execution sequencing, with lower numbers indicating higher priority for labor allocation and equipment assignment.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the wave was released to the warehouse floor for picking execution.']
  short_ship_disposition varchar [note: 'Business rule applied to handle short-picked units: cancel (remove from order), backorder (fulfill later), substitute (offer alternative SKU), split_shipment (ship partial now).']
  short_units varchar [note: 'Quantity of units that could not be picked due to inventory shortage or location discrepancies.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature control zone for this wave: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (33-40°F), frozen (0°F or below).']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the wave template configuration that defines the rules and parameters used to generate this wave.']
  total_cartons varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or shipping containers generated from this wave for outbound shipment.']
  total_order_lines varchar [note: 'Total count of order line items included in this wave batch for picking.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of individual units (eaches) to be picked across all lines in this wave.']
  units_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Productivity metric calculated as picked_units divided by labor_hours_actual, measuring warehouse picking efficiency.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing wave identifier used for operational tracking and communication across warehouse teams.']
  wave_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wave: planned (created but not released), released (available for picking), in-progress (actively being picked), short-closed (closed with shortages), complete (all lines picked), cancelled (wave aborted).']
  wave_type varchar [note: 'Classification of wave picking strategy: single-order (discrete pick per order), multi-order (batch pick across orders), cluster (cart-based multi-order), zone (pick within assigned zones), wave-less (continuous release), demand-based (dynamic priority-driven release).']

  Note: 'A wave is a batch grouping of outbound order lines released together for coordinated picking within a DC. Captures wave number, wave type (single-order, multi-order, cluster, zone, demand-based), release timestamp, planned pick start/end, total lines, total units, assigned pick zones, wave status (planned, released, in-progress, short-closed, complete), fill rate, and short-ship disposition rule. Wave management is a core Manhattan WMS workflow that drives labor scheduling, equipment allocation, and pick zone sequencing for optimal DC throughput.'
}

Table supplychain.outbound_shipment {
  outbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for outbound_shipment']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for moving the shipment from origin to destination.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, or dark store from which the shipment originates.']
  destination_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination entity (store, customer address, 3PL node, or DC) receiving the shipment.']
  wave_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse picking wave that generated the orders included in this shipment.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the shipment was delivered and received at the destination.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the shipment was delivered and signed for at the destination.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter code uniquely identifying the transportation carrier, assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shipment record was first created in the warehouse management system.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination entity type to which the shipment is being sent.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loading dock door from which the shipment was dispatched at the origin facility.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The carrier-provided or system-calculated expected delivery date at the destination.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the type of exception or issue encountered during shipment processing or transit (e.g., damaged, delayed, refused).']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any exception or issue encountered during shipment processing, loading, transit, or delivery.']
  freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total transportation cost charged by the carrier for moving the shipment from origin to destination.']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight cost amount.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or frozen).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shipment record was most recently modified or updated.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier manifest document that groups multiple shipments for a single carrier pickup or route.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional shipment instructions, special handling requirements, or operational notes.']
  on_time_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment was delivered on or before the estimated delivery date, used for carrier performance measurement.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Progressive number assigned by Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) carriers to track freight shipments.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the tamper-evident seal applied to the trailer or container to ensure shipment integrity during transit.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The transportation service tier selected for this shipment, defining speed and handling characteristics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground|express|same_day|next_day|two_day|ltl|ftl|parcel — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ship_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the shipment departed the origin facility.']
  ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the shipment departed the origin facility and was handed over to the carrier.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique shipment number used for tracking and reference across systems and with carriers.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound shipment indicating its position in the fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manifested|loading|staged|in_transit|delivered|exception|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on its business purpose and destination type.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Specifies the temperature control requirement for the shipment during transportation.']
  total_cartons varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or packages included in the shipment.']
  total_cube_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volumetric cube of the shipment in cubic meters, used for capacity planning and freight costing.']
  total_pallets varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in the shipment.']
  total_units varchar [note: 'Total number of individual SKU units included across all cartons in the shipment.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms, including packaging materials.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Primary carrier-assigned tracking number used to monitor shipment progress and delivery status.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or vehicle used to transport the shipment.']

  Note: 'Represents a physical outbound shipment dispatched from a DC or MFC to a destination (store, customer, 3PL node). Captures shipment number, carrier and SCAC code, service level (ground, express, same-day, LTL, FTL), trailer/vehicle ID, seal number, manifest ID, total cartons, total pallets, total weight/cube, ship date/time, estimated and actual delivery dates, shipment status (manifested, loading, in-transit, delivered, exception), and tracking numbers. Includes carton-level packing detail: pack station ID, operator, carton/tote ID, items packed (SKU, quantity, lot), carton weight/dimensions, void fill, shipping label reference, packing slip, QC check result (pass/fail/repack), and pack completion timestamp. SSOT for outbound shipment execution, carton-level tracking, and carrier manifesting within the distribution domain.'
}

Table supplychain.crossdock_transaction {
  crossdock_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cross-dock transaction event. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transporting the cross-docked goods to the destination.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center where the cross-dock operation occurred.']
  handling_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the handling unit (pallet, container, or carton) used to move the goods through the cross-dock process.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inbound shipment from which goods were received for cross-docking.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store or fulfillment location to which the cross-docked goods are being sent.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound order or shipment to which the cross-docked goods were allocated.']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion associated with the cross-docked merchandise, if applicable.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the product being cross-docked.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier who provided the inbound goods being cross-docked.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice number associated with the inbound shipment, providing pre-arrival inventory details.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of lading number for the outbound shipment, serving as a legal document and receipt for the goods.']
  carton_count varchar [note: 'Number of cartons or cases included in this cross-dock transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this cross-dock transaction record was first created in the system.']
  cross_dock_type varchar [note: 'Type of cross-dock operation: pre-allocated (pre-assigned to outbound orders), opportunistic (matched at dock), flow-through (continuous transfer), merge-in-transit (consolidation), retail-ready (floor-ready merchandise), or vendor-direct (DSD bypass).']
  destination_dock_door varchar [note: 'Dock door number or identifier where the goods were staged for outbound shipment.']
  dwell_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes that the goods spent in the cross-dock facility from inbound receipt to outbound staging, used to measure throughput efficiency.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or issue encountered during the cross-dock process (e.g., short shipment, damage, mismatch).']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any exception or issue that occurred during the cross-dock transaction.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for the product, providing international product identification.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling instructions or notes for the cross-dock operation, such as fragile, stack limit, or orientation requirements.']
  inbound_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inbound shipment was received at the source dock door.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cross-docked goods are classified as hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance.']
  is_promotional_merchandise boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cross-docked goods are part of a promotional campaign or seasonal event.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cross-docked goods require temperature-controlled handling (e.g., refrigerated or frozen products).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to the cross-dock transaction for operational reference.']
  outbound_staging_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods were staged at the destination dock door for outbound shipment.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets included in this cross-dock transaction.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number linked to the inbound shipment being cross-docked.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cross-dock transaction was flagged as high-priority for expedited processing.']
  quantity_cross_docked decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units successfully transferred from inbound to outbound without entering reserve storage.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the product being cross-docked.']
  source_dock_door varchar [note: 'Dock door number or identifier where the inbound shipment was received.']
  sscc varchar [note: 'Serial Shipping Container Code for the logistics unit being cross-docked, providing unique identification for tracking.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific temperature range or condition required for the cross-docked goods (e.g., 2-8°C, frozen, ambient).']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Trailer or vehicle number assigned to the outbound shipment carrying the cross-docked goods.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique transaction number for the cross-dock event, used for operational tracking and reference.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the cross-dock transaction in its lifecycle: planned, in-progress, completed, cancelled, or exception.']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods were physically transferred from the inbound dock to the outbound staging area.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the cross-docked quantity: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), LB (pound), KG (kilogram).']

  Note: 'Captures cross-docking events where inbound freight is transferred directly from receiving docks to outbound staging without entering reserve storage. Records inbound shipment reference, outbound order reference, SKU, quantity cross-docked, source dock door, destination dock door, transfer timestamp, cross-dock type (pre-allocated, opportunistic flow-through, merge-in-transit, retail-ready), handling unit ID, and priority flag. Supports DC throughput optimization, reduces storage dwell time, and enables vendor-direct-to-store flow programs common in grocery and apparel retail.'
}

Table supplychain.inventory_transfer {
  inventory_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory transfer transaction. Primary key for the inventory transfer entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier or logistics provider handling the transfer shipment. Links to the carrier master record for transportation tracking.']
  cross_dock_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this transfer to a cross-dock plan if applicable. References the cross-dock planning record for coordinated inbound-to-outbound movements.']
  destination_facility_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility to which inventory is being transferred. Target location for the stock movement.']
  origin_inventory_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_node. Business justification: Store-to-store and DC-to-store transfers require origin_inventory_node_id to track stock movement from specific nodes. Existing origin_facility_id targets dc_facility only; retail needs inventory_node']
  primary_inventory_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility from which inventory is being transferred. Source location for the stock movement.']
  replenishment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this transfer to a replenishment plan if the transfer is driven by automated replenishment logic. References the supply planning record that triggered the transfer.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this transfer to the physical shipment record. Connects the transfer order to the logistics execution layer.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product being transferred. Links to the master product record for the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) being moved.']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory was actually received and confirmed at the destination facility. Marks completion of the transfer transaction.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory was actually shipped from the origin facility. Used for performance tracking and transit time calculation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order was approved. Marks the authorization point in the transfer lifecycle for audit and compliance purposes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the transfer order. Used for audit trail and accountability in inventory movement authorization.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Ship Notice number sent to the destination facility. EDI 856 transaction identifier providing advance notification of incoming inventory.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code identifying the transportation carrier. Four-letter code assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) for carrier identification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order record was first created in the system. Audit field tracking the initial creation of the transfer transaction.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transfer value. Indicates the currency in which inventory costs are denominated.']
  destination_facility_type varchar [note: 'Type classification of the destination facility. Indicates whether the target is a Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), store, Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider, or vendor location.']
  expected_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory is expected to arrive at the destination facility. Used for receiving planning and inventory availability forecasting.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transportation cost incurred for moving the inventory between facilities. Used for total cost of ownership analysis and logistics expense tracking.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number for the product being transferred. International standard product identifier that may be UPC (Universal Product Code), EAN (European Article Number), or other GTIN format.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this transfer is part of a cross-docking operation where inventory moves directly from inbound to outbound without storage. True if cross-dock, false otherwise.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transferred inventory contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance. True if hazmat, false otherwise.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transfer order record was last modified. Audit field tracking the most recent change to any attribute of the transfer transaction.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number for the inventory being transferred. Used for traceability, quality control, and recall management, particularly for perishable or regulated goods.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the transfer. Captures special instructions, handling requirements, or contextual information for the transfer transaction.']
  origin_facility_type varchar [note: 'Type classification of the origin facility. Indicates whether the source is a Distribution Center (DC), Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC), store, Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider, or vendor location.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for the transfer order. Determines handling urgency and shipping method selection for time-sensitive inventory movements.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity received and confirmed at the destination facility. May differ from shipped quantity due to damage, loss, or discrepancies during transit.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory is requested to be shipped from the origin facility. Represents the planned or desired shipment date for supply chain planning.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for serialized inventory items being transferred. Provides unit-level traceability for high-value or regulated products.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity shipped from the origin facility. May differ from transfer quantity due to stock availability or last-minute adjustments.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code identifying the specific product variant being transferred. Unique item identifier used for inventory tracking and management.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the inventory requires temperature-controlled transportation and storage. True if temperature-sensitive, false otherwise.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific temperature control requirement for the transferred inventory. Defines the storage and transit temperature range needed to maintain product quality and safety.']
  total_transfer_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the inventory transfer calculated as unit cost multiplied by transfer quantity. Used for financial reporting and inventory valuation.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the transfer shipment. Used for real-time shipment visibility and proof of delivery.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transfer order number used for tracking and reference across systems and facilities. Externally visible identifier for the transfer transaction.']
  transfer_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units being transferred from origin to destination facility. Represents the planned or actual number of items moved in the transfer transaction.']
  transfer_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for the inventory transfer. Provides standardized classification of why the stock movement is occurring for analytics and operational planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rebalancing|replenishment|returns|excess_stock|damaged_goods|recall|seasonal_shift — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inventory transfer order. Tracks progression from draft through approval, shipment, and receipt confirmation.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer purpose. Indicates the business reason driving the inventory movement between facilities.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the inventory being transferred. Used for inventory valuation and financial accounting of inter-facility stock movements.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the transfer quantity. Defines how the transferred inventory is counted and tracked (individual units, cases, pallets, etc.).']

  Note: 'Records inter-facility inventory transfer orders and movements between DCs, MFCs, stores, and 3PL nodes. Captures transfer order number, origin facility, destination facility, SKU, transfer quantity, unit of measure, lot/batch, transfer reason (rebalancing, store replenishment, returns consolidation), requested ship date, actual ship date, receipt confirmation date, and transfer status. Distinct from inbound supplier shipments — this entity owns internal stock movement.'
}

Table supplychain.dock_appointment {
  dock_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dock appointment record. Primary key for the dock appointment entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for the shipment associated with this dock appointment.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse facility where the dock appointment is scheduled.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inbound or outbound shipment record associated with this dock appointment. Links appointment to shipment transaction.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor associated with inbound shipments for this dock appointment. Applicable primarily for inbound and cross-dock appointments.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier or trailer arrived at the facility gate or yard. Used to measure on-time performance and calculate dwell time.']
  actual_carton_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of cartons or cases handled during the dock appointment operation. Used to measure variance against expected quantities.']
  actual_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of pallets loaded or unloaded during the dock appointment operation. Used to measure variance against expected quantities.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual total weight in kilograms of the shipment handled during the dock appointment operation. Used to measure variance and validate freight charges.']
  appointment_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes allocated for the dock appointment from scheduled start to scheduled end. Used for dock capacity planning and scheduling optimization.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for scheduling and tracking dock appointments across systems and communications with carriers and suppliers.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dock appointment tracking progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|checked_in|at_door|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dock appointment indicating the nature of the operation: inbound receiving, outbound shipping, cross-dock transfer, live unload, or drop trailer.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Advanced Ship Notice document sent by the supplier or carrier to notify the facility of the incoming shipment details prior to arrival.']
  at_door_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the trailer was positioned at the assigned dock door and ready for loading or unloading operations to begin.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment. Serves as a contract of carriage and receipt.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the dock appointment was cancelled. Used for operational analysis and continuous improvement.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter unique identifier assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) to identify transportation carriers in North America.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier checked in at the facility and was assigned to the yard or dock door queue.']
  check_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier checked out of the facility and departed the yard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the dock appointment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Physical dock door identifier assigned to this appointment for loading or unloading operations. Represents the specific bay or door location at the facility.']
  dock_door_zone varchar [note: 'Logical zone or area grouping for the dock door (e.g., inbound zone, outbound zone, cross-dock zone, refrigerated zone) used for operational planning and resource allocation.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Commercial driver license number of the driver. Captured for security verification and compliance with transportation regulations.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the driver delivering or picking up the trailer for this dock appointment. Used for security and access control purposes.']
  dwell_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes that the trailer spent at the facility from arrival to departure. Key performance indicator for dock efficiency and yard management.']
  expected_carton_count varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of cartons or cases to be handled during this dock appointment based on advance shipping notice or order information.']
  expected_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of pallets to be loaded or unloaded during this dock appointment based on advance shipping notice or order information.']
  expected_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Anticipated total weight in kilograms of the shipment to be handled during this dock appointment. Used for capacity planning and equipment allocation.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and safety protocols.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the dock appointment record was most recently modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  late_arrival_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or reason code for why the carrier arrived late outside the scheduled appointment window. Used for root cause analysis and carrier performance management.']
  load_status varchar [note: 'Current loading state of the trailer indicating whether it is empty, fully loaded, or partially loaded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional instructions, special handling requirements, or operational notes related to the dock appointment.']
  on_time_arrival_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the carrier arrived within the scheduled appointment window. Used for carrier performance measurement and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  operation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the loading or unloading operation was completed and the trailer was ready for departure or yard staging.']
  operation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the actual loading or unloading operation commenced at the dock door.']
  original_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The initial scheduled start timestamp before any rescheduling occurred. Preserved for audit trail and performance analysis.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Unique shipment tracking number assigned by the carrier for less-than-truckload (LTL) shipments. Used for freight tracking and proof of delivery.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order associated with the inbound shipment for this dock appointment. Links appointment to procurement transaction.']
  reschedule_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this dock appointment has been rescheduled. Used to track scheduling volatility and carrier reliability.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the dock appointment is scheduled to occur.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the dock appointment window ends. Defines the expected completion time for the loading or unloading operation.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the dock appointment window begins. Used for dock scheduling optimization and carrier arrival planning.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the security seal applied to the trailer door to ensure cargo integrity and prevent tampering during transit.']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Celsius recorded for refrigerated or frozen trailers at check-in or during operations to ensure cold chain compliance.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification required for the shipment indicating ambient, refrigerated, or frozen storage and handling conditions.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or license plate number of the trailer or container involved in the dock appointment. Used for yard management and tracking.']
  trailer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trailer equipment type indicating the physical configuration and capabilities (dry van, refrigerated, flatbed, tanker, intermodal container).']
  yard_location varchar [note: 'Physical location identifier within the facility yard where the trailer is staged when not at a dock door (e.g., spot number, staging lane, holding area).']

  Note: 'Manages scheduled dock door appointments and yard trailer tracking for inbound and outbound operations at DC facilities. Appointment attributes include scheduled date/time, dock door assignment, appointment type (inbound, outbound, cross-dock), carrier, trailer number, supplier/PO reference, appointment status (scheduled, checked-in, at-door, loading/unloading, completed, no-show), actual arrival time, and dock dwell time. Yard management attributes include trailer yard location (door, spot, staging lane), check-in/check-out timestamps, seal number, load status (empty, loaded, partial), temperature readings for reefer trailers, trailer type (dry van, reefer, flatbed), and associated inbound/outbound shipment references. SSOT for dock scheduling efficiency, yard visibility, and trailer dwell time monitoring at DC facilities.'
}

Table supplychain.handling_unit {
  handling_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the handling unit. Primary key for the handling unit entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to move this handling unit, linking to the carrier master.']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center facility where the handling unit is currently located or was created.']
  destination_dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination entity (store, DC, customer, or vendor) where the handling unit is being shipped, interpreted based on destination_type.']
  parent_handling_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent handling unit in the containment hierarchy, enabling pallet-to-carton-to-each nested tracking.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the outbound shipment to which this handling unit has been allocated or loaded, linking to the shipment master.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor who provided the merchandise in this handling unit, linking to the supplier master.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Ship Notice identifier from the supplier EDI 856 transaction, linking the handling unit to the inbound shipment notification.']
  consumed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the handling unit was fully consumed or broken down, marking the end of its lifecycle as a discrete tracking unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the handling unit record was first created in the warehouse management system, marking the beginning of its lifecycle.']
  cube_m3 decimal(12,6) [note: 'Total volumetric cube of the handling unit in cubic meters, calculated from dimensions and used for warehouse capacity and freight optimization.']
  destination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination entity type for outbound handling units, determining routing and fulfillment logic.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date for perishable or date-sensitive SKUs, used for FEFO inventory rotation and quality control.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 global product identifier (UPC, EAN, or GTIN-14) for the SKU contained in the handling unit, enabling cross-enterprise product identification.']
  handling_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current disposition status of the handling unit within the distribution center lifecycle, controlling operational workflows and quality holds.']
  handling_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical handling unit form factor used for warehouse operations and material handling.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the handling unit in centimeters, used for space planning and carrier cube calculations.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the handling unit contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and transportation compliance.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the handling unit requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation to maintain product integrity.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent physical movement or location change of the handling unit within the distribution center.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the handling unit in centimeters, used for space planning and carrier cube calculations.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier lot or batch identifier for the SKU, enabling lot-level traceability and recall capability.']
  lpn varchar [note: 'Unique barcode identifier assigned to the handling unit for tracking through the distribution center. The LPN is the foundational Manhattan WMS tracking identifier enabling unit-level traceability.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the SKU was manufactured or produced, supporting shelf-life calculations and quality traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or exception documentation related to the handling unit.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the inbound receipt that created this handling unit, enabling PO-to-inventory traceability.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the handling unit is under quality hold, preventing allocation or shipment until quality inspection is completed.']
  quality_hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for quality hold, such as damage inspection, recall investigation, or expiry verification.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of the SKU contained within the handling unit, measured in the unit of measure specified.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Warehouse receipt transaction identifier generated when the handling unit was received and put away into inventory.']
  shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the handling unit was loaded onto a carrier and departed the distribution center for outbound delivery.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Primary product identifier for the merchandise contained in the handling unit, linking to the product master.']
  sscc varchar [note: '18-digit GS1 standard identifier for the logistics unit, enabling global supply chain traceability and EDI integration.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Current warehouse location identifier where the handling unit is stored, such as aisle-bay-level or zone-bin coordinates.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone classification for storage and transportation of the handling unit contents.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Trailer or container identifier where the handling unit is loaded for outbound shipment or was received from inbound delivery.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field, defining how the SKU quantity is counted or weighed.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the handling unit including contents and packaging, measured in kilograms, used for freight calculation and capacity planning.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the handling unit in centimeters, used for space planning and carrier cube calculations.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Logical warehouse zone classification for the current storage location, used for pick path optimization and inventory segmentation.']

  Note: 'Represents a physical handling unit (pallet, carton, tote, roll cage, each) tracked through the DC as a discrete unit load via License Plate Number (LPN). Captures handling unit type, LPN barcode, SSCC-18 identifier, parent/child hierarchy (pallet→carton→each), current storage location, contents manifest (SKU, quantity, lot, expiry), weight, dimensions, creation timestamp, last movement timestamp, and disposition status (active, in-transit, shipped, consumed, damaged, on-hold). LPN-level tracking is the foundational Manhattan WMS concept enabling unit-level traceability, inventory accuracy, lot recall capability, and quality hold enforcement. SSOT for physical unit identity within the distribution domain.'
}

Table supplychain.quality_hold {
  quality_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality hold event. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supervisor or quality manager who approved the disposition decision for the quality hold.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Quality holds are triggered by audit findings (temperature deviations, contamination, labeling violations). Hold disposition requires audit finding reference for regulatory traceability and corrective']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action. Business justification: Quality holds are released after corrective action completion and verification. Hold disposition decisions (release, destroy, return to vendor) require corrective action reference for audit trail and ']
  dc_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the distribution center or micro-fulfillment center where the quality hold event occurred.']
  escalated_from_quality_hold_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quality_hold (escalated_from_quality_hold_id)']
  handling_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the handling unit (LPN/SSCC) under quality hold, if applicable.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the inbound shipment under quality hold, if applicable.']
  primary_quality_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the employee who initiated the quality hold and performed the initial inspection.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the product master record for the item under quality hold.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier or vendor who provided the inventory under quality hold. Used for supplier performance tracking and chargeback processing.']
  affected_entity_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific entity under hold (e.g., LPN number, SSCC, location code, ASN number, lot number). The interpretation depends on affected_entity_type.']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity subject to the quality hold: handling_unit (specific LPN or SSCC), storage_location (entire warehouse location or zone), inbound_shipment (entire ASN or shipment), pallet (pallet-level hold), carton (carton-level hold), lot (lot or batch number hold).']
  affected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory units placed under quality hold. Measured in the unit of measure specified.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the disposition decision was approved by authorized personnel. Required for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advanced Ship Notice number for the inbound shipment containing the inventory under quality hold. Used for traceability and supplier accountability.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged back to the supplier for the quality hold event, including product cost, handling fees, and disposal costs.']
  chargeback_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the chargeback amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  chargeback_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quality hold event is eligible for supplier chargeback or cost recovery based on supplier accountability and contract terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality hold record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date when the disposition decision was executed and inventory was released, returned, destroyed, or otherwise processed.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the held inventory: release (approved for use), return_to_vendor (RTV for credit or replacement), destroy (dispose per hazmat or food safety protocols), rework (repackage or relabel), donate (to food bank or charity), transfer (move to alternate facility).']
  disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the disposition process, including actions taken, approvals obtained, and any deviations from standard procedures.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Product expiration or best-by date. Relevant for perishable goods and pharmaceutical items. Quality holds may be triggered when inventory approaches expiry.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'Global Trade Item Number (UPC, EAN, or GTIN-14) for the product under quality hold. Used for supplier communication and regulatory reporting.']
  hold_date date [note: 'Date when the quality hold was initiated and inventory was quarantined.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the quality hold event, used for tracking and reference in communications with suppliers and regulatory bodies.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason for the quality hold (e.g., TEMP_EXCURSION, DAMAGED_PACKAGING, CONTAMINATION_SUSPECTED, RECALL_CLASS_I). Aligns with internal quality management system codes.']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text description of the reason for the quality hold, including observations, inspector notes, and contextual information.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality hold: active (hold in effect, inventory quarantined), released (hold lifted, inventory available), pending_disposition (awaiting decision on disposition), escalated (requires senior management or regulatory review), closed (disposition completed and documented).']
  hold_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the quality hold was initiated, including time zone information. Used for SLA tracking and regulatory compliance.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality hold event indicating the nature of the issue: damage (physical damage to product or packaging), contamination (biological or chemical contamination), temperature excursion (cold chain breach), recall (supplier or regulatory recall), regulatory (compliance hold pending inspection), expiry (approaching or past expiration date), quality_defect (does not meet quality specifications). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: damage|contamination|temperature_excursion|recall|regulatory|expiry|quality_defect — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inventory under quality hold is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling and disposal procedures.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality hold record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer lot or batch number for the inventory under quality hold. Critical for traceability in food safety and recall scenarios.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the inventory under quality hold. Used for supplier communication and cost recovery.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for regulatory agency communication, recall notice, or compliance inspection related to this quality hold (e.g., FDA recall number, CPSC case number).']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the quality hold was released and inventory was made available for use, if applicable. Null if hold is still active or resulted in disposition other than release.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the quality hold was released, if applicable. Used for hold duration calculation and SLA compliance.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized items under quality hold (e.g., electronics, high-value goods).']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the product under quality hold. Denormalized for operational convenience.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location code (aisle-rack-shelf) where the held inventory resides, if applicable.']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Celsius at the time of quality hold initiation, if applicable. Critical for cold chain temperature excursion events.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone for the product under quality hold: ambient (room temperature), chilled (refrigerated 0-5°C), frozen (below 0°C).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), LB (pound), KG (kilogram), GAL (gallon), LTR (liter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|GAL|LTR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records quality hold and quarantine events applied to handling units, storage locations, or inbound shipments within a DC or MFC. Captures hold type (damage, contamination, temperature excursion, recall, regulatory, expiry), affected entity reference (handling unit ID, location, shipment), hold reason, hold date, release date, disposition decision (return to vendor, destroy, rework, release), approver, and regulatory reference. Supports food safety compliance (FSMA), cold chain temperature excursion tracking, and hazmat quarantine requirements. Essential for retail grocery and perishable goods operations.'
}

Table supplychain.po_shipment_receipt {
  po_shipment_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this purchase order shipment receipt record. Primary key.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse associate who performed receiving operations for this PO within this shipment. Used for accountability and productivity tracking.']
  distribution_inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the inbound shipment (ASN) being received at the distribution center']
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the purchase order being fulfilled by this shipment']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PO-shipment receipt record was first created in the WMS during receiving operations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this receipt record was most recently modified, capturing updates to status, quantities, or quality holds.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether goods received against this PO in this shipment are placed on quality hold pending inspection. Prevents putaway and inventory availability until released.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specific PO was receipted within the shipment. May differ from overall shipment receiving timestamps when a shipment contains multiple POs processed at different times.']
  received_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total quantity received against this specific purchase order within this shipment. Used for three-way match and supplier performance tracking.']
  receiving_status varchar [note: 'Current status of receiving operations for this specific PO within the shipment. Tracks progression from pending through completion or exception states.']
  variance_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Difference between expected quantity (from PO) and actual received quantity for this PO-shipment combination. Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage. Used for supplier scorecarding.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Receipt Event between inbound_shipment and purchase_order. It captures the operational mapping between ASNs (Advance Ship Notices) and purchase orders during receiving operations at distribution centers. Each record links one inbound shipment to one purchase order with receipt-specific attributes including received quantities, timestamps, variances, and quality holds. This is the SSOT for PO-level receipt tracking, supplier performance measurement, and three-way match reconciliation (PO-Receipt-Invoice).. Existence Justification: In retail distribution operations, a single inbound shipment (ASN) routinely contains goods for multiple purchase orders consolidated by the supplier or carrier for efficiency. Conversely, a single purchase order is frequently split across multiple shipments due to partial fulfillments, supplier capacity constraints, or phased delivery schedules. The receipt mapping between ASNs and POs is actively managed during receiving operations with specific data tracked per PO-shipment combination including received quantities, timestamps, variances, and quality holds.'
}
